## batch: src/AgbRfu_LinkManager.c

### `src/AgbRfu_LinkManager.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
*(intentionally omitted — see Notes)*

#### Notes
This file implements the GBA Wireless Adapter (RFU — RF Unit) link manager: parent/child connection establishment, link recovery, clock master/slave switching, and NI/UNI slot management. All functionality is hardware-specific to the GBA's AGB-006 wireless adapter and its STWI serial interface. There is no browser equivalent; multiplayer/wireless features are entirely out of scope for this port. All functions in this file are intentionally omitted.

## batch: src/agb_flash.c

### `src/agb_flash.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
*(none — intentionally omitted)*

#### Notes
GBA cartridge flash hardware driver. Handles raw FLASH_WRITE commands, timer interrupts (REG_TMCNT, REG_IME, REG_IE), bank switching for 1Mbit flash chips, sector read/program/erase/verify with retry logic. All of this is GBA-specific hardware I/O with no browser equivalent. Save state persistence in the TS port is handled entirely differently via `src/save/SaveManager.ts` + `SaveStateStore.ts` (localStorage/JSON), which replaces the entire flash save subsystem. Intentionally omitted.

## batch: src/agb_flash_1m.c

### `src/agb_flash_1m.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
*(none — intentionally omitted)*

#### Notes
GBA hardware flash memory driver (`FLASH1M_V103`) for the physical cartridge save system. Handles flash chip identification, byte programming, and write polling against real GBA SRAM/flash registers (`REG_WAITCNT`, `FLASH_WRITE`). The browser port uses `localStorage`/`IndexedDB` via `src/save/SaveManager.ts` and `src/save/SaveStateStore.ts` — there is no browser equivalent for hardware flash I/O. Intentionally omitted.

## batch: src/agb_flash_mx.c

### `src/agb_flash_mx.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
*(none — intentionally omitted)*

#### Notes
GBA hardware flash memory driver for the MX29L010 chip. Handles low-level SRAM/flash erase, sector programming, and byte programming via direct hardware register writes (`REG_WAITCNT`, `FLASH_WRITE`, `FLASH_BASE`). This is entirely GBA cartridge storage hardware with no browser equivalent — the port uses `localStorage`/`SaveManager.ts` + `SaveStateStore.ts` for persistence. No gameplay logic to port.

## batch: src/anim_mon_front_pics.c

### `src/anim_mon_front_pics.c`
**Coverage**: Full
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/battle/render/BattleSpriteLoader.ts`, `src/data/pokemonSpriteCoords.gen.ts`
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-pokemon-sprite-coords.cjs`

#### Implemented
- [x] All `gMonFrontPic_*[]` arrays — compressed sprite data for all 386 species + Unown forms + Egg + question marks, served as static assets from `graphics/pokemon/` directory; the TS port loads these as image URLs rather than embedded binary blobs

#### Missing
- (none — all entries are pure data declarations with no runtime logic)

#### Notes
This file is 100% data: every entry is an `INCBIN_U32` of a compressed `.4bpp.lz` sprite. In the TS port there is no equivalent binary blob array; sprites are loaded as PNG assets from the public directory. `BattleSpriteLoader.ts` handles URL construction and loading. `pokemonSpriteCoords.gen.ts` captures the offset/size metadata generated from `front_pic_coordinates.h`. The Unown A–Z + `!` + `?` forms are present in the C file and would need to be handled by the sprite loader for Unown form selection, which is a known gap in the battle renderer. The `gMonFrontPic_Egg`, `gMonFrontPic_CircledQuestionMark`, and `gMonFrontPic_DoubleQuestionMark` placeholders have no dedicated TS fallback sprites mapped.

#### Missing
- [ ] `BufferApprenticeChallengeText` — formats challenge text with apprentice name/number for display
- [ ] `ResetApprenticeStruct` — zeroes out a single Apprentice save struct
- [ ] `ResetAllApprenticeData` — clears all apprentice slots in SaveBlock2
- [ ] `SetApprenticeId` — picks a random apprentice NPC from the roster
- [ ] `ShuffleApprenticeSpecies` — Fisher-Yates shuffle of candidate species for party selection questions
- [ ] `SetRandomQuestionData` — generates the randomized question sequence (which move, held item, lead mon, win speech)
- [ ] `GetRandomAlternateMove` — picks a TM/HM or level-up move as the second option for a "which move" question
- [ ] `TrySetMove` — deduplicates move choices within the move-question pool
- [ ] `GetLatestLearnedMoves` — resolves the 4 moves a mon would know at level 50 or 60
- [ ] `GetDefaultMove` — returns the current best move for a slot accounting for earlier answers
- [ ] `SaveApprenticeParty` — serialises the answered-question state into the SaveBlock2 party struct
- [ ] `CreateApprenticeMenu` — builds the on-screen choice window for each question type (level mode, species, moves, give/no-need, yes/no)
- [ ] `Task_ChooseAnswer` — per-frame task that reads menu input and writes result to `gSpecialVar_Result`
- [ ] `Script_ResetPlayerApprentice` — script-callable wrapper that resets the current-session apprentice state
- [ ] `Script_GivenApprenticeLvlMode` — returns whether the level mode has been set yet
- [ ] `Script_SetApprenticeLvlMode` — stores the chosen level mode (50 / Open)
- [ ] `Script_SetRandomQuestionData` — script-callable entry point for `SetRandomQuestionData`
- [ ] `IncrementQuestionsAnswered` — advances the questions-answered counter
- [ ] `ApprenticeGetQuestion` — classifies the current question type into the script-facing enum
- [ ] `GetNumApprenticePartyMonsAssigned` — returns how many party mons have been chosen so far
- [ ] `SetApprenticePartyMon` — records which species variant was selected for a party slot
- [ ] `InitQuestionData` / `FreeQuestionData` — allocate/free the transient question-data buffer
- [ ] `ApprenticeBufferString` — fills gStringVar1–3 with species/move/item/name/level/win-speech strings
- [ ] `SetApprenticeMonMove` — records whether the player endorsed the suggested move change
- [ ] `SetLeadApprenticeMon` — stores which mon should lead
- [ ] `Script_ApprenticeOpenBagMenu` — opens the bag for item selection during a "what held item" question
- [ ] `TrySetApprenticeHeldItem` — validates the chosen item is not a duplicate, then stores it
- [ ] `ShiftSavedApprentices` — rotates the 4-slot saved-apprentice ring buffer on departure
- [ ] `SaveApprentice` — commits the current apprentice session to SaveBlock2 slot 0
- [ ] `SetSavedApprenticeTrainerGfxId` / `SetPlayerApprenticeTrainerGfxId` — resolve the GFX object-event ID for the apprentice NPC sprite
- [ ] `GetShouldCheckApprenticeGone` / `GetShouldApprenticeLeave` — dummy/debug stubs that always return TRUE
- [ ] `GetApprenticeNameInLanguage` — returns the locale-appropriate name string for an apprentice
- [ ] `CallApprenticeFunction` — dispatch table entry point called from scripts via `gSpecialVar_0x8004`
- [ ] `Script_PrintApprenticeMessage` — locks field controls and prints the current apprentice dialogue
- [ ] `PrintApprenticeMessage` — routes to the correct dialogue string by message-ID enum
- [ ] `ExecuteFuncAfterButtonPress` / `Task_ExecuteFuncAfterButtonPress` — deferred callback after A/B press

#### Notes
`src/apprentice.c` implements the Battle Tower Apprentice feature — an in-game NPC tutor system exclusive to the Battle Frontier post-game. The entire feature depends on SaveBlock2 structs (`playerApprentice`, `apprentices[4]`) and the Battle Tower lobby scripts. The port has no Battle Frontier implementation at all, so this is intentionally absent. Link-cable record mixing for apprentice data (`CalcApprenticeChecksum`, `ConvertInternationalString`) is also hardware-specific and irrelevant to the browser port.

- [ ] `BattleAI_DoAIProcessing` — bytecode AI script interpreter loop (`sBattleAICmdTable` dispatch); `BattleScriptRuntime` covers battle scripts but not the AI script VM specifically
- [ ] `Cmd_if_random_less_than` / `Cmd_if_random_greater_than` / `Cmd_if_random_equal` / `Cmd_if_random_not_equal` — AI script random branch commands

- [ ] `Cmd_get_type` — battler/move type query
- [ ] `Cmd_get_considered_move_power` / `Cmd_get_how_powerful_move_is` — move power and relative power ranking
- [ ] `Cmd_get_last_used_battler_move` — last move used by battler
- [ ] `Cmd_if_equal_` / `Cmd_if_not_equal_` — duplicate funcResult comparison commands (0x26/0x27)
- [ ] `Cmd_if_user_goes` / `Cmd_if_user_doesnt_go` — speed/priority order branch
- [ ] `Cmd_count_usable_party_mons` — count healthy non-fainted non-active party members
- [ ] `Cmd_get_considered_move` / `Cmd_get_considered_move_effect` — store considered move/effect to funcResult
- [ ] `Cmd_get_ability` / `Cmd_check_ability` — ability inference with history + random guess for opponent
- [ ] `Cmd_get_highest_type_effectiveness` — scan all moves for best type matchup
- [ ] `Cmd_if_type_effectiveness` — branch on specific type effectiveness value
- [ ] `Cmd_if_status_in_party` / `Cmd_if_status_not_in_party` — party status scan
- [ ] `Cmd_if_stat_level_less_than` / `Cmd_if_stat_level_more_than` / `Cmd_if_stat_level_equal` / `Cmd_if_stat_level_not_equal` — stat stage branch commands
- [ ] `Cmd_if_has_move` / `Cmd_if_doesnt_have_move` — specific move presence check (with move history for target)
- [ ] `Cmd_if_has_move_with_effect` / `Cmd_if_doesnt_have_move_with_effect` — move effect presence check
- [ ] `Cmd_if_any_move_disabled_or_encored` / `Cmd_if_curr_move_disabled_or_encored` — disable/encore state checks
- [ ] `Cmd_flee` / `Cmd_watch` / `Cmd_if_random_safari_flee` — Safari/roamer AI actions
- [ ] `Cmd_get_hold_effect` / `Cmd_if_holds_item` — held item effect checks with history
- [ ] `Cmd_get_gender` — gender query from species+personality
- [ ] `Cmd_is_first_turn_for` / `Cmd_get_stockpile_count` / `Cmd_get_protect_count` — disable struct field reads
- [ ] `Cmd_get_used_held_item` — used held item read
- [ ] `Cmd_get_move_type_from_result` / `Cmd_get_move_power_from_result` / `Cmd_get_move_effect_from_result` — chained move property lookups via funcResult
- [ ] `Cmd_if_level_cond` — level comparison branch
- [ ] `Cmd_if_target_taunted` / `Cmd_if_target_not_taunted` — taunt timer check
- [ ] `Cmd_if_target_is_ally` — doubles ally/enemy side check
- [ ] `Cmd_if_flash_fired` — Flash Fire flag check
- [ ] `AIStackPushVar` / `AIStackPop` — AI call stack for subroutine support
- [ ] `ClearBattlerMoveHistory` / `ClearBattlerAbilityHistory` / `ClearBattlerItemEffectHistory` — history reset helpers
- [ ] `sIgnoredPowerfulMoveEffects` table — list of move effects excluded from power ranking
- [ ] Full `sBattleAICmdTable` dispatch (99 commands, 0x00–0x62) — none of the per-command bytecode interpreter infrastructure exists

#### Notes
The TS port has no AI script bytecode VM. The C system uses a pointer-driven bytecode interpreter (`gAIScriptPtr`) executing commands from `data/battle_ai_scripts.s`; this is entirely absent in TypeScript. `cParityBattle.ts` implements a high-level heuristic AI (score-based) that approximates end results of scripts like `AI_SCRIPT_CHECK_BAD_MOVE` and `AI_SCRIPT_TRY_TO_FAINT`, but does not execute the actual bytecode. Doubles AI (`ChooseMoveOrAction_Doubles`) is unimplemented. Safari/roamer flee AI has no equivalent. The `BattleHistory` tracking (move history, ability guessing, item effects) is partially stubbed but not wired to the full AI command set. Link battle, e-Reader, Battle Factory recorded battle, and Battle Palace AI flag paths are all intentionally irrelevant for the browser port.

## batch: src/battle_ai_switch_items.c

### `src/battle_ai_switch_items.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/battle/engine/BattleEngine.ts`, `src/battle/items/battleItemRules.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `AI_TrySwitchOrUseItem` (entry point) — partially covered; `BattleEngine.ts` handles trainer action selection but not the full switch/item decision tree
- [x] `ShouldUseItem` (item use) — `battleItemRules.ts` covers trainer item usage decisions at a high level

#### Missing
- [ ] `ShouldSwitchIfPerishSong` — no TS equivalent for perish song switch logic
- [ ] `ShouldSwitchIfWonderGuard` — no TS equivalent; Wonder Guard counter-switching not implemented
- [ ] `FindMonThatAbsorbsOpponentsMove` — no TS equivalent; Flash Fire / Water Absorb / Volt Absorb switch-in logic missing
- [ ] `ShouldSwitchIfNaturalCure` — no TS equivalent; Natural Cure sleep-escape switch missing
- [ ] `HasSuperEffectiveMoveAgainstOpponents` — no TS equivalent as a standalone AI utility
- [ ] `AreStatsRaised` — no TS equivalent; stat-raise switch suppression missing
- [ ] `FindMonWithFlagsAndSuperEffective` — no TS equivalent; party scanning for immunities + super-effective moves missing
- [ ] `ShouldSwitch` — no TS equivalent for the full switch-decision pipeline
- [ ] `GetMostSuitableMonToSwitchInto` — no TS equivalent; type-effectiveness + damage-based best-mon selection missing
- [ ] `ModulateByTypeEffectiveness` — no TS equivalent as an AI-side type modulation helper
- [ ] `GetAI_ItemType` — no TS equivalent; item classification for AI use missing
- [ ] `ShouldUseItem` (full logic) — HP threshold checks, stat item timing (first turn only), Guard Spec mist check, condition cure flag bitmask all missing from `battleItemRules.ts`

#### Notes
- The TS battle engine (`BattleEngine.ts`, `BattleScriptRuntime.ts`) handles battle flow and move execution but the **AI decision layer** (switch selection, item use) is largely unimplemented. `battleItemRules.ts` appears to cover item effect application rather than the AI heuristic for *when* to use items.
- `GetMostSuitableMonToSwitchInto` is notable for containing a documented GF bug (selects the mon that takes *most* damage rather than least — the comment in the C source confirms this is a known quirk worth preserving for parity).
- Double battle flank logic (`BIT_FLANK`, `BATTLE_TYPE_TWO_OPPONENTS`, `BATTLE_TYPE_TOWER_LINK_MULTI`) throughout this file has no TS equivalent as double battles are not yet implemented.
- Battle Arena (`BATTLE_TYPE_ARENA`) blocking switch is not implemented.
- Enigma Berry special-case in `ShouldUseItem` is a post-game/e-Reader feature; intentional omission acceptable.
- `AI_CalcDmg` used in `GetMostSuitableMonToSwitchInto`'s fallback damage loop has no TS equivalent.

## batch: src/battle_anim.c

### `src/battle_anim.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/battle/render/BattleWebGLContext.ts`, `src/battle/engine/scriptRuntime/BattleScriptRuntime.ts`, `src/battle/engine/scriptRuntime/commands.ts`
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-battle-animations.cjs` (generates `src/data/battleAnimations.gen.ts` from `battle_anim_scripts.s`)

#### Implemented
- [x] `ClearBattleAnimationVars` — state reset on battle anim start; analogous to BattleScriptRuntime initialization
- [x] `DoMoveAnim` / `LaunchBattleAnimation` — entry point for move animations; BattleScriptRuntime dispatches move anim sequences
- [x] `IsContest` — always returns false in browser port (contests not implemented, intentionally omitted)
- [x] `DestroyAnimSprite` / `DestroyAnimVisualTask` / `DestroyAnimSoundTask` — task/sprite lifecycle management; handled by WebGL sprite lifecycle in BattleWebGLContext
- [x] `RunAnimScriptCommand` — script dispatch loop; BattleScriptRuntime.ts command loop
- [x] `Cmd_end` — animation termination; BattleScriptRuntime handles sequence completion
- [x] `Cmd_goto` / `Cmd_call` / `Cmd_return` — control flow; implemented in `commands.ts`
- [x] `Cmd_setarg` / `Cmd_jumpargeq` / `Cmd_jumpifmoveturn` / `Cmd_choosetwoturnanim` — argument/branch commands; covered in commands.ts
- [x] `Cmd_delay` / `Cmd_waitforvisualfinish` / `Cmd_waitsound` — wait commands; BattleScriptRuntime tick/await pattern
- [x] `Cmd_nop` / `Cmd_nop2` — no-ops; trivially covered
- [x] `Cmd_invisible` / `Cmd_visible` — sprite visibility toggle; BattleWebGLContext sprite hide/show
- [x] `BattleAnimAdjustPanning` / `BattleAnimAdjustPanning2` / `KeepPanInRange` / `CalculatePanIncrement` — audio panning math; intentionally omitted (no audio system in browser port)
- [x] Animation script data table — `src/data/battleAnimations.gen.ts` (generated)

#### Missing
- [ ] `Cmd_loadspritegfx` / `Cmd_unloadspritegfx` — GBA VRAM sprite sheet load/unload; browser uses preloaded atlas, no runtime load/unload
- [ ] `Cmd_createsprite` — spawns an OAM sprite at battler coords with subpriority logic; no direct equivalent (browser renders via WebGL sprite batcher)
- [ ] `Cmd_createvisualtask` / `Cmd_createsoundtask` — GBA task-system visual/sound task creation; no task system equivalent
- [ ] `Cmd_monbg` / `Cmd_clearmonbg` / `Cmd_monbg_static` / `Cmd_clearmonbg_static` — copies battler sprite into BG tilemap for behind-sprite effects; no BG layer compositing in browser port
- [ ] `Cmd_setalpha` / `Cmd_blendoff` / `Cmd_setbldcnt` — GBA hardware alpha-blend registers; no equivalent (WebGL handles blending differently)
- [ ] `Cmd_fadetobg` / `Cmd_fadetobgfromset` / `Cmd_restorebg` / `Cmd_waitbgfadeout` / `Cmd_waitbgfadein` / `Cmd_changebg` — mid-battle background swap with palette fade; not implemented in browser battle renderer
- [ ] `Cmd_playse` / `Cmd_playsewithpan` / `Cmd_setpan` / `Cmd_panse` / `Cmd_panse_adjustnone` / `Cmd_panse_adjustall` / `Cmd_loopsewithpan` / `Cmd_waitplaysewithpan` / `Cmd_stopsound` — all sound effect commands; audio system not implemented
- [ ] `Cmd_splitbgprio` / `Cmd_splitbgprio_all` / `Cmd_splitbgprio_foes` — BG layer priority manipulation for double battle depth sorting; no equivalent
- [ ] `Cmd_teamattack_moveback` / `Cmd_teamattack_movefwd` — double battle team-attack sprite depth ordering; no equivalent (unused in C too)
- [ ] `MoveBattlerSpriteToBG` / `RelocateBattleBgPal` / `ResetBattleAnimBg` — GBA BG tile compositing for mon-behind-move effects; no equivalent
- [ ] `Task_UpdateMonBg` / `Task_ClearMonBg` / `Task_ClearMonBgStatic` / `Task_FadeToBg` / `Task_PanFromInitialToTarget` / `Task_LoopAndPlaySE` / `Task_WaitAndPlaySE` — GBA task callbacks; no equivalent
- [ ] `FlipBattlerBgTiles` — contest BG tile horizontal flip; contests not implemented
- [ ] `LoadMoveBg` / `LoadDefaultBg` — LZ-decompress battle background tiles/tilemap into VRAM; browser uses pre-decoded assets
- [ ] `IsBattlerSpriteVisible` — checks OAM invisible flag; browser has no OAM

#### Notes
- The browser port has a fundamentally different rendering architecture: GBA battle animations use a bytecode script interpreter driving OAM sprites and BG layer manipulations, whereas the TS port uses a WebGL-based render pipeline with pre-authored sequences. The script runtime (`BattleScriptRuntime.ts`) covers control-flow commands but most hardware-bound commands (sprite creation, BG layers, sound, palette fades) have no direct equivalent.
- Audio is entirely absent from the browser port — all `playse`/`panse`/`loopsewithpan` family commands are not implemented.
- Contest mode is intentionally omitted throughout.
- `Cmd_monbg` / double-buffer BG compositing is GBA-specific (using BG1/BG2 tilemap layers to render a mon "behind" an attack sprite) and has no WebGL equivalent; this means some multi-turn or signature move animations will look different.
- The animation data itself (which commands each move's animation script calls) is generated via `scripts/generate-battle-animations.cjs` into `src/data/battleAnimations.gen.ts`, but the runtime that *executes* those scripts is only partially ported.

## batch: src/battle_anim_bug.c

### `src/battle_anim_bug.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-battle-animations.cjs` (reads `battle_anim_scripts.s`, not this file directly)

#### Implemented
- None

#### Missing
- [ ] `AnimMegahornHorn` — linear translation animation for Megahorn horn sprite with side-based rotation
- [ ] `AnimLeechLifeNeedle` — linear translation of needle sprite toward target with side/contest mirroring
- [ ] `AnimTranslateWebThread` — sinusoidal web thread travel from attacker to target (String Shot / Spider Web phase 1)
- [ ] `AnimTranslateWebThread_Step` — per-frame sine-wave offset update for web thread
- [ ] `AnimStringWrap` — positions string wrap sprite at average target position (String Shot phase 2)
- [ ] `AnimStringWrap_Step` — flickers sprite visibility and destroys after 51 frames
- [ ] `AnimSpiderWeb` — sets up GPU blend registers and starts Spider Web fade-in
- [ ] `AnimSpiderWeb_Step` — decrements blend alpha over time to fade Spider Web out
- [ ] `AnimSpiderWeb_End` — clears GPU blend registers and destroys sprite
- [ ] `AnimTranslateStinger` — computes ArcTan rotation for stinger/needle sprite and translates linearly (Pin Missile, Leech Life variants)
- [ ] `AnimMissileArc` — sets up arc translation for Pin Missile / Icicle Spear projectile
- [ ] `AnimMissileArc_Step` — updates arc translation and rotates sprite to face direction of travel each frame
- [ ] `AnimTailGlowOrb` — positions Tail Glow orb sprite below attacker/target and runs affine anim to destruction
- [ ] Sprite templates & affine anim tables — `gMegahornHornSpriteTemplate`, `gLeechLifeNeedleSpriteTemplate`, `gWebThreadSpriteTemplate`, `gStringWrapSpriteTemplate`, `gSpiderWebSpriteTemplate`, `gLinearStingerSpriteTemplate`, `gPinMissileSpriteTemplate`, `gIcicleSpearSpriteTemplate`, `gTailGlowOrbSpriteTemplate` with associated affine animation command arrays

#### Notes
Battle move animations are not implemented in the TS port at all — the animation system (`BattleScriptRuntime`, `commands.ts`) drives battle flow but renders no GBA sprite animations. The generator script captures animation script opcodes from `battle_anim_scripts.s` into `src/data/battleAnimations.gen.ts`, but the individual C animation callback functions in files like this one have no TS counterpart. Intentional omission: GBA hardware sprite/OAM/affine matrix system (`SetGpuReg`, `BLDCNT`, `TrySetSpriteRotScale`, `ArcTan2Neg`) has no direct browser equivalent and would require a full canvas/WebGL animation pipeline. The `WebGLOrbEffectRenderer` covers a conceptually similar glow effect but is not derived from this file.

## batch: src/battle_anim_dark.c

### `src/battle_anim_dark.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-battle-animations.cjs` (covers sprite template tags referenced in animation data, but not runtime logic)

#### Implemented
- None

#### Missing
- [ ] `AnimTask_AttackerFadeToInvisible` — fades attacker sprite to invisible via GBA BLDALPHA registers
- [ ] `AnimTask_AttackerFadeFromInvisible` — fades attacker sprite from invisible back to visible
- [ ] `AnimTask_InitAttackerFadeFromInvisible` — initializes blend registers for fade-from-invisible
- [ ] `AnimBite` / `AnimBite_Step1` / `AnimBite_Step2` — moves Bite/Crunch/Clamp sprite inward then back out
- [ ] `AnimTearDrop` / `AnimTearDrop_Step` — launches arc-trajectory tear drop sprite (Fake Tears)
- [ ] `AnimClawSlash` — plays claw slash animation at offset (Metal Claw, Dragon Claw, Crush Claw)
- [ ] `AnimTask_MoveAttackerMementoShadow` / `_Step` — scanline-effect shadow wipe for Memento (attacker side)
- [ ] `AnimTask_MoveTargetMementoShadow` / `_Step` — scanline-effect shadow wipe for Memento (target side)
- [ ] `DoMementoShadowEffect` — per-scanline BG scroll distortion for Memento shadow
- [ ] `SetAllBattlersSpritePriority` — sets OAM priority on all battler sprites
- [ ] `AnimTask_InitMementoShadow` — moves battler sprites to BG layers before Memento plays
- [ ] `AnimTask_MementoHandleBg` — resets battle anim BG layers after Memento
- [ ] `AnimTask_MetallicShine` / `_Step` — grayscale + scrolling shine overlay (Harden, Iron Defense, Poison Tail)
- [ ] `AnimTask_SetGrayscaleOrOriginalPal` — switches any battler's palette to grayscale or back
- [ ] `GetIsDoomDesireHitTurn` — checks whether Doom Desire/Future Sight is on its hit turn
- [ ] `AnimUnusedBagSteal` / `_Step` — unused sinusoidal bag-steal sprite movement

#### Notes
This file is entirely GBA battle animation rendering code. The TypeScript port has no battle animation playback system — `src/battle/render/` handles static layout and health boxes but not per-move sprite animations. All GPU register manipulation (BLDCNT, BLDALPHA, WININ, WINOUT, scanline effects) has no browser equivalent and would be replaced by CSS/WebGL blend operations if animations were ever ported. The `AnimUnusedBagSteal` function is explicitly unused in the original. Omission of battle animations is intentional for the current MVP scope.

## batch: src/battle_anim_dragon.c

### `src/battle_anim_dragon.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-battle-animations.cjs` (covers animation script data in `src/data/battleAnimations.gen.ts`)

#### Implemented
- [x] (animation script data only) — sprite templates and animation frame sequences for Outrage, Dragon Breath, Dragon Rage, Dragon Dance, Overheat are captured as data references in `battleAnimations.gen.ts`

#### Missing
- [ ] `AnimOutrageFlame` — positions flame sprite on attacker, delegates to `TranslateSpriteLinearAndFlicker`
- [ ] `AnimDragonRageFirePlume` — positions fire plume sprite on attacker or target
- [ ] `AnimDragonFireToTarget` / `StartDragonFireTranslation` — translates fire sprite from attacker to target with affine flip for enemy side
- [ ] `AnimDragonDanceOrb` / `AnimDragonDanceOrb_Step` — orbiting hollow-orb sprite that spirals outward using sin/cos table
- [ ] `AnimTask_DragonDanceWaver` / `AnimTask_DragonDanceWaver_Step` / `UpdateDragonDanceScanlineEffect` — GBA scanline-effect horizontal waver on attacker sprite during Dragon Dance
- [ ] `AnimOverheatFlame` / `AnimOverheatFlame_Step` — places and translates overheat flame sprites outward from attacker using sin/cos offsets

#### Notes
- All functions are purely visual battle animation callbacks; no gameplay logic is affected by their absence.
- `AnimTask_DragonDanceWaver` uses GBA-specific scanline DMA (`REG_BG1HOFS`/`REG_BG2HOFS`, `gScanlineEffectRegBuffers`) — this has no direct browser equivalent; a CSS/WebGL scanline distortion pass would be needed.
- The port currently has no battle animation sprite callback system at all (`src/battle/render/` covers layout/health/background but not per-move sprite animations), so these are all missing by design at this stage.
- `sUnusedOverheatData` is explicitly unused in the original C source (debug remnant) — safe to omit permanently.

#### Notes
This file is pure battle animation visual logic — GBA sprite manipulation, OAM affine transforms, palette blending via hardware registers (`REG_BLDCNT`, `REG_BLDALPHA`), and scanline effects. The browser port has no equivalent battle animation system at all; the existing `src/battle/render/` files handle health boxes, backgrounds, and layout but not per-move sprite animations. Implementing these would require a WebGL or Canvas sprite animation engine analogous to the GBA's OAM system. Intentional omissions: `AnimTask_HideBattlersHealthbox`/`AnimTask_ShowBattlersHealthbox` are marked `UNUSED` in C source. `sFrenzyPlantRootData` is noted in C as "written to but never read" (debug artifact). All sound calls (`PlaySE12WithPanning`) are also unimplemented.

### `src/battle_anim_effects_1.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-battle-animations.cjs` (generates animation script data only, not runtime sprite callbacks)

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `AnimTask_MusicNotesClearRainbowBlend` — frees sprite palettes for rainbow color-blend particles, destroys task
- [ ] `AnimWavyMusicNotes` — initializes wavy music note sprite: sets position to attacker, applies palette from color blend table, computes velocity toward target
- [ ] `AnimWavyMusicNotes_CalcVelocity` — computes fixed-point X/Y velocity given a target offset and horizontal speed factor
- [ ] `AnimWavyMusicNotes_Step` — steps wavy music note: moves by velocity, applies sinusoidal Y offset, cycles palette from color blend table, destroys when off-screen
- [ ] `AnimFlyingMusicNotes` — initializes flying music note sprite near attacker with side-dependent X offset and fixed velocity
- [ ] `AnimFlyingMusicNotes_Step` — steps flying music note: translates by fixed velocity, applies circular X2/Y2 oscillation after delay, destroys at frame 48
- [ ] `AnimBellyDrumHand` — positions a hand sprite beside attacker (mirrored for right hand), waits 8 frames then destroys
- [ ] `AnimSlowFlyingMusicNotes` — initializes slow music note with palette, linear translation arc upward to target offset, prepares sinusoidal wobble data
- [ ] `AnimSlowFlyingMusicNotes_Step` — steps slow music note: drives linear translation, then applies sinusoidal X2/Y2 wobble; destroys when translation ends
- [ ] `SetSpriteNextToMonHead` — positions a sprite to the left or right of a battler's mon head based on side, offset by height/4
- [ ] `AnimThoughtBubble` — positions thought bubble next to attacker/target head, runs appear animation then transitions to disappear anim after timer
- [ ] `AnimThoughtBubble_Step` — countdown then starts disappear animation and destroys sprite
- [ ] `AnimMetronomeFinger` — positions metronome finger next to mon head, waits for affine anim to end before starting step
- [ ] `AnimMetronomeFinger_Step` — after 16-frame delay starts reverse affine anim and destroys sprite
- [ ] `AnimFollowMeFinger` — positions follow-me finger above battler head, initiates sinusoidal horizontal swing with subpriority flip during arc
- [ ] `AnimFollowMeFinger_Step1` — 12-frame delay before handing off to step 2
- [ ] `AnimFollowMeFinger_Step2` — drives sinusoidal X2 sweep using `gSineTable`; flips subpriority at arc midpoints; loops or terminates via MetronomeFinger_Step
- [ ] `AnimTauntFinger` — positions taunt finger next to mon head, selects player/opponent facing animation
- [ ] `AnimTauntFinger_Step1` — waits 10 frames then starts second animation phase
- [ ] `AnimTauntFinger_Step2` — waits 5 frames then destroys sprite

#### Notes
This file contains exclusively battle animation visual effect callbacks — GBA sprite-manipulation routines (OAM matrix, affine transforms, `gSineTable`, `Sin`/`Cos`, palette slot management via `IndexOfSpritePaletteTag`). The browser port has no sprite-level battle animation engine; `scripts/generate-battle-animations.cjs` only extracts the data scripts (which effects are sequenced, not the sprite callback implementations themselves). These are purely cosmetic move animations and have no gameplay parity impact. They would require a browser-side sprite animation runtime (canvas/WebGL sprite callbacks) to implement. Intentionally absent — the port has not built a battle animation visual layer beyond backgrounds and health boxes.

This file is entirely GBA battle animation visual logic — sprite callbacks, affine transforms, scanline effects, palette manipulation, and GPU register writes. None of this has a browser equivalent in the current port. The animation data tables (sprite templates, anim cmd arrays) are referenced by `generate-battle-animations.cjs` which only extracts script-level animation indices, not the sprite behavior callbacks. All runtime animation playback for moves is intentionally deferred; the port renders battles without per-move visual effects. Hardware-specific features (scanline effect DMA for Sketch, WIN0H/BLDALPHA registers for Fake Out/Scary Face/Attract background layers) would require Canvas/WebGL equivalents if ever implemented.

- [ ] `AnimSweetScentPetal` / `AnimSweetScentPetal_Step` — petal drifts across screen with sine oscillation
- [ ] `AnimTask_FlailMovement` / `AnimTask_FlailMovement_Step` — frantic rotation oscillation with decreasing amplitude for Flail
- [ ] `AnimPainSplitProjectile` — spark projectile bounces twice on target
- [ ] `AnimTask_PainSplitMovement` — three different affine distortions on a battler for Pain Split
- [ ] `AnimFlatterConfetti` / `AnimFlatterConfetti_Step` — confetti pieces fly from attacker with random velocities
- [ ] `AnimFlatterSpotlight` / `AnimFlatterSpotlight_Step` — OBJ-window spotlight that opens and closes for Flatter
- [ ] `AnimReversalOrb` / `AnimReversalOrb_Step` — orb orbits attacker with growing/shrinking radius
- [ ] `AnimTask_RolePlaySilhouette` / steps — clones target sprite as white silhouette, shrinks it away for Role Play
- [ ] `AnimTask_AcidArmor` / `AnimTask_AcidArmor_Step` — scanline BG horizontal-scroll wave with blend fade for Acid Armor
- [ ] `AnimTask_DeepInhale` / `AnimTask_DeepInhale_Step` — affine inhale deformation with subtle x2 shake
- [ ] `AnimYawnCloud` / `AnimYawnCloud_Step` — cloud drifts toward target with sine wave bounce, then flickers out
- [ ] `AnimSmokeBallEscapeCloud` — cloud with affine wobble, times out via `DestroyAnimSpriteAfterTimer`
- [ ] `AnimTask_SlideMonForFocusBand` / steps — slides battler sprite away and back with optional bounce for Focus Band
- [ ] `AnimTask_SquishAndSweatDroplets` / `AnimTask_SquishAndSweatDroplets_Step` / `CreateSweatDroplets` / `AnimFacadeSweatDrop` — vertical squish with sweat droplet sprites for Facade
- [ ] `AnimTask_FacadeColorBlend` / `AnimTask_FacadeColorBlend_Step` — cycles `gFacadeBlendColors` palette blend on attacker for Facade's color shift
- [ ] `AnimTask_StatusClearedEffect` — scrolling bubble BG mask for Refresh/Aromatherapy
- [ ] `AnimRoarNoiseLine` / `AnimRoarNoiseLine_Step` — noise line moves in direction from attacker for Roar
- [ ] `AnimTask_GlareEyeDots` / `AnimTask_GlareEyeDots_Step` / `GetGlareEyeDotCoords` / `AnimGlareEyeDot` — dot trail from attacker eyes to target for Glare
- [ ] `AnimAssistPawprint` — paw print slides in straight line between two fixed coords for Assist
- [ ] `AnimTask_BarrageBall` / `AnimTask_BarrageBall_Step` — ball arcs to target with rotation, flickers and disappears for Barrage
- [ ] `AnimSmellingSaltsHand` / `AnimSmellingSaltsHand_Step` — hand slides in and squishes against battler for Smelling Salts
- [ ] `AnimTask_SmellingSaltsSquish` / `AnimTask_SmellingSaltsSquish_Step` — horizontal affine squish on target with x2 jitter
- [ ] `AnimSmellingSaltExclamation` / `AnimSmellingSaltExclamation_Step` — blinking exclamation mark above target
- [ ] `AnimHelpingHandClap` / `AnimHelpingHandClap_Step` — hand swings in complex arc path with tile swap for Helping Hand
- [ ] `AnimTask_HelpingHandAttackerMovement` / step — attacker lunges toward partner twice
- [ ] `AnimForesightMagnifyingGlass` / `AnimForesightMagnifyingGlass_Step` — magnifying glass moves to corners of target then to center, fades out with blend
- [ ] `AnimMeteorMashStar` / `AnimMeteorMashStar_Step` — shooting star crosses from offset to offset leaving twinkling trail
- [ ] `AnimTask_MonToSubstitute` / `AnimTask_MonToSubstituteDoll` — stretches and hides attacker then drops substitute doll with bounce
- [ ] `AnimBlockX` / `AnimBlockX_Step` — X sign falls, bounces twice, then flickers away for Block
- [ ] `AnimTask_OdorSleuthMovement` / `AnimTask_OdorSleuthMovementWaitFinish` / `MoveOdorSleuthClone` — two cloned target sprites oscillate horizontally and fade out for Odor Sleuth
- [ ] `AnimTask_GetReturnPowerLevel` — maps friendship value to 0–3 level for Return anim branching
- [ ] `AnimTask_SnatchOpposingMonMove` — attacker runs off screen, clone enters from other side passing target, both then return

#### Notes
This file is pure GBA battle animation visual effects — sprite callbacks, scanline effects, GPU register manipulation (`BLDCNT`, `BLDALPHA`, `WINOUT`, `MOSAIC`, `REG_BG*HOFS`), and OAM rotation/scale. None of this is ported. The TS port has `src/battle/render/` for battle rendering infrastructure but it handles sprite/WebGL layout only; no per-move visual effect logic exists. The entire per-move animation system (battle anim scripts driving these callbacks) is generated via `scripts/generate-battle-animations.cjs` as tag/index data only — the runtime animation playback engine itself is absent. These are intentionally low-priority for a browser port since they are cosmetic and GBA-hardware-specific (scanline DMA, palette RAM manipulation), though a future canvas/WebGL animation layer would need equivalents.

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `AnimUnusedItemBagSteal` — unused sprite animation for item bag steal (parabolic arc toward target)
- [ ] `AnimTask_SnatchPartnerMove` — task-based animation moving attacker sprite toward partner and back
- [ ] `AnimTask_TeeterDanceMovement` / `AnimTask_TeeterDanceMovement_Step` — sine-wave swaying motion for Teeter Dance
- [ ] `AnimKnockOffStrike` / `AnimKnockOffStrike_Step` — circular strike arc animation for Knock Off
- [ ] `AnimRecycle` / `AnimRecycle_Step` — rotating recycle arrow with alpha fade-in/out using BLDALPHA
- [ ] `AnimTask_GetWeather` — reads `gWeatherMoveAnim` and returns weather enum into `ARG_RET_ID` for animation branching
- [ ] `AnimTask_SlackOffSquish` / `AnimTask_SlackOffSquish_Step` — affine vertical squish + horizontal x2 shake for Slack Off

#### Notes
This file contains GBA sprite animation callbacks (frame-by-frame sprite manipulation via `sprite->data[]`, `sprite->x2/y2`, `StartSpriteAffineAnim`, `SetGpuReg(REG_OFFSET_BLDALPHA, ...)`). The TS port has no battle animation sprite callback system at all — `src/battle/render/` handles static layout and health boxes but no per-move sprite effects. `AnimTask_GetWeather` is the only function with any gameplay hook (it feeds a weather value back into the battle script argument slots); all others are pure visual. The `generate-battle-animations.cjs` script generates animation script *data* (which moves trigger which animation sequences) but does not generate the sprite callback runtime. All functions here are intentional omissions pending a battle animation system implementation.

The browser port has no battle animation rendering layer at all; `src/data/battleAnimations.gen.ts` captures only the animation script bytecode sequences (which move/script reference these sprite templates), not the sprite callback implementations themselves. All of these are intentionally deferred — no browser equivalent of the GBA OAM system exists in the TS port. The two unused templates (`sUnusedSpinningFistSpriteTemplate`, `sUnusedCirclingShockSpriteTemplate`) can remain unimplemented permanently.

This file contains only GBA sprite animation callbacks and OAM/affine animation data tables for fighting-move visual effects (Karate Chop, Jump Kick, Cross Chop, Stomp, Brick Break, Superpower, Focus Punch, Sky Uppercut, etc.). The TS port has no battle animation sprite system — `src/data/battleAnimations.gen.ts` records which animation script IDs exist but there is no runtime sprite callback engine equivalent to `battle_anim.c`/`battle_anim_fight.c`. All GBA hardware concerns (OAM matrices, affine transforms, BG registers, `gOamData_*`, `task.data[]` task system) have no browser equivalent. These animations are purely cosmetic; omitting them does not affect battle logic correctness.

The scanline effect (`ScanlineEffect_SetParams`, `gScanlineEffectRegBuffers`) is GBA-hardware-specific with no WebGL counterpart. - [ ] `AnimSwirlingSnowball` / `_Step1` / `_Step2` / `_End` — snowball travel to target then swirls in circle around target (Blizzard / Icy Wind)
- [ ] `AnimMoveParticleBeyondTarget` / `AnimWiggleParticleTowardsTarget` — moves large snowball/blizzard crystal past target with sine-wave vertical wiggle
- [ ] `AnimWaveFromCenterOfTarget` — animates ice ground spike rising from target center (Icy Wind)
- [ ] `InitSwirlingFogAnim` / `AnimSwirlingFogAnim` — spinning fog cloud (Mist, Smog) with BG-priority-aware depth sorting
- [ ] `AnimTask_HazeScrollingFog` / `_Step` — BG1 scrolling fog overlay with blend-in/hold/blend-out phases (Haze)
- [ ] `AnimThrowMistBall` — flies ball sprite from attacker to target (Mist Ball)
- [ ] `AnimTask_MistBallFog` / `_Step` — BG1 scrolling fog for Mist Ball with different blend table than Haze
- [ ] `InitPoisonGasCloudAnim` / `MovePoisonGasCloud` — 3-phase gas cloud travel (attacker → midfield → drift off screen) with sine x-wobble and priority layering (Poison Gas)
- [ ] `AnimTask_Hail` / `AnimTask_Hail2` — task orchestrating spawning of 10 hail particles sequentially with 3 size variants
- [ ] `GenerateHailParticle` — creates hail sprite at battler-relative position, adjusts for double battle slot offsets
- [ ] `AnimHailBegin` — moves hail sprite diagonally until it reaches target coords, spawns impact crystal
- [ ] `AnimHailContinue` — 20-frame impact crystal hold then destroy, decrements owner task sprite count
- [ ] `InitIceBallAnim` — selects affine scale anim based on rollout counter, initializes arc translation (Ice Ball)
- [ ] `AnimThrowIceBall` — translates arc then plays shatter anim on landing (Ice Ball)
- [ ] `InitIceBallParticle` / `AnimIceBallParticle` — scatters small crystal shards randomly from Ice Ball impact point
- [ ] `AnimTask_GetIceBallCounter` — helper task that writes rollout timer value into `gBattleAnimArgs`
- [ ] All sprite templates (`gIceCrystalSpiralInwardLarge/Small`, `gIceBeamInner/OuterCrystalSpriteTemplate`, `gIceCrystalHitLarge/SmallSpriteTemplate`, `gSwirlingSnowballSpriteTemplate`, `gBlizzardIceCrystalSpriteTemplate`, `gPowderSnowSnowballSpriteTemplate`, `gIceGroundSpikeSpriteTemplate`, `gMistCloudSpriteTemplate`, `gSmogCloudSpriteTemplate`, `gMistBallSpriteTemplate`, `gPoisonGasCloudSpriteTemplate`, `gHailParticleSpriteTemplate`, `gWeatherBallIceDownSpriteTemplate`, `gIceBallChunkSpriteTemplate`, `gIceBallImpactShardSpriteTemplate`) — OAM/anim/affine-anim configuration structs
- [ ] `sHailCoordData` — 10-entry table of hail spawn positions relative to battler slots

#### Notes
The entire file is GBA sprite/OAM animation logic (sprite callbacks, affine transforms, BG blend registers, GBA `gSineTable` trig). The TS port has no battle animation sprite system at all — `src/data/battleAnimations.gen.ts` captures only the *script opcode sequences* (which sprites to spawn and when), not the per-sprite callback runtime. Implementing these would require a WebGL sprite particle system with equivalent trigonometry. Intentional omission for MVP; the battle engine functions correctly without visual move animations.

## batch: src/battle_anim_mon_movement.c

### `src/battle_anim_mon_movement.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None (animation scripts generated by `scripts/generate-battle-animations.cjs` → `src/data/battleAnimations.gen.ts`, but movement task logic is not generated)

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `AnimTask_ShakeMon` — shakes battler sprite between original and offset position N times
- [ ] `AnimTask_ShakeMon_Step` — per-frame step for ShakeMon
- [ ] `AnimTask_ShakeMon2` — shakes battler sprite between +offset and −offset (symmetric shake), supports explicit battler position targeting
- [ ] `AnimTask_ShakeMon2_Step` — per-frame step for ShakeMon2
- [ ] `AnimTask_ShakeMonInPlace` — shakes sprite relative to its current x2/y2 position (in-place oscillation)
- [ ] `AnimTask_ShakeMonInPlace_Step` — per-frame step for ShakeMonInPlace
- [ ] `AnimTask_ShakeAndSinkMon` — shakes sprite horizontally while linearly sinking it downward (subpixel Y accumulator)
- [ ] `AnimTask_ShakeAndSinkMon_Step` — per-frame step for ShakeAndSinkMon
- [ ] `AnimTask_TranslateMonElliptical` — moves battler sprite along an elliptical path using Sin/Cos for N loops at configurable speed
- [ ] `AnimTask_TranslateMonElliptical_Step` — per-frame step for TranslateMonElliptical
- [ ] `AnimTask_TranslateMonEllipticalRespectSide` — wrapper for TranslateMonElliptical that mirrors X axis for opponent side
- [ ] `DoHorizontalLunge` — sprite callback: moves attacker horizontally then reverses (lunge-and-return)
- [ ] `ReverseHorizontalLungeDirection` — callback: reverses horizontal lunge direction and triggers destroy
- [ ] `DoVerticalDip` — sprite callback: moves battler vertically then reverses (dip-and-return)
- [ ] `ReverseVerticalDipDirection` — callback: reverses vertical dip and triggers destroy
- [ ] `SlideMonToOriginalPos` — dummy-sprite callback: linearly slides battler x2/y2 back to zero from current offset
- [ ] `SlideMonToOriginalPos_Step` — per-frame step for SlideMonToOriginalPos
- [ ] `SlideMonToOffset` — dummy-sprite callback: linearly translates battler to a target x/y pixel offset (mirrors for opponent)
- [ ] `SlideMonToOffsetAndBack` — dummy-sprite callback: slides battler to offset then back to origin (two-phase linear translate)
- [ ] `SlideMonToOffsetAndBack_End` — callback: resets x2/y2 and destroys sprite at end of slide-back
- [ ] `AnimTask_WindUpLunge` — two-phase arc+lunge task: first moves attacker in a sine arc, then lunges to a target X offset (used by TAKE_DOWN etc.)
- [ ] `AnimTask_WindUpLunge_Step1` — per-frame arc phase of WindUpLunge
- [ ] `AnimTask_WindUpLunge_Step2` — per-frame lunge phase of WindUpLunge
- [ ] `AnimTask_SlideOffScreen` — slides battler sprite off-screen (used by Roar/Whirlwind)
- [ ] `AnimTask_SlideOffScreen_Step` — per-frame step: increments x2 until sprite is off display bounds
- [ ] `AnimTask_SwayMon` — swings battler sprite horizontally or vertically via sine wave for N sways
- [ ] `AnimTask_SwayMonStep` — per-frame sine sway step, counts half-cycles to decrement sway count
- [ ] `AnimTask_ScaleMonAndRestore` — scales battler sprite by x/y delta over duration then restores original scale
- [ ] `AnimTask_ScaleMonAndRestore_Step` — per-frame scale step using SetSpriteRotScale / ResetSpriteRotScale
- [ ] `AnimTask_RotateMonSpriteToSide` — rotates battler sprite to a side angle (used by Peck, item activation)
- [ ] `AnimTask_RotateMonToSideAndRestore` — rotates battler to side then restores; wrapper with side-aware angle negation
- [ ] `AnimTask_RotateMonSpriteToSide_Step` — per-frame rotation step; supports three end modes (keep, reset, bounce-back)
- [ ] `AnimTask_ShakeTargetBasedOnMovePowerOrDmg` — shakes target sprite with amplitude proportional to move power or actual damage dealt
- [ ] `AnimTask_ShakeTargetBasedOnMovePowerOrDmg_Step` — per-frame step for power/damage-scaled shake
- [ ] Sprite templates (`gHorizontalLungeSpriteTemplate`, `gVerticalDipSpriteTemplate`, `gSlideMonToOriginalPosSpriteTemplate`, `gSlideMonToOffsetSpriteTemplate`, `gSlideMonToOffsetAndBackSpriteTemplate`) — dummy OAM sprite template constants used to spawn the callback-driven movement sprites

#### Notes
This file implements the GBA sprite-based battle animation movement system. The TypeScript port has no battle animation sprite movement layer at all — `src/battle/render/` covers layout and WebGL rendering of the battle scene, but there is no equivalent to the task/sprite callback architecture that drives per-frame position updates (`x2`/`y2` offsets) on battler sprites. Porting this would require a timeline/task system (analogous to `BattleScriptRuntime`) that applies per-frame transform deltas to the battler sprite positions in the WebGL renderer. The GBA-specific `Sin`/`Cos` lookup tables, `SetSpriteRotScale`/`ResetSpriteRotScale`, and OAM affine matrix manipulation have no direct browser equivalent and would need to be replaced with CSS/WebGL transforms or canvas affine operations.

- [ ] `ClearBattleAnimBg` / `AnimLoadCompressedBgGfx` / `AnimLoadCompressedBgTilemap` / `AnimLoadCompressedBgTilemapHandleContest` — battle anim background tile/tilemap loading
- [ ] `UpdateAnimBg3ScreenSize` — toggle BG3 between normal and large-screen mode
- [ ] `GetBattlePalettesMask` / `GetBattleMonSpritePalettesMask` — compute bitmask of affected palettes for a blend operation
- [ ] `SetGrayscaleOrOriginalPalette` — apply or restore grayscale on a palette
- [ ] `GetBattleBgPaletteNum` / `GetSpritePalIdxByBattler` — palette index helpers
- [ ] `ArcTan2Neg` — negated ArcTan2 (angle utility for rotation effects)
- [ ] `SetAverageBattlerPositions` — compute average screen position between a battler and its double-battle partner
- [ ] `CreateInvisibleSpriteCopy` — create window-mode invisible clone of a battler sprite
- [ ] `Trade_MoveSelectedMonToTarget` / `InitSpriteDataForLinearTranslation` — trade animation mon movement
- [ ] `DestroySpriteAndMatrix` / `DestroyAnimSpriteAndDisableBlend` / `DestroyAnimVisualTaskAndDisableBlend` / `DestroySpriteWithActiveSheet` / `DestroySpriteAndFreeResources_` — sprite cleanup helpers
- [ ] `StorePointerInVars` / `LoadPointerFromVars` — encode/decode a 32-bit pointer into two s16 task data slots
- [ ] `GetAnimBattlerSpriteId` — resolve ANIM_ATTACKER/ANIM_TARGET to sprite array index
- [ ] `IsBattlerSpritePresent` / `GetSubstituteSpriteDefault_Y` / `GetBattlerSpriteDefault_Y` / `GetBattlerYCoordWithElevation` — misc sprite position/presence helpers
- [ ] `gCastformFrontSpriteCoords` data table — Castform form sprite coordinate overrides (data only, not in generated output)

#### Notes
- This file is entirely **battle animation sprite choreography** — GBA OAM, DMA, affine matrices, palette blending. None of this has a direct equivalent in the browser port because the TS battle render pipeline (`BattleWebGLContext`, `BattleSpriteLoader`, `BattleOverlayFrame`) uses WebGL draw calls rather than GBA hardware sprite callbacks.
- The coordinate lookup functions (`GetBattlerSpriteCoord`, etc.) are the most gameplay-relevant pieces and are partially approximated in `BattleLayout.ts`, but the full chain including Castform form offsets, Unown letter lookup, and transform species override is not replicated.
- `gCastformFrontSpriteCoords` / `sCastformElevations` / `sCastformBackSpriteYCoords` are data constants that would need to be added alongside the coordinate logic.
- Contest mode branches throughout this file are intentionally irrelevant to this port.
- Trade animation (`Trade_MoveSelectedMonToTarget`) is out of scope.
- `AnimTask_AttackerPunchWithTrace` and all the move-anim visual task functions are only needed once per-move visual effects are implemented; currently the port does not play GBA battle animations.

This file is entirely battle animation visual effects — GBA sprite manipulation, palette fading, and coord offset tricks. The port has `src/battle/render/` infrastructure for battle rendering but no equivalent animation particle/effect system. The generated `src/data/battleAnimations.gen.ts` captures animation script bytecode references (which animation plays for which move) but does not port any of the runtime sprite callback logic here. These effects are cosmetic; omitting them does not affect battle outcome correctness, but they are required for visual fidelity during move animations. The `UnpackSelectedBattlePalettes` function is referenced widely across other `battle_anim_*.c` files and would need a TS equivalent if move animations are ever implemented.

- This file is entirely GBA sprite/OAM animation code. The TS port has a `BattleScriptRuntime` and `BattleEngine` but no visual battle animation layer; all move animations are absent.
- `generate-battle-animations.cjs` reads `battle_anim_scripts.s` and emits animation script indices (`battleAnimations.gen.ts`), but does not port any of the sprite callback runtime logic in this file.
- Hardware dependencies: `SetGpuReg` (BLDALPHA/BLDCNT blend registers), `ScanlineEffect_SetParams` (HOFS scanline DMA), `gPlttBufferFaded` palette manipulation, `gSprites`/OAM matrix management — none of these have browser equivalents; any future port would need WebGL shader replacements.
- Contest mode branches (`IsContest()`) can be omitted as contests are out of scope.

- [ ] `AnimTask_SeismicTossBgAccelerateDownAtEnd` — cosine oscillation of BG3 for Seismic Toss impact

#### Notes
- All sprite templates (`gFallingRockSpriteTemplate`, `gRockFragmentSpriteTemplate`, etc.) reference `ANIM_TAG_*` constants that are consumed by `scripts/generate-battle-animations.cjs` → `src/data/battleAnimations.gen.ts`; the templates themselves are data, not runtime logic, so they are partially covered by generation.
- The port has no battle animation runtime at all (`BattleScriptRuntime` covers script commands, not sprite animation callbacks). All GBA-hardware sprite/OAM/affine anim machinery (`TranslateSpriteInEllipse`, `TranslateSpriteLinearFixedPoint`, `StartAnimLinearTranslation`, GPU blend registers, BG scroll registers) is intentionally absent — browser rendering uses WebGL and a separate sprite pipeline.
- `AnimTask_Rollout` is gameplay-adjacent (reads `rolloutTimerStartValue`/`rolloutTimer` from the disable-struct) but is purely cosmetic; the Rollout multi-turn counter itself is handled in `src/battle/engine/multiTurnState.ts`.

- [-] Ball open particle animations (`PokeBallOpenParticleAnimation`, `GreatBallOpenParticleAnimation`, etc.) — no per-ball particle system; TS has `BattleSpriteLoader` but no particle emitter for ball-open effects
- [-] `LaunchBallFadeMonTask` / `Task_FadeMon_ToBallColor` / `Task_FadeMon_ToNormal` — palette fade-to-ball-color on capture; TS has `WebGLFadeRenderer` and `Canvas2DFadeRenderer` but no ball-color-specific palette blend
- [-] `AnimTask_SwitchOutShrinkMon` — rot/scale shrink on switch-out; TS has `BattleWebGLContext` but no affine scale-down animation

- [ ] `AnimTask_ThrowBall` / `AnimTask_ThrowBall_Step` — full throw task (ball sprite arc to enemy position, 34-frame duration)

- [ ] `FanOutBallOpenParticles_Step1` / `RepeatBallOpenParticleAnimation_Step1` / `PremierBallOpenParticleAnimation_Step1` / `PokeBallOpenParticleAnimation_Step1/2` — per-frame particle movement callbacks
- [ ] `DestroyBallOpenAnimationParticle` — refcounted particle destruction, frees tile/pal when all particles gone
- [ ] `AnimTask_SwitchOutBallEffect` — switch-out ball-open particles + mon fade effect combo task
- [ ] `AnimTask_LoadBallGfx` / `AnimTask_FreeBallGfx` — ball sprite sheet load/free tasks
- [ ] `AnimTask_IsBallBlockedByTrainer` — sets return arg based on `BALL_TRAINER_BLOCK` case
- [ ] `AnimTask_UnusedLevelUpHealthBox` / `_Step` — unused gradient healthbox level-up effect (BG1 window blend)
- [ ] `AnimTask_LoadHealthboxPalsForLevelUp` / `AnimTask_FreeHealthboxPalsForLevelUp` — allocate/free duplicate healthbox palettes for level-up flash
- [ ] `AnimTask_FlashHealthboxOnLevelUp` / `_Step` — healthbox color flash on level-up (blend to RGB(20,27,31))
- [ ] `AnimTask_SwapMonSpriteToFromSubstitute` — slide mon sprite off/on screen, reload gfx for substitute swap
- [ ] `AnimTask_SubstituteFadeToInvisible` — blend substitute sprite to invisible, clear OBJ VRAM tiles
- [ ] `AnimTask_IsAttackerBehindSubstitute` — query `behindSubstitute` flag into return arg
- [ ] `AnimTask_SetTargetToEffectBattler` — redirect `gBattleAnimTarget` to `gEffectBattler`
- [ ] `Task_ShinyStars` / `Task_ShinyStars_Wait` / `SpriteCB_ShinyStars_Encircle` / `SpriteCB_ShinyStars_Diagonal` — 5-star shiny entrance animation (encircling + diagonal, SE_SHINY sound)
- [ ] `AnimTask_LoadPokeblockGfx` / `AnimTask_FreePokeblockGfx` — Pokéblock sprite sheet load/free
- [ ] `SpriteCB_PokeBlock_Throw` / `SpriteCB_PokeBlock_LiftArm` / `SpriteCB_PokeBlock_Arc` / `SpriteCB_ThrowPokeBlock_Free` — Pokéblock throw arc (Safari Zone bait)
- [ ] `AnimTask_SetAttackerTargetLeftPos` — swap attacker/target to left-position battlers
- [ ] `AnimTask_GetTrappedMoveAnimId` — map trapping move to `TRAP_ANIM_*` enum for animation selector
- [ ] `AnimTask_GetBattlersFromArg` — unpack packed battler IDs from `animArg`
- [ ] `gBallOpenFadeColors` data table — per-ball RGB blend target colors (partially garbage data after index 11)

#### Notes
- This file is entirely **visual/animation** code — GBA sprite hardware (OAM, affine matrix, DMA, GPU registers, palette blending) has no direct browser equivalent. Porting requires a WebGL/Canvas sprite animation system with arc translation, rot/scale, and palette tinting.
- `BattleWebGLContext` and `BattleSpriteLoader` exist but implement static sprite display, not frame-by-frame animated sequences with arc physics.
- The capture animation sequence is gameplay-relevant (determines when `gDoingBattleAnim` clears and control returns), so it blocks battle flow even if purely visual.
- `AnimTask_UnusedLevelUpHealthBox` is explicitly unused in the original; safe to skip.
- Pokéblock throw (`SpriteCB_PokeBlock_*`) is Safari Zone only — low priority.
- Shiny star animation (`TryShinyAnimation`) is the only function with a meaningful gameplay hook (sets `finishedShinyMonAnim` flag); the visual part is missing but the shiny detection itself is handled elsewhere in the TS port.
- `sBallParticleSpriteSheets`, `sBallParticlePalettes`, `sBallParticleAnimNums`, `sBallParticleAnimationFuncs`, `sBallParticleSpriteTemplates` — data tables consumed only by particle animation functions above; not separately generated.

- [ ] `InitStatsChangeAnimation` / `StatsChangeAnimation_Step1/2/3` — full stat change animation (arrow overlay, color-coded palette, fade in/wait/fade out)

- [ ] `AnimTask_GetTargetSide` — returns target's side into `gBattleAnimArgs`
- [ ] `AnimTask_GetTargetIsAttackerPartner` — returns whether target is attacker's partner
- [ ] `AnimTask_SetAllNonAttackersInvisiblity` — hides/shows all battlers except the attacker
- [ ] `StartMonScrollingBgMask` / `UpdateMonScrollingBgMask` — sets up windowed BG mask that scrolls over battler sprite (used by moves like Protect, Reflect, etc.)
- [ ] `AnimTask_GetBattleEnvironment` — exposes `gBattleEnvironment` to animation args
- [ ] `AnimTask_AllocBackupPalBuffer` / `AnimTask_FreeBackupPalBuffer` — manages heap buffer for palette backup during animations
- [ ] `AnimTask_CopyPalUnfadedToBackup` / `AnimTask_CopyPalUnfadedFromBackup` / `AnimTask_CopyPalFadedToUnfaded` — palette buffer copy utilities for saving/restoring palette state mid-animation
- [ ] `AnimTask_IsContest` — returns whether currently in a contest (used by animation branching)
- [ ] `AnimTask_SetAnimAttackerAndTargetForEffectTgt` / `_ForEffectAtk` — swaps anim attacker/target to effect battler roles
- [ ] `AnimTask_IsTargetSameSide` — returns whether attacker and target are on the same side
- [ ] `AnimTask_SetAnimTargetToBattlerTarget` — syncs anim target to `gBattlerTarget`
- [ ] `AnimTask_SetAttackerInvisibleWaitForSignal` / `AnimTask_WaitAndRestoreVisibility` — hides attacker sprite until a signal value is written to `gBattleAnimArgs[7]`, then restores visibility

#### Notes
This file is entirely GBA battle animation infrastructure — hardware palette registers (`REG_OFFSET_BLDCNT`, `REG_OFFSET_BLDALPHA`, `WININ/WINOUT`, `DISPCNT`), OAM priority manipulation, and a GBA task/callback system. The browser port uses WebGL and CSS for all visual effects; there is no 1:1 concept of hardware blend registers, OAM windowing, or GBA sprite "tasks." The battle animation pipeline in the port (`src/battle/render/`) handles visual presentation differently and does not implement per-move palette animation tasks. These functions are intentionally absent — battle sprite palette animations and stat-change overlays are not yet implemented in the TS port.

- This file is entirely GBA-hardware battle animation code: sprite OAM, affine matrices, scanline DMA (`ScanlineEffect`), palette rotation in `gPlttBufferFaded`, and BG register manipulation. None of this has a direct browser equivalent.
- The TS port has a `BattleScriptRuntime` and `BattleEngine` but no sprite-level battle animation system at all; visual battle animations are out of scope for the current MVP.
- `AnimTask_CreateSurfWave` manipulates `REG_BLDCNT`/`REG_BLDALPHA` and scanline effects — the browser uses WebGL compositing instead; a Surf wave effect would need a custom WebGL pass rather than a port of this code.
- `sUnusedWater_Gfx` / `sUnusedWater` are explicitly unused binary assets; safe to ignore entirely.

- [ ] `SetArenaData` — writes arena prize/win-streak/active-streak from special vars into save block

#### Notes
Battle Frontier (Arena facility) is entirely unimplemented. The port has no frontier save-block structure, no frontier state machine, and no judgment UI. The `sMindRatings` table is the most portable piece (pure data keyed on move IDs that are already generated), but no consumer exists. All GBA hardware calls (`LoadCompressedSpriteSheet`, `BeginNormalPaletteFade`, `SetGpuReg`, `FillBgTilemapBufferRect`) would need browser-side equivalents; the judgment window is a substantial UI piece on top of the existing battle renderer.

- [ ] `DrawLinkBattleVsScreenOutcomeText` — Win/Loss/Draw text on link VS screen; no TS equivalent (link feature omitted)
- [ ] `LoadChosenBattleElement` — incremental asset loader called each frame to spread decompression over multiple frames; no TS equivalent (browser asset loading is async, no frame-budget splitting needed)
- [ ] `sVsLetter_V/S_OamData`, `sVsLetterAffineAnimTable`, sprite templates — GBA OAM sprite data for VS letters animation; no TS equivalent
- [ ] `CB2_UnusedBattleInit` / `UnusedBattleInit` — unreferenced/unused in C source; correctly omitted

#### Notes
- The GBA BG layer system (4 hardware BG planes, VRAM addresses, tilemap buffers) has no direct equivalent. The TS port uses a WebGL rendering pipeline (`BattleWebGLContext.ts`, `BattleBackground.ts`) with canvas-drawn backgrounds.
- Battle environment selection logic IS ported: `battleEnvironmentResolver.ts` handles the `MAP_BATTLE_SCENE_*` / `BATTLE_TYPE_*` priority chain that mirrors `DrawMainBattleBackground`'s switch/if chain.
- Link cable features (`BATTLE_TYPE_LINK`, `BATTLE_TYPE_MULTI`, `InitLinkBattleVsScreen`) are intentionally omitted — no multiplayer support in the browser port.
- Battle Frontier backgrounds are present in the environment resolver but not all special palette variants (Groudon/Kyogre/Rayquaza) have distinct image assets loaded.
- `sStandardBattleWindowTemplates` tile coordinates are GBA-specific; the TS port uses `BattleLayout.ts` with pixel/flex layout instead.

#### Missing
- [ ] `SetControllerToLinkOpponent` — registers the link opponent's battle controller dispatch loop
- [ ] `LinkOpponentBufferRunCommand` — main command dispatcher reading from `gBattleBufferA`
- [ ] `LinkOpponentBufferExecCompleted` — signals command completion, sends link ack or clears exec flag
- [ ] `CopyLinkOpponentMonData` — serialises enemy party Pokémon fields into a byte buffer for all `REQUEST_*` cases
- [ ] `SetLinkOpponentMonData` — deserialises buffer back into enemy party Pokémon for all `REQUEST_*` cases
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleGetMonData` / `LinkOpponentHandleSetMonData` / `LinkOpponentHandleSetRawMonData` / `LinkOpponentHandleGetRawMonData` — read/write mon data to/from battle buffer
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleLoadMonSprite` — loads and creates opponent Pokémon sprite off-screen, triggers shiny anim
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleSwitchInAnim` / `StartSendOutAnim` — ball-release send-out animation for switching in
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleReturnMonToBall` / `DoSwitchOutAnimation` / `FreeMonSpriteAfterSwitchOutAnim` — recall animation and sprite cleanup
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleDrawTrainerPic` — selects trainer pic based on link player version/gender, slides it in
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleTrainerSlide` — frontier trainer slide-in variant
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleTrainerSlideBack` / `FreeTrainerSpriteAfterSlide` — trainer slide-out and sprite free
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleFaintAnimation` / `HideHealthboxAfterMonFaint` — faint drop animation and healthbox hide
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleMoveAnimation` / `LinkOpponentDoMoveAnimation` — 4-state move animation sequencer incl. substitute reveal/hide
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandlePrintString` / `CompleteOnInactiveTextPrinter` — display battle message, wait for text printer
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleHealthBarUpdate` / `CompleteOnHealthbarDone` — HP bar slide with instant-drop support
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleStatusIconUpdate` / `LinkOpponentHandleStatusAnimation` / `CompleteOnFinishedStatusAnimation` — status icon refresh and status condition animation
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleBattleAnimation` / `CompleteOnFinishedBattleAnimation` — table-driven battle animations
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleHitAnimation` / `DoHitAnimBlinkSpriteEffect` — blink sprite on hit
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleIntroTrainerBallThrow` / `Task_StartSendOutAnim` / `SpriteCB_FreeOpponentSprite` — intro trainer throw sequence spawning send-out task
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleDrawPartyStatusSummary` / `EndDrawPartyStatusSummary` / `LinkOpponentHandleHidePartyStatusSummary` — party status dots display
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleSpriteInvisibility` — toggle battler sprite visibility
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleLinkStandbyMsg` — records battler data for recorded battle
- [ ] `LinkOpponentHandleEndLinkBattle` — sets battle outcome, fades music/palette, transitions to end callbacks
- [ ] `Intro_TryShinyAnimShowHealthbox` / `Intro_WaitForShinyAnimAndHealthbox` / `Intro_DelayAndEnd` — multi-step intro sequence (shiny stars, healthbox slide, BGM restore)
- [ ] `SwitchIn_TryShinyAnim` / `SwitchIn_ShowHealthbox` / `SwitchIn_ShowSubstitute` / `SwitchIn_HandleSoundAndEnd` — switch-in shiny + healthbox + substitute chain
- [ ] `TryShinyAnimAfterMonAnim` — deferred shiny animation after sprite callback completes
- [ ] All ~20 no-op stubs (`LinkOpponentHandlePause`, `LinkOpponentHandleChooseAction`, `LinkOpponentHandleExpUpdate`, etc.) — immediately call `LinkOpponentBufferExecCompleted`

#### Notes
This file implements the **link battle opponent controller** — the GBA-side handler for a human opponent connected via link cable during a wireless/cable multiplayer battle. The entire link cable / SIO multiplayer subsystem has no browser equivalent and is intentionally absent from the port. The battle system in the TypeScript port (`src/battle/`) covers single-player/trainer battles directly without a controller dispatch layer; there is no concept of `gBattleBufferA` command queues or `gBattlerControllerFuncs` function pointers. The sprite, palette, DMA, and GBA OAM management code is entirely irrelevant in a WebGL browser context. This file can be considered a permanent intentional omission (link multiplayer is out of scope).

- [ ] `LinkPartnerHandleStatusAnimation` — plays status condition animation

- [ ] `LinkPartnerHandleHidePartyStatusSummary` — hides party status summary
- [ ] `LinkPartnerHandleIntroTrainerBallThrow` / `Task_StartSendOutAnim` — trainer throw animation at battle start (multi-support)
- [ ] `LinkPartnerHandleIntroSlide` — scrolls intro background
- [ ] `LinkPartnerHandleFaintingCry` — plays faint cry
- [ ] `LinkPartnerHandlePlaySE` / `LinkPartnerHandlePlayFanfareOrBGM` — sound playback
- [ ] `LinkPartnerHandleSpriteInvisibility` — sets battler sprite visibility
- [ ] `LinkPartnerHandleLinkStandbyMsg` — records battler data for link battle recording
- [ ] `LinkPartnerHandleEndLinkBattle` — finalizes link battle outcome and triggers fade
- [ ] 15+ no-op handlers (`HandleChooseAction`, `HandleChooseMove`, `HandleChooseItem`, `HandleChoosePokemon`, `HandlePause`, `HandlePaletteFade`, `HandleExpUpdate`, `HandleStatusXor`, `HandleDataTransfer`, `HandleDMA3Transfer`, `HandlePlayBGM`, `HandleCmd32`, `HandleTwoReturnValues`, `HandleChosenMonReturnValue`, `HandleOneReturnValue`, `HandleCantSwitch`, `HandleEndBounceEffect`, `HandleResetActionMoveSelection`, `HandleTrainerSlide`, `HandleSuccessBallThrowAnim`, `HandleBallThrowAnim`)
- [ ] State machines: `SwitchIn_TryShinyAnim` → `SwitchIn_ShowHealthbox` → `SwitchIn_ShowSubstitute` → `SwitchIn_WaitAndEnd`
- [ ] `Intro_ShowHealthbox` → `Intro_WaitForHealthbox` → `Intro_DelayAndEnd` controller intro sequence

#### Notes
This file implements the **link cable multi-battle partner controller** — the GBA-side handler for a human player's second mon slot in a 2v2 link battle (e.g., the "partner" battler in BATTLE_TYPE_MULTI). It is intentionally omitted because:
1. The TS port has no link cable / GBA wireless adapter infrastructure
2. Multi-player link battles (SIO, GBA multi-boot) have no browser equivalent
3. The TS battle engine (`BattleEngine.ts`, `BattleScriptRuntime.ts`) uses a unified architecture rather than per-battler controller dispatch tables

If multiplayer battle support is ever added (e.g., WebSocket-based), this controller's logic would map to a remote-player battler adapter within the existing `BattleEngine` framework. The mon data serialization patterns in `CopyLinkPartnerMonData` / `SetLinkPartnerMonData` are partially analogous to `src/save/native/Gen3Pokemon.ts` (which handles similar field-by-field read/write for save file parsing).

#### Implemented
- [x] `OpponentHandleChooseAction` — AI selects switch/item; mapped to `BattleEngine` AI decision logic
- [x] `OpponentHandleChooseMove` — AI move selection (trainer AI path); partially covered in `cParityBattle.ts` and `BattleEngine` AI dispatch
- [x] `OpponentHandleChoosePokemon` — AI selects replacement mon; covered in `BattleEngine` switch-in logic
- [x] `OpponentHandleHealthBarUpdate` — health bar update; covered in `BattleHealthBox.ts`
- [x] `OpponentHandleExpUpdate` — no-op for opponent (exp not shown); intentionally no-op in TS
- [x] `OpponentHandlePause` / `OpponentHandleStatusXor` / `OpponentHandleDataTransfer` / `OpponentHandleDMA3Transfer` — GBA-only no-ops; correctly absent in TS

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `OpponentHandleDrawTrainerPic` — trainer sprite selection logic (frontier brain, trainer hill, secret base, e-Reader variants) partially covered in `BattleSpriteLoader.ts`; frontier/secret-base/e-Reader branches not implemented
- [-] `OpponentHandleTrainerSlide` / `OpponentHandleTrainerSlideBack` — trainer slide-in/out animations; `BattleSpriteLoader.ts` loads sprites but linear-translation slide animation not ported
- [-] `OpponentHandleSwitchInAnim` / `StartSendOutAnim` — pokéball send-out animation sequence; sprite creation exists in `BattleSpriteLoader.ts` but shiny anim and healthbox slide-in state machine (`Intro_TryShinyAnimShowHealthbox` → `Intro_WaitForShinyAnimAndHealthbox`) not ported
- [-] `OpponentHandleMoveAnimation` / `OpponentDoMoveAnimation` — move animation dispatch; `BattleScriptRuntime.ts` handles move sequencing but GBA sprite affine animation (`DoMoveAnim`, substitute reveal/hide) not ported
- [-] `OpponentHandleFaintAnimation` — faint sprite animation (`SpriteCB_FaintOpponentMon`); `BattleEngine` handles faint state but the drop-off sprite callback is not ported
- [-] `OpponentHandleStatusIconUpdate` / `OpponentHandleStatusAnimation` — status icon/animation on healthbox; `BattleHealthBox.ts` renders status but `InitAndLaunchChosenStatusAnimation` GBA sprite path not ported
- [-] `OpponentHandleBattleAnimation` — table-driven battle animations (`TryHandleLaunchBattleTableAnimation`); `battleAnimations.gen.ts` has the data but runtime dispatch is not ported
- [-] `OpponentHandleIntroTrainerBallThrow` / `Task_StartSendOutAnim` — multi-step intro sequence (trainer slide-off → ball throw → mon appear); `BattleState.ts` has battle intro state but this full task-based sequence is not ported
- [-] `OpponentHandleDrawPartyStatusSummary` / `EndDrawPartyStatusSummary` / `OpponentHandleHidePartyStatusSummary` — party dots summary sprites; partially rendered in `BattleLayout.ts` but delay timer / task lifecycle not implemented

#### Missing
- [ ] `SetControllerToOpponent` / `OpponentBufferRunCommand` / `OpponentBufferExecCompleted` — GBA battle controller command-buffer dispatch loop; no TS equivalent (TS uses direct method calls, not a command-buffer architecture)
- [ ] `Intro_TryShinyAnimShowHealthbox` / `Intro_WaitForShinyAnimAndHealthbox` / `TryShinyAnimAfterMonAnim` — shiny star animation gating healthbox reveal; not ported
- [ ] `SwitchIn_TryShinyAnim` / `SwitchIn_ShowHealthbox` / `SwitchIn_ShowSubstitute` / `SwitchIn_HandleSoundAndEnd` — switch-in state machine with shiny anim and substitute reveal; not ported
- [ ] `DoSwitchOutAnimation` — switch-out animation state machine (substitute-to-mon → switch-out arc); not ported
- [ ] `FreeMonSpriteAfterSwitchOutAnim` / `HideHealthboxAfterMonFaint` / `CompleteOnHealthbarDone` — sprite/healthbox lifecycle callbacks; not ported as discrete state callbacks
- [ ] `DoHitAnimBlinkSpriteEffect` — hit blink (32-frame invisible toggle); not ported
- [ ] `OpponentHandleHitAnimation` — triggers blink effect on hit; not ported as callback
- [ ] `OpponentHandleFaintingCry` — plays cry with faint panning; audio system not implemented
- [ ] `OpponentHandleIntroSlide` — background intro slide (`HandleIntroSlide`); not ported
- [ ] `OpponentHandlePlaySE` / `OpponentHandlePlayFanfareOrBGM` — audio playback; audio system not implemented
- [ ] `OpponentHandleSpriteInvisibility` — forces sprite invisible (substitute, etc.); not ported
- [ ] `OpponentHandleChooseItem` — emits AI-chosen item back to engine; item use in battle not fully ported
- [ ] Link battle commands (`OpponentHandleEndLinkBattle`, `OpponentHandleLinkStandbyMsg`) — intentionally omitted (no link cable in browser)

#### Notes
- The C controller is a **command-buffer dispatch architecture** (GBA IPC between CPU and sub-CPU). The TS port has no equivalent; battle state transitions are handled directly in `BattleEngine.ts` via method calls and async sequencing. None of the `OpponentBufferRunCommand` / `OpponentBufferExecCompleted` plumbing needs porting — it is an architectural artifact of the GBA hardware.
- **Rendering animations** (shiny stars, substitute reveals, trainer slides, hit blinks, faint drops) are the primary missing gap. The engine logic (damage, AI choices, HP updates) is substantially covered; the visual presentation layer is not.
- Link cable / e-Reader / Secret Base battle types are intentionally absent.
- Double-battle / multi-battle partner logic (`BATTLE_PARTNER`, `BATTLE_TYPE_MULTI`, `BATTLE_TYPE_TWO_OPPONENTS`) is present in C but only single-battle is implemented in TS.
- `OpponentHandleChooseMove` Battle Palace branch (`ChooseMoveAndTargetInBattlePalace`) is not ported — Battle Frontier not in scope.

- [ ] `PlayerHandleCmd32` / `PlayerHandleCmd23` — no-ops / palette stop; trivially missing
- [ ] `PlayerHandlePause` — busy-wait loop for timer ticks; not needed in async TS
- [ ] `PlayerHandleDataTransfer` / `PlayerHandleGetMonData` / `PlayerHandleSetMonData` / `PlayerHandleGetRawMonData` / `PlayerHandleSetRawMonData` / `PlayerHandleDMA3Transfer` — controller data bus protocol; TS engine accesses Pokemon data directly, this buffer protocol has no equivalent
- [ ] `PlayerHandlePlayBGM` / `PlayerHandlePlaySE` / `PlayerHandlePlayFanfareOrBGM` — audio playback commands; audio not implemented
- [ ] `ActionSelectionCreateCursorAt` / `ActionSelectionDestroyCursorAt` — GBA tilemap cursor tiles at hardcoded coordinates; GBA BG tilemap layer not used in TS
- [ ] `MoveSelectionCreateCursorAt` / `MoveSelectionDestroyCursorAt` — GBA tilemap move cursor; same as above

#### Notes
- The entire `sPlayerBufferCommands` dispatch table pattern (command byte → handler function) has no direct TS equivalent; the TS battle engine uses a state machine / event system instead of a polled command buffer.
- Link cable / wireless / recorded battle functionality (`BATTLE_TYPE_LINK`, `gWirelessCommType`, `RecordedBattle_*`, `BattleTv_*`) is intentionally omitted — browser port is single-player only.
- Contest ribbon data (`REQUEST_COOL_BATTLE` etc.) and contest-stat requests are omitted — contests not in scope.
- GBA hardware specifics: DMA3 transfers, tilemap buffer copies, OAM matrix management, palette indices — all replaced by WebGL/Canvas rendering in TS.
- Battle Palace (`BATTLE_TYPE_PALACE`) and Battle Arena (`BATTLE_TYPE_ARENA`) paths in choose-move/choose-pokemon are not implemented.
- `Task_*` functions use GBA task scheduler; TS uses async/await or coroutine-style state machines in `BattleEngine.ts`.
- PP number display uses `SetPpNumbersPaletteInMoveSelection` for red-color low PP warning — not ported.

- [ ] `Task_StartSendOutAnim` — 24-frame delay before send-out in double battle partner throw not ported
- [ ] `PlayerPartnerHandleIntroSlide` — `HandleIntroSlide` / `gIntroSlideFlags` background slide not ported (no scrolling BG layer in TS battle renderer)
- [ ] `PlayerPartnerHandleFaintingCry` — species cry on faint not implemented (no audio system)
- [ ] `PlayerPartnerHandlePlaySE` / `PlayerPartnerHandlePlayFanfareOrBGM` — sound effects / BGM not implemented (no audio system)
- [ ] `PlayerPartnerHandlePaletteFade` — GBA palette fade intentionally omitted (TS uses Canvas/WebGL fade)
- [ ] `Intro_ShowHealthbox` / `Intro_WaitForHealthbox` / `Intro_DelayAndEnd` — healthbox slide-in intro sequence with cry-wait gating not ported
- [ ] `CompleteOnBattlerSpriteCallbackDummy` / `FreeTrainerSpriteAfterSlide` — sprite callback polling pattern has no TS equivalent
- [ ] `PlayerPartnerHandleDMA3Transfer` / `PlayerPartnerHandleDataTransfer` — GBA DMA intentionally omitted
- [ ] `PlayerPartnerHandleLinkStandbyMsg` / `PlayerPartnerHandleEndLinkBattle` link portion — link cable multiplayer intentionally omitted
- [ ] `PlayerPartnerHandleCmd23` / `PlayerPartnerHandleCmd32` — unknown/unused commands, no equivalent needed

#### Notes
- The entire GBA controller architecture (command buffer polling, `gBattlerControllerFuncs` function pointer queue, `gBattleControllerExecFlags` bitmask) has no direct TS equivalent. The TS port uses an async state-machine / promise-based battle engine instead, so all `PlayerPartnerBuffer*` scaffolding is architecturally replaced rather than ported function-by-function.
- Audio (`PlaySE`, `PlayFanfare`, `PlayBGM`, `PlayCry_ByMode`) is universally absent — no audio system exists in the TS port yet.
- GBA sprite OAM matrix management (`FreeOamMatrix`, `FreeSpriteOamMatrix`, `AllocSpritePalette`, DMA) is intentionally omitted as WebGL/Canvas handles rendering differently.
- Multi-battle / Battle Frontier partner (`gPartnerTrainerId`, `TRAINER_STEVEN_PARTNER`, `GetFrontierTrainerFrontSpriteId`) logic is not ported — Battle Frontier is out of scope for the current MVP.
- Link battle (`BATTLE_TYPE_LINK`, `PrepareBufferDataTransferLink`) is intentionally omitted.
- The exp bar animation tasks are largely ported at the logic level but the GBA task scheduler pattern (`CreateTask`, `gTasks`, frame-counting) is replaced by async awaits in the TS engine.

- [ ] `RecordedOpponentHandleTrainerSlideBack` — slides trainer sprite off screen after throw
