- [ ] `RecordedOpponentHandleHitAnimation` / `DoHitAnimBlinkSpriteEffect` — hit-blink sprite effect
- [ ] `RecordedOpponentHandlePrintString` / `CompleteOnInactiveTextPrinter` — prints battle message string
- [ ] `RecordedOpponentHandleChooseAction` — replays recorded action byte instead of prompting AI
- [ ] `RecordedOpponentHandleChooseMove` — replays recorded move/target bytes (with Battle Palace path)
- [ ] `RecordedOpponentHandleChoosePokemon` — replays recorded switch-in index
- [ ] `RecordedOpponentHandleStatusIconUpdate` / `CompleteOnFinishedStatusAnimation` — updates status icon on healthbox
- [ ] `RecordedOpponentHandleStatusAnimation` / `CompleteOnFinishedStatusAnimation` — plays status condition animation
- [ ] `RecordedOpponentHandleBattleAnimation` / `CompleteOnFinishedBattleAnimation` — plays table-driven battle animation
- [ ] `RecordedOpponentHandleIntroSlide` — triggers background intro slide
- [ ] `RecordedOpponentHandleIntroTrainerBallThrow` / `SpriteCB_FreeOpponentSprite` — trainer throw anim leading into send-out
- [ ] `RecordedOpponentHandleDrawPartyStatusSummary` / `EndDrawPartyStatusSummary` / `RecordedOpponentHandleHidePartyStatusSummary` — party status ball summary display
- [ ] `RecordedOpponentHandleEndLinkBattle` — stores battle outcome and initiates fade-out
- [ ] `Intro_TryShinyAnimShowHealthbox` / `Intro_WaitForShinyAnimAndHealthbox` / `Intro_DelayAndEnd` — multi-step intro sequence (shiny anim, healthbox slide-in, cry, BGM restore)
- [ ] `SwitchIn_TryShinyAnim` / `SwitchIn_ShowHealthbox` / `SwitchIn_ShowSubstitute` / `SwitchIn_HandleSoundAndEnd` — switch-in animation state machine
- [ ] `TryShinyAnimAfterMonAnim` — deferred shiny star animation after load-mon-sprite
- [ ] All no-op stubs (`RecordedOpponentHandlePaletteFade`, `HandleSuccessBallThrowAnim`, `HandleBallThrowAnim`, `HandlePause`, `HandlePrintSelectionString`, `HandleYesNoBox`, `HandleChooseItem`, `HandleCmd23`, `HandleExpUpdate`, `HandleStatusXor`, `HandleDataTransfer`, `HandleDMA3Transfer`, `HandlePlayBGM`, `HandleCmd32`, `HandleTwoReturnValues`, `HandleChosenMonReturnValue`, `HandleOneReturnValue`, `HandleOneReturnValue_Duplicate`, `HandleClearUnkVar`, `HandleSetUnkVar`, `HandleClearUnkFlag`, `HandleToggleUnkFlag`, `HandleCantSwitch`, `HandlePlaySE`, `HandlePlayFanfareOrBGM`, `HandleFaintingCry`, `HandleEndBounceEffect`, `HandleSpriteInvisibility`, `HandleLinkStandbyMsg`, `HandleResetActionMoveSelection`, `HandleTrainerSlide`, `RecordedOpponentCmdEnd`) — controller commands that complete immediately or handle audio/link

#### Notes
- The recorded-battle ("Battle Video") controller is entirely absent from the TypeScript port. The project has no equivalent feature and no plans noted in the backlog.
- The file's sole purpose is replaying a saved battle byte-stream; it reuses the same 57-command dispatch table as the live opponent controller but reads actions from `RecordedBattle_GetBattlerAction` instead of AI or player input.
- All GBA sprite/OAM machinery (`CreateSprite`, `FreeSpriteOamMatrix`, `SetBattlerShadowSpriteCallback`, etc.) is intentionally absent — the TS port uses `BattleSpriteLoader`/`BattleWebGLContext` for rendering.
- Link-cable paths (`BATTLE_TYPE_LINK`, `PrepareBufferDataTransferLink`) are intentionally omitted as there is no multiplayer in the browser port.
- Contest ribbon fields (`REQUEST_COOL_BATTLE` … `REQUEST_TOUGH_RIBBON_BATTLE`) in `CopyRecordedOpponentMonData` / `SetRecordedOpponentMonData` are also absent — contests are not implemented.
- The closest existing TS infrastructure would be `src/battle/engine/BattleEngine.ts` (battle logic) and `src/battle/render/` (rendering), but neither models a controller-dispatch layer or recorded-battle playback.

#### Notes
This file implements the **recorded-battle player controller** — a replay variant of the human player controller that reads pre-recorded actions from `RecordedBattle_GetBattlerAction` instead of waiting for real input. The TS port has no battle-replay/recording system (`recorded_battle.c` is not ported). The entire GBA battle-controller dispatch architecture (`gBattlerControllerFuncs`, `gBattleControllerExecFlags`, `gBattleBufferA`) has no direct TS equivalent; the TS `BattleEngine` uses a different event-driven model. Link-cable multi and Battle Palace are also unimplemented in TS. This file can be treated as intentionally out-of-scope until recorded-battle playback is a target feature.

- [ ] `SafariHandleIntroTrainerBallThrow` — shows Safari-specific healthbox (balls remaining), slides it in

#### Notes
- Safari Zone battles are a distinct battle sub-mode with no moves or party management; the player chooses Ball / Bait / Go Near / Run instead of a move menu.
- The entire GBA battle controller architecture (command buffer dispatch, `gBattlerControllerFuncs`, `gBattleControllerExecFlags`) has no direct equivalent in the TS port; the TS `BattleEngine` handles all battler logic in a single engine rather than per-controller dispatch tables.
- Link cable / multiplayer paths (`BATTLE_TYPE_LINK`, `PrepareBufferDataTransferLink`) are intentionally irrelevant in a browser port.
- Pokéblock case (`OpenPokeblockCaseInBattle`) — the Pokéblock/Contest system is not ported.
- Safari Zone wild encounter flow is partially scaffolded in `src/game/encounters/wildEncounterService.ts` and `overworldWildEncounterFlow.ts` but the Safari-specific battle controller and UI (Safari Balls counter, bait/rock mechanics) are entirely absent.
- The healthbox Safari Balls display (`HEALTHBOX_SAFARI_BALLS_TEXT`, `HEALTHBOX_SAFARI_ALL_TEXT`) is a specialization of `BattleHealthBox.ts` that has not been implemented.

## batch: src/battle_controller_wally.c

### `src/battle_controller_wally.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/battle/engine/BattleEngine.ts`, `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts`, `src/states/BirchSpeechState.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `SetControllerToWally` — initializes Wally's battle controller state; analogous to BirchSpeechState/BattleEngine initialization
- [x] `WallyHandleGetMonData` / `CopyWallyMonData` — reads Pokémon data for battle; covered by `src/pokemon/stats.ts` and `src/save/native/Gen3Pokemon.ts` field access
- [x] `WallyHandleSetMonData` / `SetWallyMonData` — writes Pokémon data back after battle; covered by party state management in `src/contexts/PartyContext.tsx`
- [x] `WallyHandleHealthBarUpdate` / `CompleteOnHealthbarDone` — HP bar animation; covered by `src/battle/render/BattleHealthBox.ts`
- [x] `WallyHandleHitAnimation` / `DoHitAnimBlinkSpriteEffect` — sprite blink on hit; covered by `src/battle/render/BattleOverlayFrame.ts`
- [x] `WallyHandlePrintString` — battle message display; covered by `src/battle/messages/battleMessageSequences.ts` and `src/core/prompt/`
- [x] `WallyHandleMoveAnimation` / `WallyDoMoveAnimation` — move animation sequencing; covered by `src/battle/engine/BattleEngine.ts` turn execution
- [x] `WallyHandleFaintingCry` — plays Pokémon cry on faint; cry playback intentionally omitted (no audio system)

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `WallyHandleChooseMove` — scripted move selection (always picks move index 0 after delay); `BattleEngine` handles move selection but no Wally-specific scripted AI state machine (`wallyMovesState` / `wallyMoveFrames` timing loop not replicated)
- [-] `WallyHandleChooseAction` / `WallyHandleActions` — scripted action sequence (attack→attack→throw→item); `BattleEngine` has AI action selection but the specific Wally tutorial state machine (states 0–5 with `wallyBattleState`, `wallyWaitFrames`, `B_ACTION_WALLY_THROW`) is not implemented
- [-] `WallyHandleChooseItem` / `CompleteOnChosenItem` / `OpenBagAfterPaletteFade` — opens bag menu during Wally tutorial; `src/menu/components/BagMenu.tsx` exists but the battle-context bag-open flow for Wally is not wired
- [-] `WallyHandleIntroTrainerBallThrow` / `Task_StartSendOutAnim` / `StartSendOutAnim` — Wally trainer throw intro animation; `src/battle/render/BattleSpriteLoader.ts` handles send-out but Wally-specific back-pic slide-in and delayed ball throw are not implemented
- [-] `WallyHandleDrawTrainerPic` / `WallyHandleTrainerSlide` — loads and slides in Wally's back sprite (`TRAINER_BACK_PIC_WALLY`); `BattleSpriteLoader` handles trainer pics generically but Wally-specific positioning/slide is not covered

#### Missing
- [ ] `WallyBufferRunCommand` / `WallyBufferExecCompleted` — GBA battle controller command buffer dispatch loop; no equivalent (TS uses a different async/promise-based command pipeline in `BattleEngine`)
- [ ] `Intro_TryShinyAnimShowHealthbox` / `Intro_WaitForShinyAnimAndHealthbox` — shiny sparkle animation gating healthbox slide-in during intro; shiny animation not implemented
- [ ] `WallyHandleIntroSlide` / `HandleIntroSlide` — scrolls battle background environment during intro; battle background intro slide not implemented
- [ ] `WallyHandleDrawPartyStatusSummary` — creates party status summary dots sprite; no equivalent in TS battle UI
- [ ] `WallyHandleBallThrowAnim` / `WallyHandleSuccessBallThrowAnim` — Poké Ball throw animation with trainer; ball throw animations not implemented
- [ ] `WallyHandleBattleAnimation` — dispatches table-driven battle animations (entry/exit effects); `src/data/battleAnimations.gen.ts` has the data but runtime dispatch (`TryHandleLaunchBattleTableAnimation`) is not ported
- [ ] `WallyHandleEndLinkBattle` — link battle teardown; intentionally omitted (no link cable support)
- [ ] `WallyHandlePlayBGM` / `WallyHandlePlaySE` / `WallyHandlePlayFanfareOrBGM` — audio playback; intentionally omitted (no audio system)
- [ ] `DoSwitchOutAnimation` / `WallyHandleReturnMonToBall` — switch-out shrink animation; not implemented
- [ ] `WallyHandlePaletteFade` — palette fade to/from black; `src/rendering/WebGLFadeRenderer.ts` exists for field but battle-context palette fade controller hook not wired

#### Notes
- This file is the **Wally tutorial battle controller** — a highly specialized scripted AI controller used only for the Route 103 catching tutorial. It is architecturally a GBA "battle controller" (one of five: player, opponent, link player, link opponent, Wally), driving a command buffer dispatch pattern that has no direct equivalent in the TS port.
- The TS port's `BattleEngine` consolidates all battler logic into a single engine with explicit `BattleStartRequest` typing rather than per-controller command buffers. Wally's scripted behavior (`wallyBattleState` 0–5 state machine) would need to be implemented as a special AI mode or scripted battle controller in `BattleEngine`.
- Link battle code (`BATTLE_TYPE_LINK`, `PrepareBufferDataTransferLink`, `SetBattleEndCallbacks`) is intentionally not applicable.
- Audio (`PlaySE`, `PlayBGM`, `PlayFanfare`, `PlayCry_Normal`) is intentionally omitted — no audio system exists in the port.
- Contest ribbon data fields (`REQUEST_COOL_BATTLE` through `REQUEST_TOUGH_RIBBON_BATTLE`) are read/written but contests are not implemented; these are low priority.
- The Wally tutorial is a blocker for the new-game flow (see `src/states/BirchSpeechState.ts` and `src/game/NewGameFlow.ts`), making the scripted action state machine (`WallyHandleActions`) the most critical missing piece.

### `src/battle_controllers.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/battle/engine/BattleEngine.ts`, `src/scripting/BattleCommandRunner.ts`, `src/battle/engine/scriptRuntime/BattleScriptRuntime.ts`, `src/battle/engine/scriptRuntime/commands.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `SetUpBattleVarsAndBirchZigzagoon` — battle init with first-battle Zigzagoon enemy party setup; partially covered by `BattleEngine` init and `BattleStartRequest.ts`
- [x] `InitBattleControllers` (single-player path) — single-player controller routing to player vs. opponent; TS uses a flat engine model without battler controller dispatch tables
- [x] `SetBattlePartyIds` — finding first healthy non-egg mon per side; covered implicitly in `BattleEngine` party slot resolution

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `BtlController_EmitChooseAction` — action selection signaling; TS has action-phase handling in `BattleEngine` but no explicit emit/buffer serialization layer
- [-] `BtlController_EmitChooseMove` — move selection prompt; TS opens move menu via `BattleScriptRuntime`/`commands.ts` but lacks the full `ChooseMoveStruct` serialization
- [-] `BtlController_EmitPrintString` / `BtlController_EmitPrintSelectionString` — battle message dispatch with full `BattleMsgData` context; `battleMessageSequences.ts` covers message sequencing but not the full struct serialization
- [-] `BtlController_EmitHealthBarUpdate` — HP bar delta signaling; `BattleHealthBox.ts` renders HP but there is no explicit emit/buffer protocol
- [-] `BtlController_EmitStatusIconUpdate` / `BtlController_EmitStatusAnimation` — status display updates; `BattleHealthBox.ts` renders status icons but without the controller emit layer

#### Missing
- [ ] `HandleLinkBattleSetup` — link cable / wireless battle connection setup; intentionally omitted (no multiplayer)
- [ ] `InitLinkBtlControllers` — full link battle controller routing (4-player multi, Battle Tower link, partner/opponent assignment); intentionally omitted
- [ ] `CreateTasksForSendRecvLinkBuffers` — GBA task system for link buffer send/receive; intentionally omitted
- [ ] `PrepareBufferDataTransfer` / `PrepareBufferDataTransferLink` — binary serialization into `gBattleBufferA/B` or link send buffer; no equivalent (TS uses direct object passing)
- [ ] `Task_HandleSendLinkBuffersData` — link send buffer task state machine; intentionally omitted
- [ ] `TryReceiveLinkBattleData` — link receive buffer polling; intentionally omitted
- [ ] `Task_HandleCopyReceivedLinkBuffersData` — copy received link data into battle buffers; intentionally omitted
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitGetMonData` / `BtlController_EmitSetMonData` — controller-to-engine mon data read/write messages; no equivalent (TS accesses party objects directly)
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitLoadMonSprite` — trigger sprite load for active battler; no equivalent emit layer
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitSwitchInAnim` / `BtlController_EmitReturnMonToBall` — switch animation signals; no controller emit layer
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitDrawTrainerPic` / `BtlController_EmitTrainerSlide` / `BtlController_EmitTrainerSlideBack` — trainer sprite animation signals; not wired via emit layer
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitFaintAnimation` / `BtlController_EmitFaintingCry` — faint animation/cry signals; not wired via emit layer
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitBallThrowAnim` — ball throw animation signal; no equivalent
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitMoveAnimation` — move animation trigger with full context (weather, disable struct, multihit); no equivalent emit
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitExpUpdate` — EXP gain signal to controller; no equivalent
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitChooseItem` / `BtlController_EmitChoosePokemon` — bag/party selection signals; no controller emit layer
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitYesNoBox` — forfeit yes/no prompt (Battle Frontier); no equivalent
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitDataTransfer` / `BtlController_EmitTwoReturnValues` / `BtlController_EmitOneReturnValue` / `BtlController_EmitOneReturnValue_Duplicate` / `BtlController_EmitChosenMonReturnValue` — generic return value messaging; no equivalent (TS uses direct return values)
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitHitAnimation` — hit flash animation signal; no equivalent emit
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitCantSwitch` — can't switch signal; no equivalent
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitPlaySE` / `BtlController_EmitPlayFanfareOrBGM` — audio signals; audio system not implemented
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitIntroSlide` / `BtlController_EmitIntroTrainerBallThrow` — battle intro animation signals; no equivalent
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitDrawPartyStatusSummary` / `BtlController_EmitHidePartyStatusSummary` — party status balls UI; no equivalent
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitEndBounceEffect` — bounce animation end signal; no equivalent
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitSpriteInvisibility` — sprite hide/show signal; no equivalent
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitBattleAnimation` — generic battle animation dispatch; no equivalent emit
- [ ] `BtlController_EmitLinkStandbyMsg` / `BtlController_EmitResetActionMoveSelection` / `BtlController_EmitEndLinkBattle` — link battle coordination messages; intentionally omitted

#### Notes
The entire `BtlController_Emit*` family (~40 functions) implement a binary message-passing protocol between the battle engine and per-battler controllers over GBA memory buffers (or link cable). The TS port has no equivalent architecture — `BattleEngine.ts` is a monolithic engine that directly manipulates state and calls rendering hooks, so this serialization layer has no analogue and is intentionally absent. Link/wireless battle infrastructure (`BATTLE_TYPE_LINK`, `BATTLE_TYPE_MULTI`, recorded battles, Battle Tower link) is entirely omitted as the port targets single-player only. The `SetBattlePartyIds` / `InitSinglePlayerBtlControllers` logic for single-player single battles is the only meaningful portion with a TS equivalent.

- [ ] `GetTypeEffectivenessPoints` — computes type effectiveness score for a move vs a species in good/bad/AI-vs-AI modes
- [ ] `TournamentIdOfOpponent` — finds a trainer's current-round opponent by tournament ID
- [ ] `TrainerIdOfPlayerOpponent` — returns trainer ID of the player's current opponent
- [ ] `SetDomeOpponentId` — writes `gTrainerBattleOpponent_A` with player's current dome opponent
- [ ] `SetDomeOpponentGraphicsId` — sets opponent trainer sprite GFX ID
- [ ] `BufferDomeRoundText` — copies round name string to `gStringVar1`
- [ ] `BufferDomeOpponentName` — copies round name + opponent name to `gStringVar1`/`gStringVar2`
- [ ] `InitDomeOpponentParty` — creates enemy party mons for the dome opponent
- [ ] `CreateDomeOpponentMon` / `CreateDomeOpponentMons` — instantiates Pokémon structs for enemy party
- [ ] `SaveDomeChallenge` — persists challenge status and pauses challenge
- [ ] `IncrementDomeStreaks` — increments win streak and championship counters
- [ ] `ShowDomeOpponentInfo` — launches the trainer info card display for the upcoming opponent
- [ ] `ShowDomeTourneyTree` / `ShowPreviousDomeTourneyTree` / `ShowNonInteractiveDomeTourneyTree` — entry points for tourney tree UI
- [ ] `SetFacilityTrainerAndMonPtrs` — sets up facility trainer/mon pointer globals
- [ ] `ResetSketchedMoves` — restores Sketched moves after dome battle
- [ ] `RestoreDomePlayerPartyHeldItems` — restores held items stripped before battle
- [ ] `ReduceDomePlayerPartyToSelectedMons` — reduces player party to only the selected dome mons
- [ ] `GetPlayerSeededBeforeOpponent` — determines if player's seed rank is higher than opponent's
- [ ] `BufferLastDomeWinnerName` — copies the tournament winner's name to a string buffer
- [ ] `InitRandomTourneyTreeResults` — simulates AI-vs-AI battles to populate a full tournament bracket
- [ ] `ResolveDomeRoundWinners` / `DecideRoundWinners` — determines round winners for NPC bracket matches
- [ ] `TrainerIdToTournamentId` — converts a trainer ID back to its tournament slot index
- [ ] `Task_ShowTourneyTree` / `Task_HandleStaticTourneyTreeInput` / `CB2_TourneyTree` — full tourney tree UI task/callback system
- [ ] `Task_ShowTourneyInfoCard` / `Task_HandleInfoCardInput` / `Task_GetInfoCardInput` — info card UI (trainer info + match info cards with scrolling)
- [ ] `DisplayTrainerInfoOnCard` / `DisplayMatchInfoOnCard` — renders text/sprites onto an info card
- [ ] `BufferDomeWinString` — builds the win result string for a match card
- [ ] `UpdateTourneyTreeCursor` — moves cursor around tourney tree via `sTourneyTreeCursorMovementMap`
- [ ] `DrawTourneyAdvancementLine` — draws highlighted advancement lines on the tourney tree tilemap
- [ ] `GetOpposingNPCTournamentIdByRound` — finds which NPC a given trainer faces in a given round
- [ ] `SpriteCB_HorizontalScrollArrow` / `SpriteCB_VerticalScrollArrow` — scroll arrow sprite visibility logic
- [ ] All `SpriteCB_*CardScroll*` functions (8 functions) — animate trainer/mon icon sprites during card scrolling transitions
- [ ] `SpriteCB_MonIconDomeInfo` — idle animation update for mon icons on info card
- [ ] `GetDomeBrainTrainerPicId` / `GetDomeBrainTrainerClass` / `CopyDomeBrainTrainerName` / `CopyDomeTrainerName` — helper accessors for trainer display info
- [ ] `VblankCb_TourneyInfoCard` / `VblankCb_TourneyTree` / `HblankCb_TourneyTree` — GBA hardware callbacks (intentionally omitted: GBA-specific)
- [ ] All `sBattleStyleMovePoints` / `sBattleStyleThresholds` data — per-move style point tables and threshold lookup for determining a trainer's battle style label on info cards
- [ ] All tourney tree line section data (`sTourneyTreeLineSections`, `LINESECTION_*` macros) — tilemap coordinates for drawing bracket advancement highlight lines
- [ ] All bracket layout tables (`sIdToOpponentId`, `sIdToMatchNumber`, `sTourneyTreeTrainerIds`, `sTrainerNamePositions`, `sTourneyTreePokeballCoords`, etc.)

#### Notes
- This file implements the entire Battle Dome facility: bracket initialization, AI team selection, type-effectiveness-based mon picking, tourney tree UI with scrollable info cards, and win streak tracking. None of it is ported.
- The tourney tree UI is GBA-specific (BG tilemaps, OAM sprites, scanline effects, DMA) — a browser port would need a completely different rendering approach.
- `sBattleStyleMovePoints` is a large per-move data table (one entry per Gen III move) used only for the Dome info card flavor text — not related to battle mechanics proper.
- The `GetTypeEffectivenessPoints` function duplicates type-chart logic already present in `src/battle/engine/DamageCalculator.ts` but uses a simplified 5-bucket scoring system rather than exact multipliers.
- Battle Frontier (Dome, Tower, Palace, etc.) is generally considered post-game/optional content; the entire facility is unimplemented in the TS port.

- [ ] `RestoreDomePlayerPartyHeldItems` — restores held items on the battle party from the save block after a dome match
- [ ] `ReduceDomePlayerPartyToSelectedMons` — reduces player party to only the dome-selected mons

- [ ] `BufferLastDomeWinnerName` — finds the last non-eliminated trainer and copies their name to `gStringVar1`
- [ ] `InitRandomTourneyTreeResults` — generates a fake completed tourney tree for the lobby computer pre-challenge display
- [ ] `TrainerIdToTournamentId` — maps a trainer ID to their position in the tournament bracket (static)
- [ ] `TrainerIdToDomeTournamentId` — same as above but with global scope

- [ ] `GetDomeBrainTrainerPicId` — returns Tucker's trainer pic ID
- [ ] `GetDomeBrainTrainerClass` — returns Tucker's trainer class
- [ ] `CopyDomeBrainTrainerName` — copies Tucker's name into a buffer

#### Notes
The entire Battle Dome subsystem is unimplemented. This covers the 16-trainer single-elimination tournament bracket UI (tourney tree), info cards for trainers and matches, NPC vs NPC outcome simulation (`DecideRoundWinners`, `GetWinningMove`), and post-round result display. This is Battle Frontier content — a late/post-game feature. The NPC simulation logic (`GetWinningMove`, `DecideRoundWinners`) contains actual gameplay logic (type effectiveness scoring, stat totals, seeding) that would be relevant if Battle Frontier were ever ported, but `src/scripting/runtime/frontierState.ts` suggests only minimal Frontier scaffolding exists. Hardware-specific code (GBA BG/OBJ registers, DMA, scanline effects, VRAM writes) has no browser equivalent and would need to be replaced with WebGL/Canvas rendering.

#### Notes
Battle Factory is entirely unimplemented. The port has `src/scripting/runtime/frontierState.ts` which likely stubs out frontier special vars, but none of the Factory-specific party generation, rental swap screens, win-streak persistence, or AI scaling logic exists. Battle Frontier as a whole (Factory, Tower, Dome, etc.) appears out of scope for the current port milestone — the existing battle engine covers wild/trainer battles only. `SetMonMoveAvoidReturn` is a small utility also used by Battle Tower party generation and would be needed if/when frontier is added.

#### Notes
- The entire Battle Factory rental-selection and post-battle swap UI has no equivalent in the TS port. The Battle Frontier (`src/scripting/runtime/frontierState.ts`) has some frontier state scaffolding, but none of the screen-level UI exists.
- GBA-specific concerns (BG layers, palette blending, OAM affine matrices, WIN0 hardware registers, VRAM DMA) have no direct browser analog; a port would use Canvas/WebGL overlays and CSS transitions instead.
- `CreateFrontierFactorySelectableMons` and `CreateSlateportTentSelectableMons` depend on `gBattleFrontierMons`/`gSlateportBattleTentMons` and frontier save-block fields that are not yet modelled in the TS save system.
- The summary screen integration (`ShowPokemonSummaryScreen`) would map to the existing `PokemonSummaryContent` menu component.
- This is post-game Battle Frontier content; it is a low priority relative to the main story path.

## batch: src/battle_gfx_sfx_util.c

### `src/battle_gfx_sfx_util.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/battle/render/BattleHealthBox.ts`, `src/battle/render/BattleSpriteLoader.ts`, `src/battle/render/BattleLayout.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `BattleLoadOpponentMonSpriteGfx` / `BattleLoadPlayerMonSpriteGfx` — sprite loading for battlers; covered by `BattleSpriteLoader.ts`
- [x] `BattleLoadAllHealthBoxesGfx` / `BattleLoadAllHealthBoxesGfxAtOnce` — healthbox sprite sheet loading; handled by `BattleHealthBox.ts` render setup
- [x] `GetMonHPBarLevel` / `HandleLowHpMusicChange` — HP bar level logic; `BattleHealthBox.ts` tracks HP thresholds
- [x] `HandleSpeciesGfxDataChange` (Castform form swap path) — form-based sprite swap; partially handled in `BattleEngine.ts` form tracking

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `ChooseMoveAndTargetInBattlePalace` — Battle Palace move/target selection; no Palace-specific logic in `BattleEngine.ts`; the engine supports move selection and targeting generally but lacks nature-to-move-group lookup tables and Palace-specific fallback logic
- [-] `BattleInitAllSprites` — multi-state sprite initialization sequencer; `BattleSpriteLoader.ts` loads sprites but doesn't replicate the exact GBA state machine (cases 0–6) since VRAM management is irrelevant in WebGL
- [-] `HandleSpeciesGfxDataChange` (Transform path) — full Transform sprite/palette swap including Deoxys form handling; `BattleEngine.ts` tracks `transformSpecies` but palette blending (white tint) and DMA copy are not applicable/implemented
- [-] `BattleLoadSubstituteOrMonSpriteGfx` / `LoadBattleMonGfxAndAnimate` — substitute doll sprite loading; substitute is tracked in engine state but doll graphics not loaded separately in WebGL renderer

#### Missing
- [ ] `AllocateBattleSpritesData` / `FreeBattleSpritesData` — GBA heap allocation for sprite data structs; not needed (JS GC)
- [ ] `AllocateMonSpritesGfx` / `FreeMonSpritesGfx` — GBA VRAM buffer allocation for mon sprite sheets; not needed
- [ ] `SpriteCB_WaitForBattlerBallReleaseAnim` — sprite callback waiting for Poké Ball release affine animation to end
- [ ] `SpriteCB_TrainerSlideIn` / `SpriteCB_TrainerSlideVertical` — trainer sprite slide-in intro animation callbacks
- [ ] `InitAndLaunchChosenStatusAnimation` — dispatches GBA battle animation for status conditions (freeze, burn, sleep, etc.)
- [ ] `TryHandleLaunchBattleTableAnimation` — launches general battle table animation and blocks on completion via task
- [ ] `InitAndLaunchSpecialAnimation` — launches special battle animation (weather continues, snatch, etc.)
- [ ] `IsMoveWithoutAnimation` — stub returning FALSE; always returns false, trivially omitted
- [ ] `IsBattleSEPlaying` — checks if sound effect is still playing, with 30-frame timeout
- [ ] `BattleStopLowHpSound` — stops low-HP music SE on battle end
- [ ] `HandleBattleLowHpMusicChange` — top-level per-frame low-HP music check for both player battlers
- [ ] `LoadAndCreateEnemyShadowSprites` — creates drop-shadow sprites under enemy Pokémon
- [ ] `SpriteCB_EnemyShadow` / `SpriteCB_SetInvisible` — shadow sprite callbacks tracking battler position/visibility
- [ ] `SetBattlerShadowSpriteCallback` / `HideBattlerShadowSprite` — show/hide enemy shadow based on species elevation
- [ ] `SetBattlerSpriteAffineMode` — toggles OAM affine mode for all battler sprites (GBA-specific)
- [ ] `FillAroundBattleWindows` — fills VRAM tilemap palette around action/move windows (GBA VRAM manipulation)
- [ ] `ClearTemporarySpeciesSpriteData` — resets transform species, form index, and substitute bit
- [ ] `CopyAllBattleSpritesInvisibilities` / `CopyBattleSpriteInvisibility` — copies sprite invisible flags into battler data struct
- [ ] `ClearSpritesHealthboxAnimData` / static `ClearSpritesBattlerHealthboxAnimData` — zeroes healthbox/animation/battler data structs
- [ ] `GetBattlePalaceMoveGroup` (static) — categorizes a move into Attack/Defense/Support group by target type
- [ ] `GetBattlePalaceTarget` (static) — selects target in doubles based on nature preference (stronger/weaker/random)
- [ ] `LoadBattleBarGfx` — LZ-decompresses HP bar graphics into sprite GFX buffer
- [ ] `DecompressTrainerFrontPic` / `DecompressTrainerBackPic` — decompresses trainer sprite into VRAM position
- [ ] `FreeTrainerFrontPicPalette` — frees trainer front sprite palette by tag
- [ ] `BattleGfxSfxDummy2` / `BattleGfxSfxDummy3` — empty stubs (species/gender params unused)
- [ ] `ShouldPlayNormalMonCry` — returns true if mon should play normal cry (not low HP or statused)
- [ ] `TrySetBehindSubstituteBit` / `ClearBehindSubstituteBit` — set/clear substitute hiding flag on battler sprite data

#### Notes
- All GBA hardware concerns (VRAM, OAM, DMA, palette buffer management, sprite callbacks, task system) are intentionally omitted — the WebGL renderer handles display completely differently.
- Battle Palace logic (`ChooseMoveAndTargetInBattlePalace`, `GetBattlePalaceMoveGroup`, `GetBattlePalaceTarget`) is missing because the Battle Frontier is post-game content not yet in scope.
- Status animations (`InitAndLaunchChosenStatusAnimation`) and general battle table animations (`TryHandleLaunchBattleTableAnimation`, `InitAndLaunchSpecialAnimation`) have no equivalent yet — the TS port has `BattleOverlayFrame.ts` for some visual effects but no animation sequencer driven by battle scripts.
- Low-HP music (`HandleLowHpMusicChange`, `HandleBattleLowHpMusicChange`, `BattleStopLowHpSound`) is missing as audio is not yet implemented.
- Transform graphics swap is partially tracked in engine state but the palette-blend white tint is not reproduced in WebGL.

### `src/battle_interface.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/battle/render/BattleHealthBox.ts`, `src/battle/render/BattleLayout.ts`

#### Implemented
- [x] `GetHPBarLevel` — maps HP fraction to color tier (green/yellow/red/empty); implemented in `BattleHealthBox.ts`
- [x] `GetScaledHPFraction` — scales HP to pixel count with min-1 guard; implemented in `BattleHealthBox.ts`
- [x] `UpdateHealthboxAttribute` — dispatches to sub-updaters for level, HP text, HP bar, exp bar, nick, status icon; partial equivalent in `BattleHealthBox.ts`
- [x] `InitBattlerHealthboxCoords` — positions healthbox sprites per battle slot (singles/doubles); layout constants in `BattleLayout.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `MoveBattleBar` / `CalcNewBarValue` / `CalcBarFilledPixels` — animated bar-drain logic with Q8.24 fixed-point stepping; `BattleHealthBox.ts` renders the bar statically at correct fill level but does not animate the drain frame-by-frame
- [-] `UpdateStatusIconInHealthbox` — status icon rendering (PSN/PAR/SLP/FRZ/BRN) with palette swap; status icons are read and displayed in `BattleHealthBox.ts` but GBA palette manipulation and per-battler tile offsets are not replicated
- [-] `UpdateNickInHealthbox` — renders nickname + gender symbol into OBJ VRAM tiles; nickname and gender are displayed in TS but via DOM/canvas text, not tile-blit
- [-] `UpdateHpTextInHealthbox` — blits HP numbers into healthbox OBJ tiles; TS renders HP numbers via canvas/DOM rather than tile-copying into sprite VRAM

#### Missing
- [ ] `CreateBattlerHealthboxSprites` — allocates and configures the left/right healthbox sprites + healthbar sprite with correct OAM tile layout
- [ ] `CreateSafariPlayerHealthboxSprites` — safari-mode variant of healthbox sprite creation
- [ ] `SetBattleBarStruct` — initialises the animated bar state machine struct for a battler slot
- [ ] `SetHealthboxSpriteInvisible` / `SetHealthboxSpriteVisible` — shows/hides all three sub-sprites (left, right, bar) together
- [ ] `DestoryHealthboxSprite` — destroys all three sub-sprites
- [ ] `UpdateOamPriorityInAllHealthboxes` — sets OAM priority on all healthbox/healthbar sprites
- [ ] `SwapHpBarsWithHpText` — toggles between HP bar display and numeric HP text in doubles (tap to toggle)
- [ ] `CreatePartyStatusSummarySprites` — creates the 6-ball party summary tray that slides in at battle start
- [ ] `Task_HidePartyStatusSummary` / `Task_HidePartyStatusSummary_BattleStart_1/2` / `Task_HidePartyStatusSummary_DuringBattle` — animated slide-out + fade of party summary tray
- [ ] `SpriteCB_StatusSummaryBar_Enter/Exit` — sprite callbacks that animate the summary bar sliding in/out
- [ ] `SpriteCB_StatusSummaryBalls_Enter/Exit/OnSwitchout` — sprite callbacks animating individual ball icons
- [ ] `SpriteCB_HealthBar` — syncs healthbar sprite position to parent healthbox each frame
- [ ] `SpriteCB_HealthBoxOther` — syncs right half of healthbox to left half each frame
- [ ] `UpdateLvlInHealthbox` — blits "Lv##" text into healthbox OBJ tiles
- [ ] `UpdateHpTextInHealthboxInDoubles` — doubles-specific HP number tile-blit with "bars vs text" toggle
- [ ] `PrintSafariMonInfo` — renders nature + catch/flee rates into safari healthbox (debug-style)
- [ ] `UpdateSafariBallsTextOnHealthbox` / `UpdateLeftNoOfBallsTextOnHealthbox` — safari ball count text
- [ ] `TryAddPokeballIconToHealthbox` — adds caught-ball icon to opponent healthbar if Pokémon is registered as caught
- [ ] `MoveBattleBarGraphically` — internal tile-copy loop that draws the bar segments at the correct pixel fill
- [ ] `GetScaledExpFraction` — scales EXP bar drain speed relative to bar length
- [ ] `AddTextPrinterAndCreateWindowOnHealthbox` / `RemoveWindowOnHealthbox` — temporary BG window used as a font rendering surface to blit into OBJ VRAM
- [ ] `TextIntoHealthboxObject` / `HpTextIntoHealthboxObject` / `SafariTextIntoHealthboxObject` / `FillHealthboxObject` — low-level tile-copy helpers that write rendered text tiles into sprite VRAM at specific offsets
- [ ] `Debug_DrawNumber` / `Debug_DrawNumberPair` / `Debug_TestHealthBar` / `Debug_TestHealthBar_Helper` — unused debug helpers (intentionally skippable)

#### Notes
- The TS port renders the battle UI via canvas/DOM elements in `BattleHealthBox.ts` and `BattleLayout.ts` rather than blitting into GBA OBJ VRAM tile memory. The entire "sprite VRAM tile offset arithmetic" that dominates this file has no equivalent and is intentionally absent — the TS approach draws HP bars, numbers, and status icons directly as canvas 2D primitives.
- Animated bar drain (frame-by-frame `CalcNewBarValue` stepping) is the most significant missing gameplay-visible feature; the TS health box jumps immediately to the final value.
- Party status summary tray (6-ball slideout) has no TS implementation yet.
- Safari-zone healthbox variants are entirely unimplemented.
- GBA hardware dependencies (OBJ VRAM addresses, palette buffer manipulation, OAM priority registers, `CpuCopy32`) are all intentionally omitted as they have no browser analogue.

## batch: src/battle_intro.c

### `src/battle_intro.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/battle/render/BattleBackground.ts`, `src/battle/render/BattleLayout.ts`, `src/rendering/webgl/WebGLScanlineRenderer.ts`
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-battle-backgrounds.cjs` (environment constants only)

#### Implemented
- [x] `HandleIntroSlide` (environment dispatch) — battle environment type is resolved in `src/battle/render/battleEnvironmentResolver.ts` and `src/battle/render/battlePresentationMode.ts`
- [x] Battle environment constants — `BATTLE_ENVIRONMENT_*` values generated into `src/data/battleEnvironments.gen.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `BattleIntroSlide1` / `BattleIntroSlide2` / `BattleIntroSlide3` — the WebGL scanline wipe/split effect is present in `src/rendering/webgl/WebGLScanlineRenderer.ts`, but the per-environment BG scrolling speeds, WIN0V manipulation, and BLDALPHA fade sequencing are not reproduced frame-for-frame
- [-] `DrawBattlerOnBg` — sprite-to-BG blit during intro exists conceptually in `src/battle/render/BattleSpriteLoader.ts` and `src/battle/render/BattleWebGLContext.ts`, but the GBA tilemap write logic is replaced by WebGL texture uploads

#### Missing
- [ ] `SetAnimBgAttribute` / `GetAnimBgAttribute` — GBA BG control register manipulation; no browser equivalent needed (WebGL handles BG config)
- [ ] `BattleIntroSlideLink` — link battle VS screen animation with `SpriteCB_VsLetterInit`; link battles are intentionally omitted
- [ ] `BattleIntroSlidePartner` — multi-battle partner intro slide (Steven partner battle); multi-battle not implemented
- [ ] `BattleIntroSlideEnd` — GBA register cleanup (BLDCNT, WININ, WINOUT reset); equivalent cleanup handled implicitly by WebGL render state
- [ ] `DrawBattlerOnBgDMA` — DMA copy variant; unused in C source (`UNUSED`), no port needed

#### Notes
The GBA hardware intro slide is a scanline-effect-driven wipe using WIN0V, BG scroll registers, and alpha blending registers — all GBA-specific. The TS port approximates the visual with CSS/WebGL transitions in `BattleBackground.ts` and `WebGLScanlineRenderer.ts`, but does not replicate the exact frame timing or multi-state task machine. Link battle and ingame partner battle intro variants are intentionally omitted as those game modes are out of scope.

- [x] `FaintClearSetData` — clears battler state on faint; `BattleEngine.ts` faint handling resets stat stages, status2, DisableStruct

- [x] `TryDoEventsBeforeFirstTurn` — switch-in ability/item effects before first turn; `BattleEngine.ts` handles switch-in ability order by speed
- [x] `HandleTurnActionSelectionState` — action selection state machine; `BattleEngine.ts` implements action selection loop including encore lock, multi-turn auto-action, no-valid-moves handling

- [x] `SpriteCB_FaintOpponentMon` / `SpriteCB_AnimFaintOpponent` — faint drop animation; `BattleEngine.ts` + render layer handle faint visuals

- [-] `CB2_HandleStartBattle` — 18-state machine covering link battle handshake, party exchange, RNG seed sync, sprite init; only the non-link path (state 15→18) is relevant and partially covered by `BattleState.ts`; all link/wireless states intentionally omitted
- [-] `HandleEndTurn_BattleWon` / `HandleEndTurn_BattleLost` / `HandleEndTurn_RanFromBattle` — battle outcome handlers; `BattleEngine.ts` tracks outcome but post-battle flow (exp, evolution trigger, return to overworld) is only partially wired to `src/game/overworld/load/loadSelectedOverworldMap.ts`
- [-] `BattleIntroDrawTrainersOrMonsSprites` — draws trainer/wild mon sprites with pokedex seen flag; `BattleState.ts` / `BattleSpriteLoader.ts` load sprites but pokedex seen-flag setting on battle intro is not confirmed implemented
- [-] `BattleIntroDrawPartySummaryScreens` — draws party status dots; `BattleHealthBox.ts` / `BattleLayout.ts` render health info but party summary dots on intro are not fully replicated
- [-] `SwitchPartyOrder` / `UpdateBattlerPartyOrdersOnSwitch` — party order bookkeeping for double/multi battles; single-battle switch is handled; double-battle partner order sync is partial

#### Missing
- [ ] `CB2_HandleStartMultiBattle` — 4-player multi-battle link setup state machine; not relevant (no link multiplayer)
- [ ] `CB2_HandleStartMultiPartnerBattle` — ingame partner multi-battle init (Steven, etc.); not implemented
- [ ] `CB2_PreInitMultiBattle` — pre-battle partner party showcase menu for link multi; not implemented
- [ ] `CB2_PreInitIngamePlayerPartnerBattle` — ingame partner party showcase; not implemented
- [ ] `CB2_InitEndLinkBattle` / `CB2_EndLinkBattle` / `EndLinkBattleInSteps` — post-link-battle VS screen and reconnect flow; not relevant (no link)
- [ ] `CB2_InitAskRecordBattle` / `CB2_AskRecordBattle` / `AskRecordBattle` — recorded battle save prompt; not implemented

- [ ] `SetPlayerBerryDataInBattleStruct` / `SetAllPlayersBerryData` — Enigma Berry sync across link players; not relevant (no link)
- [ ] `TryCorrectShedinjaLanguage` — fixes Shedinja language tag from Japanese link partner; not relevant
- [ ] `SpriteCB_WildMon` / `SpriteCB_MoveWildMonToRight` / `SpriteCB_WildMonShowHealthbox` / `SpriteCB_WildMonAnimate` — wild mon intro slide-in animation sequence; not fully implemented as discrete sprite callbacks
- [ ] `SpriteCB_OpponentMonFromBall` / `SpriteCB_PlayerMonFromBall` — mon emerge-from-ball animation callbacks; not implemented as discrete callbacks
- [ ] `SpriteCB_BattleSpriteStartSlideLeft` / `SpriteCB_BattleSpriteSlideLeft` — trainer sprite slide-in; not implemented
- [ ] `SpriteCB_TrainerThrowObject` / `AnimSetCenterToCornerVecX` — trainer throw-ball animation; not implemented
- [ ] `SpriteCB_FaintSlideAnim` — faint slide (trainer defeat animation); not implemented
- [ ] `SpriteCB_VsLetterInit` / `SpriteCB_VsLetter` — VS screen letter zoom animation; not implemented
- [ ] `SpriteCB_ShowAsMoveTarget` / `SpriteCB_BlinkVisible` / `SpriteCB_HideAsMoveTarget` — double battle move target blink; not implemented
- [ ] `GetBattleBgTemplateData` / `GetBattleWindowTemplatePixelWidth` — BG/window template accessors; GBA-specific, not needed
- [ ] `BattleMainCB2` — main battle loop (B-button recorded battle abort); `BattleState.ts` runs the loop but recorded-battle abort is not implemented
- [ ] `HBlankCB_Battle` — HBlank scanline register tweak; GBA hardware, not applicable
- [ ] `FreeRestoreBattleData` — frees GBA battle memory allocations; not applicable (no manual memory management)
- [ ] `BeginBattleIntro` / `BattleIntroGetMonsData` / `BattleIntroPrepareBackgroundSlide` — intro sequence state machine steps; browser battle skips GBA controller-exec sequencing
- [ ] `BattleIntroPrintTrainerWantsToBattle` / `BattleIntroPrintWildMonAttacked` / `BattleIntroPrintOpponentSendsOut` / `BattleIntroPrintPlayerSendsOut` — intro string sequencing; partially covered by `battleMessageSequences.ts` but not as discrete state-machine steps
- [ ] `BattleIntroOpponent1SendsOutMonAnimation` / `BattleIntroOpponent2SendsOutMonAnimation` / `BattleIntroPlayer1SendsOutMonAnimation` / `BattleIntroPlayer2SendsOutMonAnimation` — ball-throw animation sequencing; not implemented as discrete callbacks
- [ ] `BattleIntroRecordMonsToDex` — sets Pokédex seen flags during battle intro; not confirmed implemented
- [ ] `CheckFocusPunch_ClearVarsBeforeTurnStarts` — clears Focus Punch charging flag before turn; not confirmed in TS
- [ ] `FreeResetData_ReturnToOvOrDoEvolutions` — post-battle cleanup and evolution check entry point; `EvolutionState.ts` exists but integration from battle end not fully wired
- [ ] `TryEvolvePokemon` / `WaitForEvoSceneToFinish` — post-battle evolution trigger; `EvolutionState.ts` exists but battle→evolution handoff not confirmed
- [ ] `ReturnFromBattleToOverworld` — returns to overworld after battle; partially wired via `loadSelectedOverworldMap.ts`
- [ ] `HandleEndTurn_FinishBattle` / `HandleEndTurn_MonFled` — specific battle end outcomes; partial
- [ ] `RunTurnActionsFunctions` — dispatches turn action handlers via `sTurnActionsFuncsTable`; equivalent dispatch exists in `BattleEngine.ts`
- [ ] `AllAtActionConfirmed` — checks all battlers confirmed actions; internal to action selection loop, covered implicitly
- [ ] `SwitchPartyOrderInGameMulti` — party order swap for ingame multi; not implemented

#### Notes
- All GBA link cable / wireless (SIO, RFU), recorded battle, and multi-player link battle code is intentionally omitted — the browser port is single-player only.
- The GBA sprite callback system (`SpriteCB_*`) has no direct browser equivalent; animations are handled declaratively in `BattleSpriteLoader.ts` and `BattleOverlayFrame.ts`.
- Battle intro sequencing uses the GBA `BtlController_Emit*` controller exec pattern which is replaced by Promise-based sequencing in `BattleState.ts`.
- `gTypeEffectiveness` is fully covered by the generator script.
- `gTrainerMoneyTable` trainer class money factors are not confirmed to have a TS equivalent for post-battle reward calculation.
- The `HandleTurnActionSelectionState` state machine (9 states) is the most complex function here and is substantially implemented in `BattleEngine.ts`, though Safari Zone and Battle Palace sub-paths may be incomplete.
- Post-battle evolution flow (`TryEvolvePokemon` → `WaitForEvoSceneToFinish`) has infrastructure (`EvolutionState.ts`, `EvolutionRenderer.ts`) but the handoff from `BattleEngine` end-of-battle to `EvolutionState` is not confirmed fully wired.

- [ ] `HandleEndTurn_MonFled` — wild mon flee end-turn script (`BattleScript_WildMonFled` with nickname buffer); not fully dispatched as distinct end-turn state
- [ ] `ReturnFromBattleToOverworld` — Pokérus spread, roamer HP update, roamer deactivation on catch/win; Pokérus and roamer systems not yet ported

#### Notes
- Link cable, e-Reader trainer, recorded battle, and Battle Frontier branches throughout this file are intentionally omitted — the TS port targets single-player offline only.
- Badge 03 speed boost (110%) is present in the C `GetWhoStrikesFirst` and should be verified in the TS equivalent.
- The `sEndTurnFuncsTable` / `sTurnActionsFuncsTable` dispatch tables are replaced in TS by explicit state machine transitions in `BattleEngine.ts` rather than function pointer arrays.
- Enigma Berry handling in `GetWhoStrikesFirst` (Quick Claw branch) has no TS equivalent — Enigma Berry is a Battle Frontier item, intentionally omitted.

#### Notes
- Link cable, Union Room, recorded battle, Battle Frontier (Dome/Arena/Palace/Tower/Pike/Pyramid), e-Reader trainer, and Secret Base battle contexts are all intentionally out of scope for the browser port — roughly half of `BufferStringBattle`'s branching logic covers these.
- The TS port's `battleMessageSequences.ts` uses a higher-level sequence model rather than a GBA string ID dispatch table; the semantic coverage is reasonable for single-player trainer/wild battles but the placeholder substitution depth is shallower than the C implementation.
- `sTextOnWindowsInfo_*` structs are GBA-specific window rendering configuration with no meaningful browser equivalent — intentional omission.
- Safari Zone battle messages (Pokéblock, "crept closer", ball counts) are missing as that game mode hasn't been ported.

## batch: src/battle_message.c (part 2/2)

### `src/battle_message.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/battle/messages/battleMessageSequences.ts`, `src/pokemon/pp.ts`, `src/battle/render/BattleHealthBox.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `GetCurrentPpToMaxPpState` — PP color state logic (0–3) is implemented in `src/pokemon/pp.ts` as PP tier classification for UI coloring

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `BattlePutTextOnWindow` — text rendering to battle windows exists conceptually via `PromptPrinterEngine`/`BattleScriptRuntime`, but the full window-slot system (B_WIN_MSG, ARENA_WIN_JUDGMENT_TEXT, font/color per window type, link battle speed overrides) is not replicated; the TS port uses a dialog/prompt abstraction rather than GBA window IDs
- [-] `SetPpNumbersPaletteInMoveSelection` — PP palette swapping based on `GetCurrentPpToMaxPpState` is partially present in `BattleHealthBox.ts` and the move selection UI, but the direct GBA palette buffer manipulation has no equivalent (CSS color classes are used instead)

#### Missing
- [ ] `ChooseMoveUsedParticle` — Japanese grammar particle selection for move-used strings; irrelevant in English port but the lookup table logic is absent
- [ ] `ChooseTypeOfMoveUsedString` — Japanese grammar suffix selection (5 variants); English always uses "used", so the multi-suffix logic is not needed, but the function itself is unported

#### Notes
- `ChooseMoveUsedParticle` and `ChooseTypeOfMoveUsedString` are Japanese-localization artifacts; the English port hardcodes "used" and "!" so these have no meaningful TS equivalent needed.
- `BattlePutTextOnWindow` covers ~30 window IDs with per-slot font/color/speed metadata (`sBattleTextOnWindowsInfo`); the TS port uses a higher-level prompt/dialog system and does not need to replicate GBA VRAM window management.
- The PP color tier logic in `GetCurrentPpToMaxPpState` appears to be faithfully ported in `src/pokemon/pp.ts`.
- GBA palette buffer writes (`gPlttBufferUnfaded`, `CpuCopy16`) in `SetPpNumbersPaletteInMoveSelection` have no browser equivalent; color is handled via CSS/WebGL shader parameters.

- [ ] `SetupRoomObjectEvents` — sets `VAR_OBJ_GFX_ID_0/1` based on room type, calls trainer-prep helpers
- [ ] `GetBattlePikeData` / `SetBattlePikeData` — read/write frontier save block fields (prize, win streak, record streak, total streaks, active streak flag)
- [ ] `IsNextRoomFinal` — checks if `curChallengeBattleNum > NUM_PIKE_ROOMS`
- [ ] `GetRoomType` — returns `sRoomType` IWRAM variable
- [ ] `SetInWildMonRoom` / `ClearInWildMonRoom` — set/clear `sInWildMonRoom` flag
- [ ] `SavePikeChallenge` — writes challenge status, clears temp var, sets paused flag, saves map view and SRAM
- [ ] `GetRoomInflictedStatus` — maps `sStatusFlags` bitmask to `PIKE_STATUS_*` enum result
- [ ] `GetRoomInflictedStatusMon` — returns which mon graphic (Dusclops/Kirlia) delivered the status
- [ ] `HealOneOrTwoMons` — heals 1–2 random mons, returns count
- [ ] `BufferNPCMessage` — selects speech ID from `sNPCTable` based on room progress, calls `FrontierSpeechToString`
- [ ] `StatusInflictionScreenFlash` / `Task_DoStatusInflictionScreenFlash` / `StartStatusInflictionScreenFlash` / `StatusInflictionFadeOut` / `StatusInflictionFadeIn` — palette fade task for status room screen flash effect
- [ ] `GetInBattlePike` / `InBattlePike` — checks current map layout ID against 4 Pike layout constants
- [ ] `SetHintedRoom` / `GetHintedRoomIndex` / `GetRoomTypeHint` — assigns hinted room index and type at corridor entry, returns hint category (`PIKE_HINT_PEOPLE/WHISPERING/NOSTALGIA/POKEMON/BRAIN`)
- [ ] `ClearPikeTrainerIds` — resets all `trainerIds[]` to `0xFFFF`
- [ ] `BufferTrainerIntro` — formats trainer pre-battle speech for opponent A or B
- [ ] `PrepareOneTrainer` / `PrepareTwoTrainers` — selects non-duplicate scaled frontier trainer IDs, sets `gTrainerBattleOpponent_A/B` and GFX IDs
- [ ] `GetCurrentRoomPikeQueenFightType` / `GetPikeQueenFightType` — determines if Lucy appears (silver/gold/streak) based on win streak and symbol count
- [ ] `HealSomeMonsBeforePikeQueen` — heals N mons (from `sNumMonsToHealBeforePikeQueen` table) before brain fight
- [ ] `SetHealingroomTypesDisabled` — sets `pikeHealingRoomsDisabled` flag in save block
- [ ] `IsPartyFullHealed` — checks all 3 frontier party mons for full HP, no status, full PP
- [ ] `SaveMonHeldItems` / `RestoreMonHeldItems` — backup/restore held items to `pikeHeldItemsBackup[]` in save block
- [ ] `InitPikeChallenge` — resets challenge state, clears win streak if no active streak flag
- [ ] `TryInflictRandomStatus` — randomly inflicts a status (toxic 35%, freeze 25%, paralysis 20%, sleep 10%, burn 10%) on up to N party mons, respecting ability/type immunities
- [ ] `HealMon` — fully restores HP, PP (with bonuses), and clears status on a single mon
- [ ] `DoesAbilityPreventStatus` / `DoesTypePreventStatus` — immunity checks for status infliction
- [ ] `AtLeastOneHealthyMon` / `AtLeastTwoAliveMons` — party health checks used for room type gating
- [ ] `TryGenerateBattlePikeWildMon` — creates the wild encounter mon for the Pike wild room (scaled level, random ability, preset moves from `sWildMons` tables)
- [ ] `GetBattlePikeWildMonHeaderId` — returns 0–3 wild mon table index based on cumulative win streak
- [ ] `SpeciesToPikeMonId` — maps Seviper→0, Milotic→1, other→2
- [ ] `CanEncounterWildMon` — applies Keen Eye/Intimidate level-5-gap avoidance (50% chance skip)
- [ ] `GetNPCRoomGraphicsId` — picks random NPC from `sNPCTable`, stores `sNpcId`

#### Notes
- Entire Battle Pike subsystem is unimplemented. This is Battle Frontier content — a large post-game feature. The map script files (`BattleFrontier_BattlePike*.gen.ts`) are generated and will invoke `special CallBattlePikeFunction` with various `gSpecialVar_0x8004` values; without the runtime dispatcher these scripts cannot execute.
- The `sNPCSpeeches` table uses Easy Chat words (a structured two-byte word encoding system) — these require `FrontierSpeechToString` to render, which itself is unimplemented.
- The status infliction screen flash is a GBA palette-blend task; in the browser port this would need a CSS/WebGL fade overlay equivalent.
- `InBattlePike` checks `mapLayoutId` constants — the TS port would need to check equivalent map identifiers from the generated map data.
- Wild mon data tables (`sLvl50Mons`, `sLvlOpenMons`) and NPC table (`sNPCTable`) are const ROM data embedded in this file; they would need to be either extracted by a generator script or hand-ported as data constants if Battle Pike is ever implemented.

## batch: src/battle_pyramid.c

### `src/battle_pyramid.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `CallBattlePyramidFunction` — dispatch table entry point called from scripts via `gSpecialVar_0x8004`
- [ ] `InitPyramidChallenge` — resets frontier challenge state, win streaks, bag items on new run
- [ ] `GetBattlePyramidData` / `SetBattlePyramidData` — read/write pyramid save data (prize, win streak, active flags) via special vars
- [ ] `SavePyramidChallenge` — checkpoints challenge state and triggers save
- [ ] `SetBattlePyramidPrize` / `GiveBattlePyramidPrize` — determines and gives streak reward item (short: vitamins, long: hold items)
- [ ] `SeedPyramidFloor` — fills `pyramidRandoms[4]` from RNG, resets trainer flags
- [ ] `SetPickupItem` — determines which item ball gives based on floor/round/RNG using `sPickupItemsLvl50` / `sPickupItemsLvlOpen` tables
- [ ] `HidePyramidItem` — moves picked-up item object event to `SHRT_MAX,SHRT_MAX` to hide it without using flags
- [ ] `SetPyramidFacilityTrainers` — sets `gFacilityTrainers = gBattleFrontierTrainers`
- [ ] `ShowPostBattleHintText` — post-battle hint system: tells player direction to exit, remaining items, remaining trainers (6 text groups × 3 hint categories)
- [ ] `UpdatePyramidWinStreak` — increments win streak, updates record streak, caps at 999
- [ ] `GetCurrentBattlePyramidLocation` — returns `PYRAMID_LOCATION_FLOOR`, `_TOP`, or `_NONE` based on map layout ID
- [ ] `UpdatePyramidLightRadius` — sets or incrementally grows the pyramid darkness light radius, drives scanline effect
- [ ] `ClearPyramidPartyHeldItems` — strips held items from selected frontier party mons
- [ ] `SetPyramidFloorPalette` / `Task_SetPyramidFloorPalette` — applies per-floor palette after fade completes
- [ ] `RestorePyramidPlayerParty` — copies modified frontier party back to `playerParty`/`playerParty` save slot, handles Sketch replacement
- [ ] `GenerateBattlePyramidFloorLayout` — stitches 4×4 grid of sub-map layouts into `backupMapData`, replaces exit metatiles in non-exit squares, sets player spawn
- [ ] `LoadBattlePyramidObjectEventTemplates` — places trainer and item-ball object events across pyramid floor squares based on floor template
- [ ] `LoadBattlePyramidFloorObjectEventScripts` — assigns `BattlePyramid_TrainerBattle` or `BattlePyramid_FindItemBall` script to each object event
- [ ] `GenerateBattlePyramidWildMon` — builds wild mon from `PyramidWildMon` table (species, level ±5 RNG, ability, moves, IVs at high streak)
- [ ] `GetPyramidRunMultiplier` — returns `runMultiplier` from floor template (controls flee probability)
- [ ] `LocalIdToPyramidTrainerId` — maps object event local ID → frontier trainer ID via save data
- [ ] `GetBattlePyramidTrainerFlag` — checks if a specific trainer has been defeated this floor
- [ ] `MarkApproachingPyramidTrainersAsBattled` / `MarkPyramidTrainerAsBattled` — sets trainer defeated flag, switches movement to `WANDER_AROUND`
- [ ] `GetTrainerEncounterMusicIdInBattlePyramid` — maps trainer class → encounter music using `sTrainerClassEncounterMusic` table
- [ ] `CopyPyramidTrainerSpeechBefore` / `CopyPyramidTrainerWinSpeech` / `CopyPyramidTrainerLoseSpeech` — copies facility trainer speech strings to `gStringVar1`
- [ ] `PausePyramidChallenge` — handles mid-challenge soft pause (restores party, sets pause status, unloads music)
- [ ] `SoftResetInBattlePyramid` — triggers soft reset if currently in pyramid
- [ ] `InBattlePyramid_` — bool check for current map being pyramid floor or top
- [ ] `GetBattlePyramidPickupItemId` — ability-pickup item lookup (round + RNG → item from tables)
- [ ] `GetNumBattlePyramidObjectEvents` — counts active object event templates until `LOCALID_NONE`
- [ ] `InitPyramidBagItems` — resets pyramid bag, adds starting Hyper Potion + Ether
- [ ] 8 static placement helpers (`GetPyramidFloorLayoutOffsets`, `GetPyramidEntranceAndExitSquareIds`, `SetPyramidObjectPositionsUniformly`, `SetPyramidObjectPositionsInAndNearSquare`, `SetPyramidObjectPositionsNearSquare`, `TrySetPyramidObjectEventPositionInSquare`, `TrySetPyramidObjectEventPositionAtCoords`, `GetPyramidFloorTemplateId`) — RNG-driven object placement and floor template selection algorithms
- [ ] `GetPostBattleDirectionHintTextIndex` — scans map grid for `METATILE_BattlePyramid_Exit`, computes cardinal direction and distance for hint text selection

#### Notes
Battle Pyramid is a Battle Frontier facility — entirely unimplemented in the TypeScript port. The project's current scope focuses on the main story (Route 101 through first badge), so Battle Frontier content (`battle_pyramid.c`, `battle_tower.c`, `battle_dome.c`, etc.) is out of scope. The pyramid's procedural floor generation (`GenerateBattlePyramidFloorLayout`) is architecturally novel — it stitches 16 sub-map layout chunks at runtime, which would require significant extension to the existing `WorldManager`/map-loading pipeline. The darkness/light-radius scanline effect (`UpdatePyramidLightRadius`, `WriteBattlePyramidViewScanlineEffectBuffer`) has no WebGL equivalent. The `sPickupItemsLvl50`/`sPickupItemsLvlOpen` data tables (items per round) and `sPyramidFloorTemplates` (floor configs) are all missing from generated data.

- [ ] `ShowItemIcon` / `FreeItemIconSprite` / `FreeItemIconSpriteByAltId` — alternating item icon sprite slots
- [ ] `CreatePyramidBagSprite` / `ShakePyramidBag` / `SpriteCB_BagWaitForShake` — animated bag sprite with affine shake
- [ ] `CreateSwapLine` / `UpdateSwapLinePos` / `SetSwapLineInvisibility` — swap-line sprite for reordering UI

- [ ] `SetBagItemsListTemplate` / `CopyBagItemName` — list menu template population with berry number formatting
- [ ] `OpenMenuActionWindowById` / `CloseMenuActionWindowById` / `PrintMenuActionText_SingleRow` / `PrintMenuActionText_MultiRow` / `IsValidMenuAction` — dynamic windowed action menu management

- [ ] `DrawTossNumberWindow` / `ShowNumToToss` / `UpdateNumToToss` — quantity input widget

#### Notes
The Battle Pyramid (post-game Battle Frontier facility) is entirely unimplemented in the TypeScript port. This file is the bag UI specifically for that facility — a stripped-down bag with only the items collected during a pyramid run, supporting field use, giving, tossing, battle use, and SELECT-based reordering. The normal bag (`BagMenu.tsx`, `BagManager.ts`) covers a different code path. All GBA hardware setup (`InitPyramidBagBgs`, palette DMA, OAM) is intentionally absent as a browser port wouldn't replicate it directly, but the gameplay logic (toss flow, swap, context menus, give-to-party) also has no TS equivalent. This is post-game content that would be low priority.

The Trainer Hill records screen (`ShowTrainerHillRecords` / `PrintOnTrainerHillRecordsWindow`) is a post-game UI feature; Trainer Hill runtime logic exists in `src/game/trainerHillRuntime.ts` but the dedicated records display screen is not ported. - [ ] `AI_CalcDmg` — AI damage calculation helper
- [ ] `AccuracyCalcHelper` / `JumpIfMoveFailed` / `JumpIfMoveAffectedByProtect` — internal accuracy helpers (partially in `commands.ts`)
- [ ] `TrySetDestinyBondToHappen` — Destiny Bond flag setter
- [ ] `AttacksThisTurn` — checks if it's a charging turn
- [ ] `CheckWonderGuardAndLevitate` — Wonder Guard / Levitate miss-type message on accuracy miss
- [ ] `ChangeStatBuffs` — stat stage change with cap, fail conditions, Mirror Armor etc.
- [ ] `IsMonGettingExpSentOut` — exp eligibility check
- [ ] `sNaturePowerMoves` — Nature Power move by environment (data; partially in `battleEnvironmentResolver.ts`)
- [ ] `sWeightToDamageTable` — Low Kick/Grass Knot weight table
- [ ] `sPickupItems` / `sRarePickupItems` / `sPickupProbabilities` — Pickup ability item tables
- [ ] `sBallCatchBonuses` — catch rate multipliers per ball type
- [ ] `sMovesForbiddenToCopy` — Mimic/Metronome/Assist forbidden move lists
- [ ] `sFlailHpScaleToPowerTable` — Flail/Reversal power table
- [ ] `sEnvironmentToType` — Secret Power type by environment
- [ ] `gBattlePalaceNatureToMoveGroupLikelihood` / `sBattlePalaceNatureToFlavorTextId` — Battle Palace data (Battle Frontier not implemented)
- [ ] `sProtectSuccessRates` — Protect successive failure rates
- [ ] Level-up banner graphics / sprite data — GBA hardware; intentionally omitted

#### Notes
- The battle script command table has 249 commands (0x00–0xF8). The TS `BattleScriptRuntime` implements the VM loop and many core commands, but the majority of the 200+ individual `Cmd_*` implementations remain missing or are stubs. Priority implementations appear to be the damage pipeline (critcalc → damagecalc → typecalc → adjustnormaldamage), status effects, and basic flow control.
- Link battle commands (`Cmd_endlinkbattle`, link-specific guards throughout) are intentionally omitted — no multiplayer.
- Battle Frontier (`BATTLE_TYPE_FRONTIER`, `BATTLE_TYPE_ARENA`, `BATTLE_TYPE_PIKE`, `BATTLE_TYPE_PYRAMID`, Palace data) is entirely unimplemented.
- E-Reader trainer guards (`BATTLE_TYPE_EREADER_TRAINER`) are intentionally omitted.
- GBA controller emit functions (`BtlController_EmitHealthBarUpdate`, `BtlController_EmitSetMonData`, etc.) are replaced by direct state mutation in the TS engine.
- The `Cmd_getexp` multi-state machine is complex; the TS port likely has a simplified version without the battle controller round-trips.
- `sAccuracyStageRatios` is directly ported as a lookup table in the TS damage calculator.
- `Cmd_various` is a large sub-dispatcher (~50+ sub-commands) and is almost certainly incomplete in the TS port.

## batch: src/battle_script_commands.c (part 2/3)

### `src/battle_script_commands.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/battle/engine/scriptRuntime/BattleScriptRuntime.ts`, `src/battle/engine/scriptRuntime/commands.ts`, `src/battle/engine/BattleEngine.ts`, `src/battle/engine/MoveEffects.ts`, `src/battle/engine/StatusEffects.ts`
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-battle-scripts.cjs` (generates `src/data/battleScripts.gen.ts`)

#### Implemented
- [x] `Cmd_waitstate` — tick-gated wait for controller execution flags to clear
- [x] `Cmd_return` / `Cmd_end` / `Cmd_end2` — script stack pop and action-finish transitions
- [x] `Cmd_call` — push return address and jump to subroutine
- [x] `Cmd_pause` — frame-counted pause between script steps
- [x] `Cmd_healthbar_update` — emit HP bar update to battler controller
- [x] `Cmd_moveend` (partial states) — rage, defrost, synchronize, choice band, item effects, substitute, last-move tracking (see Partially Implemented)
- [x] `Cmd_typecalc2` — Levitate ground immunity, Wonder Guard, type effectiveness flags
- [x] `Cmd_statbuffchange` / `ChangeStatBuffs` — stat stage clamping, Mist/Clear Body/Keen Eye/Hyper Cutter/Shield Dust guards
- [x] `Cmd_normalisebuffs` — Haze, reset all stat stages to default
- [x] `Cmd_setbide` — set Bide multi-turn status
- [x] `Cmd_setsubstitute` — deduct HP, set STATUS2_SUBSTITUTE, set substituteHP
- [x] `Cmd_setrain` / `Cmd_setsandstorm` — set weather flags and duration
- [x] `Cmd_setreflect` / `Cmd_setlightscreen` / `Cmd_setmist` / `Cmd_setfocusenergy` — side-status and battler-status setters
- [x] `Cmd_setseeded` — Leech Seed application with Grass-type immunity
- [x] `Cmd_trysetrest` — full HP restore, force 3-turn sleep via Rest
- [x] `Cmd_jumpifcantmakeasleep` — Insomnia/Vital Spirit/Uproar sleep prevention
- [x] `Cmd_stockpile` / `Cmd_stockpiletobasedamage` / `Cmd_stockpiletohpheal` — Stockpile counter and Spit Up/Swallow damage/heal
- [x] `Cmd_confuseifrepeatingattackends` — Thrash/Outrage confusion on end
- [x] `Cmd_setmultihitcounter` — multi-hit RNG (2–5 hit distribution)
- [x] `Cmd_tryexplosion` — Damp check, attacker HP → 0
- [x] `Cmd_setatkhptozero` — set attacker HP to 0 directly
- [x] `Cmd_tryhealhalfhealth` — half-HP heal (Milk Drink / Soft-Boiled / Moonlight skeleton)
- [x] `Cmd_trymirrormove` — Mirror Move, scan lastTakenMoveFrom, pick random valid mirror target
- [x] `Cmd_damagetohalftargethp` — Super Fang 50% current HP
- [x] `Cmd_weatherdamage` — Sandstorm/Hail end-of-turn chip (type/ability immunities)
- [x] `Cmd_tryinfatuating` — Attract gender check, Oblivious immunity
- [x] `Cmd_transformdataexecution` — Transform: copy BattlePokemon data, cap PP at 5
- [x] `Cmd_setprotectlike` — Protect/Endure success rate decay and flag set
- [x] `Cmd_tryKO` — OHKO accuracy formula (level difference), Sturdy immunity, endure/focus-band survival
- [x] `Cmd_manipulatedamage` — sign flip, recoil-from-miss halving, doubling
- [x] `Cmd_negativedamage` — drain-back half of damage dealt
- [x] `Cmd_counterdamagecalculator` / `Cmd_mirrorcoatdamagecalculator` — Counter/Mirror Coat 2× physical/special damage back
- [x] `Cmd_disablelastusedattack` — Disable last used move for random turns
- [x] `Cmd_trysetencore` — Encore lock on last used move
- [x] `Cmd_painsplitdmgcalc` — Pain Split HP averaging
- [x] `Cmd_tryconversiontypechange` — Conversion random move-type change
- [x] `Cmd_settypetorandomresistance` — Conversion 2 random resistance type
- [x] `Cmd_dmgtolevel` — level-as-damage (Seismic Toss / Night Shade)
- [x] `Cmd_psywavedamageeffect` — Psywave random-multiplier damage
- [x] `Cmd_nop` — no-op advance
- [x] `Cmd_givepaydaymoney` — Pay Day money accumulation
- [x] `Cmd_getmoneyreward` — trainer prize money calculation (level × class value × multiplier)
- [x] `Cmd_handlelearnnewmove` — post-battle level-up move learning loop
- [x] `Cmd_jumpifnotfirstturn` — isFirstTurn gate (for charging moves)
- [x] `Cmd_jumpiftype2` — branch if battler has a given type
- [x] `Cmd_jumpifabilitypresent` — branch if ability present on field

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `Cmd_moveend` — states MOVEEND_RAGE, MOVEEND_DEFROST, MOVEEND_SYNCHRONIZE_*, MOVEEND_ON_DAMAGE_ABILITIES, MOVEEND_IMMUNITY_ABILITIES, MOVEEND_CHOICE_MOVE, MOVEEND_SUBSTITUTE implemented; MOVEEND_NEXT_TARGET (doubles second target redirect) is absent as doubles are not implemented; MOVEEND_MIRROR_MOVE lastTakenMoveFrom bookkeeping may be incomplete
- [-] `Cmd_switchineffects` — Spikes damage on switch-in, ability/item on-switchin hooks, Truant hack present; Baton Pass stat/status2 carry-over exists in `BattleEngine.ts` but full party-order and multi-battle edge cases not handled
- [-] `Cmd_openpartyscreen` / `Cmd_getswitchedmondata` / `Cmd_switchindataupdate` / `Cmd_switchinanim` / `Cmd_switchhandleorder` — single-battle faint replacement handled; BATTLE_TYPE_DOUBLE / MULTI / TWO_OPPONENTS simultaneous-faint coordination branches absent (doubles not in scope)
- [-] `Cmd_forcerandomswitch` — Roar/Whirlwind single-battle path ported; multi/link/Tower multi-flank branches absent
- [-] `Cmd_playanimation` / `Cmd_playanimation_var` — animation dispatch exists as no-op / visual stub; semi-invulnerability suppression flag respected but no actual GFX system
- [-] `Cmd_playstatchangeanimation` / `Cmd_setgraphicalstatchangevalues` — stat-change anim selection logic partially present in BattleEngine but animation emission is stubbed
- [-] `Cmd_various` — many sub-cases implemented (CANCEL_MULTI_TURN_MOVES, IS_RUNNING_IMPOSSIBLE, GET_MOVE_TARGET, RESET_INTIMIDATE_TRACE_BITS, SET_TELEPORT_OUTCOME, PLAY_TRAINER_DEFEATED_MUSIC, PALACE_FLAVOR_TEXT partially); Battle Arena sub-cases (ARENA_JUDGMENT_WINDOW, ARENA_OPPONENT/PLAYER_MON_LOST, ARENA_BOTH_MONS_LOST, ARENA_JUDGMENT_STRING etc.) absent — Battle Arena not in scope
- [-] `Cmd_adjustsetdamage` — Focus Band and endure survival path present; Enigma Berry path absent (Battle Frontier item, low priority)
- [-] `Cmd_yesnoboxlearnmove` / `Cmd_yesnoboxstoplearningmove` — move learning yes/no UI handled in `src/pokemon/moveLearningPromptAdapter.ts` but not wired through the battle script runtime; battle-context palette fade states absent
- [-] `Cmd_drawlvlupbox` — level-up stat banner state machine not ported to battle script runtime; post-battle level-up handled outside script flow in TS

#### Missing
- [ ] `Cmd_orbyte` / `Cmd_orhalfword` / `Cmd_orword` / `Cmd_bicbyte` / `Cmd_bichalfword` / `Cmd_bicword` — raw bitwise OR/BIC into arbitrary RAM pointers; no equivalent (GBA pointer arithmetic, not applicable in TS)
- [ ] `Cmd_end3` — pop main battle callback stack (link battle / multi-battle callback management; not applicable)
- [ ] `Cmd_endselectionscript` — marks selection script finished for a battler slot (Battle Dome / multi selection flow; not implemented)
- [ ] `Cmd_returnatktoball` / `Cmd_returntoball` — emit sprite return-to-ball animation to controller
- [ ] `Cmd_trainerslidein` — trainer slide-in animation controller emit
- [ ] `Cmd_playse` / `Cmd_fanfare` / `Cmd_playfaintcry` — sound/fanfare emission (audio system not implemented)
- [ ] `Cmd_endlinkbattle` — link battle end packet (link cable; intentionally omitted)
- [ ] `Cmd_jumpifcantswitch` — escape-prevention / wrap / rooted check before switching; multi/link party-slot range logic
- [ ] `Cmd_updatebattlermoves` — refresh battle-mon move array from controller buffer (unused in C too)
- [ ] `Cmd_swapattackerwithtarget` — swap attacker/target + toggle HITMARKER_SWAP
- [ ] `Cmd_incrementgamestat` — increment persistent game stat counters
- [ ] `Cmd_drawpartystatussummary` / `Cmd_hidepartystatussummary` — party HP/status summary widget (battle UI)
- [ ] `Cmd_jumptocalledmove` — redirect to called move's effect script (Sleep Talk / Assist)
- [ ] `Cmd_statusanimation` / `Cmd_status2animation` / `Cmd_chosenstatusanimation` — status condition sprite animations
- [ ] `Cmd_yesnobox` — generic yes/no box in battle (used for wild-catch prompt etc.)
- [ ] `Cmd_cancelallactions` — cancel all pending battler actions this turn
- [ ] `Cmd_removeitem` — strip held item and notify controller
- [ ] `Cmd_atknameinbuff1` — fill text buffer with attacker nickname
- [ ] `Cmd_drawlvlupbox` — level-up stat box slide-in/out state machine
- [ ] `Cmd_resetsentmonsvalue` — reset sent-mons-to-opponent tracking
- [ ] `Cmd_setatktoplayer0` — force attacker to player-left slot
- [ ] `Cmd_makevisible` — emit sprite-visibility TRUE to controller
- [ ] `Cmd_recordlastability` — record last-used ability for battle log
- [ ] `Cmd_buffermovetolearn` — fill gBattleTextBuff2 with move-to-learn name
- [ ] `Cmd_jumpifplayerran` — TryRunFromBattle check + branch
- [ ] `Cmd_hpthresholds` / `Cmd_hpthresholds2` — set hpScale for trainer AI messaging
- [ ] `Cmd_useitemonopponent` — AI uses item on own party member
- [ ] `Cmd_hitanimation` — emit hit-flash animation to controller
- [ ] `Cmd_initmultihitstring` / multihit string buffer setup
- [ ] `UproarWakeUpCheck` — shared helper for Uproar sleep prevention (used by multiple commands)
- [ ] `GetTrainerMoneyToGive` — money formula helper (trainer class table lookup) — **partially**: formula is implemented in `Cmd_getmoneyreward` but trainer class money table (`gTrainerMoneyTable`) is not generated
- [ ] Level-up banner helpers (`InitLevelUpBanner`, `SlideInLevelUpBanner`, `SlideOutLevelUpBanner`, `DrawLevelUpBannerText`, `PutMonIconOnLvlUpBanner`, `SpriteCB_MonIconOnLvlUpBanner`, `DrawLevelUpWindow1/2`, `IsMonGettingExpSentOut`) — GBA BG layer banner animation; not applicable to WebGL renderer

#### Notes
- The TS battle script runtime (`BattleScriptRuntime.ts` + `commands.ts`) implements a subset of commands sufficient for single-battle trainer/wild encounters. The C file contains ~80 distinct `Cmd_*` functions; roughly 45–50 have meaningful TS equivalents.
- All link-cable, e-Reader, Battle Dome, Battle Arena, Battle Palace, Battle Pike, Battle Pyramid, and multi-battle branches are intentionally out of scope.
- GBA controller-emit calls (`BtlController_Emit*`) map to TS state mutations and render callbacks rather than IPC buffers — the architecture is substantially different but functionally equivalent for single battles.
- Sound emission commands (`playse`, `fanfare`, `playfaintcry`) have no implementation because the audio system is not yet ported.
- The `Cmd_various` dispatch table has ~25 sub-cases; approximately half are implemented, half are Battle Frontier / Arena specific and intentionally omitted.
- `gTrainerMoneyTable` (trainer class → money value mapping) does not appear in any generator manifest and is not present in TS — `Cmd_getmoneyreward` will produce incorrect values without it.
