- [x] `Cmd_selectfirstvalidtarget` — Find first non-absent opposing battler → `BattleEngine.ts`
- [x] `Cmd_jumpifconfusedandstatmaxed` — Conditional jump if confused and stat at MAX → `BattleEngine.ts`
- [x] `Cmd_jumpifnodamage` — Conditional jump if attacker took no physical or special damage → `BattleEngine.ts`
- [x] `Cmd_jumpifhasnohp` — Conditional jump if battler HP == 0 → `BattleEngine.ts`
- [x] `Cmd_finishaction` / `Cmd_finishturn` — battle loop control → `BattleEngine.ts`
- [x] `Cmd_subattackerhpbydmg` — subtract battle damage from attacker HP (recoil/crash) → `BattleEngine.ts`
- [x] `Cmd_removeattackerstatus1` — zero attacker's primary status → `BattleEngine.ts`
- [x] `Cmd_switchoutabilities` — Natural Cure clears status on switch-out → `StatusEffects.ts`
- [x] `Cmd_settypebasedhalvers` — Mud Sport / Water Sport halve Electric/Fire → `MoveEffects.ts`
- [x] `Cmd_setweatherballtype` — Weather Ball type and 2× power in active weather → `MoveEffects.ts`
- [x] `Cmd_getsecretpowereffect` — Secret Power secondary effect per environment → `MoveEffects.ts`
- [x] `Cmd_weightdamagecalculation` — Low Kick / Grass Knot weight-to-power table → `MoveEffects.ts`
- [x] `Cmd_callenvironmentattack` — Nature Power maps environment to move → `MoveEffects.ts`
- [x] `Cmd_settypetoenvironment` — Camouflage sets user's type to terrain type → `MoveEffects.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `Cmd_presentdamagecalculation` — Present random power tiers (40/80/120/heal); damage calculation likely present, but heal-target branch and exact script redirects (`BattleScript_HitFromCritCalc`, `BattleScript_PresentHealTarget`) may not be wired up → `MoveEffects.ts`
- [-] `Cmd_tryspiteppreduce` — Spite PP reduction; core logic plausible, but `CancelMultiTurnMoves` side-effect and permanent-move PP-back suppression need verification → `MoveEffects.ts`
- [-] `Cmd_trydobeatup` — Beat Up per-party-member damage with species base stats; complex multi-hit loop, partial → `MoveEffects.ts`
- [-] `Cmd_tryswapitems` — Trick; core swap present but knocked-off-item tracking (`gWishFutureKnock.knockedOffMons`), Sticky Hold check, Enigma Berry / mail checks may be incomplete → `MoveEffects.ts`
- [-] `Cmd_tryimprison` — Imprison Gen 3 logic (fails if no shared moves); STATUS3_IMPRISONED_OTHERS set but Pressure-PP side-call may be missing → `MoveEffects.ts`
- [-] `Cmd_trysetgrudge` — Grudge STATUS3_GRUDGE flag; flag set, but grudge trigger on faint (zeroing PP) integration unclear → `StatusEffects.ts`
- [-] `Cmd_handleballthrow` — Catch formula (sqrt-of-sqrt odds, ball multipliers, status bonus); core formula likely present in `BattleEngine.ts` but Wally tutorial branch, Safari Ball rate, full ball-type table, and repeat-ball Pokedex check may be incomplete → `BattleEngine.ts`
- [-] `Cmd_trycastformdatachange` / `Cmd_docastformchangeanimation` — Castform form change logic; data generation exists but runtime form-change animation dispatch and sprite swap likely partial → `BattleEngine.ts`
- [-] `Cmd_jumpifnopursuitswitchdmg` — Pursuit intercept on switch; turn-order manipulation and multi-hit-counter side may be partial → `BattleEngine.ts`
- [-] `Cmd_snatchsetbattlers` / `Cmd_trysetsnatch` / `Cmd_trysetmagiccoat` — Snatch and Magic Coat reactive interception; flags set, but battler-swap bookkeeping and last-to-move check may not be fully wired → `commands.ts`
- [-] `Cmd_trymemento` — Memento success path (HP-to-zero animation dispatch) may need `BtlController_EmitHealthBarUpdate` equivalent → `MoveEffects.ts`
- [-] `Cmd_tryrecycleitem` — Recycle; depends on `usedHeldItems` tracking being maintained throughout battle → `BattleEngine.ts`
- [-] `Cmd_assistattackselect` — Assist forbidden-move list (`sMovesForbiddenToCopy`) may not be fully enumerated → `MoveEffects.ts`
- [-] `Cmd_trygetintimidatetarget` — Intimidate multi-target loop; entry-hazard-style sequencing across multiple opponents may be incomplete → `BattleEngine.ts`
- [-] `Cmd_copymovepermanently` — Sketch; move replacement + PP sync to controller; permanent move data write to party struct may be partial → `MoveEffects.ts`

#### Missing
- [ ] `Cmd_givecaughtmon` — transfers caught mon to player party or PC box with box-full/Lanette messaging → no TS equivalent
- [ ] `Cmd_trysetcaughtmondexflags` — sets FLAG_GET_CAUGHT in Pokédex after catch → no TS equivalent
- [ ] `Cmd_displaydexinfo` — shows Pokédex entry page after first catch (multi-step fade + task) → no TS equivalent (GBA-specific UI flow)
- [ ] `HandleBattleWindow` / `BattleCreateYesNoCursorAt` / `BattleDestroyYesNoCursorAt` — GBA BG tilemap window drawing for yes/no cursor → intentionally omitted (browser uses React UI)
- [ ] `Cmd_trygivecaughtmonnick` — yes/no naming prompt + `DoNamingScreen` call after catch → no TS equivalent (naming screen not implemented)
- [ ] `Cmd_pickup` — post-battle Pickup ability item grant (normal and Battle Pyramid variants) → no TS equivalent
- [ ] `Cmd_trainerslideout` — trainer sprite slide-back animation controller emit → render-layer omission; `BattleLayout.ts` handles layout but not this specific controller dispatch
- [ ] `Cmd_pursuitdoubles` — doubles-specific Pursuit partner interception (marked unused in C) → not ported

#### Notes
- The TS battle script runtime (`BattleScriptRuntime.ts` + `commands.ts`) mirrors the C `Cmd_*` dispatch table but uses a TypeScript command registry rather than a flat function array. Most gameplay-critical commands are present; the primary gaps are post-catch UI flow (naming screen, Pokédex display), Pickup ability post-battle loop, and some nuanced Gen 3-specific edge cases (Soundproof interaction in Heal Bell's party loop, `knockedOffMons` for Trick).
- GBA controller emit calls (`BtlController_EmitSetMonData`, `BtlController_EmitHealthBarUpdate`) have no direct TS equivalent — the port uses state-based rendering rather than message passing to battler controllers. This means "worked" branches exist but the "notify controller" side-effects are handled architecturally differently.
- Ball catch formula (`Cmd_handleballthrow`) is one of the more complex omissions: it requires Pokédex caught-flag checks, Battle Pyramid pickup tables, and the multi-shake animation sequencing — the `BattleParity.test.ts` suite likely covers the odds formula but not the full branching.
- `Cmd_displaydexinfo` and GBA window rendering helpers (`HandleBattleWindow`, cursor functions) are intentionally not ported — the browser port uses React components for all battle UI overlays.

### `src/battle_setup.c`

**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/battle/BattleStartRequest.ts`, `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts`, `src/scripting/trainerFlags.ts`, `src/game/encounters/overworldWildEncounterFlow.ts`, `src/pages/gamePage/trainerBattleFallback.ts`
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-flag-var-maps.cjs` (for `TRAINER_FLAGS_START`, rematch flag constants)

#### Implemented
- [x] `HasTrainerBeenFought` / `SetTrainerFlag` / `ClearTrainerFlag` — trainer flag get/set/clear via `src/scripting/trainerFlags.ts`
- [x] `BattleSetup_StartTrainerBattle` (entry point) — partially via `src/pages/gamePage/trainerBattleFallback.ts` which routes into `BattleStartRequest`
- [x] `BattleSetup_StartWildBattle` / `DoStandardWildBattle` — wild encounter flow in `src/game/encounters/overworldWildEncounterFlow.ts`
- [x] `BattleSetup_GetEnvironmentId` — battle environment resolver `src/battle/render/battleEnvironmentResolver.ts`
- [x] `IsPlayerDefeated` — battle outcome check in `src/battle/engine/types.ts` / `BattleEngine.ts`
- [x] `BattleSetup_ConfigureTrainerBattle` (partial) — trainer battle configuration partially in `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts` `trainerbattle` command handling
- [x] `GetTrainerFlag` / `SetBattledTrainersFlags` — trainer flags via `src/scripting/trainerFlags.ts`
- [x] `gRematchTable` data — rematch trainer IDs referenced in generated `src/data/trainerIds.gen.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `GetTrainerBattleTransition` — transition type selection by trainer class (Elite Four, Magma, Aqua, Champion) not implemented; TS just uses a default transition
- [-] `GetWildBattleTransition` — level-based transition selection not implemented; map-type-based routing partially exists in environment resolver
- [-] `GetSpecialBattleTransition` — frontier/pyramid/dome transition tables not implemented; Battle Frontier not in scope but structure missing
- [-] `BattleSetup_ConfigureTrainerBattle` — multi-mode dispatch (TRAINER_BATTLE_DOUBLE, TRAINER_BATTLE_REMATCH, TRAINER_BATTLE_PYRAMID, TRAINER_BATTLE_HILL, etc.) not fully ported; TS handles basic single/double cases only
- [-] `ShowTrainerIntroSpeech` — pyramid/trainer hill variants unimplemented; normal trainer intro speech partially covered via script runner dialog
- [-] `CB2_EndTrainerBattle` / `CB2_EndWildBattle` — post-battle routing (white-out vs return to field) partially in `src/states/BattleState.ts` but white-out not fully implemented

#### Missing
- [ ] `StartWallyTutorialBattle` — creates Ralts for Wally catch tutorial, sets `BATTLE_TYPE_WALLY_TUTORIAL`
- [ ] `BattleSetup_StartLegendaryBattle` — species-specific transition/music dispatch (Groudon, Kyogre, Rayquaza, Deoxys, Mew, Lugia, Ho-Oh)
- [ ] `StartGroudonKyogreBattle` — Kyogre/Groudon version-dependent encounter setup
- [ ] `StartRegiBattle` — Regi species-specific transition selection
- [ ] `BattleSetup_StartRoamerBattle` — roamer flag on `gBattleTypeFlags`
- [ ] `DoBattlePikeWildBattle` / `BattleSetup_StartBattlePikeWildBattle` — Battle Pike wild battle type
- [ ] `DoSafariBattle` — Safari Zone battle type
- [ ] `BattleSetup_StartScriptedWildBattle` / `BattleSetup_StartLatiBattle` — scripted wild battle entry points
- [ ] `BattleSetup_StartRematchBattle` — rematch battle entry point
- [ ] `ChooseStarter` / `CB2_GiveStarter` / `CB2_StartFirstBattle` / `CB2_EndFirstBattle` — starter selection → first battle → birch scripted flow
- [ ] `TryUpdateGymLeaderRematchFromWild` / `TryUpdateGymLeaderRematchFromTrainer` — rematch unlock on battle count milestones
- [ ] `IncrementRematchStepCounter` / `TryUpdateRandomTrainerRematches` — step-counter-gated rematch probability system
- [ ] `UpdateRandomTrainerRematches` / `SetRematchIdForTrainer` — 31% random rematch scheduling logic
- [ ] `UpdateRematchIfDefeated` — forced rematch state update after defeat
- [ ] `DoesSomeoneWantRematchIn` / `IsRematchTrainerIn` — map-local rematch query (used by Match Call)
- [ ] `ShouldTryRematchBattle` / `IsTrainerReadyForRematch` / `GetRematchTrainerId` — rematch readiness checks
- [ ] `CountBattledRematchTeams` — counts how many rematch stages of a trainer have been beaten
- [ ] `GetLastBeatenRematchTrainerId` — returns highest-stage defeated rematch trainer
- [ ] `ShouldTryGetTrainerScript` — two-trainer approach post-battle script routing flag
- [ ] `BattleSetup_GetScriptAddrAfterBattle` — returns end-of-battle script pointer
- [ ] `BattleSetup_GetTrainerPostBattleScript` — A/B trainer post-battle custom script routing
- [ ] `GetTrainerALoseText` / `GetTrainerBLoseText` / `GetTrainerWonSpeech` / `ShowTrainerCantBattleSpeech` — trainer speech accessors
- [ ] `PlayTrainerEncounterMusic` — trainer class → encounter music mapping (14 music IDs)
- [ ] `SetMapVarsToTrainer` — sets `gSpecialVar_LastTalked` and `gSelectedObjectEvent` from trainer local ID
- [ ] `ConfigureAndSetUpOneTrainerBattle` / `ConfigureTwoTrainersBattle` / `SetUpTwoTrainersBattle` — approach-triggered battle configuration entry points
- [ ] `GetTrainerFlagFromScriptPointer` — reads trainer flag from raw script byte pointer
- [ ] `SetTrainerFacingDirection` — locks trainer movement type to face-direction after interaction
- [ ] `ResetTrainerOpponentIds` — clears `gTrainerBattleOpponent_A/B`
- [ ] `GetTrainerBattleMode` — exposes `sTrainerBattleMode`
- [ ] `RegisterTrainerInMatchCall` — sets `TRAINER_REGISTERED_FLAGS_START + i` flag for Match Call unlock
- [ ] `HasAtLeastFiveBadges` — badge count prerequisite check for rematch step counter
- [ ] `gRematchTable` runtime — full rematch table with 75 entries and map associations (data exists in gen files but runtime lookup logic is absent)
- [ ] `CB2_EndRematchBattle` — rematch-specific post-battle routing with `HandleRematchVarsOnBattleEnd`
- [ ] `GetSumOfPlayerPartyLevel` / `GetSumOfEnemyPartyLevel` — level sum helpers for transition selection
- [ ] `GetBattleTransitionTypeByMap` — flash/water/cave/normal transition type from metatile behavior
- [ ] `TrainerBattleLoadArgs` + parameter specs — binary script argument parsing machinery (8/16/32-bit load/clear, script ret addr)

#### Notes
- Battle transition animations are a GBA-hardware visual effect system; no browser equivalent is needed for gameplay correctness, but the *selection logic* (which transition to use) matters for authenticity and is largely absent.
- The rematch system (Match Call, step counter, 31% probability, `trainerRematches[]` save array) is entirely unimplemented — this is a post-game feature and an intentional deferral.
- `BATTLE_TYPE_SAFARI`, `BATTLE_TYPE_PIKE`, `BATTLE_TYPE_PYRAMID`, `BATTLE_TYPE_TRAINER_HILL`, `BATTLE_TYPE_WALLY_TUTORIAL` battle type flags exist in generated constants but runtime setup for these modes is missing.
- `TrainerBattleLoadArgs` / parameter spec tables are a binary script parsing mechanism; the TS port handles `trainerbattle` as a script command in `ScriptRunner.ts` with named arguments rather than raw byte streams, so this machinery is intentionally replaced architecturally.
- White-out (`CB2_WhiteOut`) is referenced but not yet implemented in the TS port.
- Link cable, e-Reader (`B_TRANSITION_GROUP_E_READER`), and Secret Base trainer battles are intentionally out of scope.

#### Notes
- Battle Tents are post-intro optional content (Verdanturf, Fallarbor, Slateport). Slateport's tent is a Battle Factory lite (rental mons, swap screen); the other two are Palace/Arena variants. None of this system exists in the TS port — no frontier save-block fields, no `DoBattleFactorySelectScreen`, no `gFacilityTrainers`/`gFacilityTrainerMons` data, and no `frontier.rentalMons` state. The generated map scripts for the three tent maps exist (`VerdanturfTown_BattleTentBattleRoom.gen.ts`, etc.) but their `special` calls resolve to unimplemented stubs. This is intentionally low-priority optional content.

- [ ] `InitTowerChallenge` — resets win streak, challenge state, dynamic warp for frontier run start
- [ ] `GetTowerData` / `SetTowerData` — read/write frontier save block win streak and level mode vars
- [ ] `SetTowerBattleWon` — increments win counter, saves streak after each frontier battle won
- [ ] `ChooseSpecialBattleTowerTrainer` — selects record-mix friend or apprentice trainer from save data
- [ ] `SetNextFacilityOpponent` — selects next opponent trainer ID for each battle in a challenge run
- [ ] `GetRandomScaledFrontierTrainerId` — picks trainer ID scaled to challenge number from difficulty ranges
- [ ] `AwardBattleTowerRibbons` — awards Winning/Victory ribbons after 56+ win streak
- [ ] `SaveTowerChallenge` / `SaveBattleTowerRecord` / `SaveCurrentWinStreak` — persist frontier progress to save block

- [ ] `TowerTryCloseLink` — closes link connection (link cable; intentional omission)
- [ ] `SetMultiPartnerGfx` — sets partner NPC sprite via `VAR_OBJ_GFX_ID_E`
- [ ] `SetTowerInterviewData` — saves post-battle interview data (opponent name, species, outcome) to save block

- [ ] `CalcApprenticeChecksum` / `ValidateApprenticesChecksums` — checksum validation for apprentice save data
- [ ] `GetBattleTowerTrainerLanguage` — returns language byte for a trainer (for international string conversion)
- [ ] `GetFrontierTrainerFixedIvs` — returns fixed IV value scaled to trainer ID range
- [ ] `ValidateEReaderTrainer` / `SetEReaderTrainerChecksum` / `ClearEReaderTrainer` — e-Reader trainer validation (e-Reader; intentional omission)
- [ ] `GetEreaderTrainerFrontSpriteId` / `GetEreaderTrainerClassId` / `GetEreaderTrainerName` / `CopyEReaderTrainerGreeting` — e-Reader trainer accessors (intentional omission)
- [ ] `TryHideBattleTowerReporter` — hides the Battle Tower reporter NPC based on challenge status flag
- [ ] `TrySetLinkBattleTowerEnemyPartyLevel` — levels up enemy party for link battles (link cable; intentional omission)
- [ ] `GetBattleFacilityTrainerGfxId` — returns GFX object ID for a facility trainer (non-void variant)
- [ ] `SetEReaderTrainerGfxId` — convenience wrapper for e-Reader trainer sprite (intentional omission)
- [ ] `GetMonCountForBattleMode` — returns party size constant for a given frontier battle mode
- [ ] `GetApprenticeMultiPartnerParty` / `GetRecordMixFriendMultiPartnerParty` — selects non-conflicting mons from apprentice/record-mix partner for multi battle

#### Notes
- The entire Battle Frontier runtime (challenge state machine, win streak tracking, party generation, record mixing, apprentice system) is **not ported**. This is a post-game feature and is an intentional deferral.
- Link cable functions (`LoadLinkMultiOpponentsData`, `TowerTryCloseLink`, `TrySetLinkBattleTowerEnemyPartyLevel`) are hardware-specific and have no browser equivalent.
- e-Reader trainer functions are out of scope (e-Reader accessory, Japan-only).
- Trainer and frontier mon **data tables** (`gBattleFrontierTrainers`, `gBattleFrontierMons`, `gBattleFrontierHeldItems`) are partially covered by `src/data/trainerParties.gen.ts` and `src/data/items.ts`, but the runtime party-building logic that uses them is absent.
- `sStevenMons[]` (Steven's multi-battle partner team) has no TS equivalent; would be needed for the Mossdeep Space Center battle.
- `sRubyFacilityClassToEmerald` RS→Emerald class migration table is irrelevant to the browser port.
- `src/scripting/runtime/frontierState.ts` exists as a stub for frontier-related script special vars but does not implement any of the above battle logic.

- [ ] `WeatherTrio_BgFadeBlack` / `WeatherTrio_WaitFade` / `WeatherDuo_FadeOut` / `WeatherDuo_End` — shared BG/sprite fade helpers for weather trio
- [ ] `Task_Rayquaza` + `Rayquaza_Init` … `Rayquaza_TriRing` — multi-step Rayquaza palette + circular mask transition
- [ ] `Task_PokeballsTrail` / `PokeballsTrail_Init` / `PokeballsTrail_Main` / `PokeballsTrail_End` / `FldEff_PokeballTrail` / `SpriteCB_FldEffPokeballTrail` — five animated Poké Balls crossing the screen
- [ ] `Task_ClockwiseWipe` + five sub-states — clockwise WIN0 wipe around screen edge
- [ ] `Task_Ripple` / `Ripple_Init` / `Ripple_Main` — H-blank sine VOFS ripple fade
- [ ] `Task_Wave` / `Wave_Init` / `Wave_Main` / `Wave_End` — WIN0 sine-wave sweep
- [ ] `Task_Slice` / `Slice_Init` / `Slice_Main` / `Slice_End` — alternating-row horizontal slice
- [ ] `Task_ShredSplit` / `ShredSplit_Init` / `ShredSplit_Main` / `ShredSplit_BrokenCheck` / `ShredSplit_End` — accelerating row-pair split (note: `ShredSplit_BrokenCheck` is a known infinite-loop bug in the original)
- [ ] `Task_Blackhole` / `Blackhole_Init` / `Blackhole_Vibrate` / `Blackhole_GrowEnd` — shrinking then growing circular mask blackhole
- [ ] `Task_BlackholePulsate` / `BlackholePulsate_Main` — pulsating circular mask variant
- [ ] `Task_RectangularSpiral` / `RectangularSpiral_Init` / `RectangularSpiral_Main` / `RectangularSpiral_End` / `UpdateRectangularSpiralLine` — four spiraling tile lines drawing/erasing BG tiles
- [ ] `Task_Sidney` … `Task_Champion` + full mugshot pipeline (`Mugshot_Init` … `Mugshot_End`) — Elite Four trainer-pic banner slide transition
- [ ] `Mugshots_CreateTrainerPics` / `SpriteCB_MugshotTrainerPic` / `MugshotTrainerPic_Pause` … `MugshotTrainerPic_SlideOffscreen` — trainer sprite slide-in/decelerate/stop sprites
- [ ] `Task_WhiteBarsFade` + sub-states / `SpriteCB_WhiteBarFade` — 8 horizontal white bars expand then fade to black
- [ ] `Task_GridSquares` / `GridSquares_Init` / `GridSquares_Main` / `GridSquares_End` — shrinking box tile animation covering screen in grid
- [ ] `Task_AngledWipes` + sub-states / `AngledWipes_*` — 7 successive diagonal WIN0 wipes
- [ ] `Task_FrontierLogoWiggle` / `FrontierLogoWiggle_Init` / `FrontierLogoWiggle_SetGfx` — Battle Frontier logo pattern weave
- [ ] `Task_FrontierLogoWave` / `FrontierLogoWave_Init` … `FrontierLogoWave_Main` — Battle Frontier logo wave + blend-in transition
- [ ] `Task_FrontierSquares` / `FrontierSquares_Init` / `FrontierSquares_Draw` / `FrontierSquares_Shrink` / `FrontierSquares_End` — tiled squares appear then shrink away
- [ ] `Task_FrontierSquaresSpiral` / `FrontierSquaresSpiral_Init` / `FrontierSquaresSpiral_Outward` / `FrontierSquaresSpiral_SetBlack` / `FrontierSquaresSpiral_Inward` — squares appear/disappear in spiral order
- [ ] `Task_FrontierSquaresScroll` / `FrontierSquaresScroll_Init` … `FrontierSquaresScroll_End` / `Task_ScrollBg` — squares appear/disappear in pseudo-random order with scrolling BG
- [ ] `InitTransitionData` / `FadeScreenBlack` / `SetSinWave` / `SetCircularMask` / `InitBlackWipe` / `UpdateBlackWipe` / `GetBg0TilesDst` / `GetBg0TilemapDst` — general transition helpers
- [ ] Frontier circles transitions (`Task_FrontierCirclesMeet` etc., referenced but defined in `battle_transition_frontier.c`) — not in this file but referenced in the task table

#### Notes
This file is entirely GBA hardware-specific (DMA, H-blank/V-blank interrupts, scanline effect register buffers, OAM matrix manipulation, VRAM BG tile writes). The browser port has no equivalent battle transition system whatsoever. A browser replacement would use CSS/canvas animations instead of scanline tricks; the visual effects could be approximated but the implementation would be architecturally unrelated. All 40+ transition types are unimplemented. The mugshot transition also depends on trainer sprite loading (`CreateTrainerSprite`) which is partially handled by `BattleSpriteLoader.ts`, but the transition animation layer itself is absent.

- [ ] `CreateSlidingLogoCircleSprite` — creates a sprite that slides linearly toward a target position (one of 3 circle positions)
- [ ] `SpriteCB_LogoCircleSlide` — per-frame callback: advances sprite toward target using speed/delay counters
- [ ] `CreateSpiralingLogoCircleSprite` — creates a sprite that spirals inward via polar coordinates
- [ ] `SpriteCB_LogoCircleSpiral` — per-frame callback: updates x2/y2 from angle+radius, decrements radius until target reached
- [ ] `DestroyLogoCirclesGfx` — frees sprite tiles/palette and destroys the 3 circle sprites
- [ ] `IsLogoCirclesAnimFinished` — checks if all 3 circle sprites have reached `SpriteCallbackDummy`

- [ ] `FadeInCenterLogoCircle` — state: increments blend from 0→16 showing center logo, then triggers palette fade to black
- [ ] `WaitForLogoCirclesAnim` — state: waits until all 3 sprites finish animating before advancing
- [ ] `Task_FrontierCirclesMeet` / `CirclesMeet_CreateSprites` / `CirclesMeet_End` — variant: 3 circles slide from offscreen edges toward center simultaneously
- [ ] `Task_FrontierCirclesCross` / `CirclesCross_CreateSprites` / `CirclesCross_End` — variant: circles slide from opposite directions, crossing each other
- [ ] `Task_FrontierCirclesAsymmetricSpiral` / `CirclesAsymmetricSpiral_CreateSprites` / `CirclesAsymmetricSpiral_End` — variant: 3 circles spiral in from different starting angles simultaneously
- [ ] `Task_FrontierCirclesSymmetricSpiral` / `CirclesSymmetricSpiral_CreateSprites` / `CirclesSymmetricSpiral_End` — variant: 3 circles spiral in symmetrically from same center point
- [ ] `Task_FrontierCirclesMeetInSeq` / `CirclesMeetInSeq_CreateSprites` / `CirclesMeetInSeq_End` — variant: circles launched sequentially (16-frame delay between each)
- [ ] `Task_FrontierCirclesCrossInSeq` / `CirclesCrossInSeq_CreateSprites` / `CirclesCrossInSeq_End` — variant: cross pattern launched sequentially
- [ ] `Task_FrontierCirclesAsymmetricSpiralInSeq` / `CirclesAsymmetricSpiralInSeq_CreateSprites` / `CirclesAsymmetricSpiralInSeq_End` — variant: asymmetric spiral launched sequentially
- [ ] `Task_FrontierCirclesSymmetricSpiralInSeq` / `CirclesSymmetricSpiralInSeq_CreateSprites` / `CirclesSymmetricSpiralInSeq_End` — variant: symmetric spiral launched sequentially

#### Notes
This file handles Battle Frontier battle transition category 3 (logo circles). The port has `src/field/FadeController.ts` for fade logic but no battle transition system at all — the browser port renders battles without GBA-style transition animations. This is intentionally omitted; battle transitions are cosmetic and GBA-hardware-specific (BG registers, OAM sprites, palette blending via `BLDALPHA`). The `Sin2`/`Cos2` calls use a 512-entry fixed-point trig table that would need a JS equivalent if implemented. All 8 task variants share the same 4-state pipeline differing only in sprite creation parameters — if ever implemented, the TS port could unify these into a single parameterized function.

### `src/battle_tv.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `BattleTv_SetDataBasedOnString` — tracks battle events (effectiveness, status, hazards, faint causes) by string ID to accumulate TV show scoring data
- [ ] `BattleTv_SetDataBasedOnMove` — records per-move attacker/defender data and weather-boosted move points for TV scoring
- [ ] `BattleTv_SetDataBasedOnAnimation` — awards set-up move points when Future Sight / Doom Desire animations fire
- [ ] `TryPutLinkBattleTvShowOnAir` — selects the best-performing player Pokémon/move and calls `PutBattleUpdateOnTheAir` to queue a link-battle TV segment
- [ ] `AddMovePoints` — central accumulator: routes point values from `sPointsArray` into `gBattleStruct->tvMovePoints` based on case ID
- [ ] `AddPointsOnFainting` — awards faint-bonus points to whichever mon/move caused the KO (curse, leech seed, poison, burn, explosion, recoil, etc.)
- [ ] `TrySetBattleSeminarShow` — compares damage of all attacker moves; if a better move exists, queues a Battle Seminar TV show
- [ ] `ShouldCalculateDamage` — filters variable-damage / zero-power moves before the seminar damage calculation loop
- [ ] `BattleTv_ClearExplosionFaintCause` — resets explosion-related faint-cause fields on both sides (link battles only)
- [ ] `GetBattlerMoveSlotId` — returns the move-slot index (0–3) for a given move on a battler's party mon
- [ ] `AddPointsBasedOnWeather` — dispatches weather-specific point lookups (rain/sun/sandstorm/hail) for the used move
- [ ] All `sPoints_*` data tables — point values for move effects, type effectiveness, weather synergy, stat changes, status damage, hazards, screens, etc.
- [ ] `sVariableDmgMoves` table — list of moves whose damage cannot be pre-calculated for the Battle Seminar

#### Notes
- This entire file is Link Cable Battle TV / Battle Seminar infrastructure — a post-game multiplayer feature that records highlight-reel data for in-game TV shows. The port has no link cable, no TV system (`tv.c`), and no `PutBattleUpdateOnTheAir` / `TryPutBattleSeminarOnAir` equivalents, so there is no reason to port this file for the current scope.
- `GetBattlerMoveSlotId` is a utility used elsewhere in battle (e.g. `battle_message.c`); a TS equivalent may be needed if move-slot lookups are required in the script runtime, but it is not currently present.
- The point-scoring tables (`sPoints_MoveEffect`, `sPoints_Effectiveness`, etc.) are self-contained data that could theoretically be extracted if a TV/seminar feature were ever added, but they have no current use in the port.

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `AbilityBattleEffects` — switch-in weather abilities (Drizzle/Drought/Sand Stream), Intimidate, Forecast/Castform, Trace, end-turn abilities (Rain Dish, Shed Skin, Speed Boost, Truant), contact abilities (Rough Skin, Static, Flame Body, Poison Point, Effect Spore, Cute Charm), absorbing abilities (Volt/Water Absorb, Flash Fire), Immunity/Synchronize/Color Change all have partial coverage in BattleEngine and StatusEffects; ABILITYEFFECT_CHECK_* query cases (12–19) used as field queries are missing or stub-only
- [-] `DoFieldEndTurnEffects` — Wish heal, Future Sight/Doom Desire (`HandleWishPerishSongOnTurnEnd`) partially covered; Arena judgment (`BattleScript_ArenaDoJudgment`) missing (Battle Frontier feature)
- [-] `DoBattlerEndTurnEffects` — ENDTURN_ABILITIES delegates to `AbilityBattleEffects(ABILITYEFFECT_ENDTURN)` which is partial; ENDTURN_ITEMS1/2 delegates to `ItemBattleEffects` which is partial
- [-] `HandleFaintedMonActions` — exp gain (BattleScript_GiveExp), fainted mon switch-in logic, Intimidate/Trace/Forecast re-trigger on switch-in partially covered; exp distribution to party members not fully ported
- [-] `ItemBattleEffects` (ITEMEFFECT_MOVE_END, ITEMEFFECT_KINGSROCK_SHELLBELL) — end-of-move berry cures and King's Rock flinch/Shell Bell drain present in battleItemRules but not fully exercised through the script runtime path
- [-] `HandleAction_UseItem` — AI item use (Full Restore, X-stat, Guard Spec, cure condition) partially covered; player item use path exists but AI item type/flags logic is incomplete
- [-] `TrySetCantSelectMoveBattleScript` — move selection limitation scripts (disabled, torment, taunt, imprison, choice band, no-PP) exist in BattleEngine but Battle Palace palace-mode variants are not implemented

#### Missing
- [ ] `HandleAction_UseMove` — full move selection pipeline: encore override, encored-move-overwritten detection, noValidMoves→Struggle, lockedMoves, Follow Me redirect, Lightning Rod redirect in doubles, absent battler target fallback, Battle Palace failure branch, Arena mind points; the TS engine handles a simplified subset
- [ ] `HandleAction_Switch` — switch action dispatch with script execution path (BattleScript_ActionSwitch)
- [ ] `HandleAction_WatchesCarefully` / `HandleAction_SafariZoneBallThrow` / `HandleAction_ThrowPokeblock` / `HandleAction_GoNear` / `HandleAction_SafariZoneRun` / `HandleAction_WallyBallThrow` — Safari Zone and Wally tutorial battle actions; not implemented (intentional — Safari Zone not in scope)
- [ ] `HandleAction_TryFinish` / `HandleAction_NothingIsFainted` / `HandleAction_ActionFinished` — turn action queue management with hitmarker cleanup; TS engine has its own loop but doesn't replicate these state machine cases
- [ ] `HandleWishPerishSongOnTurnEnd` — Future Sight/Doom Desire damage delivery and Perish Song countdown/kill; partially present but not wired through the script execution path
- [ ] `HasNoMonsToSwitch` — double battle multi/partner/two-opponents party availability check; single-battle equivalent exists but doubles variants missing
- [ ] `GetBattlerForBattleScript` — BS_* battler selector used by battle script commands; not explicitly ported (inline in script runtime)
- [ ] `PrepareStringBattle` / `ResetSentPokesToOpponentValue` / `OpponentSwitchInResetSentPokesToOpponentValue` / `UpdateSentPokesToOpponentValue` — battle results tracking and sent-mons bitmask; not ported
- [ ] `MarkBattlerForControllerExec` / `MarkBattlerReceivedLinkData` — controller execution marking; no TS equivalent (architecture differs — no GBA controllers)
- [ ] `BattleScriptExecute` / `BattleScriptPushCursorAndCallback` — callback stack push for main battle function; TS runtime handles this differently without a function pointer stack
- [ ] `PressurePPLose*` controller emit path — the `BtlController_EmitSetMonData` calls for PP sync have no equivalent (no split controller architecture)
- [ ] `sSoundMovesTable` + Soundproof check in `AbilityBattleEffects(ABILITYEFFECT_MOVES_BLOCK)` — sound move list and Soundproof blocking; not ported
- [ ] Safari/link battle outcome handling in `HandleAction_Run` — `BATTLE_TYPE_LINK` outcome flags, `B_OUTCOME_LINK_BATTLE_RAN` not applicable

#### Notes
- The TS battle engine is a custom single-battle implementation designed for parity testing, not a 1:1 port of the GBA state machine. It replicates outcomes (damage, status, stat changes) but uses a flat turn resolution loop rather than the GBA's action-by-action callback/script stack model.
- All GBA controller communication (`BtlController_EmitSetMonData`, `MarkBattlerForControllerExec`) is intentionally omitted — the TS engine mutates state directly.
- Link battle, Battle Palace, Battle Arena, Battle Frontier, and Safari Zone branches are intentionally out of scope.
- `BATTLE_TYPE_DOUBLE` logic throughout is largely missing — the TS engine targets single battles only.
- The `ItemBattleEffects` berry logic is covered via `src/battle/items/battleItemRules.ts` and tested in `ScriptRunner.berry.test.ts`, but the full `caseID` dispatch (ITEMEFFECT_ON_SWITCH_IN, ITEMEFFECT_MOVE_END, ITEMEFFECT_KINGSROCK_SHELLBELL) is not fully wired into the battle script runtime flow.

- [-] `GetMoveTarget` — target selection logic exists in `BattleEngine.ts`; Follow Me redirect, Lightning Rod redirect, and full double-battle random targeting not fully implemented
- [-] `IsMonDisobedient` — obedience level thresholds (badge flags) and disobedience outcomes partially scaffolded; badge flag checks (`FLAG_BADGE02_GET`–`FLAG_BADGE08_GET`), illegal Mew/Deoxys (`IsBattlerModernFatefulEncounter`), and all four disobedience branches (sleep, random move, self-hit, loafing) not fully ported

- Link/recorded battle guards (`BATTLE_TYPE_LINK`, `BATTLE_TYPE_RECORDED_LINK`) are intentionally omitted — no multiplayer support planned.
- `HandleAction_RunBattleScript` maps to the script-VM architecture in `src/battle/engine/scriptRuntime/`; the controller-exec-flag mechanism is not needed in the single-threaded browser model.

- [ ] `InitLocalPlayers` — populates `gLinkPlayers` with NPC names for local games
- [ ] `PrintBlendingRanking` — draws score ranking screen (BEST/GOOD/MISS counts per player)
- [ ] `PrintBlendingResults` — draws results screen: berry used, time, max RPM, pokeblock made
- [ ] `PrintMadePokeblockString` — builds "X POKEBLOCK was made! Level Y, feel Z" string
- [ ] `SortScores` / `SortBasedOnPoints` — ranks players by weighted score
- [ ] `TryUpdateBerryBlenderRecord` — saves new max RPM record to SaveBlock1
- [ ] `ShowBerryBlenderRecordWindow` — displays all-time blender RPM records
- [ ] `TryAddContestLinkTvShow` — queues link TV show if player wins/loses with style
- [ ] `Task_PlayPokeblockFanfare` — plays level-up fanfare then restores map music
- [ ] `SpriteCB_PlayerArrow` — offsets arrow sprite by screen shake amount
- [ ] `SpriteCB_CountdownNumber` — animates 3-2-1 countdown sprites bouncing down/up
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Start` — animates "START" banner sprite
- [ ] `SpriteCB_ScoreSymbol` / `SpriteCB_ScoreSymbolBest` — floats score icons upward
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Berry` — bouncing berry throw animation at start
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Particle` — sparkle particle sprites on hit
- [ ] `UpdateBlenderLandScreenShake` — screen shake when blender drops in at start
- [ ] `ShakeBgCoordForHit` / `RestoreBgCoords` — screen shake on high-speed BEST hit
- [ ] `LoadBerryBlenderGfx` — multi-step GBA tile/palette/sprite sheet loader
- [ ] `ProcessLinkPlayerCmds` — handles link/wireless play-again negotiation commands
- [ ] `ResetLinkCmds` — zeroes send/receive command buffers
- [ ] `PrintMessage` — draws dialog frame and prints text with wait
- [ ] `DrawBlenderCenter` — writes affine BG transform parameters for rotating center
- [ ] All sprite/OAM/animation data tables — GBA-specific graphics data

#### Notes
The Berry Blender is a standalone multiplayer minigame (berry → pokeblock crafting). It has no TypeScript equivalent at all. The entire feature is intentionally omitted from the browser port — it requires GBA link cable / RFU wireless multiplayer (`SIO`, `gSendCmd`, `gRecvCmds`), GBA affine BG hardware for the rotating center graphic, and GBA-specific sprite/tile/DMA systems. The gameplay-relevant output — the `Pokeblock` struct with `color`, `spicy/dry/sweet/bitter/sour`, and `feel` values — is the only piece that might eventually need a stub for save compatibility. The pokeblock color formula (`CalculatePokeblockColor`) and flavor calculation (`CalculatePokeblock`) are the only portions worth porting if pokeblock contest mechanics are ever implemented.

#### Notes
Berry Crush is a GBA wireless multiplayer (RFU) minigame — entirely absent from the TS port, which has no wireless link or multiplayer infrastructure. The minigame is a standalone screen that replaces the overworld; it is not called from any field script in the main story path. Intentional omission: multiplayer minigames (Berry Crush, Pokémon Colosseum link, etc.) are out of scope for a single-player browser port. The only field-facing hook is `ShowBerryCrushRankings` (called from a script via `special`) and `StartBerryCrush` (called from link lobby code) — neither has a TS equivalent. Berry powder storage (`gSaveBlock2Ptr->berryCrush`) is also not modelled in the TS save system.

- [ ] `LoadBerryFixGraphics` — loads GBA hardware graphics for the Berry Fix link cable program (GBA-to-GBA berry distribution system)

#### Notes
Intentional omission. This file is exclusively for the GBA Berry Fix program — a link cable utility that patched a bug in Ruby/Sapphire berry growth. It has no gameplay relevance in a browser port: it requires GBA SIO link hardware, GBA VRAM/palette registers (`REG_DISPCNT`, `REG_BG0CNT`, `BG_PLTT`), and LZ77 VRAM decompression. The function is also marked `UNUSED` in the original. No browser equivalent is needed or warranted.

It has no gameplay relevance to the browser port — it depends on GBA hardware SIO/multiboot (`MultiBootInit`, `MultiBootMain`, `MultiBootStartMaster`), DMA, hardware registers, and a binary blob (`gMultiBootProgram_BerryGlitchFix_Start`) that only exists on physical cartridge. There is nothing to port.

Berry Crush is a GBA link cable multiplayer minigame (`berry_crush.c`). Berry powder is earned exclusively through Berry Crush, which is intentionally omitted in the browser port (no link cable / wireless adapter equivalent). The data model (a `u32` field in `SaveBlock2`) is preserved for native save compatibility in `Gen3SaveParser`, but the entire vendor UI and Berry Crush flow are out of scope. The `MAX_BERRY_POWDER` cap (99999) and the `gSpecialVar_0x8004`-based script specials (`HasEnoughBerryPowder`, `TakeBerryPowder`) would only matter if Berry Crush were ever emulated; they can be safely omitted.

- Flavor circle sprites (5 dots for the 5 berry flavors) would need a visual component in the TS port.
- The size conversion (`1000 * berry->size / 254` for cm→inches) is pure logic that could trivially be ported if the screen is ever built.

## batch: src/bg.c

### `src/bg.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/rendering/webgl/WebGLTileRenderer.ts`, `src/rendering/TileRenderer.ts`, `src/rendering/gbaTilemap.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] BG layer visibility/priority management — handled by WebGL pass renderer and layer compositor (`src/rendering/webgl/WebGLPassRenderer.ts`, `src/rendering/LayerCompositor.ts`)
- [x] BG scroll offset (bg_x/bg_y) — camera scroll implemented in `src/game/CameraController.ts` and passed to WebGL uniforms
- [x] Tilemap buffer management — abstracted in `src/rendering/MapBackingStore.ts` and `src/rendering/webgl/WebGLBufferManager.ts`
- [x] Tile loading/uploading to GPU — `src/rendering/webgl/TilesetUploader.ts`, `src/rendering/TileRenderer.ts`

#### Missing
- [ ] `ResetBgs` / `ResetBgControlStructs` — GBA hardware register reset; no equivalent needed in browser
- [ ] `SetBgMode` / `GetBgMode` — GBA BG mode 0/1/2 switching (text vs affine); browser uses WebGL pipelines, not GBA modes
- [ ] `LoadBgVram` / `LoadBgTiles` / `LoadBgTilemap` — DMA-based VRAM uploads; browser uses WebGL texture uploads instead
- [ ] `ShowBg` / `HideBg` — writes to DISPCNT hardware register; browser handles visibility through render pass toggling
- [ ] `SetBgAffine` / `SetBgAffineInternal` — GBA affine transformation matrix (BgAffineSet); no affine BG mode in browser port
- [ ] `ChangeBgX` / `ChangeBgY` / `ChangeBgY_ScreenOff` — GBA hardware scroll register writes (BGnHOFS/BGnVOFS)
- [ ] `GetBgX` / `GetBgY` — returns stored GBA scroll positions
- [ ] `IsDma3ManagerBusyWithBgCopy` — DMA busy-flag polling; irrelevant in browser
- [ ] `CopyToBgTilemapBuffer` / `CopyBgTilemapBufferToVram` — CPU-side tilemap buffer manipulation + DMA upload
- [ ] `CopyToBgTilemapBufferRect` / `CopyToBgTilemapBufferRect_ChangePalette` / `CopyRectToBgTilemapBufferRect` — rectangular tilemap region copy with palette remapping
- [ ] `FillBgTilemapBufferRect_Palette0` / `FillBgTilemapBufferRect` / `WriteSequenceToBgTilemapBuffer` — fill tilemap region with tile sequence
- [ ] `CopyTileMapEntry` — per-tile palette/offset merge logic; partially analogous to tile instance building in `src/rendering/webgl/TileInstanceBuilder.ts`
- [ ] `GetTileMapIndexFromCoords` — GBA screen-size-aware tilemap index calculation (handles 32x32, 64x32, 32x64, 64x64 screen blocks)
- [ ] `GetBgMetricTextMode` / `GetBgMetricAffineMode` — returns screen block count/width based on GBA screen size field
- [ ] `GetBgType` — determines normal vs affine BG based on bg index + mode
- [ ] `IsInvalidBg` / `IsInvalidBg32` / `IsTileMapOutsideWram` — GBA-specific validation guards
- [ ] `Unused_AdjustBgMosaic` — GBA mosaic effect register (REG_MOSAIC); not implemented in browser
- [ ] `SetBgTilemapBuffer` / `UnsetBgTilemapBuffer` / `GetBgTilemapBuffer` — pointer to WRAM tilemap buffer; browser uses typed arrays instead
- [ ] `ResetBgsAndClearDma3BusyFlags` / `InitBgsFromTemplates` / `InitBgFromTemplate` — GBA BG initialization from template structs
- [ ] `SetBgAttribute` / `GetBgAttribute` — generic attribute getter/setter for GBA BG control registers
- [ ] `BgTileAllocOp` — dummied-out tile allocator from FRLG; no equivalent needed

#### Notes
`src/bg.c` is a GBA hardware abstraction layer for the 4-layer BG rendering system (BGCNT, DISPCNT, scroll registers, DMA). The browser port replaces this entire subsystem with WebGL: tile layers are rendered via 3-pass WebGL pipeline (`src/rendering/webgl/WebGLRenderPipeline.ts`), camera scrolling via uniform uploads, and tilemap data via GPU textures. No 1:1 port is needed or desirable. The `GetTileMapIndexFromCoords` screen-block wrapping logic is the only piece with potential gameplay relevance (used when writing to BG tilemaps from C scripts), but the browser's tilemap is a flat 2D array without GBA screen-block subdivision.

- [-] `MovePlayerOnAcroBike` / `CheckMovementInputAcroBike` / `AcroBikeHandleInput*` (7 handlers) — acro bike state machine partially ported; wheelie, bunny-hop, side-jump, turn-jump states exist in PlayerController but full history-trick detection (see below) may be incomplete
- [-] `AcroBikeTransition_WheelieHoppingMoving` — ledge-hopping wheelie and collision branches; basic hop movement ported but `COLLISION_WHEELIE_HOP` special case uncertain

The test suite does not appear to cover the full acro trick system (history-based side-jump / turn-jump detection from `sAcroBikeTricksList`).
- `IsPlayerCollidingWithFarawayIslandMew` / `PlayerOnBikeCollideWithFarawayIslandMew` referenced throughout — Faraway Island Mew collision is handled in `src/game/legendary/farawayMew.ts`.
- Cycling Road challenge (`gBikeCyclingChallenge`, `gBikeCollisions`) is a post-game feature; intentional omission is reasonable for MVP.
- Link cable / record mixing references are absent from this file; no omissions needed there.

This file's logic is only reachable via a `special` command in map scripts (e.g. `LittlerootTown_ProfessorBirchsLab.gen.ts`); since `special` dispatch for Pokédex-related specials is not wired up in `ScriptRunner.ts`, the whole subsystem is effectively blocked. The Jirachi/Deoxys mythical-exclusion edge case in `GetPokedexRatingText` is non-trivial and will need explicit handling when this is ported.

## batch: src/blit.c

### `src/blit.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `BlitBitmapRect4BitWithoutColorKey` — copies a 4bpp GBA-tiled bitmap region with no color key (pass-through to BlitBitmapRect4Bit)
- [ ] `BlitBitmapRect4Bit` — copies a 4bpp GBA-tiled bitmap region with optional transparent color key
- [ ] `FillBitmapRect4Bit` — flood-fills a rectangular region of a 4bpp GBA-tiled bitmap with a solid nibble value
- [ ] `BlitBitmapRect4BitTo8Bit` — blits a 4bpp GBA-tiled region into an 8bpp GBA-tiled surface with palette offset and color key
- [ ] `FillBitmapRect8Bit` — flood-fills a rectangular region of an 8bpp GBA-tiled bitmap with a solid byte value

#### Notes
`blit.c` implements GBA-specific tile-order bitmap addressing (8×8-pixel tile row-major layout encoded as `(x>>3)<<5 + (x&7)>>1` etc.). The browser port uses standard row-major pixel buffers backed by `ImageData` / WebGL textures — there is no concept of GBA tile-interleaved pixel memory. All sprite/tile blitting is handled at a higher level by the WebGL pipeline (`src/rendering/webgl/`, `src/rendering/SpriteBatcher.ts`, `src/rendering/TileRenderer.ts`) which uploads pre-decoded PNG assets directly to the GPU. These low-level GBA bitmap manipulation functions have no browser equivalent and are intentionally omitted; their only consumers would be the font/window rendering path, which the TS port handles via the `PromptCanvasRenderer` / Canvas 2D API instead.

### `src/braille.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/core/prompt/PromptPrinterEngine.ts`, `src/core/prompt/PromptCanvasRenderer.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `FontFunc_Braille` (state machine core) — text printing state machine logic (RENDER_STATE_HANDLE_CHAR, RENDER_STATE_WAIT, RENDER_STATE_CLEAR, RENDER_STATE_SCROLL_START, RENDER_STATE_SCROLL, RENDER_STATE_PAUSE) is structurally equivalent to `PromptPrinterEngine`; text speed, A/B speed-up, scroll, pause, and EOS handling are all ported

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `FontFunc_Braille` (control codes) — `EXT_CTRL_CODE_COLOR`, `EXT_CTRL_CODE_HIGHLIGHT`, `EXT_CTRL_CODE_SHADOW`, `EXT_CTRL_CODE_PAUSE`, `EXT_CTRL_CODE_PAUSE_UNTIL_PRESS`, `EXT_CTRL_CODE_SHIFT_RIGHT`, `EXT_CTRL_CODE_SHIFT_DOWN` handled; `EXT_CTRL_CODE_WAIT_SE`, `EXT_CTRL_CODE_PLAY_BGM`, `EXT_CTRL_CODE_PLAY_SE` not fully implemented (audio system missing)

- [ ] `DecompressGlyph_Braille` — GBA tile decompression for braille font bitmap; not needed (browser uses canvas text/image rendering, no tile format)
- [ ] `GetGlyphWidth_Braille` — always returns 16; braille glyph width query not ported as a separate function
- [ ] `RENDER_STATE_WAIT_SE` branch — waits for SE (sound effect) to finish before continuing; no SE playback system exists

#### Notes
The braille font is a GBA-specific hardware concern (4bpp tile decompression into VRAM). In the TS port, the `PromptPrinterEngine` handles the equivalent state machine logic for all fonts uniformly via canvas rendering — there is no separate braille font renderer. The `sFont_Braille` bitmap data and `DecompressGlyph_Braille` tile unpacking are intentionally omitted as browser rendering replaces GBA tile-based font rendering entirely. The `sScrollDistances` table is equivalent to `src/core/prompt/textSpeed.ts`. Audio-dependent states (`RENDER_STATE_WAIT_SE`) are stubs pending audio implementation.

## batch: src/braille_puzzles.c

### `src/braille_puzzles.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/scripting/specials/legendaryIslandSpecials.ts`, `src/game/scriptEffects/mirageTowerCollapseRuntime.ts` (structural analog only)
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `CheckRelicanthWailord` — party order check (Wailord first, Relicanth last) present in `src/scripting/specials/legendaryIslandSpecials.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `ShouldDoBrailleRegicePuzzle` — step-tracking logic via `VAR_REGICE_STEPS_1/2/3` and `FLAG_TEMP_REGICE_PUZZLE_STARTED/FAILED` is partially modeled in `legendaryIslandSpecials.ts`, but the 36-tile perimeter walk validation loop against `sRegicePathCoords` is not fully implemented
- [-] `DoBrailleDigEffect` / `DoBrailleRegirockEffect` / `DoBrailleRegisteelEffect` — metatile mutation (opening cave entrances) is architecturally supported via `mapMetatileUtils.ts` and the `MapGridSetMetatileIdAt` analog, but these specific coordinate sets and metatile IDs are not wired up as special handlers

#### Missing
- [ ] `ShouldDoBrailleDigEffect` — position check at Sealed Chamber outer room (x=9/10/11, y=3) before Dig puzzle
- [ ] `ShouldDoBrailleRegirockEffect` — position check at Desert Ruins (x=5/6/7, y=23)
- [ ] `ShouldDoBrailleRegisteelEffect` — position check at Ancient Tomb (x=8, y=25) with `sIsRegisteelPuzzle` flag
- [ ] `SetUpPuzzleEffectRegirock` / `SetUpPuzzleEffectRegisteel` — `FLDEFF_USE_TOMB_PUZZLE_EFFECT` field effect launch via `GetCursorSelectionMonId`
- [ ] `UseRegirockHm_Callback` / `UseRegisteelHm_Callback` — field effect completion callbacks that invoke metatile mutation
- [ ] `FldEff_UsePuzzleEffect` — unified field move task that branches on `sIsRegisteelPuzzle`
- [ ] `DoSealedChamberShakingEffect_Long` / `DoSealedChamberShakingEffect_Short` — camera pan shake tasks (50 shakes vs 2 shakes)
- [ ] `Task_SealedChamberShakingEffect` — per-frame camera oscillation task with delay counter

#### Notes
- The Regice perimeter walk (`sRegicePathCoords`, 36 tiles) requires step-by-step tracking across three 16-bit vars (`VAR_REGICE_STEPS_1/2/3`) — this bit-accumulation pattern has no TS equivalent yet.
- `MAP_OFFSET` (7) is baked into all `MapGridSetMetatileIdAt` calls here; per project memory the TS port must subtract MAP_OFFSET — content coords for Sealed Chamber dig are (9,1), (10,1), (11,1), (9,2), (10,2), (11,2); Regirock/Registeel entrance coords are (7,19)–(9,20).
- Camera shaking (`SetCameraPanning`) has a partial analog via `FadeController`/camera pan infrastructure but the task-based shake loop is not implemented.
- `ShouldDoBrailleRegirockEffectOld` is an intentional nullsub (dead code); omission is correct.
- `DoBrailleWait` is marked `UNUSED`; omission is correct.
- All three Regi puzzles are post-game optional content; lower priority than main story path.

### `src/cable_car.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/LavaridgeWarpSequencer.ts`, `src/hooks/useLavaridgeWarpSequencer.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `CableCar` (entry point) — weather transition and warp into cable car scene is handled via `LavaridgeWarpSequencer`
- [x] Weather transition logic — going up starts sunny→ash, going down starts ash→sunny; mirrored in `LavaridgeWarpSequencer`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `Task_CableCar` / state machine — timer-based state machine (wait for weather, fade out, end) partially exists in `LavaridgeWarpSequencer` but the full 570-frame timing and weather sprite priority manipulation are not reproduced
- [-] `CB2_EndCableCar` — warp-back-to-map and cleanup handled generically by warp system, but cable car-specific cleanup (ash sprite nulling, VRAM clear) is not modeled
- [-] `SpriteCB_CableCar` / `SpriteCB_Player` — linear motion along diagonal trajectory (0.14×timer horizontal, 0.067×timer vertical) not implemented; browser port has no animated cable car sprite sequence

#### Missing
- [ ] `CB2_LoadCableCar` — full GBA BG initialization (4 BG layers, VRAM upload, tilemap buffers for ground/trees/mountains/pylon)
- [ ] `SetBgRegs` — GBA GPU register setup for cable car BG layers
- [ ] `CreateCableCarSprites` — cable car body, door, cable segment sprites (9 cable sprites), player sprite on car, random hiker NPC on ground
- [ ] `Task_AnimateBgGoingUp` / `Task_AnimateBgGoingDown` — per-frame BG3/BG1 scroll offset animation including pylon redraw at specific offsets
- [ ] `AnimateGroundGoingUp` / `AnimateGroundGoingDown` — ground tilemap scrolling with diagonal offset math
- [ ] `DrawNextGroundSegmentGoingUp` / `DrawNextGroundSegmentGoingDown` — incremental ground tile streaming into BG0 tilemap buffer
- [ ] `BufferNextGroundSegment` — pre-buffers next 9×12 ground tile block from tilemap data
- [ ] `InitGroundTilemapData` — initializes ground segment state for up/down direction
- [ ] `VBlankCB_CableCar` — VBlank DMA: copies tilemap buffers, sets 6 BG scroll registers
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Cable` — cable segment sprite callback (no-op in C, still absent)
- [ ] `SpriteCB_HikerGoingUp` / `SpriteCB_HikerGoingDown` — random background hiker NPC walking along mountain path
- [ ] `CB2_CableCar` — main loop (RunTasks, AnimateSprites, BuildOam, UpdatePaletteFade, MapMusicMain)

#### Notes
The TypeScript port handles the Mt. Chimney cable car as a scripted warp transition (`LavaridgeWarpSequencer` / `useLavaridgeWarpSequencer`) that triggers a fade and warps the player to the destination map, but omits the full animated cable car cinematics entirely. The GBA version renders a dedicated 4-layer BG scene with parallax scrolling mountains, animated ground tiles streaming past, a physically moving cable car sprite with the player riding it, 9 cable segment sprites, and a 570-frame timer for the full ride. None of the visual simulation (BG scroll math, ground segment streaming, sprite trajectory, pylon redraw triggers) exists in the TS port. The random hiker background NPC (1/64 chance, with Zigzagoon bug) is also absent. This is an intentional scope reduction — the warp correctly delivers the player to Mt. Chimney/Lavaridge without the cinematic.

- [ ] `PlayerEnteredTradeSeat` — dispatches wired/wireless trade entry

#### Notes
This entire file is intentionally out of scope for the browser port. It implements GBA link cable (SIO serial hardware) and wireless adapter (RFU) multiplayer infrastructure — 2–4 player battles, trades, record mixing, berry blending, contests, and the Union Room handshake. There is no physical link hardware in a browser context, and none of the underlying primitives (`OpenLink`, `GetLinkPlayerCount_2`, `SendBlock`, `gBlockRecvBuffer`, `gLinkPlayers`, etc.) exist in the TS port. Any multiplayer features would require a completely different networking architecture (WebSockets, WebRTC) and are not planned in the current scope.

**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

*(none)*

#### Missing

- [ ] `Task_DoClearSaveDataScreenYesNo` — draws the confirmation prompt and Yes/No menu
- [ ] `Task_ClearSaveDataScreenYesNoChoice` — handles Yes/No input; branches to clear or cancel
- [ ] `Task_ClearSaveData` — calls `ClearSaveData()` then resets
- [ ] `SetupClearSaveDataScreen` — multi-frame GBA hardware init (BG, palette, windows, fade-in)

- [ ] `InitClearSaveDataScreenWindows` — initialises text windows for the screen

#### Notes
This screen is the "erase all save data" confirmation accessible from the title screen (hold Up+B+Select). The port has no equivalent UI; `SaveManager` / `SaveStateStore` exist but expose no destructive wipe flow. GBA-specific hardware setup (GPU registers, DMA, VBlank callback, palette buffer manipulation) is intentionally omitted — only the logical flow (prompt → confirm → wipe → reset) would need porting. A browser equivalent would be a modal confirmation dialog calling `localStorage.clear()` or the save store's wipe method, followed by a page reload.

- [x] `FlagSet(FLAG_SYS_CLOCK_SET)` / `FlagGet` — flag store exists in `src/game/GameFlags.ts`
- [x] `VarSet(VAR_DAYS)` / `GetVarPointer(VAR_DAYS)` — variable store exists in `src/game/GameVariables.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `UpdatePerMinute` — berry tree update path exists, but `CalcTimeDifference` / `lastBerryTreeUpdate` save-block tracking is not wired to a real RTC; time delta is approximated or not persisted across sessions
- [-] `InitTimeBasedEvents` — `FLAG_SYS_CLOCK_SET` can be set, `VAR_DAYS` can be written, but there is no wall-clock setup flow equivalent to `StartWallClock` / `ReturnFromStartWallClock`

#### Missing
- [ ] `DoTimeBasedEvents` — top-level per-frame clock check; no equivalent polling loop that reads a real/simulated clock and dispatches daily/minutely updates
- [ ] `UpdatePerDay` — daily update dispatcher: `ClearDailyFlags`, `UpdateDewfordTrendPerDay`, `UpdateTVShowsPerDay`, `UpdateWeatherPerDay`, `UpdatePartyPokerusTime`, `UpdateMirageRnd`, `UpdateBirchState`, `UpdateFrontierManiac`, `UpdateFrontierGambler`, `SetShoalItemFlag`, `SetRandomLotteryNumber` — none of these daily callbacks are implemented
- [ ] `StartWallClock` / `ReturnFromStartWallClock` — wall-clock UI entry/exit flow; no wall-clock screen exists

#### Notes
- The GBA RTC (`rtc.h`) has no browser port; time-of-day and day-counting would need to use `Date.now()` or a simulated in-save timestamp.
- All of the `UpdatePerDay` callbacks (`DewfordTrend`, `TV shows`, `Pokérus time`, `Mirage Rnd`, `Birch state`, `Frontier` daily resets, `Shoal Cave` tides, `Lottery`) are post-early-game features and have no TS equivalents yet.
- Berry time tracking in `BerryManager` covers the minute-granularity path but lacks the save-block `lastBerryTreeUpdate` persistence that the C code uses to survive power cycles.
- `InPokemonCenter()` guard in `DoTimeBasedEvents` has no TS equivalent (Pokémon Center healing flow is not yet implemented).

The XOR encryption pattern mirrors the money encryption in `gSaveBlock2Ptr->encryptionKey`, which should be replicated in the save layer (`src/save/native/Gen3SaveParser.ts`).

#### Notes
This is a GBA OAM sprite pool utility used for confetti particle effects (likely Hall of Fame / contest celebrations). It maps directly to hardware OAM slots 64–127. The browser port has no equivalent hardware OAM layer; sprite batching is handled via WebGL (`SpriteBatcher.ts`, `WebGLSpriteRenderer.ts`). A confetti particle system could be implemented as a lightweight canvas or WebGL particle emitter if needed, but no gameplay-critical path depends on it. Safe to omit until contest/HoF scenes are prioritized.

- [ ] `GetAppealHeartTileOffset` / `GetNumHeartsFromAppealPoints` / `GetStarTileOffset` / `GetStatusSymbolTileOffset` / `GetMoveEffectSymbolTileOffset` — tile offset calculation helpers
- [ ] `SwapMoveDescAndContestTilemaps` — swaps UI and move-description tilemap halves
- [ ] `ApplyNextTurnOrder` — applies `nextTurnOrder` values to rearrange turn sequence
- [ ] `SetContestLiveUpdateFlags` / `CalculateContestLiveUpdateData` / `SetConestLiveUpdateTVData` — TV show live update data (BravoTrainer integration)
- [ ] `ContestDebugDoPrint` / `ContestDebugPrintBitStrings` — debug display (point totals, winner/loser flags)
- [ ] `StripPlayerNameForLinkContest` / `StripMonNameForLinkContest` — sanitize names for link contest display
- [ ] `Contest_IsMonsTurnDisabled` / `ContestantCanUseTurn` — turn-disabled checks (exported, used by AI)
- [ ] `TryPutPlayerLast` / `IsPlayerLinkLeader` / `ResetLinkContestBoolean` — link contest utility functions
- [ ] `CreateContestMonFromParty` — builds ContestMon from party slot with scarf item boost
- [ ] 4x link contest task stubs (`Task_CommunicateMonIdxs`, `Task_ReadyStartLinkContest`, etc.) — link cable synchronization (intentionally omittable)
- [ ] `sContestExcitementTable[5][5]` — category vs. category crowd excitement modifier table
- [ ] All sprite/OAM/affine animation data tables — GBA-specific sprite configuration (no browser equivalent needed)

#### Notes
- Contest is **entirely unimplemented** in the TypeScript port. No equivalent files exist anywhere in the TS codebase.
- The contest system is a major standalone feature (5 categories × 4 ranks, AI opponents, link multiplayer, appeal animations, crowd reactions, TV show integration). It is not on the critical path for reaching the first rival battle.
- Link cable/wireless adapter functionality (`Task_CommunicateMonIdxs`, `Task_TryStartLinkContest`, wireless sync tasks) has no browser equivalent and should be intentionally omitted.
- The `BravoTrainerPokemonProfile_BeforeInterview1` call at end of appeals (TV show data) would also be out of scope.
- Contest AI (`ContestAI_ResetAI`, `ContestAI_GetActionToUse`, `gContestEffectFuncs[]`) lives in separate C files (`contest_ai.c`, `contest_effect.c`) also not ported.
- Move animations during appeals reuse the battle animation system (`DoMoveAnim`, `gAnimScriptCallback`) — the battle animation pipeline would need to be functional first.
- `CalculateRound1Points` and `CalculateAppealMoveImpact` contain the core contest scoring math and would be the highest-value targets if contest is ever implemented.

- [ ] `SetContestantEffectStringID` / `SetContestantEffectStringID2` — set effect string IDs for contestant status display
- [ ] `SetStartledString` — maps jam amount to startled string ID thresholds
- [ ] `PrintAppealMoveResultText` — prints appeal move result text with category-specific flavor string
- [ ] `MakeContestantNervous` — marks contestant as nervous, clears their move
- [ ] `ApplyNextTurnOrder` — recalculates turn order for next round (reversal algorithm with explicit overrides)
- [ ] `SpriteCB_JudgeSpeechBubble` / `DoJudgeSpeechBubble` — animates judge's speech bubble sprite with symbol-specific tile/SFX
- [ ] `UpdateApplauseMeter` — copies applause meter GFX tiles to OBJ VRAM
- [ ] `Contest_GetMoveExcitement` — looks up excitement value from 2D table by category
- [ ] `StartApplauseOverflowAnimation` / `Task_ApplauseOverflowAnimation` — palette flash animation when applause overflows
- [ ] `SlideApplauseMeterIn` / `Task_SlideApplauseMeterIn` — slides applause meter sprite in from off-screen
- [ ] `SlideApplauseMeterOut` / `Task_SlideApplauseMeterOut` — slides applause meter sprite off-screen
- [ ] `ShowAndUpdateApplauseMeter` / `Task_ShowAndUpdateApplauseMeter` — orchestrates slide-in, wait, update sequence
- [ ] `AnimateAudience` / `Task_AnimateAudience` — flips between two audience GFX frames via DMA
- [ ] `BlendAudienceBackground` / `Task_BlendAudienceBackground` — blends audience BG palette yellow or black based on excitement direction
- [ ] `ShowHideNextTurnGfx` — shows/hides next-turn order number sprites for each contestant
- [ ] `GetTurnOrderNumberGfx` — returns pointer to correct turn-number tile GFX
- [ ] `DrawUnnervedSymbols` — draws swirl status symbol tiles for unnerved contestants
- [ ] `IsContestantAllowedToCombo` — returns whether contestant can use combo moves
- [ ] `SetBgForCurtainDrop` / `UpdateContestantBoxOrder` — reconfigures BG registers for curtain drop/raise
- [ ] `Task_StartDropCurtainAtRoundEnd` / `Task_UpdateCurtainDropAtRoundEnd` — animates curtain dropping at round end
- [ ] `Task_ResetForNextRound` — resets contestant UI, handles link sync, redraws status symbols
- [ ] `Task_WaitRaiseCurtainAtRoundEnd` / `Task_StartRaiseCurtainAtRoundEnd` / `Task_UpdateRaiseCurtainAtRoundEnd` — animates curtain rising
- [ ] `AnimateSliderHearts` / `Task_WaitForSliderHeartAnim` — affine scale animation for slider heart sprites
- [ ] `SanitizeMove` / `SanitizeSpecies` — clamp move/species IDs to valid ranges
- [ ] `SetMoveSpecificAnimData` — sets up battle anim variables for move-specific overrides (Curse ghost check, Transform target, etc.)
- [ ] `ClearMoveAnimData` / `SetMoveAnimAttackerData` — manage move animation state struct
- [ ] `CreateInvisibleBattleTargetSprite` / `SetBattleTargetSpritePosition` / `SetMoveTargetPosition` — set up battle sprite targeting for contest move anims
- [ ] `Contest_PrintTextToBg0WindowStd` / `Contest_PrintTextToBg0WindowAt` / `Contest_StartTextPrinter` — text printing helpers for contest windows
- [ ] `ContestBG_FillBoxWithIncrementingTile` / `ContestBG_FillBoxWithTile` — fill BG tilemap regions
- [ ] `Contest_RunTextPrinters` / `Contest_SetBgCopyFlags` — text printer polling and BG copy flag management
- [ ] `ResetContestLinkResults` — zeroes link contest results in save block 2
- [ ] `SaveContestWinner` / `GetContestWinnerSaveIdx` — saves winner data to save block (hall/museum slots)
- [ ] `ClearContestWinnerPicsInContestHall` — resets contest hall winner pics to defaults
- [ ] `SetContestLiveUpdateFlags` — records which noteworthy events happened per contestant for TV show generation
- [ ] `CalculateContestLiveUpdateData` — computes winner/loser stats and most-used move for TV show
- [ ] `SetConestLiveUpdateTVData` — selects specific flags to comment on and calls ContestLiveUpdates init functions
- [ ] `ContestDebugToggleBitfields` / `ContestDebugPrintBitStrings` — debug mode to print winner/loser flag bitstrings (unused/debug)
- [ ] `GetMonNicknameLanguage` — heuristic to detect if a nickname is Japanese based on character values
- [ ] `StripPlayerNameForLinkContest` / `StripMonNameForLinkContest` / `StripPlayerAndMonNamesForLinkContest` — normalizes names for link contest transmission

#### Notes
The Contest system is entirely absent from the TypeScript port. Contests are a substantial self-contained mini-game (4 contestants, appeal rounds, judging, applause meter, audience animation, curtain sequences, move animations reusing battle anim infrastructure, and TV show generation). This is a significant post-MVP feature. Link contest functions (`StripPlayerAndMonNamesForLinkContest`, `ResetContestLinkResults`) are also omitted as link cable support is intentionally out of scope for a browser port. The debug functions (`ContestDebugToggleBitfields`, `ContestDebugPrintBitStrings`) are marked unused in the original and can remain unported. The TV live update logic (`SetContestLiveUpdateFlags`, `CalculateContestLiveUpdateData`, `SetConestLiveUpdateTVData`) ties into the in-game TV show system which is also unimplemented.

#### Notes
Contests (Pokémon Contests — Cool, Beauty, Cute, Smart, Tough) are entirely unimplemented in the TypeScript port. This file is the bytecode interpreter for the Contest AI's move-scoring scripts. There is no contest subsystem at all in the TS codebase — no contest state, no appeal/jam scoring, no `ContestAIInfo` struct equivalent, no `gContestMoves`/`gContestEffects` data, and no contest AI script tables. Contest is a significant post-core-game feature and appears to be an intentional out-of-scope omission for the current port milestone.

- [ ] `ContestEffect_MakeFollowingMonsNervous` — probabilistically nerves all later contestants, odds adjusted by combo/condition

- [ ] `ContestEffect_BadlyStartlesMonsInGoodCondition` — jams previous mons harder if they have condition

- [ ] `ContestEffect_BetterIfLast` — doubles appeal bonus if user goes last this turn
- [ ] `ContestEffect_AppealAsGoodAsPrevOnes` — adds half the sum of all prior appeals
- [ ] `ContestEffect_AppealAsGoodAsPrevOne` — copies the immediately preceding contestant's appeal
- [ ] `ContestEffect_BetterWhenLater` — appeal scales linearly with turn order position
- [ ] `ContestEffect_QualityDependsOnTiming` — random appeal value from 10–80
- [ ] `ContestEffect_BetterIfSameType` — doubles appeal if previous non-skipped move was same category
- [ ] `ContestEffect_BetterIfDiffType` — doubles appeal if immediately preceding move was different category
- [ ] `ContestEffect_AffectedByPrevAppeal` — doubles appeal if higher than preceding; zeroes if lower

- [ ] `ContestEffect_BetterWithGoodCondition` — sets appealTripleCondition flag for condition-scaled appeal
- [ ] `ContestEffect_NextAppealEarlier` — shifts user to front of next-turn order, pushes others back
- [ ] `ContestEffect_NextAppealLater` — shifts user to back of next-turn order, pulls others forward
- [ ] `ContestEffect_MakeScramblingTurnOrderEasier` — dummied out in C source
- [ ] `ContestEffect_ScrambleNextTurnOrder` — randomly reassigns all contestants' next-turn order
- [ ] `ContestEffect_ExciteAudienceInAnyContest` — allows off-category move to excite audience normally
- [ ] `ContestEffect_BadlyStartleMonsWithGoodAppeals` — jams previous mons by half their own appeal value
- [ ] `ContestEffect_BetterWhenAudienceExcited` — appeal scales with applause level (10/20/30/50/60)
- [ ] `ContestEffect_DontExciteAudience` — freezes audience excitement until next turn
- [ ] `JamByMoveCategory` — helper: jams (40) previous mons matching category, others get 10
- [ ] `CanUnnerveContestant` — checks immune/jamSafetyCount/turn state before applying jam
- [ ] `WasAtLeastOneOpponentJammed` — processes jamQueue, applies moreEasilyStartled/resistant modifiers, calls JamContestant
- [ ] `JamContestant` — subtracts jam from contestant appeal and accumulates jam total

- [ ] `RoundUp` — rounds score up to nearest 10

#### Notes
Contests are entirely unimplemented in the TypeScript port. The contest system (Pokémon Contests, contest halls, contest moves/combos, judge attention, audience excitement, contestant status) has no TS counterpart anywhere in the file tree. This is an intentional omission — contests are a significant optional post-game feature not on the main story critical path. The contest move data (`gContestMoves`, `gContestEffects`) referenced here is also not generated. If contests are ever scoped, this entire file plus `contest.c`, `contest_link.c`, `contest_util.c`, and associated data files would need porting.

- [ ] `Task_LinkContest_CommunicateMoveSelections` — syncs each contestant's current move choice each appeal round
- [ ] `Task_LinkContest_CommunicateFinalStandings` — multi-phase sync of total points, appeal point totals, round-2 points, and final standings; leader's data wins
- [ ] `Task_LinkContest_CommunicateAppealsState` — multi-phase sync of `ContestantStatus`, appeal results, excitement struct, and turn order; leader's data wins

- [ ] `Task_LinkContest_CommunicateRound1Points` — syncs round-1 points; leader's data wins

#### Notes
This file is entirely link-cable / wireless multiplayer infrastructure for the Contest minigame. The browser port has no link-cable emulation layer and no contest system at all. Intentional omission: GBA serial I/O (`SendBlock`, `GetBlockReceivedStatus`, `SetLinkStandbyCallback`, etc.) has no browser equivalent. The entire Contest feature (both single-player and link) is out of scope for the current port.

- [ ] `GetNumRound2Points` — converts round 2 points to heart count with sign (±0–10)
- [ ] `CalculateContestantsResultData` — normalizes all contestants' scores into relative bar lengths and star/heart counts
- [ ] `UpdateContestResultBars` — spawns per-bar update tasks for each contestant as stars/hearts animate
- [ ] `Task_UpdateContestResultBar` — incrementally animates a single contestant's score bar
- [ ] `AllocContestResults` / `FreeContestResults` — heap management for results screen data
- [ ] `AddContestTextPrinter` — queues a text printer for contestant name windows
- [ ] `BounceMonIconInBox` / `Task_BounceMonIconInBox` — animates winner's mon icon bouncing (alternates frames)
- [ ] `SpriteCB_WinnerMonSlideIn` / `SpriteCB_WinnerMonSlideOut` — winner mon sprite slide animations
- [ ] `Task_CreateConfetti` / `SpriteCB_Confetti` — creates and animates confetti sprites during winner reveal
- [ ] `TryEnterContestMon` — checks eligibility, sets up contestants and calculates round 1 points (script special)
- [ ] `HasMonWonThisContestBefore` — checks if player's mon already has the ribbon for this rank/category
- [ ] `GiveMonContestRibbon` — awards the appropriate ribbon to the player's mon on winning
- [ ] `BufferContestantTrainerName` — copies contestant trainer name into `gStringVar1`
- [ ] `BufferContestantMonNickname` — copies contestant mon nickname into `gStringVar3`
- [ ] `GetContestMonConditionRanking` — (unused) gets rank of contestant's round 1 score among all contestants
- [ ] `GetContestMonCondition` — returns contestant's round 1 points into script var
- [ ] `GetContestWinnerId` — finds contestant index with final standing 0 (first place)
- [ ] `BufferContestWinnerTrainerName` / `BufferContestWinnerMonName` — buffers winner's names into string vars
- [ ] `CB2_SetStartContestCallback` / `Task_StartContest` / `StartContest` — fades out and transitions to contest
- [ ] `BufferContestantMonSpecies` — stores contestant species in script var
- [ ] `Task_StartShowContestResults` / `ShowContestResults` — fades out and transitions to results screen
- [ ] `GetContestPlayerId` — returns player's contestant index into script var
- [ ] `ContestLinkTransfer` — initiates link contest data exchange flow
- [ ] `Task_StartCommunication` / `Task_StartCommunicateRngRS` / `Task_StartCommunicateLeaderIdsRS` / `Task_StartCommunicateCategoryRS` — RS link contest communication chain
- [ ] `Task_LinkContest_SetUpContestRS` — validates category agreement, calculates round 1 points for RS link
- [ ] `Task_LinkContest_CalculateTurnOrderRS` — sorts contestants and communicates turn order for RS link
- [ ] `LinkContest_GetLeaderIndex` — picks link contest leader by highest player ID
- [ ] `Task_LinkContest_FinalizeConnection` — completes link contest setup or disconnects on mismatch
- [ ] `Task_LinkContest_Disconnect` / `Task_LinkContest_WaitDisconnect` — link contest disconnection
- [ ] `SetContestTrainerGfxIds` — writes contestant trainer GFX IDs into save vars
- [ ] `GetNpcContestantLocalId` — (unused) maps contestant index to local NPC ID
- [ ] `BufferContestTrainerAndMonNames` — calls the three Buffer* functions together as a script special
- [ ] `DoesContestCategoryHaveMuseumPainting` — (unused) checks if a museum painting slot is filled
- [ ] `SaveMuseumContestPainting` — saves contest winner as a museum painting
- [ ] `ShouldReadyContestArtist` — checks if player qualifies for the artist (800+ points, Master, 1st place)
- [ ] `CountPlayerMuseumPaintings` — counts how many museum painting slots are filled
- [ ] `GetContestantNamesAtRank` — (unused) gets contestant names at a given rank, handling ties
- [ ] `ShowContestPainting` / `ExitContestPainting` — transitions to/from the contest painting viewer
- [ ] `SetLinkContestPlayerGfx` — sets RS player GFX IDs based on link player gender/version
- [ ] `LoadLinkContestPlayerPalettes` — loads RS or Emerald player palettes for link contestant sprites
- [ ] `GiveMonArtistRibbon` — awards artist ribbon if qualifying conditions are met
- [ ] `IsContestDebugActive` — always returns FALSE (debug mode stub)
- [ ] `ShowContestEntryMonPic` / `HideContestEntryMonPic` / `Task_ShowContestEntryMonPic` — shows a mon front pic in a window during contest entry
- [ ] `GetContestMultiplayerId` — returns multiplayer ID or MAX_LINK_PLAYERS if not a 4-player cable link
- [ ] `GenerateContestRand` / `GetContestRand` — RNG for contests (synchronized over link)
- [ ] `LinkContestWaitForConnection` / `Task_LinkContestWaitForConnection` — wireless link: waits for connection then enables script
- [ ] `LinkContestTryShowWirelessIndicator` / `LinkContestTryHideWirelessIndicator` — shows/hides wireless status sprite
- [ ] `IsContestWithRSPlayer` / `ClearLinkContestFlags` / `IsWirelessContest` — link contest flag accessors

#### Notes
Contests are entirely unimplemented in the TypeScript port. This file covers the contest results screen UI (animated score bars, sliding text boxes, confetti, winner reveal), contest entry/ribbon logic, link/wireless contest multiplayer coordination, and ~30 script special functions called from map scripts. The link cable and wireless adapter subsystems (`gLinkContestFlags`, `SetCloseLinkCallback`, `IsLinkTaskFinished`, etc.) have no browser equivalent and would need to be stubbed or omitted. The GBA-specific rendering (DMA tile copies into VRAM, `WriteSequenceToBgTilemapBuffer`, scanline effects) requires a Canvas/WebGL reimplementation. This is a non-critical post-game feature; the main story path does not require contests.

## batch: src/coord_event_weather.c

### `src/coord_event_weather.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/WorldManager.ts`, `src/weather/WeatherManager.ts`, `src/scripting/specials/weatherSpecials.ts`
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-weather.cjs` (constants only), `scripts/generate-weather-runtime.cjs` (field_weather logic)

#### Implemented
- [x] `CoordEventWeather_Clouds` through `CoordEventWeather_Route123Cycle` — all 13 individual weather setter stubs; the mapping from coord-event weather enum → field weather enum exists implicitly via `src/weather/runtime.gen.ts` constants and `WeatherManager`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `DoCoordEventWeather` — the dispatch logic (coord event weather enum → call `SetWeather`) is not implemented as a dedicated function; weather triggering from map coord events happens ad-hoc in `WorldManager`/`overworldGameUpdate.ts` rather than through a unified coord-event weather dispatcher

#### Missing
- [ ] `DoCoordEventWeather` — no single TS function that takes a `coordEventWeather` byte from a map coord event and routes it to the correct `SetWeather` call; the COORD_EVENT_WEATHER_* → WEATHER_* mapping table has no direct TS equivalent

#### Notes
The TS port handles weather primarily through `WeatherManager` and map metadata, but the specific coord-event-driven weather dispatch path (`DoCoordEventWeather` called from `TryDoCoordEventWeather` in field_map_obj.c) is not explicitly replicated. The 13 weather type constants are available via generated files. The cycling weather variants (`ROUTE119_CYCLE`, `ROUTE123_CYCLE`) — which cycle between sunny/rain based on steps — are likely not implemented since they require step-based cycling logic beyond a simple `SetWeather` call.

- [ ] `Task_CreditsSoftReset` — triggers `SoftReset` after "THE END" sequence
- [ ] `DrawTheEnd` / `DrawLetterMapTiles` / `GetLetterMapTile` — renders pixel-mapped "THE END" text into VRAM tilemap

- [ ] `SpriteCB_CreditsMonBg` — mirrors position/visibility/blend state from associated Pokémon sprite
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Player` / `SpriteCB_Rival` — player/rival sprite movement callbacks for 5 bike riding states each

- [ ] `InitCreditsBgsAndWindows` / `FreeCreditsBgsAndWindows` — BG/window setup and teardown
- [ ] `ResetCreditsTasks` — destroys all sub-tasks (bg animation, bike scene, palette cycling, show mons) on scene transition
- [ ] `ResetGpuAndVram` — clears all GPU registers and DMA-fills VRAM/OAM/palette to zero

#### Notes
- Credits sequence is entirely post-game content (triggered after Hall of Fame entry). Not in scope for the current port which targets new-game-through-first-battle.
- Heavy GBA hardware dependency: VRAM direct writes, OAM matrix affine transforms (`SetOamMatrix`), GPU register manipulation, `m4a` music system, intro credits graphics (`gIntroCredits_MovingSceneryVOffset`, `CycleSceneryPalette`, `CreateBicycleBgAnimationTask`) — all require browser-specific reimplementation.
- `DeterminePokemonToShow` is the only function with gameplay-data logic (Pokédex flags, starter VAR) that would need `GameFlags`/`GameVariables` integration if credits were ever ported.
- `gHasHallOfFameRecords` global is referenced in `CB2_Credits` for B-button speed-up; no equivalent in TS save system yet.

### `src/data.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
