**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-trainer-parties.cjs`, `scripts/generate-pokemon-sprite-coords.cjs`

#### Implemented
- [x] `gTrainerBackPicTable_*` (Brendan, May, Red, Leaf, Wally, Steven, RS variants) — trainer back-sprite frame tables; frame counts and structure covered by `BattleSpriteLoader.ts` which loads trainer back sprites by trainer ID
- [x] Trainer party data (`#include "data/trainer_parties.h"`, `#include "data/trainers.h"`) — fully generated into `src/data/trainerParties.gen.ts`
- [x] Pokemon sprite coordinates (`#include "data/pokemon_graphics/front_pic_coordinates.h"`, `back_pic_coordinates.h`, `enemy_mon_elevation.h`) — generated into `src/data/pokemonSpriteCoords.gen.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `gBattlerPicTable_PlayerLeft/OpponentLeft/PlayerRight/OpponentRight` — GBA uses heap offsets for double-battle slot addressing; TS `BattleSpriteLoader.ts` loads sprites by species/side but does not model the 4-slot heap buffer layout (irrelevant in browser context)
- [-] Affine animation tables (`gAffineAnims_BattleSpritePlayerSide`, `gAffineAnims_BattleSpriteOpponentSide`, `gAffineAnims_BattleSpriteContest`) — individual named anims (emerge, return, shrink, spin, etc.) are not ported as data tables; `BattleOverlayFrame.ts` / `BattleSpriteLoader.ts` handle sprite scaling procedurally without a table-driven affine system
- [-] `gAnims_MonPic` / `sAnim_MonPic_*` — GBA sprite animation command arrays for the 4-frame mon pic slots; browser renders directly from PNG sheets without a GBA AnimCmd system

#### Missing
- [ ] `gMinigameDigits_Pal` / `gMinigameDigits_Gfx` / `sMinigameDigitsThin_Gfx` — link minigame digit graphics; intentionally omitted (link cable / minigame features not ported)
- [ ] `#include "data/pokemon_graphics/front_pic_anims.h"` / `unused_anims.h` / `unknown_table.h` — GBA-specific per-species front-pic animation sequences; no browser equivalent (static PNG rendering)
- [ ] `#include "data/pokemon_graphics/still_front_pic_table.h"` / `back_pic_table.h` / `front_pic_table.h` — raw GBA sprite pointer tables; superseded by asset URL resolution in `spriteUtils.ts` / `BattleSpriteLoader.ts`
- [ ] `#include "data/pokemon_graphics/palette_table.h"` / `shiny_palette_table.h` — GBA palette slot tables; color extraction handled differently via `colorExtractor.ts`
- [ ] `#include "data/trainer_graphics/front_pic_anims.h"` / `front_pic_tables.h` / `back_pic_anims.h` / `back_pic_tables.h` — trainer sprite animation/pointer tables; browser loads trainer sprites by ID without GBA table indirection
- [ ] `#include "data/text/trainer_class_names.h"` / `move_names.h` / `species_names.h` — text string tables; species names generated into `src/data/species.ts`, move names into `src/data/moves.ts`, but trainer class names have no dedicated TS equivalent
- [ ] `sAnim_GeneralFrame0` / `sAnim_GeneralFrame3` — generic single-frame AnimCmd sequences used by battle sprite OAM; not needed in browser

#### Notes
- This file is almost entirely **GBA hardware data** (INCBIN graphics, heap-addressed sprite frame tables, OAM affine/anim command arrays) that has no direct browser equivalent. The meaningful semantic data it includes (trainer parties, pokemon coords, species names, move names) is fully covered by generation scripts.
- The affine animation tables are the only gameplay-relevant runtime data that is partially missing — emerge/return/shrink animations are approximated procedurally in the TS battle renderer rather than driven by these tables.
- Link minigame graphics (`gMinigameDigits_*`) are intentionally omitted — no link cable / multiplayer support planned.
- Trainer class names string table is the one data gap with mild gameplay relevance (displayed in battle UI).

- [ ] `BuildEggMoveset` — constructs hatched egg's moveset: egg moves from father, TM/HM moves, shared parent moves

- [ ] `ShowDaycareLevelMenu` — renders list menu for picking which daycare mon to withdraw

#### Notes
- The entire daycare system (deposit, withdraw, breeding, egg generation, egg hatching trigger) is absent from the TypeScript port. This is a non-trivial post-new-game feature.
- `egg_hatch.c` (hatching sequence/cutscene) is a separate C file also not in scope here but similarly unported.
- `gEggMoves` data (referenced via `#include "data/pokemon/egg_moves.h"`) has no generated TypeScript equivalent in the manifest; breeding logic depends on it.
- The Gen 3 IV inheritance bug (`RemoveIVIndexFromList` removing fixed indices 0/1/2 instead of the chosen index) is documented in comments; any future port should decide whether to replicate or fix it.
- `ShowDaycareLevelMenu` / `Task_HandleDaycareLevelMenuInput` are GBA window/task UI that would be replaced by a React menu component; the underlying data logic (cost, nicknames, levels) still needs porting.
- Link cable / record mixing path (`InitDaycareMailRecordMixing`) can be intentionally omitted as a multiplayer feature.

## batch: src/decompress.c

### `src/decompress.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None (absorbed into asset pipeline)
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] LZ77 decompression (implicit) — browser loads pre-decompressed PNG/binary assets; no runtime LZ77 needed
- [x] Unown sprite selection by personality — `src/rendering/spriteUtils.ts` handles Unown letter lookup for rendering
- [x] Spinda spot rendering — `src/rendering/spriteUtils.ts` (`DrawSpindaSpots` equivalent exists for Spinda personality overlay)

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `LoadSpecialPokePic` / `LoadSpecialPokePic_2` — Unown letter selection and species fallback logic exists in `src/rendering/spriteUtils.ts` and `src/battle/render/BattleSpriteLoader.ts`, but Deoxys tile duplication (`DuplicateDeoxysTiles`) is not explicitly ported

#### Missing
- [ ] `DuplicateDeoxysTiles` — copies Deoxys form tile data; Deoxys form variation via personality is not handled
- [ ] `LoadCompressedSpriteSheet` / `LoadCompressedSpriteSheetUsingHeap` — GBA VRAM sprite sheet loading; not applicable (browser uses image assets)
- [ ] `LoadCompressedSpritePalette` / `LoadCompressedSpritePaletteUsingHeap` — GBA palette loading; not applicable
- [ ] `DecompressPicFromTable` / `DecompressPicFromTable_2` / `DecompressPicFromTable_DontHandleDeoxys` — GBA decompression dispatch; not applicable
- [ ] `StitchObjectsOn8x8Canvas` — GB-era tile stitching (marked UNUSED in C source); intentionally omitted
- [ ] `GetDecompressedDataSize` — reads LZ77 header size; not applicable
- [ ] `LZDecompressWram` / `LZDecompressVram` — thin wrappers over hardware LZ77; not applicable

#### Notes
This file is entirely GBA hardware infrastructure (LZ77 decompression, VRAM/WRAM loading, sprite sheet management). The browser port pre-processes all assets at build time into PNG/binary formats, so no runtime decompression is needed. The only gameplay-relevant logic — Unown letter selection by personality and Spinda spot drawing — is absorbed into `src/rendering/spriteUtils.ts` and `src/battle/render/BattleSpriteLoader.ts`. `StitchObjectsOn8x8Canvas` is marked `UNUSED` even in the original C source and has no TS equivalent. Deoxys tile duplication is the one missing piece with minor visual impact (Deoxys forme differences would not render correctly).

- [ ] `AttemptPlaceDecoration_` / `PlaceDecorationPrompt` / `PlaceDecoration` / `PlaceDecoration_` — confirmation and commit of decoration placement
- [ ] `ContinueDecorating` / `CancelDecoratingPrompt` / `CancelDecorating` / `CancelDecorating_` / `c1_overworld_prev_quest` — cancellation and warp-back flow
- [ ] `ShowDecorationOnMap` / `ShowDecorationOnMap_` — writes decoration metatiles into the live map grid with proper elevation and impassability flags
- [ ] `SetDecoration` — spawns sprite-type decoration object events after placement via script
- [ ] `Task_ContinuePuttingAwayDecorations` / `ContinuePuttingAwayDecorations` / `SetUpPuttingAwayDecorationPlayerAvatar` — "put away" mode entry
- [ ] `AttemptPutAwayDecoration_` / `AttemptMarkDecorUnderCursorForRemoval` / `AttemptMarkSpriteDecorUnderCursorForRemoval` / `MarkSpriteDecorsInBoundsForRemoval` — cursor-based decoration removal selection
- [ ] `Task_PutAwayDecoration` / `PutAwayDecoration` / `ClearRearrangementNonSprites` — commits removal, restores metatiles, runs put-away script
- [ ] `ReturnDecorationPrompt` / `StopPuttingAwayDecorationsPrompt` / `StopPuttingAwayDecorations` / `Task_StopPuttingAwayDecorations` / `FieldCB_StopPuttingAwayDecorations` — stop/confirm exit from put-away mode
- [ ] `PutAwayDecorationIteration` — script-callable special that iterates decorator removal (called by `SecretBase_EventScript_PutAwayDecoration`)
- [ ] `DecorationItemsMenuAction_AttemptToss` / `TossDecorationPrompt` / `TossDecoration` — toss (permanently delete) decoration from inventory
- [ ] `gpu_pal_decompress_alloc_tag_and_upload` — decompresses decoration metatile graphics into a sprite for the placement cursor
- [ ] `AddDecorationIconObject` / `AddDecorationIconObjectFromIconTable` / `AddDecorationIconObjectFromObjectEvent` — creates decoration icons for use in item menus
- [ ] `ShowDecorationCategoriesWindow` / `CopyDecorationCategoryName` — trader-facing category menu API
- [ ] `IsSelectedDecorInThePC` — checks whether selected decoration is available (not already placed) for toss/place validation
- [ ] `IdentifyOwnedDecorationsCurrentlyInUse` — builds index buffers of which owned decorations are already placed in the secret base or player room
- [ ] Helper sprite callbacks: `InitializePuttingAwayCursorSprite`, `InitializePuttingAwayCursorSprite2`, `InitializeCameraSprite1` — blinking/movement animation for placement cursor sprites
- [ ] `ApplyCursorMovement_IsInvalid` — bounds-clamps cursor during decoration placement
- [ ] `GetDecorationElevation` — returns elevation value for STAND/SLIDE decorations
- [ ] `AddDecorationWindow` / `RemoveDecorationWindow` / all window management helpers — GBA BG window setup (browser equivalent would be React UI)

#### Notes
- Secret Bases are a post-game feature (unlocked after beating the Elite Four). The entire decoration system depends on `gSaveBlock1Ptr->secretBases` and `gSaveBlock1Ptr->playerRoomDecorations` — neither of which is modeled in the TS save layer (`Gen3SaveParser` exists but secret base decoration state is not exposed).
- The placement flow is deeply tied to GBA-specific hardware: OAM sprites, `gFieldCamera.spriteId`, `MapGridSetMetatileEntryAt`, palette DMA — all would require significant architectural translation.
- `ShowDecorationOnMap` / `SetDecoration` are the only functions with any overworld impact (modifying live metatiles and spawning object events) and would be the minimum needed to render an already-saved decoration layout. Everything else is interactive UI that can be deferred until Secret Base gameplay is scoped.
- No generator script consumes this file; decoration data (`gDecorations[]`, `gDecorationInventories[]`) comes from `data/decoration/tiles.h`, `header.h`, `description.h` — none of which are currently generated.
- The trader integration (`ExitTraderDecorationMenu`, `DECOR_MENU_TRADE`) is Colosseum/e-Reader link cable functionality — intentional omission.

- [ ] `GetNumOwnedDecorations` — counts total owned decorations across all categories

#### Notes
Secret Base decoration inventory is post-game/link-adjacent content. The port has no Secret Base system (`decoration.h`, `decoration.c`) and no corresponding save-block fields for decoration slots. This is a reasonable intentional omission for an MVP — the decoration inventory is only needed once Secret Bases are implemented, which requires multiplayer record mixing (link cable analogue). No generator covers decorations data either.

- [ ] `InitDewfordTrend` — initializes 5 saved trends with random Easy Chat word pairs and seeds RNG values
- [ ] `UpdateDewfordTrendPerDay` — ages all saved trends daily, cycling trendiness up/down, re-sorts
- [ ] `TrySetTrendyPhrase` — evaluates submitted phrase against saved trends, inserts if trendier, returns whether it became the current phrase
- [ ] `ReceiveDewfordTrendData` — merges record-mixed trends from other players, deduplicates by keeping higher trendiness
- [ ] `BufferTrendyPhraseString` — converts current trend's Easy Chat words to display string in `gStringVar1`
- [ ] `IsTrendyPhraseBoring` — checks whether current phrase is "boring" (used by Dewford Hall NPC), writes to `gSpecialVar_Result`
- [ ] `GetDewfordHallPaintingNameIndex` — derives painting title index from current phrase words, writes to `gSpecialVar_Result`

- [ ] `CompareTrends` (static) — three-mode comparator (NORMAL, MAX_FIRST, FULL) used for sorting

- [ ] `IsPhraseInSavedTrends` (static) — checks if a word pair already exists in saved trends

- [ ] `GetSavedTrendIndex` (static) — finds index of a matching phrase in a trend array, returns -1 if absent

#### Notes
Dewford Trend is a non-critical social/multiplayer feature (link cable record mixing, TV show triggers, Feebas tile seeding). The `rand` field from `DewfordTrend` feeds into Feebas tile generation and slot machines — those downstream consumers are also unimplemented. The Easy Chat word system (`GetRandomEasyChatWordFromGroup`, `ConvertEasyChatWordsToString`) has no TS equivalent either. `TryPutTrendWatcherOnAir` (TV show) is a dependency not present in TS. The entire subsystem can be stubbed for main-story coverage but would be needed for full Dewford Hall NPC dialogue and Feebas tile logic.

- [ ] `DigitObjUtil_Init` — allocates pool of digit-sprite printers
- [ ] `DigitObjUtil_Free` — frees all digit printers and pool
- [ ] `DigitObjUtil_CreatePrinter` — sets up a numeric OAM sprite display (loads tiles/palette, computes pow10 for digit extraction)
- [ ] `DigitObjUtil_PrintNumOn` — renders a number onto OAM digit sprites using selected formatting mode
- [ ] `DigitObjUtil_DeletePrinter` — hides and frees a single printer's OAM slots and optionally its tile/palette resources
- [ ] `DigitObjUtil_HideOrShow` — toggles visibility of all OAM slots in a printer
- [ ] `GetTilesPerImage` — looks up tiles-per-sprite from shape/size table
- [ ] `DrawNumObjsLeadingZeros` — formats number with leading zeros across digit sprites (static)
- [ ] `DrawNumObjsMinusInFront` — formats number with minus sign before first significant digit (static)
- [ ] `DrawNumObjsMinusInBack` — formats number with minus sign after last digit (static)
- [ ] `CopyWorkToOam` — initialises OAM buffer entries from printer config (static)
- [ ] `GetFirstOamId` — finds a free OAM slot block of sufficient size (static)
- [ ] `SharesTileWithAnyActive` / `SharesPalWithAnyActive` — ref-count helpers to avoid double-freeing shared tile/palette tags (static)

#### Notes
This module is a GBA OAM (hardware sprite) number renderer. The browser port uses Canvas/WebGL for all number and text rendering (see `src/core/prompt/`, `src/battle/render/BattleHealthBox.ts`, `src/menu/components/HPBar.tsx`), making this entire file intentionally obsolete. The digit-formatting logic (leading zeros, minus-in-front, minus-in-back) could theoretically be reused in a pure-TS helper, but currently no such extraction exists — each UI component handles its own numeric display.

## batch: src/dma3_manager.c

### `src/dma3_manager.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
*(none — intentionally omitted)*

#### Notes
GBA hardware-specific DMA (Direct Memory Access) controller used to bulk-copy/fill VRAM, palette RAM, and OAM during VBlank. The browser port uses the GPU (WebGL texture uploads, buffer writes) instead of DMA transfers. All five functions (`ClearDma3Requests`, `ProcessDma3Requests`, `RequestDma3Copy`, `RequestDma3Fill`, `CheckForSpaceForDma3Request`) are hardware abstractions with no browser equivalent — intentional omission is correct.

### `src/dodrio_berry_picking.c`

#### Missing
- [ ] `StartDodrioBerryPicking` — entry point, allocates game state, starts game task
- [ ] `InitDodrioGame` — initializes all game state fields
- [ ] `Task_StartDodrioGame` — multi-step init: communicates mon info, loads GFX, fades in

- [ ] `DoGameIntro` — plays intro animation and shows player names
- [ ] `InitCountdown` / `DoCountdown` — pre-game countdown using `StartMinigameCountdown`
- [ ] `WaitGameStart` — waits for all players to signal ready
- [ ] `PlayGame_Leader` / `PlayGame_Member` — handles D-pad input to pick berries
- [ ] `WaitEndGame_Leader` / `WaitEndGame_Member` — waits for berries to stop falling, transitions to results

- [ ] `DoResults` — shows results screen with berry counts and rankings
- [ ] `AskPlayAgain` — shows yes/no prompt, exchanges decisions over link
- [ ] `EndLink` / `ExitGame` / `ResetGame` — game teardown and replay flow
- [ ] `Task_NewGameIntro` — slides tree borders apart, animates status bar entry
- [ ] `Task_CommunicateMonInfo` — exchanges shiny status of each player's Dodrio over link
- [ ] `RecvLinkData_Leader` / `SendLinkData_Leader` — leader-side link packet handling
- [ ] `RecvLinkData_Member` / `SendLinkData_Member` — member-side link packet handling
- [ ] `RecvLinkData_Gameplay` / `RecvLinkData_ReadyToEnd` — gameplay packet receive logic
- [ ] `HandleSound_Leader` / `HandleSound_Member` — plays berry pick/miss/game-over sounds
- [ ] `HandlePickBerries` — resolves berry pick attempts across all players each frame
- [ ] `TryPickBerry` — checks if a player's pick attempt hits a berry in range
- [ ] `UpdateFallingBerries` — advances berry fall positions, spawns new berries, squishes missed ones
- [ ] `UpdateBerrySprites` / `UpdateAllDodrioAnims` / `SetAllDodrioDisabled` — sprite update per frame
- [ ] `UpdateGame_Leader` / `UpdateGame_Member` — calls sprite/anim update functions
- [ ] `GetActiveBerryColumns` — maps player count to active column range
- [ ] `AllPlayersReadyToStart` / `ResetReadyToStart` — synchronizes game start
- [ ] `ReadyToEndGame_Leader` / `ReadyToEndGame_Member` — checks end conditions
- [ ] `TryIncrementDifficulty` — increases difficulty after enough berries eaten
- [ ] `GetPlayerIdAtColumn` — maps column index to responsible player id
- [ ] `GetNewBerryId` / `GetNewBerryIdByDifficulty` — selects next berry type based on difficulty
- [ ] `IncrementBerryResult` — records berry pick/miss per player per column
- [ ] `UpdateBerriesPickedInRow` / `SetMaxBerriesPickedInRow` — tracks consecutive pick streak
- [ ] `ResetForPlayAgainPrompt` — resets game state for another round
- [ ] `SetRandomPrize` — randomly picks prize berry from difficulty-based prize table
- [ ] `GetScore` / `GetHighestScore` / `GetScoreByRanking` / `SetScoreResults` — score calculation and ranking
- [ ] `TryUpdateRecords` — saves high score/berries to `SaveBlock2`
- [ ] `TryGivePrize` — awards prize berry to highest-scoring player
- [ ] `UpdatePickStateQueue` — 4-frame input queue for leader's pick state
- [ ] `HandleWaitPlayAgainInput` — allows Dodrio animation while waiting for play-again response
- [ ] `IsDodrioInParty` — script special: checks if player has a Dodrio in party
- [ ] `ShowDodrioBerryPickingRecords` / `Task_ShowDodrioBerryPickingRecords` / `PrintRecordsText` — shows record window from script
- [ ] `SendPacket_ReadyToStart` / `RecvPacket_ReadyToStart` — RFU packet for start sync
- [ ] `SendPacket_GameState` / `RecvPacket_GameState` — RFU packet with full per-frame berry/pick state
- [ ] `SendPacket_PickState` / `RecvPacket_PickState` — RFU packet for member pick input
- [ ] `SendPacket_ReadyToEnd` / `RecvPacket_ReadyToEnd` — RFU packet for end sync
- [ ] All GFX functions: `LoadGfx`, `ShowNames`, `ShowResults`, `Msg_WantToPlayAgain`, `Msg_SavingDontTurnOff`, `Msg_CommunicationStandby`, `EraseMessage`, `Msg_SomeoneDroppedOut`, `StopGfxFuncs`, `GfxIdle`, `PrintRankedScores`
- [ ] All sprite functions: `LoadDodrioGfx`, `CreateDodrioSprite`, `SpriteCB_Dodrio`, `DoDodrioMissedAnim`, `DoDodrioIntroAnim`, `CreateStatusBarSprites`, `DoStatusBarIntro`, `UpdateStatusBarAnim`, `LoadBerryGfx`, `CreateBerrySprites`, `CreateCloudSprites`, `SpriteCB_Cloud`

#### Notes
This is the **Dodrio Berry Picking** multiplayer minigame, which requires GBA Wireless Adapter (RFU) link play with 3–5 players. The entire file is intentionally unported:

- **Link cable / RFU networking** (`Rfu_SendPacket`, `gRecvCmds`, `GetLinkPlayerCount`, etc.) has no browser equivalent — the whole game is structured around a leader/member packet protocol.
- **GBA hardware** (BG registers, DMA, OAM, palette fades, `SetVBlankCallback`) has no direct TS equivalent.
- **Minigame countdown** (`StartMinigameCountdown`) is a separate GBA-specific subsystem not ported.
- The only browser-portable surface is `IsDodrioInParty` (party check) and the save-block record fields (`berryPick.bestScore`, etc.) — the latter would need exposing via `SaveManager` if records are ever shown in-game.
- `ShowDodrioBerryPickingRecords` is callable from a map script (`special ShowDodrioBerryPickingRecords`) and would need a TS special implementation stub that either no-ops or shows a placeholder UI.

## batch: src/dynamic_placeholder_text_util.c

### `src/dynamic_placeholder_text_util.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts`, `src/core/prompt/PromptPrinterEngine.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `DynamicPlaceholderTextUtil_ExpandPlaceholders` — placeholder substitution in dialog strings is handled inline within ScriptRunner's text expansion and the prompt/dialog pipeline when rendering NPC dialog strings

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `DynamicPlaceholderTextUtil_SetPlaceholderPtr` / `DynamicPlaceholderTextUtil_GetPlaceholderPtr` — no dedicated slot-indexed pointer registry exists; placeholder values are passed contextually (player name, rival name, Pokémon name) rather than through an 8-slot indexed array

#### Missing
- [ ] `DynamicPlaceholderTextUtil_Reset` — no explicit reset of a shared placeholder slot array; the TS port has no stateful 8-slot sStringPointers equivalent
- [ ] `DynamicPlaceholderTextUtil_SetPlaceholderPtr` — no indexed slot API; callers cannot set arbitrary slot indices 0–7 for deferred expansion

#### Notes
The C system uses a global 8-slot `sStringPointers` array and a `CHAR_DYNAMIC` sentinel byte in raw GBA charset strings. The TS port operates on Unicode strings and handles substitution implicitly in `ScriptRunner.ts` (player/rival/Pokémon name tokens) and in the dialog/prompt pipeline. There is no structural equivalent to the slot-indexed register; the design is intentionally different due to the absence of raw GBA byte strings. The functionality is largely covered for the cases that matter (name substitution in dialog), but the generic indexed-slot API is absent.

#### Notes
- Easy Chat is a **fully absent subsystem** in the TS port. There is no word-composition UI, no EC word database decoder, no keyboard/word-select overlay, and no `ShowEasyChatScreen` special handler.
- The feature is used by: Dewford Trendy Phrase, Berry Master wife, Mystery Gift questionnaire, Mauville Bard, Lilycove Lady quiz, Gabby & Ty interview, contest interview, fan club, player profile, mail composition, apprentice trainer phrases, and Battle Tower interview — all of which are therefore also unimplemented to the extent they depend on Easy Chat.
- Link cable / multiplayer Easy Chat (Union Room, record mixing) is intentionally out of scope for a browser port.
- The GBA-specific rendering (BG tilemaps, DMA, OAM sprites, palette fade, GPU register writes, `WIN0` hardware windowing) would be replaced by React/canvas/WebGL equivalents, but none have been started.
- `gabbyAndTySpecials.ts` exists and references the Gabby & Ty quote slot in save data, but does not implement the Easy Chat word-selection UI itself — it only sets/reads the already-stored EC word index.

- [ ] `GetWordFromSelectedGroup` — returns EC word by index from currently selected group
- [ ] `GetNumWordsInSelectedGroup` — returns count of words in currently selected group
- [ ] `SetSelectedWordGroup_GroupMode` — builds word list for a given group in category-browse mode
- [ ] `SetSelectedWordGroup_AlphabetMode` — builds word list for alphabet-browse mode
- [ ] `IsEasyChatGroupUnlocked2` — checks sWordData unlocked-groups list (screen-side version)
- [ ] `IsEasyChatIndexAndGroupUnlocked` — per-group unlock logic (Pokédex seen, trendy saying bitfield, enabled flag)
- [ ] `IsRestrictedWordSpecies` — checks if a species requires Pokédex-seen before appearing in national EC group
- [ ] `IsEasyChatWordUnlocked` — combines group-unlock + index-unlock checks
- [ ] `InitializeEasyChatWordArray` — fills a word array with EC_EMPTY_WORD
- [ ] `InitQuestionnaireWords` — clears questionnaire word slots in SaveBlock1
- [ ] `IsEasyChatAnswerUnlocked` — checks both group and index unlock for a questionnaire answer word

#### Notes
Easy Chat is a GBA-era multiplayer/social feature (Pokémon Wireless Club, record mixing, mail, Dewford trendy phrase, Battle Frontier profiles, questionnaire) that has no counterpart in this browser port. The system depends on SaveBlock1 fields (`easyChatProfile`, `easyChatBattleStart`, `easyChatBattleWon`, `easyChatBattleLost`, `mail[].words`, `unlockedTrendySayings`) and the EC word group/alphabet data tables, none of which have been ported. Intentional omission is reasonable: link cable, record mixing, Union Room, and mail features are all out of scope for a single-player browser port. The `InitEasyChatPhrases` call that fires on new game could eventually need a stub to zero the relevant SaveBlock1 fields if native save compatibility is required.

## batch: src/egg_hatch.c

### `src/egg_hatch.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/pokemon/evolution.ts`, `src/states/EvolutionState.ts`, `src/evolution/EvolutionRenderer.ts`, `src/evolution/types.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `GetEggCyclesToSubtract` — Magma Armor/Flame Body halving of egg cycles; ability check logic exists in `src/data/abilities.ts` and party iteration pattern exists
- [x] `AddHatchedMonToParty` (partial data flow) — party mon creation, species name, Pokédex seen/caught registration, ball/met-level/met-location setting are all modeled in `src/pokemon/` and `src/game/` layer

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `CreateHatchedMon` — Core stat/move/IV transfer from egg to hatched mon; `src/pokemon/stats.ts` handles stat calculation, but the full egg→hatched mon personality/IV/move preservation pipeline is not explicitly implemented as a standalone function
- [-] `CountPartyAliveNonEggMons` / `CountPartyAliveNonEggMonsExcept` — Party counting utilities exist scattered in the TS codebase but no dedicated egg-aware non-egg-mon counter
- [-] `ScriptHatchMon` — Script special entry point; `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts` handles specials but `ScriptHatchMon` (triggered via `special ScriptHatchMon` in map scripts) is not wired up

#### Missing
- [ ] `EggHatch` (full sequence) — The complete egg-hatch scene: fade to black, sprite animation state machine, shake phases, shard particles, white flash reveal
- [ ] `CB2_LoadEggHatch` / `CB2_EggHatch` — GBA callback-based loading and main hatch loop; no browser-side equivalent scene/state exists
- [ ] `Task_EggHatch` / `Task_EggHatchPlayBGM` — Task-based BGM transition (stop map music → play MUS_EVOLUTION_INTRO → MUS_EVOLUTION)
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Egg_Shake1` / `SpriteCB_Egg_Shake2` / `SpriteCB_Egg_Shake3` — Three-phase egg shake animation with Sin-based oscillation and progressive cracking frames
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Egg_WaitHatch` — Hold state before hatch burst
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Egg_Hatch` — Fade-to-white, 16-shard burst emission
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Egg_Reveal` — Reveal hatched mon with affine emerge animation and fade-from-white
- [ ] `SpriteCB_EggShard` — Physics-based shard sprite: velocity + gravity acceleration, self-destroy on landing
- [ ] `CreateEggShardSprite` / `CreateRandomEggShardSprite` — Shard particle spawning from `sEggShardVelocities` table
- [ ] `EggHatchCreateMonSprite` — Load and position hatched mon front sprite in battle sprite slot
- [ ] `EggHatchPrintMessage` — Windowed text printer for hatch messages
- [ ] `EggHatchSetMonNickname` — Callback after naming screen: write nickname, free resources, return to field
- [ ] `CheckDaycareMonReceivedMail` / `_CheckDaycareMonReceivedMail` — Daycare mail ownership validation (nickname/OT mismatch detection)
- [ ] `VBlankCB_EggHatch` — VBlank handler (browser irrelevant, but the render-loop equivalent is missing)
- [ ] Egg sprite data (`sEggHatchTiles`, `sEggShardTiles`, `sEggPalette`, `sEggShardVelocities`) — No browser asset pipeline for egg hatch graphics or shard velocity table

#### Notes
- The TS project has an `EvolutionState` and `EvolutionRenderer` covering the evolution sequence, but **no egg-hatch scene** exists as a distinct state — egg hatching is a separate scene in Emerald that precedes evolution.
- The naming screen (`DoNamingScreen`) post-hatch has no TS equivalent; nickname entry is entirely missing from the port.
- `sEggShardVelocities` is a fixed Q8.8 physics table — would need conversion to floating-point pixel velocities for the browser renderer.
- `CountPartyAliveNonEggMons` calls `CountStorageNonEggMons` (PC box storage counting) which is also not ported.
- Mail system (`CheckDaycareMonReceivedMail`) is entirely out of scope for the current port.
- GBA hardware (BG templates, OAM, DMA, palette fade registers) are intentionally omitted — the browser equivalent would use WebGL sprite/canvas layers.

## batch: src/ereader_helpers.c

### `src/ereader_helpers.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `TryWriteTrainerHill` / `TryReadTrainerHill` / `ReadTrainerHillAndValidate` — TrainerHill runtime exists (`src/game/trainerHillRuntime.ts`, `src/data/trainerHillData.gen.ts`, `src/data/trainerHillParties.gen.ts`) but the e-Reader data ingestion path (writing received trainer data into a save sector) is absent; the TS port uses statically generated floor data instead of live e-Reader transfers

#### Missing
- [ ] `EReader_Send` / `EReader_Recv` — GBA Serial I/O send/receive loop over SIO link cable
- [ ] `EReaderHandleTransfer` — state machine driving GBA link-cable serial transfer (HANDSHAKE → TRANSFER → CHECKSUM → DONE)
- [ ] `EReaderHelper_SerialCallback` / `EReaderHelper_Timer3Callback` — hardware interrupt handlers for serial and timer 3
- [ ] `EReaderHelper_SaveRegsState` / `EReaderHelper_RestoreRegsState` / `EReaderHelper_ClearSendRecvMgr` — GBA register save/restore around serial transfers
- [ ] `ValidateTrainerHillData` / `ValidateTrainerChecksum` / `ValidateTrainerHillChecksum` — checksum validation for e-Reader-received `EReaderTrainerHillSet` payloads
- [ ] `TryWriteTrainerHill_Internal` / `TryReadTrainerHill_Internal` — read/write `TrainerHillChallenge` to special save sector
- [ ] `GetTrainerHillUnkVal` — returns incremented `unused` byte from TrainerHill save data
- [ ] `sTrainerHillTrainerTemplates_JP` — JP-locale fallback trainer templates used when a floor has an odd number of trainers
- [ ] `DetermineSendRecvState` / `SetUpTransferManager` / `OpenSerialMulti` / `OpenSerial32` / `CloseSerial` / `EnableSio` / `DisableTm3` / `StartTm3` / `GetKeyInput` — low-level GBA serial/timer hardware helpers

#### Notes
This file is entirely GBA hardware-specific (SIO link cable, timer interrupts, REG_SIOCNT/REG_TM3CNT, VBlank wait). None of it is portable to the browser. The e-Reader accessory and link-cable multiplayer have no browser equivalent and are intentionally out of scope. The only gameplay-adjacent concern — TrainerHill floor data populated via e-Reader — is covered in the TS port by statically generated data (`trainerHillData.gen.ts`, `trainerHillParties.gen.ts`) and the `trainerHillRuntime.ts` runtime, making the live transfer path unnecessary.

None of this is applicable to a browser port. The Trainer Hill runtime itself is partially ported (`src/game/trainerHillRuntime.ts`, `src/data/trainerHillData.gen.ts`), but the e-Reader ingestion pathway (multiboot, card scan, link exchange) has no browser equivalent and is permanently out of scope.

## batch: src/event_data.c

### `src/event_data.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/GameFlags.ts`, `src/game/GameVariables.ts`
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-flag-var-maps.cjs` (for flag/var ID constants)

#### Implemented
- [x] `FlagSet` / `FlagClear` / `FlagGet` — flag read/write/test via bit manipulation → `GameFlags.ts` `setFlag`, `clearFlag`, `getFlag`
- [x] `VarGet` / `VarSet` — variable read/write keyed by ID → `GameVariables.ts` `getVar`, `setVar`
- [x] `GetFlagPointer` — routing between save flags and special (session) flags → implicit in `GameFlags.ts` split between persistent and session storage
- [x] `GetVarPointer` — routing between save vars and special vars (`gSpecialVar_0x8000`–`800B`) → `GameVariables.ts` handles both persistent and special-var slots
- [x] `InitEventData` — zero all flags/vars on new game → called in `NewGameFlow.ts`
- [x] `ClearTempFieldEventData` — clear temp flags/vars on map load → partially handled in `WorldManager.ts` / `loadSelectedOverworldMap.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `VarGetObjectEventGraphicsId` — reads `VAR_OBJ_GFX_ID_0 + id` for dynamic NPC graphics — `DynamicObjectGfx.ts` exists but binding to `VAR_OBJ_GFX_ID_*` may not be fully wired
- [-] `ClearTempFieldEventData` — specific flag clears (`FLAG_SYS_ENC_UP_ITEM`, `FLAG_SYS_USE_STRENGTH`, `FLAG_SYS_CTRL_OBJ_DELETE`, `FLAG_NURSE_UNION_ROOM_REMINDER`) not all guaranteed to be cleared; temp-var range clearing needs verification against `GameVariables.ts`

#### Missing
- [ ] `ClearDailyFlags` — zeroes the daily-flag band in save block; no daily-flag range concept in `GameFlags.ts`
- [ ] `EnableNationalPokedex` / `DisableNationalPokedex` / `IsNationalPokedexEnabled` — National Dex toggle with `nationalMagic`/`VAR_NATIONAL_DEX`/`FLAG_SYS_NATIONAL_DEX`; Pokédex not implemented
- [ ] `EnableMysteryEvent` / `DisableMysteryEvent` / `IsMysteryEventEnabled` — Mystery Event flag helpers; no equivalent
- [ ] `EnableMysteryGift` / `DisableMysteryGift` / `IsMysteryGiftEnabled` — Mystery Gift flag helpers; no equivalent
- [ ] `ClearMysteryGiftFlags` / `ClearMysteryGiftVars` — bulk clear of Mystery Gift flags/vars; no equivalent
- [ ] `EnableResetRTC` / `DisableResetRTC` / `CanResetRTC` — RTC reset gating via flag+var pair; no RTC system

#### Notes
- Special vars (`gSpecialVar_0x8000`–`gSpecialVar_Result`, `gSpecialVar_LastTalked`, `gSpecialVar_Facing`, etc.) are session-scoped in C (EWRAM). `GameVariables.ts` models these as in-memory slots separate from save-block vars — architecturally equivalent.
- Special flags (`sSpecialFlags`) are also session-scoped (not saved). `GameFlags.ts` uses a separate in-memory set for these, matching the C design.
- National Pokédex, Mystery Event/Gift, and RTC reset are post-game or Wi-Fi features intentionally omitted from the MVP browser port.
- `SPECIAL_FLAGS_SIZE`/`TEMP_FLAGS_SIZE`/`DAILY_FLAGS_SIZE` sizing constants are derived from `flagVarMaps.gen.ts` constants — no direct TS equivalent for the byte-range arithmetic, but the ranges are used implicitly when clearing sub-ranges.

## batch: src/event_object_lock.c

### `src/event_object_lock.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/trainers/trainerApproachRuntime.ts`, `src/game/PlayerController.ts`, `src/game/ObjectEventManager.ts`, `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `IsPlayerStandingStill` — checks `tileTransitionState != T_TILE_TRANSITION`; equivalent logic exists in `PlayerController.ts` movement state checks
- [x] `FreezeObjects_WaitForPlayer` — freeze all objects then wait for player to stop; equivalent in `trainerApproachRuntime.ts` freeze-before-dialogue flow
- [x] `FreezeForApproachingTrainers` — freeze all objects except approaching trainer(s), then freeze trainer+player after movement finishes; covered by `trainerApproachRuntime.ts` / `runTrainerSightSequence.ts`
- [x] `ScriptUnfreezeObjectEvents` — unfreeze all objects after script; `ScriptRunner.ts` calls unfreeze on script end
- [x] `Script_FacePlayer` — NPC faces opposite direction of player facing; `ObjectEventManager.ts` handles `faceOppositeDirection` / `faceDirection` for scripted NPCs

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `FreezeObjects_WaitForPlayerAndSelected` — freeze all except selected object and player, then freeze both after movement; trainer approach path is covered, but the generic "selected object + player" freeze for non-trainer scripts (e.g. interaction with moving NPCs) is not explicitly modelled as a task queue
- [-] `IsFreezePlayerFinished` / `IsFreezeSelectedObjectAndPlayerFinished` / `IsFreezeObjectAndPlayerFinished` — C uses task polling; TS uses async/await or promise chains in `trainerApproachRuntime.ts` and `ScriptRunner.ts`, so the polling functions are implicit rather than explicit

#### Missing
- [ ] `Task_FreezePlayer` — explicit deferred-task pattern (wait for player standstill, then freeze); TS handles this inline with async movement awaits rather than a separate task slot
- [ ] `Task_FreezeSelectedObjectAndPlayer` — same deferred-task pattern for a specific selected object event; no direct TS equivalent for the generic selected-object case outside trainer approach
- [ ] `Task_FreezeObjectAndPlayer` — per-object deferred freeze task used for both trainer 1 and trainer 2 in double battles; double-trainer freeze sequencing may not be fully replicated
- [ ] `UnionRoom_UnlockPlayerAndChatPartner` — Union Room specific unlock; Union Room multiplayer is not implemented
- [ ] `Script_ClearHeldMovement` — clears queued movement on the selected object event; no explicit TS equivalent for aborting held movement mid-script

#### Notes
- The C file's core pattern (freeze-world → wait-for-movement → freeze-actor) is architecturally replaced in TS by async/await chains in `trainerApproachRuntime.ts` and `ScriptRunner.ts`, which await movement completion before proceeding rather than registering per-frame tasks. The observable behaviour should be equivalent for the single-trainer case.
- Double-trainer (`gNoOfApproachingTrainers == 2`) parallel freeze tasks may not be fully covered; `trainerApproachRuntime.ts` should be audited for two-trainer tag battles.
- `UnionRoom_UnlockPlayerAndChatPartner` is intentionally omitted — link/Union Room features are out of scope for the browser port.

- [ ] `OverrideSecretBaseDecorationSpriteScript` — secret base decoration script override; Secret Base not implemented

- [ ] `DoGroundEffects_OnSpawn` / `DoGroundEffects_OnBeginStep` / `DoGroundEffects_OnFinishStep` — ground effect trigger dispatch; partially in `StepCallbackManager` but not all effect types
- [ ] `DoTracksGroundEffect_Footprints` / `DoTracksGroundEffect_BikeTireTracks` — footprint/tire track field effect spawns; not ported
- [ ] `DoRippleFieldEffect` — water ripple on step; not ported
- [ ] `DoShadowFieldEffect` — NPC shadow spawn; not ported
- [ ] `ApplyLevitateMovement` / `CreateLevitateMovementTask` / `DestroyLevitateMovementTask` — levitate movement for special NPCs (e.g. Latias/Latios); not ported
- [ ] `VirtualObject_UpdateAnim` / `InitSpriteForFigure8Anim` / `AnimateSpriteInFigure8` — figure-8 animation for virtual objects; not ported
- [ ] `MovementType_Disguise_Callback` / `MovementType_Buried_Callback` — disguise/buried internal callbacks; trainer disguise partially ported but buried callback missing
- [ ] `IsCoordOutsideObjectEventMovementRange` — range boundary check for walk-back-and-forth; logic is inlined in TS movement types
- [ ] `IsMetatileDirectionallyImpassable` — directional passability check using metatile behavior functions; `NPCCollision.ts` handles this but may not use the same direction-blocked metatile function table
- [ ] `NpcTakeStep` / `IsElevationMismatchAt` / `AreElevationsCompatible` — NPC step execution with elevation check; elevation handling in `ObjectEventManager` but not as these exact functions
- [ ] `SetJumpSpriteData` / `DoJumpSpriteMovement` / `DoJumpSpecialSpriteMovement` / `SetWalkSlowSpriteData` / `UpdateWalkSlowAnim` — movement action sprite data helpers; jump arc in `jumpArc.ts` but walk-slow and jump-special not ported
- [ ] `Unref_TryInitLocalObjectEvent` — unreferenced helper to spawn single NPC by localId scan; not ported (unused in C)
- [ ] `gReflectionEffectPaletteMap` — 16-slot palette reflection mapping table; GBA-specific, not needed in browser
- [ ] `sMovementTypeHasRange` / `sMovementDelaysMedium` / `sMovementDelaysLong` / `sMovementDelaysShort` — movement timing constants; partially inlined in TS movement types but not as a shared table
- [ ] `sFaceDirectionAnimNums` / `sMoveDirectionAnimNums` / `sMoveDirectionFast*` / `sJumpSpecialDirectionAnimNums` / `sAcro*DirectionAnimNums` / `sFishing*DirectionAnimNums` / `sRunningDirectionAnimNums` — direction→animation index tables; animation selection handled differently in TS sprite system

#### Notes
- GBA sprite/palette system (OAM, palette slots, affine matrices) has no browser equivalent; all palette-related functions are intentionally omitted
- Camera object (invisible tracking sprite) pattern replaced by `CameraController.ts` direct math
- Virtual object system (Contest/Battle Dome audiences, Union Room) not ported — multiplayer omitted
- Berry tree growth sparkle field effect (`FLDEFF_BERRY_TREE_GROWTH_SPARKLE`) not ported; berry stage graphics are handled
- `MovementType_WalkSequence*` 24 variants covered by a single parameterized `walkSequence.ts`; per-variant wrap-around index resets need verification for full parity
- Trainer Hill and Battle Pyramid object count branches in spawn logic not ported (partially in `trainerHillRuntime.ts`)
- `MAP_OFFSET` (7) is applied in C when reading template coords; TS templates already store content coords, so this offset is not applied — matches the documented lesson in MEMORY.md

## batch: src/event_object_movement.c (part 2/3)

### `src/event_object_movement.c`

**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/npc/NPCMovementEngine.ts`, `src/game/npc/NPCAnimationEngine.ts`, `src/game/npc/NPCCollision.ts`, `src/game/npc/movementTypes/walkSequence.ts`, `src/game/npc/movementTypes/copyPlayer.ts`, `src/game/npc/movementTypes/walkInPlace.ts`, `src/game/PlayerController.ts`, `src/field/ReflectionRenderer.ts`, `src/utils/elevation.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `MovementType_WalkSequence*` (all 16 variants) — directional patrol sequences with axis-flip logic; ported in `walkSequence.ts`
- [x] `MovementType_CopyPlayer_Step0/1/2` — mirror-player NPC movement type; ported in `copyPlayer.ts`
- [x] `MovementType_WalkInPlace` / `WalkSlowlyInPlace` / `JogInPlace` / `RunInPlace` — stationary cycling anims; ported in `walkInPlace.ts`
- [x] `MovementType_Invisible` — invisible NPC movement type; handled in `NPCMovementEngine.ts`
- [x] `GetCollisionAtCoords` — primary collision check (range, impassable, elevation, object); ported in `NPCCollision.ts`
- [x] `IsCoordOutsideObjectEventMovementRange` — movement range bounding box; ported in `NPCCollision.ts`
- [x] `DoesObjectCollideWithObjectAt` — NPC-vs-NPC occupancy check; ported in `NPCCollision.ts`
- [x] `IsElevationMismatchAt` — elevation compatibility check; ported in `utils/elevation.ts`
- [x] `AreElevationsCompatible` — elevation pair check; ported in `utils/elevation.ts`
- [x] `ElevationToPriority` / `ObjectEventUpdateElevation` — elevation-to-render-priority; ported in `utils/elevation.ts`
- [x] `MoveCoords` — single-tile direction offset; ported as direction vector utility
- [x] `ObjectEventMoveDestCoords` — destination tile calculation; ported in `NPCMovementEngine.ts`
- [x] `ObjectEventSetHeldMovement` / `ClearHeldMovement` / `CheckHeldMovementStatus` — scripted movement queue; ported in `NPCMovementEngine.ts`
- [x] `FreezeObjectEvent` / `UnfreezeObjectEvent` / `FreezeObjectEvents` / `UnfreezeObjectEvents` — freeze/thaw NPCs; ported in `NPCMovementEngine.ts`
- [x] `GetOppositeDirection` — direction inversion utility; ported in `utils/direction.ts`
- [x] `GetDirectionToFace` — face-toward-target direction; ported in `NPCMovementEngine.ts`
- [x] `SetTrainerMovementType` — hot-swap movement type on trainer reveal; ported in `trainers/trainerApproachRuntime.ts`
- [x] `MovementAction_FaceDown/Up/Left/Right` — face direction actions; ported as movement action handlers
- [x] `MovementAction_WalkNormal*` / `WalkFast*` / `WalkFaster*` / `Slide*` (all 4 directions each) — walk movement actions; handled in `NPCMovementEngine.ts`
- [x] `MovementAction_WalkInPlace*` (slow/normal/fast/faster, all 4 directions) — in-place walk actions; ported in `walkInPlace.ts`
- [x] `MovementAction_Jump*` / `Jump2*` / `JumpInPlace*` / `JumpSpecial*` (all directions) — jump movement actions; ported in `NPCMovementEngine.ts` and `jumpArc.ts`
- [x] `MovementAction_Delay1/2/4/8/16` — timed wait actions; ported as delay movement actions
- [x] `MovementAction_SetInvisible` / `SetVisible` — visibility toggle actions; ported in `NPCMovementEngine.ts`
- [x] `MovementAction_EmoteExclamationMark` / `EmoteQuestionMark` / `EmoteHeart` — emote field effects; ported in `ScriptFieldEffectAnimationManager.ts`
- [x] `MovementAction_RevealTrainer` — disguise reveal sequence; ported in `trainerDisguise.ts`
- [x] `MovementAction_FacePlayer` / `FaceAwayPlayer` — face toward/away player actions; ported in `NPCMovementEngine.ts`
- [x] `MovementAction_LockFacingDirection` / `UnlockFacingDirection` — direction lock flag; ported in `NPCMovementEngine.ts`
- [x] `GetGroundEffectFlags_Reflection` / `ObjectEventGetNearbyReflectionType` — reflection type detection; ported in `ReflectionRenderer.ts`
- [x] `GroundEffect_WaterReflection` / `IceReflection` — spawn reflections; ported in `ReflectionRenderer.ts`
- [x] `MovementAction_RockSmashBreak` / `CutTree` — obstacle removal animations; ported in `NPCMovementEngine.ts`
- [x] `NpcTakeStep` / `SetSpriteDataForNormalStep` — step timing engine with speed tiers; ported in `NPCMovementEngine.ts`
- [x] `UpdateWalkSlowAnim` / `SetWalkSlowSpriteData` — half-speed walk tick logic; ported in `NPCMovementEngine.ts`
- [x] `GetLedgeJumpDirection` — ledge-jump metatile behavior check; ported in `StepCallbackManager.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `MovementType_TreeDisguise` / `MountainDisguise` — disguise spawn logic exists in `trainerDisguise.ts` but field effect sprite creation (`FieldEffectStart(FLDEFF_TREE_DISGUISE)`) is stubbed
- [-] `MovementType_Buried` — buried trainer type partially handled in `trainerDisguise.ts`; sprite priority/subsprite mode overrides not fully applied
- [-] `CopyablePlayerMovement_WalkNormal` — Faraway Island Mew special case (`GetMewMoveDirection`, `IsFarawayIslandMew`) ported in `farawayMew.ts` but tile callback (`tileCallback`) integration is incomplete
- [-] `MovementType_CopyPlayerInGrass` — exists as variant of CopyPlayer but `MetatileBehavior_IsPokeGrass` tile callback not passed through in TS
- [-] `GetGroundEffectFlags_*` (all 12 flag collectors) — grass/sand/water/puddle/seaweed flags exist in `StepCallbackManager.ts` but not as unified flag-bitmask pattern matching C; hot springs, sand pile, shallow water logic partial
- [-] `DoGroundEffects_OnSpawn/OnBeginStep/OnFinishStep` — step callback triggers exist in `StepCallbackManager.ts` but ground effect flag system is not a direct port; some effects missing
- [-] `GroundEffect_SandTracks` / `DeepSandTracks` / `Ripple` / `StepOnPuddle` / `SandHeap` — field effect triggers partially ported; visual effects may be stubbed
- [-] `UpdateObjectEventElevationAndPriority` / `SetObjectSubpriorityByElevation` — elevation→subpriority math present in `elevationPriority.ts` but pixel-level subpriority formula may differ
- [-] Acro bike movement actions (`AcroWheelieHop*`, `AcroWheelieJump*`, `AcroPopWheelie*`, `AcroEndWheelie*`, `AcroWheelieMove*`, `AcroWheelieInPlace*`) — bike riding partially covered in `PlayerController.bikeParity.test.ts` but acro-specific NPC actions are likely unimplemented

- [ ] `MovementAction_Figure8` / `InitFigure8Anim` / `DoFigure8Anim` — figure-8 Rayquaza/special NPC animation pattern
- [ ] `AnimateSpriteInFigure8` / `GetFigure8XOffset` / `GetFigure8YOffset` / offset tables — figure-8 pixel offset math
- [ ] `MovementAction_Levitate` / `StopLevitate` / `StopLevitateAtTop` — levitation task for floating NPCs (e.g. Latias/Latios)
- [ ] `MovementAction_NurseJoyBowDown` — Nurse Joy bow animation action
- [ ] `MovementAction_InitAffineAnim` / `ClearAffineAnim` — OAM affine matrix setup/teardown for rotating NPCs
- [ ] `MovementAction_HideReflection` / `ShowReflection` — per-NPC reflection toggle actions
- [ ] `MovementAction_WalkDownStartAffine` / `WalkDownAffine` / `WalkLeftAffine` / `WalkRightAffine` — affine-animated walk (used for S.S. Tidal/truck scenes)
- [ ] `MovementAction_AcroWheelieFace*` / `AcroPopWheelie*` / `AcroEndWheelieFace*` — acro bike face/transition actions for NPC scripted sequences
- [ ] `MovementAction_RideWaterCurrent*` (4 directions) — water current movement speed tier
- [ ] `MovementAction_EnableJumpLandingGroundEffect` / `DisableJumpLandingGroundEffect` — jump landing effect flag toggle
- [ ] `MovementAction_DisableAnimation` / `RestoreAnimation` — inanimate flag toggle actions
- [ ] `MovementAction_SetFixedPriority` / `ClearFixedPriority` — render priority lock actions
- [ ] `MovementAction_FaceOriginalDirection` — face initial spawn direction action
- [ ] `MovementAction_StartAnimInDirection` / `WaitSpriteAnim` / `PauseSpriteAnim` — sprite animation control actions
- [ ] `MovementAction_PlayerRun*` (4 directions) — player-run NPC scripted movement
- [ ] `GetCollisionFlagsAtCoords` — bitfield variant of collision check (used for trainer sight)
- [ ] `SetObjectEventSpriteOamTableForLongGrass` — long grass subsprite table override
- [ ] `GetGroundEffectFlags_JumpLanding` — jump landing ground effect dispatch table
- [ ] `DoTracksGroundEffect_BikeTireTracks` — bike tire track transition table logic
- [ ] `GroundEffect_JumpOnTallGrass` / `JumpOnLongGrass` / `JumpOnShallowWater` / `JumpOnWater` / `JumpLandingDust` — jump-landing field effects
- [ ] `GroundEffect_Seaweed` / `FlowingWater` / `ShortGrass` / `HotSprings` — remaining step-on ground effect triggers
- [ ] `UpdateObjectEventOffscreen` — off-screen culling flag logic
- [ ] `filters_out_some_ground_effects` / `FilterOutStepOnPuddleGroundEffectIfJumping` — ground effect filter passes
- [ ] `IsBerryTreeSparkling` / `SetBerryTreeJustPicked` — berry tree sprite flag accessors
- [ ] `GetMapCoordsFromSpritePos` / `SetSpritePosToMapCoords` / `SetSpritePosToOffsetMapCoords` — GBA sprite-pixel ↔ map-tile coordinate transforms (browser uses CSS/canvas transforms instead)
- [ ] `GetObjectEventMovingCameraOffset` — sub-tile camera scroll offset for NPC pixel positioning
- [ ] `GetCopyDirection` / `GetPlayerDirectionForCopy` — mirror-player direction remapping table logic (partially implied by `copyPlayer.ts` but tables not verified)
- [ ] `sJumpY_High` / `sJumpY_Low` / `sJumpY_Normal` jump arc tables — per-frame Y offsets for jump arcs (TS uses `jumpArc.ts` which may approximate rather than match exactly)

#### Notes
- The GBA sprite coordinate system (`sprite->x`, `sprite->y`, `sprite->x2`, `sprite->y2`, `sprite->subpriority`, OAM matrices) has no direct browser equivalent; the TS port uses CSS/canvas pixel coordinates and z-index/WebGL ordering instead. All sprite positioning functions (`GetMapCoordsFromSpritePos`, `SetSpritePosToMapCoords`, etc.) are architecturally replaced.
- `sNpcStepFuncTables` / `NpcTakeStep` step-timing engine (MOVE_SPEED_NORMAL=16 frames, FAST_1=8, etc.) is the critical NPC movement interpolation loop — must be verified against `NPCMovementEngine.ts` for exact frame counts.
- Acro bike NPC actions are only relevant for scripted sequences (mostly unused in normal play); deprioritized.
- Figure-8 animation is used for Rayquaza in Sky Pillar cutscene — needed for story completion.
- Link cable / Union Room / Record Corner movement types are intentionally omitted (no multiplayer).
- `BUGFIX` preprocessor guards exclude camera object from ground effects — TS should replicate this.

## batch: src/event_object_movement.c (part 3/3)

### `src/event_object_movement.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/npc/NPCAnimationEngine.ts`, `src/game/npc/NPCMovementEngine.ts`, `src/game/npc/ObjectEventAffineManager.ts`, `src/game/jumpArc.ts`, `src/game/ObjectEventManager.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `GetJumpY` — jump arc Y-offset lookup via table; ported in `src/game/jumpArc.ts`
- [x] `DoJumpSpriteMovement` — normal-speed jump movement (16/32 frame timings); ported in `src/game/jumpArc.ts` / `NPCMovementEngine.ts`
- [x] `DoJumpSpecialSpriteMovement` — half-speed jump variant (32/64 frame timings); covered in jump arc logic
- [x] `SetMovementDelay` / `WaitForMovementDelay` — per-sprite delay counter; ported as movement step delay in `NPCMovementEngine.ts`
- [x] `FreezeObjectEventsExceptTwo` — freeze all NPCs except two trainers; ported in `ObjectEventManager.ts` (trainer approach freeze)
- [x] `MovementAction_FlyUp_Step0/Step1` / `MovementAction_FlyDown_Step0/Step1` / `MovementAction_Fly_Finish` — fly-in/fly-out sprite Y2 animation (8px/frame); ported in `NPCMovementEngine.ts` movement actions
- [x] `MovementAction_LockAnim_Step0` — locks NPC animation (inanimate + facingDirectionLocked); ported in `ObjectEventManager.ts`
- [x] `MovementAction_UnlockAnim_Step0` / `FindLockedObjectEventIndex` — unlocks NPC animation; ported in `ObjectEventManager.ts`
- [x] `UpdateObjectEventSpriteInvisibility` — culls sprites outside display bounds; equivalent bounds-check in `NPCRenderer.ts`
- [x] `StartFieldEffectForObjectEvent` — triggers field effect for an NPC; ported in `FieldEffectManager.ts`
- [x] `DoShadowFieldEffect` — spawns shadow field effect once per NPC; ported in `NPCMovementEngine.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `DoRippleFieldEffect` — ripple effect when NPC walks on water; field effect framework exists but ripple spawn coordinates (graphicsInfo height offset) may not match C exactly
- [-] `SetAndStartSpriteAnim` / `SpriteAnimEnded` — sprite animation control; covered generically in `NPCAnimationEngine.ts` but GBA sprite anim index semantics differ from TS animation state machine
- [-] `TurnVirtualObject` — sets facing-direction animation on a virtual object sprite; virtual objects partially supported via `ObjectEventAffineManager.ts` but full virtual-object registry is not present
- [-] `SetVirtualObjectGraphics` — swaps OAM graphics/palette for a virtual object; no direct equivalent; virtual object gfx swapping not fully implemented in TS
- [-] `SetVirtualObjectInvisibility` / `IsVirtualObjectInvisible` — per-virtual-object invisible flag; partially covered by NPC hide/show but virtual object id registry absent
- [-] `SetVirtualObjectSpriteAnim` / `IsVirtualObjectAnimating` — Union Room slide-in/slide-out animation state; `VirtualObject_UpdateAnim` / `MoveUnionRoomObjectDown` / `MoveUnionRoomObjectUp` not implemented

#### Missing
- [ ] `CreateLevitateMovementTask` / `ApplyLevitateMovement` / `DestroyLevitateMovementTask` — task-based levitation (y2 oscillation ±1 every 4 frames, flip every 16 frames); no TS equivalent
- [ ] `SpriteCB_VirtualObject` / `VirtualObject_UpdateAnim` / `MoveUnionRoomObjectUp` / `MoveUnionRoomObjectDown` — Union Room virtual NPC spawn-in/spawn-out slide animation; no TS equivalent
- [ ] `GetVirtualObjectSpriteId` / `DestroyVirtualObjects` — virtual object sprite registry search; no TS equivalent

#### Notes
- Virtual object system (Union Room multiplayer avatars) is intentionally low-priority — link/Union Room is post-game and requires multiplayer infrastructure not present in the browser port.
- Levitate movement is used by a small number of NPCs (e.g. Latias/Latios, certain legendaries); not yet ported.
- GBA-specific sprite fields (`oam.tileNum`, `subspriteTables`, `coordOffsetEnabled`, `gSpriteCoordOffsetX/Y`) have no direct TS equivalent; the port handles sprite positioning through canvas/WebGL coordinate math instead.
- `sLockedAnimObjectEvents` global allocation pattern has been replaced with a simple Set/Map in `ObjectEventManager.ts`.

## batch: src/evolution_graphics.c

### `src/evolution_graphics.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/evolution/EvolutionRenderer.ts`, `src/evolution/types.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `CycleEvolutionMonSprite` — alternating grow/shrink animation between pre/post-evo sprites; logic present in `EvolutionRenderer.ts`
- [x] `EndOnPostEvoMon` / `EndOnPreEvoMon` — finalize which sprite remains visible after cycling

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `SpriteCB_Sparkle_SpiralUpward` / `CreateSparkle_SpiralUpward` — spiral-upward sparkle particle; no direct port found, `EvolutionRenderer.ts` likely has simplified particle rendering without the trig-based spiral path
- [-] `SpriteCB_Sparkle_ArcDown` / `CreateSparkle_ArcDown` — arc-down sparkle; same as above, trig math not ported
- [-] `SpriteCB_Sparkle_CircleInward` / `CreateSparkle_CircleInward` — inward-circle sparkle; same as above
- [-] `SpriteCB_Sparkle_Spray` / `CreateSparkle_Spray` — spray-and-flash sparkle; same as above

#### Missing
- [ ] `LoadEvoSparkleSpriteAndPal` — loads compressed sparkle sprite sheet and palette; GBA-specific but the asset-loading equivalent is absent
- [ ] `EvolutionSparkles_SpiralUpward` — task entry point with palette fade (white flash) for spiral phase
- [ ] `Task_Sparkles_SpiralUpward_Init` / `Task_Sparkles_SpiralUpward` / `Task_Sparkles_SpiralUpward_End` — full task lifecycle for spiral sparkles including `BeginNormalPaletteFade` and SE_M_MEGA_KICK sound cue
- [ ] `EvolutionSparkles_ArcDown` — task entry point for arc-down sparkle phase with SE_M_BUBBLE_BEAM2
- [ ] `Task_Sparkles_ArcDown_Init` / `Task_Sparkles_ArcDown` / `Task_Sparkles_ArcDown_End`
- [ ] `EvolutionSparkles_CircleInward` — task entry point for inward-circle phase with SE_SHINY
- [ ] `Task_Sparkles_CircleInward_Init` / `Task_Sparkles_CircleInward` / `Task_Sparkles_CircleInward_End`
- [ ] `EvolutionSparkles_SprayAndFlash` — task entry point for spray+flash phase with palette fade to white
- [ ] `Task_Sparkles_SprayAndFlash_Init` / `Task_Sparkles_SprayAndFlash` / `Task_Sparkles_SprayAndFlash_End`
- [ ] `EvolutionSparkles_SprayAndFlash_Trade` — trade-evolution variant with different fade color mask
- [ ] `Task_Sparkles_SprayAndFlashTrade_Init` / `Task_Sparkles_SprayAndFlashTrade`
- [ ] `SetEvoSparklesMatrices` — sets 12 OAM affine matrices for sparkle scaling; browser equivalent would be CSS/WebGL transform setup
- [ ] `Task_CycleEvolutionMonSprite_Init` / `Task_CycleEvolutionMonSprite_TryEnd` / `Task_CycleEvolutionMonSprite_UpdateSize` — full task state machine for the grow/shrink cycling with escalating speed

#### Notes
- The GBA task system (`CreateTask`, `gTasks`, `DestroyTask`) maps conceptually to animation frame callbacks in the TS port; `EvolutionRenderer.ts` likely has a simplified version of the cycling animation without the four distinct sparkle effect phases.
- OAM affine matrix manipulation (`SetOamMatrix`) has no direct browser equivalent; the TS port would use CSS transforms or WebGL matrix uniforms for the scale animation.
- Palette fade (`BeginNormalPaletteFade`) maps to the fade system in `src/field/FadeController.ts` but the evolution-specific white flash sequences are not wired up.
- Sound effects (`PlaySE`) are noted as missing in project memory — all four sparkle sound cues are unimplemented.
- Trade evolution sparkle variant (`SprayAndFlash_Trade`) uses a different palette fade mask; both variants are unported.

## batch: src/evolution_scene.c

### `src/evolution_scene.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/states/EvolutionState.ts`, `src/evolution/EvolutionRenderer.ts`, `src/evolution/types.ts`, `src/pokemon/moveLearning.ts`, `src/pokemon/moveLearningPromptAdapter.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `BeginEvolutionScene` / `EvolutionScene` entry points — `src/states/EvolutionState.ts` handles the state-machine entry
- [x] `Task_EvolutionScene` (core evolution state machine) — ported as state machine in `EvolutionState.ts`; states FADE_IN through SET_MON_EVOLVED covered
- [x] `EVOSTATE_SET_MON_EVOLVED` logic — `SetMonData` species update, `CalculateMonStats`, Pokédex flags, game stat increment all mapped in TS evolution flow
- [x] `EVOSTATE_TRY_LEARN_MOVE` / `EVOSTATE_LEARNED_MOVE` — `src/pokemon/moveLearning.ts` (`MonTryLearningNewMove` equivalent), `src/pokemon/moveLearningPromptAdapter.ts` handles the prompt flow
- [x] `CreateShedinja` — Ninjask→Shedinja side-evolution logic ported in `src/pokemon/evolution.ts`
- [x] `EvolutionRenameMon` call — handled in TS evolution flow
- [x] `EvoScene_DoMonAnimAndCry` / `EvoScene_IsMonAnimFinished` — sprite animation and cry callbacks implemented in `EvolutionRenderer.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `EVOSTATE_REPLACE_MOVE` (full move-replace sub-state machine, MVSTATE_*) — move forgetting with Yes/No, move summary screen, HM block, "1, 2, and... Poof!" messages are wired via `src/menu/moves/openMoveForgetMenu.ts` and `MoveForgetMenuContent.tsx`, but the exact MVSTATE step sequencing (RETRY_AFTER_HM, CANCEL path, ellipsis message before returning to EVOSTATE_LEARNED_MOVE) may not be fully parity-matched
- [-] `StartBgAnimation` / `Task_AnimateBg` / `Task_UpdateBgPalette` — the revolving dual-layer BG with palette cycling (`sBgAnim_PaletteControl`, `sBgAnim_PalIndexes`) is not ported; `EvolutionRenderer.ts` has a simpler CSS/canvas flash effect instead of the GBA tilemap scroll + blend animation
- [-] `StopBgAnimation` / `RestoreBgAfterAnim` — no TS equivalent for BG teardown; irrelevant in WebGL but the timing-coupled palette restore is not replicated

#### Missing
- [ ] `TradeEvolutionScene` / `Task_TradeEvolutionScene` — full trade-evolution scene with link cable window (`DrawTextOnTradeWindow`), wireless indicator sprite, trade BG setup; entirely omitted (link play not in scope)
- [ ] `CB2_TradeEvolutionSceneLoadGraphics` / `CB2_TradeEvolutionSceneUpdate` — trade scene graphics reload loop; omitted
- [ ] `CB2_EvolutionSceneLoadGraphics` — handles returning from move-select summary screen back into the evolution scene by reloading GBA VRAM; TS handles this differently (no VRAM teardown needed) but the palette fade re-entry path may differ
- [ ] `VBlankCB_EvolutionScene` / `VBlankCB_TradeEvolutionScene` — GBA VBlank register writes; intentionally omitted (hardware-specific)
- [ ] `EvoDummyFunc` — GBA HBlank dummy callback; intentionally omitted
- [ ] `InitMovingBgPalette` — builds the `sBgAnim_PalIndexes`-indexed palette array; no TS equivalent (BG animation not ported)
- [ ] `PauseBgPaletteAnim` (UNUSED in C) — not ported
- [ ] `sBgAnim_Gfx` / `sBgAnim_Inner_Tilemap` / `sBgAnim_Outer_Tilemap` / `sBgAnim_Pal` / `sBgAnim_Intro_Pal` — evolution BG tilemap and palette binary assets; not loaded in browser port

#### Notes
- Trade evolution (`TradeEvolutionScene`, `Task_TradeEvolutionScene`, all `T_EVOSTATE_*` / `T_MVSTATE_*` states) is intentionally omitted — link cable/wireless multiplayer is out of scope for this browser port.
- The GBA background animation (two scrolling tilesets with blend registers + a multi-stage palette cycling task) has no direct equivalent; the TS port uses a simpler visual effect in `EvolutionRenderer.ts`. The gameplay logic (state transitions, timing) is what matters for parity.
- `sEvoGraphicsTaskId` / `sEvoCursorPos` are GBA task-index globals with no direct TS counterpart; the TS state machine inlines this coordination.
- The `EVOSTATE_REPLACE_MOVE` sub-machine integrates with `ShowSelectMovePokemonSummaryScreen` (a full screen switch in C); in TS this is handled via the menu overlay system in `MoveForgetMenuContent.tsx` without a full callback2 swap, so the re-entry path differs architecturally but achieves the same result.

## batch: src/faraway_island.c

### `src/faraway_island.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/legendary/farawayMew.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `GetMewMoveDirection` — full hide-and-seek AI: rock trap avoidance, visibility toggle every 8 steps, stay-put on 9th step, directional candidate selection with randomization
- [x] `CanMewMoveToCoords` — checks PokeGrass metatile and player occupancy
- [x] `GetValidMewMoveDirection` — last-resort any-valid-direction fallback using step counter modulo
- [x] `ShouldMewMoveNorth/South/East/West` — directional candidate helpers
- [x] `GetRandomMewDirectionCandidate` — picks from candidates using step counter
- [x] `UpdateFarawayIslandStepCounter` — increments VAR_FARAWAY_ISLAND_STEP_COUNTER, wraps at 9999, guarded to interior map
- [x] `IsMewPlayingHideAndSeek` — checks map, FLAG_CAUGHT_MEW, FLAG_HIDE_MEW
- [x] `ObjectEventIsFarawayIslandMew` — checks map + graphicsId == MEW
- [x] `ShouldMewShakeGrass` — every 4th step condition

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `SetMewAboveGrass` — Mew visibility/priority manipulation ported; the grass-shake sprite creation (`CreateSpriteAtEnd`, `sGrassSpriteId`, `gFieldEffectObjectTemplatePointers[FLDEFFOBJ_LONG_GRASS]`) likely not ported — browser has no direct sprite layer priority equivalent and field effect sprites are not wired the same way
- [-] `DestroyMewEmergingGrassSprite` — may be no-op in TS if the grass sprite creation in `SetMewAboveGrass` is not implemented

#### Missing
- [ ] `sFarawayIslandRockCoords` MAP_OFFSET baked-in coords — the C source uses coords with `MAP_OFFSET` added (e.g. `14 + MAP_OFFSET`); per project lessons these must be stored as content coords (subtract 7) in TS; needs audit that `farawayMew.ts` correctly stores `{7, 2}`, `{11, 2}`, `{2, 3}`, `{6, 6}` (after subtracting MAP_OFFSET=7)

#### Notes
- `sGrassSpriteId` / grass-shaking field effect sprite is GBA-specific (sprite OAM priority, `subspriteMode`, `coordOffsetEnabled`); browser equivalent would need a CSS/canvas overlay, likely intentionally simplified or omitted.
- `gSpecialVar_0x8004` / `gSpecialVar_Facing` used in `SetMewAboveGrass` to distinguish post-battle-ran vs. first-emerge; the TS port would need to thread these through the script special call context.
- `src/scripting/specials/legendaryIslandSpecials.ts` and `src/game/legendary/__tests__/farawayMew.test.ts` confirm the core AI is ported and tested.

## batch: src/field_camera.c

### `src/field_camera.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/CameraController.ts`, `src/rendering/TileRenderer.ts`, `src/rendering/webgl/WebGLTileRenderer.ts`, `src/game/buildWorldCameraView.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `ResetFieldCamera` / `ResetCameraUpdateInfo` — camera state reset on map load, handled in `CameraController`
- [x] `CameraUpdate` — per-frame tile offset accumulation and delta tile scroll, mirrored in `CameraController` update loop
- [x] `GetCameraOffsetWithPan` — pixel offset + pan exposed for sprite coord offset calculation, equivalent in `buildWorldCameraView.ts`
- [x] `DrawWholeMapView` — full viewport tile redraw on map load; TS redraws the full WebGL tilemap in `TileRenderer`/`WebGLTileRenderer`
- [x] `DrawMetatile` (SPLIT/COVERED/NORMAL layer routing) — 3-pass BG layer assignment is the core of the WebGL 3-pass tile rendering pipeline (`WebGLPassRenderer`, `LayerRenderer`, `TileInstanceBuilder`)
- [x] `UpdateCameraPanning` — `gSpriteCoordOffsetX/Y` update maps to sprite coordinate offset applied in `renderOverworldSprites.ts`
- [x] `SetCameraPanning` / `InstallCameraPanAheadCallback` — camera pan state exists in `CameraController`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `CameraUpdate` delta-tile logic with `UpdateObjectEventsForCameraUpdate`, `RotatingGatePuzzleCameraUpdate`, `SetBerryTreesSeen` — tile delta triggers NPC/object updates and berry tree visibility; NPC side handled in `ObjectEventManager`, but the exact per-tile-scroll hook sequence coupling is looser than C
- [-] `RedrawMapSliceNorth/South/East/West` — incremental single-row/column redraw on scroll; TS WebGL pipeline redraws full visible region rather than dirty slices (handled by `DirtyRegionTracker` / `ViewportBuffer` but not with the same toroidal 32×32 BG tilemap ring-buffer semantics)
- [-] `MapPosToBgTilemapOffset` — toroidal tilemap offset math specific to the GBA 32×32 BG tilemap ring buffer; not needed in TS (canvas/WebGL has no such constraint), but the concept of "is tile in viewport?" is reproduced in viewport culling logic

#### Missing
- [ ] `FieldUpdateBgTilemapScroll` — GBA BG scroll register writes (REG_BG1HOFS etc.); hardware-only, intentionally omitted
- [ ] `DrawDoorMetatileAt` — targeted single-metatile redraw for door animation tiles; door rendering exists (`DoorRenderer.ts`, `DoorSequencer.ts`) but doesn't replicate the BG tilemap offset write path
- [ ] `CurrentMapDrawMetatileAt` — single-tile dirty redraw by map position; TS triggers full region redraws rather than single-tile patches
- [ ] `CameraPanningCB_PanAhead` — bike-ahead camera pan (pan ahead of player on bike); `gUnusedBikeCameraAheadPanback` is always FALSE so this code is dead in vanilla Emerald; not ported
- [ ] `MoveCameraAndRedrawMap` — marked unused in C source; not ported
- [ ] `InitCameraUpdateCallback` / `CameraUpdateCallback` — GBA sprite-based camera tracking object with `AddCameraObject`; TS camera follows player position directly without a sprite proxy

#### Notes
- The GBA tilemap is a 32×32 toroidal ring buffer in VRAM; the TS port uses a flat WebGL framebuffer so the `xTileOffset`/`yTileOffset` modulo-32 wrapping logic and `RedrawMapSlice*` incremental updates have no direct equivalent and are intentionally replaced by viewport-aware full redraws.
- `gTotalCameraPixelOffsetX/Y` and `gSpriteCoordOffsetX/Y` are tracked in `CameraController` and fed into sprite rendering to keep sprites aligned with the scrolling map.
- `sVerticalCameraPan` has a constant +32 bias in C (`SetCameraPanning` adds 32, `FieldUpdateBgTilemapScroll` adds 8) accounting for GBA status bar; the TS port has no status bar offset to compensate for.
- Link-cable (`SIO`) and e-Reader dependencies: none in this file.

**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `FieldClearPlayerInput` — input struct zeroing; equivalent in PlayerController input reset each frame
- [x] `FieldGetPlayerInput` — maps held/pressed keys to semantic input flags; covered by `PlayerController` + `useInput` hook
- [x] `ProcessPlayerFieldInput` — main per-frame input dispatch; covered by `overworldGameUpdate.ts` and `PlayerController`
- [x] `GetPlayerPosition` — player dest coords + elevation; covered by `playerCoords.ts` helpers
- [x] `GetInFrontOfPlayerPosition` — one-step-forward position with elevation; covered in `PlayerController` interaction logic
- [x] `GetPlayerCurMetatileBehavior` — metatile behavior at player dest coords; covered in `metatileBehaviors.ts` + controllers
- [x] `TryStartInteractionScript` — A-button NPC/tile interaction dispatch; covered by `FieldActionResolver.ts`
- [x] `GetInteractionScript` — priority chain: object → bg → metatile → water; covered by `FieldActionResolver.ts`
- [x] `GetInteractedObjectEventScript` — NPC lookup at position with counter support; covered by `ObjectEventManager` interaction
- [x] `GetInteractedBackgroundEventScript` — bg event dispatch including hidden items; covered by `mapEventLoader.ts` + ScriptRunner bg event handling
- [x] `TryArrowWarp` — arrow warp metatile detection and warp trigger; covered by `WarpTriggerProcessor.ts` + `PlayerController.arrowWarpInput` tests
- [x] `TryStartWarpEventScript` — warp metatile dispatch including escalator/Lavaridge/Aqua/Mossdeep variants; covered by `WarpTriggerProcessor.ts`, `LavaridgeWarpSequencer.ts`, `RotatingGateManager.ts`
- [x] `TryDoorWarp` — north-facing door warp with animation; covered by `DoorActionDispatcher.ts`, `DoorSequencer.ts`
- [x] `TryStartCoordEventScript` — coord event lookup and script setup; covered by `mapEventLoader.ts` `runMapEntryScripts.ts`
- [x] `TryStartMiscWalkingScripts` — cracked floor, Battle Pyramid warp; covered by `StepCallbackManager` tests (bridgeAndCrackedFloor)
- [x] `TryStartStepBasedScript` — orchestrates all step-based checks; covered by `StepCallbackManager.ts`
- [x] `CheckStandardWildEncounter` — immunity step counter + delegate; covered by `wildEncounterService.ts` + `overworldWildEncounterFlow.ts`
- [x] `UpdateFriendshipStepCounter` — every 128 steps adjust friendship; covered by `StepCallbackManager.ts`
- [x] `UpdatePoisonStepCounter` — every 4 steps trigger poison field effect; covered by `StepCallbackManager.ts`
- [x] `GetWarpEventAtPosition` — linear scan of warp events by x/y/elevation; covered by `WarpTriggerProcessor.ts`
- [x] `GetWarpEventAtMapPosition` — MAP_OFFSET-adjusted wrapper; covered in warp processors
- [x] `SetupWarp` — resolves warp destination including dynamic warp and TrainerHill; covered by `WarpExecutor.ts`, `DynamicWarp.ts`, `trainerHillRuntime.ts`
- [x] `TryDoDiveWarp` — auto-dive warp on movement step (underwater emerge or dive down); covered by `DiveWarpResolver.ts`, `executeDiveFieldAction.ts`
- [x] `TrySetDiveWarp` — checks diveable tile and sets warp; covered by `DiveWarpResolver.ts`
- [x] `GetCoordEventScriptAtPosition` — scans coord events; covered by `mapEventLoader.ts`
- [x] `TryRunCoordEventScript` — var-gated coord event execution; covered by `ScriptRunner` coord event handling
- [x] `GetBackgroundEventAtPosition` — scans bg events by position; covered by `mapEventLoader.ts`
- [x] `IsWarpMetatileBehavior` — checks all warp-type metatile behaviors; covered by `metatileBehaviors.ts`
- [x] `IsArrowWarpMetatileBehavior` — directional arrow warp check; covered by `PlayerController.arrowWarpInput` tests
- [x] `RestartWildEncounterImmunitySteps` — resets 4-step immunity counter; covered by `wildEncounterService.ts`
- [x] `ClearPoisonStepCounter` — resets VAR_POISON_STEP_COUNTER; covered by `StepCallbackManager`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `GetInteractedMetatileScript` — metatile-based interaction scripts (TV, PC, bookshelves, vase, trash, etc.) are partially handled; Secret Base decoration interactions (`CheckInteractedWithFriendsFurniture*`, `SecretBase_EventScript_*`) and wireless/cable box results are not implemented (Secret Base, link features intentionally omitted)
- [-] `GetInteractedWaterScript` — Surf prompt and Waterfall prompt covered; badge-check logic may not fully gate on badge flags in all paths
- [-] `TryStartStepCountScript` — core step callbacks covered but several match-call triggers (`ShouldDoWallyCall`, `ShouldDoScottFortreeCall`, `ShouldDoScottBattleFrontierCall`, `ShouldDoRoxanneCall`, `ShouldDoRivalRayquazaCall`) and `SafariZoneTakeStep`, `CountSSTidalStep`, `TryStartMatchCall` are stub/missing
- [-] `TrySetupDiveDownScript` / `TrySetupDiveEmergeScript` — dive scripting covered but badge-07 flag gate may be partial
- [-] `GetObjectEventScriptPointerPlayerFacing` — used by script commands; partially wired in `ScriptRunner` but not fully exposed as standalone utility

#### Missing
- [ ] `GetInteractedLinkPlayerScript` — link player object event interaction (link cable multiplayer; intentionally omitted)
- [ ] `SetCableClubWarp` — cable club warp setup (link cable; intentionally omitted)
- [ ] `TryStartInteractionScript` sound exclusion list — SE_SELECT suppression for specific PC/SecretBase scripts not replicated

#### Notes
- The C file is the central "field loop" dispatcher; the TS port spreads this across multiple files (`PlayerController`, `overworldGameUpdate`, `WarpTriggerProcessor`, `StepCallbackManager`, `FieldActionResolver`) following a more modular architecture.
- Link cable features (`GetInteractedLinkPlayerScript`, `SetCableClubWarp`, Union Room warp, Record Mixing PC) are intentionally omitted — no multiplayer in the browser port.
- Secret Base decoration interaction callbacks (`CheckInteractedWithFriendsFurnitureBottom/Middle/Top`, `CheckInteractedWithFriendsPosterDecor`) are not ported; Secret Base is post-game content.
- Match call system (`TryStartMatchCall`, `ShouldDoWallyCall`, etc.) is scripted-event infrastructure not yet implemented.
- Safari Zone step counter (`SafariZoneTakeStep`) and SS Tidal step counter (`CountSSTidalStep`) are not ported.
- MAP_OFFSET subtraction when looking up events is correctly handled per the documented "MAP_OFFSET trap" in project memory.

- [x] `FieldAnimateDoorClose` — triggers close animation; covered by `DoorSequencer`/`DoorSequenceRunner`

- [x] `FieldSetDoorClosed` — immediately draws closed state; covered by `DoorActionDispatcher`

- [x] `sDoorAnimGraphicsTable` — metatile → graphics/sound/size mapping; mirrored in `src/data/doorAssets.ts`

- [x] `sBigDoorOpenAnimFrames` / `sBigDoorCloseAnimFrames` — 2-wide door frames; reproduced in `DoorSequencer`
- [x] `GetDoorGraphics` — metatile lookup in graphics table; equivalent lookup in `doorAssets`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `DrawCurrentDoorAnimFrame` / `BuildDoorTiles` — GBA VRAM tile-blitting logic not applicable; TS equivalent redraws door sprite frames via `DoorRenderer` using canvas/WebGL, but the per-tile palette remapping (8-palette-slot assignment per frame) may not be fully replicated
- [-] `ShouldUseMultiCorridorDoor` — Battle Tower multi-corridor dual-door sync; `DoorSequencer` likely does not implement the second door draw at `gSpecialVar_0x8004/8005` coordinates

#### Missing
- [ ] `CopyDoorTilesToVram` — GBA-specific VRAM DMA copy; intentionally omitted (hardware-specific), TS uses sprite sheet frames instead
- [ ] `Task_AnimateDoor` / `AnimateDoorFrame` — GBA task-system frame ticker; TS replaces with `requestAnimationFrame`-driven sequencer, but the exact per-frame counter logic (`tCounter`, `tFrameId`) may not be structurally equivalent
- [ ] `DrawDoor` (multi-corridor branch) — second door drawn at special-var coordinates for Battle Tower multi-corridor; not ported
- [ ] `DrawClosedDoorTiles` / `DrawOpenedDoor` — direct `CurrentMapDrawMetatileAt` / `MapGridGetMetatileIdAt` calls for restoring underlying metatile; TS equivalent redraws from map data but the exact metatile restore path is unclear

#### Notes
- All door tile binary assets (`INCBIN_U8`) and null palettes are purely GBA VRAM data; TS uses pre-sliced sprite sheet PNGs in `public/graphics/door_anims/`.
- The `DOOR_SOUND_ARENA` → `SE_REPEL` mapping is an Emerald quirk (Battle Arena door reuses the Repel sound); this should be preserved in `doorAssets.ts`.
- `ShouldUseMultiCorridorDoor` is Battle Frontier post-game content (multi-battle corridor) — low priority, likely intentionally deferred.
- `Debug_FieldAnimateDoorOpen` (marked `UNUSED`) is not ported and not needed.
