- [-] `StartEscapeRopeFieldEffect` / `Task_EscapeRopeWarpOut` + `Task_EscapeRopeWarpIn` + spin substeps — warp destination set in `src/game/FixedEscapeWarp.ts`; player spin animation missing
- [-] `FldEff_TeleportWarpOut` / `Task_TeleportWarpOut` + `Task_TeleportWarpIn` + spin/rise substeps — warp destination (`SetWarpDestinationToLastHealLocation`) handled in `src/game/WarpExecutor.ts`; teleport spin-and-rise sprite animation not implemented
- [-] `FldEff_UseWaterfall` / `Task_UseWaterfall` + 5 substeps — waterfall climb exists as metatile behavior traversal in `src/game/PlayerController.ts`; show-mon banner and walk-slow-climb sequence not implemented

#### Missing
- [ ] `FldEff_HallOfFameRecord` / `Task_HallOfFameRecord` + HOF monitor substeps — Hall of Fame recording effect with glowing pokeballs and flickering monitor sprites
- [ ] `FldEff_FieldMoveShowMon` / `FldEff_FieldMoveShowMonInit` — outdoor/indoor "show mon during field move" banner effect (streaking background + mon slide-in)
- [ ] `Task_FieldMoveShowMonOutdoors` + 7 substeps — outdoor banner: window registers, streak tilemap, mon sprite slide
- [ ] `Task_FieldMoveShowMonIndoors` + 7 substeps — indoor banner: blue streak background, slide-on/off
- [ ] `SpriteCB_FieldMoveMonSlideOnscreen` / `SpriteCB_FieldMoveMonWaitAfterCry` / `SpriteCB_FieldMoveMonSlideOffscreen` — mon sprite callbacks for field move show
- [ ] `ReturnToFieldFromFlyMapSelect` / `FieldCallback_UseFly` / `Task_UseFly` / `FieldCallback_FlyIntoMap` / `Task_FlyIntoMap` — Fly HM map-select return and fade-in sequence
- [ ] `FldEff_UseFly` / `Task_FlyOut` + 9 substeps — fly-out animation: field move pose, show mon, bird leave ball, swoop, jump on bird, fly off
- [ ] `FldEff_FlyIn` / `Task_FlyIn` + 7 substeps — fly-in animation: bird swoop down, player detach, jump off, field move pose, bird return to ball
- [ ] `CreateFlyBirdSprite` / `SpriteCB_FlyBirdLeaveBall` / `SpriteCB_FlyBirdSwoopDown` / `SpriteCB_FlyBirdReturnToBall` — bird sprite with affine scale animations for Fly
- [ ] `FldEff_NPCFlyOut` / `SpriteCB_NPCFlyOut` — NPC fly-out sprite (used when NPC leaves on a bird)
- [ ] `FldEff_RayquazaSpotlight` — Rayquaza spotlight effect (BG blend, window registers, spotlight tiles)
- [ ] `CreateTrainerSprite` — creates trainer front sprite (used in battle intro, not fully wired)
- [ ] `CreateMonSprite_PicBox` / `CreateMonSprite_FieldMove` / `FreeResourcesAndDestroySprite` — mon pic sprite creation for field move display
- [ ] `FieldEffectScript_LoadTiles` / `FieldEffectScript_LoadFadedPalette` / `FieldEffectScript_LoadPalette` / `FieldEffectScript_CallNative` / `FieldEffectStart` — GBA field effect script interpreter (hardware-specific; intentionally replaced by TS field effect manager)
- [ ] `FieldEffectFreeGraphicsResources` / `FieldEffectStop` / `FieldEffectFreeTilesIfUnused` / `FieldEffectFreePaletteIfUnused` — GBA sprite/palette resource management (intentionally omitted; no equivalent needed in browser)
- [ ] `Task_DeoxysRockCameraShake` / `DestroyDeoxysRockEffect_CameraShake/RockFragments/WaitAndEnd` / `CreateDeoxysRockFragments` / `SpriteCB_DeoxysRockFragment` — rock destruction camera shake and fragment sprites (palette fade handled; sprite fragments missing)

#### Notes
- GBA hardware register writes (`SetGpuReg`, `REG_WININ`, `REG_WIN0H`, VRAM direct writes) have no browser equivalent and are intentionally omitted; the browser port achieves similar effects through WebGL passes and CSS.
- The `FieldEffectStart`/script-interpreter system is replaced by the TS `FieldEffectManager` which dispatches effects by ID without a bytecode interpreter.
- Fly HM is a major missing feature; the fly map select UI and full bird animation sequence are unimplemented.
- The "show mon for field move" banner (streaking background + mon cry) is missing for all HMs that use it (Surf, Waterfall, Dive, Fly, Escape Rope).
- Pokémon Center heal visual (glowing pokéballs + monitor flicker) is missing; only the heal data operation is implemented.
- Hall of Fame record effect is entirely missing.
- Escalator, Escape Rope, and Teleport warp *destinations* are set correctly but their visual spin/ride animations are not implemented.
- `FldEff_RayquazaSpotlight` is a post-game event effect; low priority.

### `src/field_effect_helpers.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/field/ReflectionRenderer.ts`, `src/field/ReflectionShimmer.ts`, `src/field/ArrowOverlay.ts`, `src/field/ArrowAnimationConstants.ts`, `src/game/surfing/SurfBlobRenderer.ts`, `src/game/surfing/SurfingController.ts`, `src/game/FieldEffectManager.ts`, `src/rendering/fieldEffectUtils.ts`, `src/game/ScriptFieldEffectAnimationManager.ts`
**Generation script**: None (field effect object templates referenced from `src/data/fieldEffects.gen.ts`)

#### Implemented
- [x] `SetUpReflection` — creates reflection sprite for object events; covered by `ReflectionRenderer.ts`
- [x] `UpdateObjectReflectionSprite` — tracks main sprite, flips vertically, matches position; covered by `ReflectionRenderer.ts`
- [x] `LoadObjectReflectionPalette` / `LoadObjectRegularReflectionPalette` / `LoadObjectHighBridgeReflectionPalette` — bridge-type palette selection for reflections; covered by `ReflectionRenderer.ts`
- [x] `GetReflectionVerticalOffset` — height-based vertical offset for reflection; covered in `ReflectionRenderer.ts`
- [x] `CreateWarpArrowSprite` / `ShowWarpArrowSprite` / `SetSpriteInvisible` — arrow overlay for warp hints; covered by `ArrowOverlay.ts` and `ArrowAnimationConstants.ts`
- [x] `FldEff_SurfBlob` / `UpdateSurfBlobFieldEffect` — surf blob creation and per-frame update; covered by `SurfBlobRenderer.ts` and `SurfingController.ts`
- [x] `SynchroniseSurfAnim` / `SynchroniseSurfPosition` — direction-based anim sync and position tracking for surf blob; covered by `SurfingController.ts`
- [x] `UpdateBobbingEffect` — vertical bobbing with velocity, interval-based update; covered by `SurfBlobRenderer.ts`
- [x] `StartUnderwaterSurfBlobBobbing` / `SpriteCB_UnderwaterSurfBlob` — separate bobbing driver for underwater surf; covered by `SurfBlobRenderer.ts`
- [x] `SetSurfBlob_BobState` / `SetSurfBlob_DontSyncAnim` / `SetSurfBlob_PlayerOffset` — surf blob state setters; covered by `SurfingController.ts`
- [x] `UpdateAshFieldEffect` / `UpdateAshFieldEffect_Wait` / `UpdateAshFieldEffect_Show` / `UpdateAshFieldEffect_End` / `StartAshFieldEffect` — ash grass animation lifecycle; covered by `src/game/__tests__/StepCallbackManager.ashGrass.test.ts` and `FieldEffectManager.ts`
- [x] `FldEff_BerryTreeGrowthSparkle` — sparkle effect on berry tree growth; referenced in berry manager
- [x] `ShowTreeDisguiseFieldEffect` / `ShowMountainDisguiseFieldEffect` / `ShowSandDisguiseFieldEffect` / `ShowDisguiseFieldEffect` / `UpdateDisguiseFieldEffect` / `StartRevealDisguise` / `UpdateRevealDisguise` — trainer disguise field effects (Kecleon/Steven disguise); covered by `src/game/trainers/trainerDisguise.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `FldEff_TallGrass` / `UpdateTallGrassFieldEffect` — tall grass animation on player movement; `FieldEffectManager.ts` and `StepCallbackManager` handle trigger logic, but the per-frame sprite lifecycle (camera offset correction, `sObjectMoved` tracking) is not a direct port
- [-] `FldEff_LongGrass` / `UpdateLongGrassFieldEffect` — long grass variant; same as above
- [-] `FldEff_ShortGrass` / `UpdateShortGrassFieldEffect` — short grass (Route 113 Soot); trigger exists in step callbacks but sprite-level y2 offset tracking not explicitly ported
- [-] `FldEff_SandFootprints` / `FldEff_DeepSandFootprints` / `FldEff_BikeTireTracks` / `UpdateFootprintsTireTracksFieldEffect` — footprint/tire track effects with fade-out; `FieldEffectManager.ts` references these effect types but the 40-frame wait + flicker-out logic is not explicitly present
- [-] `FldEff_Splash` / `UpdateSplashFieldEffect` — shallow water splash on step; trigger exists in `StepCallbackManager` but sprite position-tracking update loop not fully ported
- [-] `FldEff_HotSpringsWater` / `UpdateHotSpringsWaterFieldEffect` — Lavaridge hot springs water effect; step trigger exists but sprite y-offset update not ported
- [-] `FldEff_SandPile` / `UpdateSandPileFieldEffect` — deep sand pile effect; trigger exists but sprite anim restart logic not ported
- [-] `FldEff_Shadow` / `UpdateShadowFieldEffect` — NPC drop shadow; referenced conceptually in NPC rendering but the per-frame position-tracking sprite loop is not explicitly ported
- [-] `FldEff_Ash` / `FldEff_Dust` / `FldEff_Ripple` / `FldEff_Bubbles` / `UpdateBubblesFieldEffect` / `FldEff_WaterSurfacing` / `FldEff_Sparkle` / `UpdateSparkleFieldEffect` — various one-shot or looping sprite effects; data templates exist in `fieldEffects.gen.ts` but individual sprite-level update callbacks are not individually ported

#### Missing
- [ ] `FldEff_JumpTallGrass` — jump-into-tall-grass impact sprite (uses `UpdateJumpImpactEffect`)
- [ ] `FldEff_JumpLongGrass` — jump-into-long-grass impact sprite (effectively unused in vanilla)
- [ ] `FldEff_JumpSmallSplash` / `FldEff_JumpBigSplash` — water splash on jump landing
- [ ] `FldEff_FeetInFlowingWater` / `UpdateFeetInFlowingWaterFieldEffect` — flowing water feet effect with per-step sound; `SE_PUDDLE` play on coord change
- [ ] `FldEff_UnusedGrass` / `FldEff_UnusedGrass2` / `FldEff_UnusedSand` — unused effects, omission intentional
- [ ] `UpdateRayquazaSpotlightEffect` — 8-state Rayquaza intro spotlight animation (figure-8 movement + BG scroll); `src/pages/Rayquaza3DDebugPage.tsx` exists but full spotlight state machine not ported
- [ ] `UpdateJumpImpactEffect` — generic jump landing impact sprite lifecycle
- [ ] `WaitFieldEffectSpriteAnim` — generic "wait for anim end then stop" callback used by ripple, sparkle, etc.
- [ ] `FindTallGrassFieldEffectSpriteId` — sprite search by local ID + coords for reuse
- [ ] `UpdateGrassFieldEffectSubpriority` — subpriority elevation correction for grass sprites overlapping NPCs

#### Notes
- The TS port handles field effects at a higher abstraction level: rather than per-frame sprite callbacks registered in a GBA sprite system, effects are driven by React/WebGL render passes and step-callback hooks. The conceptual coverage is good for gameplay-blocking effects (surf blob, reflections, ash, disguise), but the low-level sprite lifecycle callbacks (`WaitFieldEffectSpriteAnim`, `UpdateJumpImpactEffect`, footprint fade, etc.) lack direct equivalents.
- Rayquaza spotlight effect (`UpdateRayquazaSpotlightEffect`) involves GBA GPU register manipulation (`REG_BG0HOFS`/`VOFS`, BG tilemap writes) that maps to WebGL shader uniforms; partial debug page exists but the state machine is not ported.
- `sFigure8XOffsets` / `sFigure8YOffsets` at the bottom are noted in the C source as unused duplicates; no TS equivalent needed.
- Shadow sprites (`FldEff_Shadow`) are conceptually present in NPC rendering but the GBA sprite-based implementation (linked sprite position tracking, metatile behavior suppression) is not replicated per-frame in TS.
- Link cable / e-Reader / Battle Frontier facility effects: none applicable in this file.

**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `ShowFieldMessage` — dialog display gated on hidden state, maps to `showMessage` in `DialogContext`
- [x] `HideFieldMessageBox` — close/clear dialog, maps to dialog dismissal in `DialogContext`
- [x] `IsFieldMessageBoxHidden` — query dialog visibility state, available via dialog context state
- [x] `GetFieldMessageBoxMode` — mode query, implicit in dialog context state
- [x] `ShowFieldMessageFromBuffer` — show pre-expanded string (gStringVar4 equivalent handled by ScriptRunner string substitution before calling dialog)
- [x] `InitFieldMessageBox` — reset dialog state and text flags, performed during state transitions in `GameStateManager`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `ShowFieldAutoScrollMessage` — auto-scroll variant; `DialogContext` supports auto-advancing text but the distinct `FIELD_MESSAGE_BOX_AUTO_SCROLL` mode with no A-button skip is not explicitly modeled as a separate mode
- [-] `StopFieldMessage` — immediate cancel without clearing window graphics; dialog context `close()` exists but may not suppress the clear animation in all cases

#### Missing
- [ ] `ShowPokenavFieldMessage` — PokeNav-specific overlay message tied to match call task lifecycle; `IsMatchCallTaskActive` / `StartMatchCallFromScript` have no TS equivalent (PokeNav/match-call system not ported)
- [ ] `Task_DrawFieldMessage` — GBA task-based two-step draw sequence (load gfx → draw frame → wait for printer); replaced by React render cycle, but the explicit load-gfx step and `RunTextPrintersAndIsPrinter0Active` polling have no direct analog
- [ ] `Task_HidePokenavMessageWhenDone` — lifecycle task tied to match call; not ported (match call system absent)
- [ ] `ReplaceFieldMessageWithFrame` (UNUSED) — replaces message with bare window frame; not ported (no equivalent concept in TS UI layer)
- [ ] `ForceShowFieldAutoScrollMessage` (UNUSED) — force-show auto-scroll bypassing hidden check; not ported

#### Notes
- The C implementation uses a GBA task system with frame-by-frame state machines for drawing; the TS port replaces this entirely with React state + the `PromptService`/`DialogContext` render pipeline.
- The `sFieldMessageBoxMode` mutex (preventing double-show) is partially replicated by dialog context's `isOpen` guard, but the three-way `HIDDEN / NORMAL / AUTO_SCROLL` distinction is flattened.
- PokeNav / match-call subsystem is intentionally absent from the port; `ShowPokenavFieldMessage` has no path to implementation until that system exists.
- Text flag initialization (`canABSpeedUpPrint`, `autoScroll`, `forceMidTextSpeed`) maps conceptually to `src/config/timing.ts` and `src/core/prompt/textSpeed.ts`, though not as a direct `InitFieldMessageBox` call.

- [x] `GetPlayerAvatarGraphicsIdByCurrentState` — reverse flag→GFX lookup

- [x] `UpdatePlayerAvatarTransitionState` — T_NOT_MOVING / T_TILE_TRANSITION / T_TILE_CENTER

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `TryPushBoulder` — Strength flag check and boulder collision tested at script level; the Task-based push animation (`StartStrengthAnim` / `PushBoulder_Start/Move/End`) has no dedicated TS task sequencer
- [-] `Task_StopSurfingInit` / `Task_WaitStopSurfing` — surf-stop jump animation exists conceptually in `SurfingController.ts` but the two-task frame sequence may not be fully replicated
- [-] `DoPlayerSpinEntrance` / `DoPlayerSpinExit` — warp-hole spin warp visual effect; partial coverage via `FallWarpArrivalSequencer.ts` for fall warps, but the rising spin-exit and spin-entrance sequences are not explicitly ported
- [-] `player_get_pos_including_state_based_drift` — in-transit coordinate correction for interaction-during-walk; not directly ported, interaction triggers use tile-center events

#### Missing
- [ ] `StartFishing` / `Task_Fishing` + all 16 `Fishing_*` state functions — full fishing mini-game (rod animation, dot prompt, bite detection with Suction Cups/Sticky Hold ability, A-press timing, encounter trigger)
- [ ] `AlignFishingAnimationFrames` — fishing sprite offset logic (x2/y2 based on anim frame type)
- [ ] `DoPlayerMatJump` / `PlayerAvatar_DoSecretBaseMatJump` — secret base jump mat task sequencer
- [ ] `DoPlayerMatSpin` / `PlayerAvatar_SecretBaseMatSpinStep0-3` — secret base spin mat task (face-cycle with slowing delays, end walk)
- [ ] `ForcedMovement_MatJump` / `ForcedMovement_MatSpin` — forced movement dispatch hooks for jump/spin mats (the underlying tasks above are what's missing)
- [ ] `SetPlayerAvatarFishing` / `SetPlayerAvatarWatering` / `PlayerUseAcroBikeOnBumpySlope` — fishing/watering/bumpy-slope sprite animation setters
- [ ] `GetFRLGAvatarGraphicsIdByGender` / `GetRSAvatarGraphicsIdByGender` — link-RS and FRLG avatar GFX IDs (multiplayer/link, intentionally omitted)
- [ ] `GetRivalAvatarGraphicsIdByStateIdAndGender` / `unref_GetRivalAvatarGenderByGraphicsId` — rival avatar GFX tables (used in link battles, omittable)
- [ ] `PartyHasMonWithSurf` — scan party for Surf move knowledge (used for CanSurf checks upstream)
- [ ] `TrySpinPlayerForWarp` / `Task_DoPlayerSpinEntrance` / `Task_DoPlayerSpinExit` — complete spin-warp entrance/exit task with camera freeze, speed curve, and facing-lock

#### Notes
- Core walking, running, forced-movement tiles, collision, and avatar transitions are well-covered by `PlayerController.ts` + `StepCallbackManager.ts`; tests confirm muddy slope, ice, bike parity, ledge jump, and arrow warp parity.
- **Fishing mini-game** is the largest unported system in this file — the 16-state dot/bite/timeout machine with rod-specific probabilities and sprite alignment has no TS equivalent.
- **Secret base mats** (jump and spin) have no TS task sequencer; the metatile behavior constants are generated but the runtime task logic is absent.
- **Boulder push** (Strength) is handled declaratively at the script-command level but the animated two-character movement task (`PushBoulder_Start/Move/End`) is not ported.
- **Spin warp** (used at warp holes like Battle Frontier entrance) is distinct from fall-warp; only fall-down is covered by `FallWarpArrivalSequencer`.
- `GetFRLGAvatarGraphicsIdByGender` and RS link-avatar tables are intentionally irrelevant — link cable multiplayer is out of scope.
- `MovePlayerToMapCoords` is marked unused in C source; correctly omitted.
- `PartyHasMonWithSurf` is missing but relevant for future HM-gate checks.

## batch: src/field_poison.c

### `src/field_poison.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts`, `src/game/WorldManager.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `IsMonValidSpecies` — species validity check exists implicitly in party iteration logic throughout TS codebase
- [x] `AllMonsFainted` — party HP/faint checking exists in overworld wild encounter and battle flow code

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `DoPoisonFieldEffect` — field poison step damage exists conceptually via `StepCallbackManager` step hooks, but the per-step HP decrement, `FldEffPoison_Start` screen flash, and return codes (`FLDPSN_PSN`/`FLDPSN_FNT`/`FLDPSN_NONE`) are not fully wired through
- [-] `TryFieldPoisonWhiteOut` — white-out detection when all party faint exists in battle/game-over flow, but the field-poison-specific white-out path (including Battle Frontier/Trainer Hill variants via `CurrentBattlePyramidLocation`/`InBattlePike`/`InTrainerHillChallenge`) is not implemented as a dedicated script-resuming task

#### Missing
- [ ] `FaintFromFieldPoison` — adjusts friendship on field poison faint (`FRIENDSHIP_EVENT_FAINT_FIELD_PSN`), clears status, sets `gStringVar1` to nickname
- [ ] `MonFaintedFromPoison` — checks if a party slot fainted specifically due to poison status
- [ ] `Task_TryFieldPoisonWhiteOut` — GBA task that sequences fainted-from-poison messages per party slot, sets `gSpecialVar_Result` (`FLDPSN_FRONTIER_WHITEOUT`/`FLDPSN_WHITEOUT`/`FLDPSN_NO_WHITEOUT`), then re-enables script context

#### Notes
- The overworld poison step mechanic (HP tick per step while poisoned) is a known missing feature; `StepCallbackManager` has hooks for special tiles but no poison drain callback is registered.
- Friendship penalty on field faint (`AdjustFriendship` with `FRIENDSHIP_EVENT_FAINT_FIELD_PSN`) is not implemented anywhere in the TS port.
- Battle Frontier white-out branching (`FLDPSN_FRONTIER_WHITEOUT`) is irrelevant until Battle Frontier is implemented; can be deferred.
- The `ScriptContext_Stop`/`ScriptContext_Enable` pattern (suspending script execution while the task runs) maps to the TS `ScriptRunner` yield/resume mechanism, but the field-poison task does not have a TS counterpart using it.

### `src/field_region_map.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
_(none)_

#### Partially Implemented
_(none)_

#### Missing
- [ ] `FieldInitRegionMap` — entry point; allocates state and begins region map initialization sequence
- [ ] `MCB2_InitRegionMapRegisters` — resets GPU/sprite state, initializes BG templates and windows, chains to update callback
- [ ] `VBCB_FieldUpdateRegionMap` — VBlank callback: OAM upload, sprite copy requests, palette transfer
- [ ] `MCB2_FieldUpdateRegionMap` — main loop: runs update, animates sprites, builds OAM, updates palette fade, copies tilemaps
- [ ] `FieldUpdateRegionMap` — 7-state machine: init region map, draw title/mapsec frame, fade in, handle A/B/cursor input, fade out, free resources
- [ ] `PrintRegionMapSecName` — prints the hovered map section name into the mapsec name window (or clears it if no section)

#### Notes
The wall-mounted Region Map (found in Pokémon Centers) has no TypeScript equivalent at all. The port has no region map feature — neither the field variant here nor the PokéNav variant (`pokenav_region_map.c`) nor the Pokédex variant. All GBA hardware specifics (BG templates, GPU register setup, DMA, VBlank/Main callbacks, OAM) are intentionally irrelevant to the browser port, but the gameplay feature itself (viewable map of Hoenn with player icon and cursor, closeable with A/B) is entirely absent.

- [x] `WriteFlashScanlineEffectBuffer` — pre-fill scanline buffer for flash level; covered by `FlashController`
- [x] `DoOrbEffect` — Magma/Aqua orb screen effect (circle expand + shake); covered by `orbEffectRuntime.ts`
- [x] `FadeOutOrbEffect` — transition orb effect to fade-out phase; covered by `orbEffectRuntime.ts`
- [x] `SetFlashScanlineEffectWindowBoundaries` — Bresenham circle rasterizer for flash window; covered by `FlashController`
- [x] `Script_FadeOutMapMusic` — fade out music and re-enable script context; covered by `ScriptRunner` music commands

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `DoEscalatorWarp` — escalator movement + warp; escalator metatile behavior exists but escalator warp animation sequencer (`StartEscalatorWarp`) may not be fully implemented
- [-] `DoLavaridgeGymB1FWarp` / `DoLavaridgeGym1FWarp` — Lavaridge gym hole warps; `LavaridgeWarpSequencer.ts` exists but completeness uncertain
- [-] `DoTeleportTileWarp` — teleport tile with spin entrance; `DoSpinEnterWarp`/spin entrance may be partial (no `DoPlayerSpinEntrance` equivalent confirmed)
- [-] `DoSpinExitWarp` — player spins up offscreen then fades in; spin animations not confirmed fully ported
- [-] `FieldCB_ContinueScript` / `FieldCB_ContinueScriptHandleMusic` — resume script after fade; `storyScriptFadeRecovery.ts` covers recovery but `ScriptContext_Enable` flow may differ
- [-] `Task_OrbEffect` — full multi-state orb effect with GPU blending, scanline window, camera pan; `orbEffectRuntime.ts` covers the logic but hardware registers (BLDALPHA, WININ, WINOUT) are approximated via WebGL

#### Missing
- [ ] `DoMossdeepGymWarp` — saves object events, skips load flag, spin warp; `SetObjectEventLoadFlag(SKIP_OBJECT_EVENT_LOAD)` + `SaveObjectEvents` not ported
- [ ] `DoPortholeWarp` — SS Tidal porthole view warp; `FieldCB_ShowPortholeView` not ported
- [ ] `DoCableClubWarp` — cable club link room warp; intentionally omitted (link cable)
- [ ] `FieldCB_ReturnToFieldCableLink` / `Task_ReturnToFieldCableLink` — return from cable club; intentionally omitted (link cable)
- [ ] `FieldCB_ReturnToFieldWirelessLink` / `Task_ReturnToFieldWirelessLink` — return from wireless link; intentionally omitted (RFU/wireless)
- [ ] `Task_ReturnToFieldRecordMixing` — return from record mixing; intentionally omitted (link)
- [ ] `ReturnFromLinkRoom` / `Task_ReturnToWorldFromLinkRoom` — return from trade/colosseum; intentionally omitted (link)
- [ ] `DoContestHallWarp` / `Task_DoContestHallWarp` — contest hall specific warp callback; contests not ported
- [ ] `ReturnToFieldOpenStartMenu` / `FieldCB_ReturnToFieldOpenStartMenu` — re-open start menu after return; not confirmed ported
- [ ] `FieldCB_ContinueScriptUnionRoom` — union room fade in; intentionally omitted (link)
- [ ] `WriteBattlePyramidViewScanlineEffectBuffer` — pyramid darkness effect; Battle Frontier not ported
- [ ] `SetOrbFlashScanlineEffectWindowBoundaries` — orb-specific circle rasterizer (wider clamp 0–240 vs 0–255); subtle difference from flash version, may not be distinguished in TS
- [ ] `UpdateOrbEffectBlend` — BLDALPHA register blend update for orb fade-out; hardware blend not directly mapped
- [ ] `LoadOrbEffectPalette` — loads red/blue palette for orb effect into BG palette slot 15; approximated in WebGL

#### Notes
- All GBA hardware register manipulation (REG_DISPCNT, REG_BLDCNT, REG_BLDALPHA, REG_WININ, REG_WINOUT, DMA) is approximated via WebGL shader parameters or simply omitted — the browser port cannot replicate GBA scanline effects exactly.
- Link cable / RFU / record mixing / cable club functions are intentionally omitted as multiplayer is out of scope.
- Contest hall warp is omitted as contests are not ported.
- `ScanlineEffect` flash uses a Bresenham circle algorithm — `FlashController.ts` replicates this for the WebGL scanline renderer (`WebGLScanlineRenderer.ts`).
- The `Task_*` state machine pattern maps to async sequences / promise chains in TypeScript rather than frame-tick task arrays.

- [-] `Task_HandlePorthole` — 4-state porthole task (fade-in, move player east/west, exit via dive warp); movement script execution and cruise-state logic largely missing; `DoDiveWarp` path exists but the full SS Tidal cruise step counter is absent

The `MAP_OFFSET` subtraction rule documented in MEMORY.md applies here — the C source adds `MAP_OFFSET` (7) to tile coords (e.g. `4 + MAP_OFFSET`), which the TS port should strip back to content coords (4, 1/2/3).
- The porthole / SS Tidal cruise subsystem (`VAR_SS_TIDAL_STATE`, `CountSSTidalStep`, `GetSSTidalLocation`, `DoPortholeWarp`) is largely unimplemented — it is post-early-game content (Slateport→Lilycove sea voyage) and can be deferred.
- `sSSTidalSailEastMovementScript` / `sSSTidalSailWestMovementScript` are trivial inline movement arrays; they have no standalone TS equivalent but would be inlined if the porthole task were implemented.

- [x] `CableCarWarp` — sets warp destination based on direction var → `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts`
- [x] `StorePlayerCoordsInVars` — copies pos to script vars → `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts`

- [x] `MonOTNameNotPlayer` — checks OT name/language against player → `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts`
- [x] `IsBadEggInParty` — scans party for bad eggs → `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts`

- [x] `Script_DoRayquazaScene` — triggers Rayquaza cutscene → `src/states/BirchSpeechState.ts` / Rayquaza debug page

- [x] `GetPlayerTrainerIdOnesDigit` — last digit of trainer ID → `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `MoveElevator` / `Task_MoveElevator` / `MoveElevatorWindowLights` / `Task_MoveElevatorWindowLights` — elevator animation with camera pan and metatile light cycling; camera pan and metatile writes present in TS specials but light window animation (metatile cycling) is not fully implemented
- [-] `ShowScrollableMultichoice` — menu layout dispatch logic ported, but Battle Frontier exchange corner item icon display (`FillFrontierExchangeCornerWindowAndItemIcon`, `ShowFrontierExchangeCornerItemIcon`) not implemented
- [-] `ShowDeptStoreElevatorFloorSelect` / `CloseDeptStoreElevatorWindow` — window exists in script specials but floor name rendering not confirmed implemented
- [-] `ShowFrontierManiacMessage` / `ShowFrontierGamblerLookingMessage` / `ShowFrontierGamblerGoMessage` / `FrontierGamblerSetWonOrLost` — frontier NPC messages wired to `showMessage` in ScriptRunner but streak-based message selection logic not confirmed
- [-] `BufferBattleTowerElevatorFloors` / `ShowBattlePointsWindow` / `UpdateBattlePointsWindow` — BP window rendering not implemented (no canvas BP HUD)
- [-] `ShowNatureGirlMessage` — nature-indexed messages wired in ScriptRunner; full per-nature message table not confirmed
- [-] `TryGainNewFanFromCounter` / `UpdateTrainerFansAfterLinkBattle` / fan club bitfield system — fan club state machine partially present in flag/var store but the full fan gain/loss logic is not ported

#### Missing
- [ ] `Special_ShowDiploma` — opens diploma screen; no diploma UI exists
- [ ] `Special_ViewWallClock` — opens wall clock screen; not implemented
- [ ] `SpawnLinkPartnerObjectEvent` / `LoadLinkPartnerObjectEventSpritePalette` — link cable partner spawning; intentionally omitted (no link cable)
- [ ] `GetLinkPartnerNames` — link player names; intentionally omitted
- [ ] `GetSlotMachineId` — slot machine luck seeding; game corner not implemented
- [ ] `SetTrickHouseNuggetFlag` / `ResetTrickHouseNuggetFlag` — sets specVar + flag pair; trivially scriptable but not confirmed present
- [ ] `GetPokeblockNameByMonNature` — Pokéblock system not implemented
- [ ] `GetSecretBaseNearbyMapName` — secret base feature not implemented
- [ ] `GetBattleTowerSinglesStreak` / `GetDaysUntilPacifidlogTMAvailable` / `SetPacifidlogTMReceivedDay` — time-based TM and streak specials; not confirmed ported
- [ ] `BufferLottoTicketNumber` — lottery ticket zero-padded string; not confirmed ported
- [ ] `GetMysteryGiftCardStat` — Mystery Gift not implemented
- [ ] `BufferEReaderTrainerName` — e-Reader not implemented; intentionally omitted
- [ ] `LoopWingFlapSE` / `Task_LoopWingFlapSE` — repeating SE task; audio system not implemented
- [ ] `CloseBattlePikeCurtain` / `Task_CloseBattlePikeCurtain` — Battle Pike curtain metatile animation; not implemented
- [ ] `ShakeCamera` / `Task_ShakeCamera` / `StopCameraShake` — camera shake task; `src/game/CameraController.ts` has pan but not this shake task pattern
- [ ] `OffsetCameraForBattle` — sets camera pan offset before battle; not confirmed
- [ ] `FieldShowRegionMap` — opens region map overlay; not implemented
- [ ] `InMultiPartnerRoom` — Battle Tower multi partner room check; not confirmed
- [ ] `BufferBattleFrontierTutorMoveName` / `GetBattleFrontierTutorMoveIndex` / `ShowBattleFrontierTutorWindow` / `CloseBattleFrontierTutorWindow` — move tutor UI in Frontier; not confirmed implemented
- [ ] `UpdateBattlePointsWindow` / `ShowBattlePointsWindow` / `CloseBattlePointsWindow` — BP HUD window rendering
- [ ] `ShowFrontierExchangeCornerItemIconWindow` / `CloseFrontierExchangeCornerItemIconWindow` — item icon window for exchange corner
- [ ] `SetBattleTowerLinkPlayerGfx` — sets NPC gfx vars for link Tower; link-only, low priority
- [ ] `BattleTowerReconnectLink` / `LinkRetireStatusWithBattleTowerPartner` / `Task_LinkRetireStatusWithBattleTowerPartner` — link Battle Tower retire protocol; intentionally omitted (no link cable)
- [ ] `ResetHealLocationFromDewford` — resets bad heal warp; not confirmed
- [ ] `ResetFanClub` / `UpdateTrainerFanClubGameClear` / full fan club bitfield system — post-game Lilycove fan club; not implemented
- [ ] `ShouldDoWallyCall` / `ShouldDoScottFortreeCall` / `ShouldDoScottBattleFrontierCall` / `ShouldDoRoxanneCall` / `ShouldDoRivalRayquazaCall` — PokeNav call step counters; not implemented (no PokeNav)
- [ ] `BufferFanClubTrainerName` / `BufferFanClubTrainerName_` — fan club NPC trainer name buffering
- [ ] `TryInitBattleTowerAwardManObjectEvent` — removed/empty in Emerald; no-op
- [ ] `ShouldDistributeEonTicket` — always FALSE in Emerald; no-op
- [ ] `GetMartEmployeeObjectEventId` — returns local ID 1; trivial but not confirmed
- [ ] `UpdateFrontierGambler` / `ShowFrontierGamblerGoMessage` / `ShowFrontierGamblerLookingMessage` / `FrontierGamblerSetWonOrLost` — not confirmed implemented beyond var update

#### Notes
- Link cable functions (`SpawnLinkPartnerObjectEvent`, `BattleTowerReconnectLink`, `Task_LinkRetireStatusWithBattleTowerPartner`, `GetLinkPartnerNames`, `SetBattleTowerLinkPlayerGfx`) are intentionally omitted — no multiplayer in browser port.
- e-Reader (`BufferEReaderTrainerName`) and Mystery Gift (`GetMysteryGiftCardStat`) are intentionally omitted.
- The scrollable multichoice menu (`ShowScrollableMultichoice`, `sScrollableMultichoiceOptions`) is covered by `ScriptMenuHost.ts` and `ScriptChoiceMenuContent.tsx` but item icon display for the Frontier Exchange Corner is not implemented.
- The Deoxys rock puzzle has split coverage: palette loading in `deoxysRockPaletteRuntime.ts`, step counting and state transitions in `legendaryIslandSpecials.ts`.
- `MAP_OFFSET` is baked into `sMauvilleGymSwitchCoords` — the TS port must subtract 7 from those coordinates per the documented MAP_OFFSET trap.
- Game corner / slot machines not implemented — `GetSlotMachineId` omitted.
- Post-game content (fan club, Battle Frontier streaks, cycling road records) is partially wired but not fully validated.

## batch: src/field_tasks.c

### `src/field_tasks.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/StepCallbackManager.ts`, `src/game/TruckSequenceRunner.ts`, `src/game/TruckSequence.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `AshGrassPerStepCallback` — ash removal on step, ash gather count increment → `StepCallbackManager.ts` ash grass handler
- [x] `FortreeBridgePerStepCallback` — lower/raise Fortree log bridges on step with bounce animation → `StepCallbackManager.ts` Fortree bridge handler
- [x] `PacifidlogBridgePerStepCallback` — sink/raise Pacifidlog floating logs with half-submerge transitions → `StepCallbackManager.ts` Pacifidlog bridge handler
- [x] `SootopolisGymIcePerStepCallback` — crack/break Sootopolis Gym ice tiles on step → `StepCallbackManager.ts` Sootopolis ice handler
- [x] `CrackedFloorPerStepCallback` — crack cave/Mirage Tower floors on step with delay → `StepCallbackManager.ts` cracked floor handler
- [x] `EndTruckSequence` — moves truck boxes to final position → `TruckSequenceRunner.ts` / `TruckSequence.ts`
- [x] `ActivatePerStepCallback` — sets active per-step callback by ID → `StepCallbackManager.ts` activation logic
- [x] `SetSootopolisGymCrackedIceMetatiles` — restores cracked ice metatiles from saved var state on map load → `StepCallbackManager.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `Task_MuddySlope` — muddy slope metatile animation with 4-slot tracking and camera offset correction; TS has `StepCallbackManager` muddy slope step detection but the per-frame metatile animation cycling (`sMuddySlopeMetatiles` frames 0–3 over `SLOPE_ANIM_TIME=32`) and camera-offset coordinate adjustment for the animation slots are not fully verified
- [-] `Task_RunTimeBasedEvents` — time-based event updates and ambient cry scheduling; time-based events have no direct TS equivalent (no RTC), ambient cry is not implemented

#### Missing
- [ ] `RunTimeBasedEvents` — calls `DoTimeBasedEvents()` on a vblank counter interval; no RTC/time system in TS port
- [ ] `UpdateAmbientCry` — schedules ambient Pokémon cries in the overworld; no audio ambient cry system implemented
- [ ] `SetUpFieldTasks` / `ResetFieldTasksArgs` — GBA task setup/teardown wrappers; replaced by TS module initialization, not directly relevant
- [ ] `SecretBasePerStepCallback` — records decorations stepped on in friend's secret base; Secret Base feature not implemented
- [ ] `DummyPerStepCallback` — no-op default callback; trivially covered by default no-op path

#### Notes
- The GBA task system (`gTasks`, `CreateTask`, `FindTaskIdByFunc`) has no direct TS equivalent; all callbacks are instead methods/handlers within `StepCallbackManager` driven by the game loop.
- `MAP_OFFSET` (7) is used in `SetSootopolisGymCrackedIceMetatiles` and `SootopolisGymIcePerStepCallback` when calling `MapGridSetMetatileIdAt`/`MarkIcePuzzleCoordVisited`; the MEMORY.md MAP_OFFSET trap applies — TS map data has no border so offsets must be handled carefully.
- `SecretBasePerStepCallback` is intentionally omitted (Secret Base multiplayer feature not in scope).
- Ambient cry and RTC (`DoTimeBasedEvents`) are intentionally omitted (no audio system, no real-time clock in browser port).
- Bridge bounce animation in `FortreeBridgePerStepCallback` uses a `% 7` modulo pattern; verify TS parity test `StepCallbackManager.bridgeAndCrackedFloor.test.ts` covers the bounce frames.

## batch: src/field_weather.c

### `src/field_weather.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/weather/WeatherManager.ts`, `src/weather/WeatherColorPipeline.ts`, `src/weather/WeatherColorWebGLPass.ts`, `src/weather/effects/`, `src/weather/runtime.gen.ts`
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-weather-runtime.cjs` (reads this file), `scripts/generate-weather.cjs`

#### Implemented
- [x] `StartWeather` — weather system initialization, mapped to WeatherManager startup
- [x] `SetNextWeather` / `SetCurrentAndNextWeather` — weather transition entry points, covered by WeatherManager state transitions
- [x] `GetCurrentWeather` — exposed via WeatherManager
- [x] `None_Init` / `None_Main` / `None_Finish` — no-op weather, covered by `NoopWeatherEffect.ts`
- [x] Weather effect dispatch table (`sWeatherFuncs`) — TS registry in `src/weather/registry.ts` maps weather IDs to effect classes
- [x] Individual weather effect init/main/finish (Clouds, Sunny, Rain, Snow, Thunderstorm, FogHorizontal, Ash, Sandstorm, FogDiagonal, Shade, Drought, Downpour, Bubbles) — each has a corresponding `src/weather/effects/*.ts` class
- [x] `Weather_SetBlendCoeffs` / `Weather_SetTargetBlendCoeffs` / `Weather_UpdateBlend` — blend coefficient animation ported in WeatherColorPipeline/WeatherColorWebGLPass
- [x] `FadeScreen` — integrated into `src/field/FadeController.ts` and WeatherManager
- [x] `IsWeatherNotFadingIn` — state query exposed by WeatherManager
- [x] `IsWeatherChangeComplete` — exposed by WeatherManager
- [x] `SetWeatherScreenFadeOut` / `SetWeatherPalStateIdle` — state setters in WeatherManager

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `BuildColorMaps` — the contrast/darkened-contrast lookup table generation is ported conceptually in `WeatherColorPipeline.ts`, but the exact GBA 5-bit RGB piecewise curve (shadow vs midtone branching, `brightnessDelta` logic) may not be bit-exact; WebGL uses shader-based color transforms rather than prebuilt lookup tables
- [-] `ApplyColorMap` / `ApplyColorMapWithBlend` / `ApplyDroughtColorMapWithBlend` — palette-slot-level color map application ported as GPU shader passes in `WeatherColorWebGLPass.ts`; the per-slot `COLOR_MAP_NONE/CONTRAST/DARK_CONTRAST` dispatch and the exact `sBasePaletteColorMapTypes[32]` slot assignments are partially reflected but not slot-for-slot identical (no GBA palette buffer concept)
- [-] `ApplyFogBlend` — fog sprite lightening (`LightenSpritePaletteInFog`, `MarkFogSpritePalToLighten`) is not ported; TS fog effect uses a uniform tint rather than per-sprite-palette brightening
- [-] `FadeInScreen_RainShowShade` / `FadeInScreen_Drought` / `FadeInScreen_FogHorizontal` — weather-specific screen fade-in sequences partially handled in FadeController but the 16-step counter and per-weather branching logic is not fully replicated
- [-] `UpdateWeatherColorMap` — gradual color map stepping (colorMapStepCounter/Delay) is conceptually present in WeatherManager transitions but the exact step-delay frame counter mechanism is approximated
- [-] `DroughtStateInit` / `DroughtStateRun` — drought sine-wave shimmer state machine present in `src/weather/effects/ColorTintEffect.ts` but the exact brightness stage/timer/sine table oscillation from C is approximated rather than bit-exact
- [-] `PreservePaletteInWeather` / `ResetPreservedPalettesInWeather` — no direct TS equivalent; field effect palette preservation is handled implicitly by layer separation in WebGL, not by a palette slot exemption list

#### Missing
- [ ] `LoadCustomWeatherSpritePalette` — no TS equivalent for loading a custom palette into the weather sprite palette slot
- [ ] `LoadDroughtWeatherPalettes` / `ResetDroughtWeatherPaletteLoading` / `LoadDroughtWeatherPalette` — incremental drought palette loading (streamed over frames to avoid lag); not needed in browser (no DMA limits) but the `sDroughtWeatherColors` lookup table itself has no JS equivalent
- [ ] `UpdateSpritePaletteWithWeather` — per-sprite-palette weather color map application on sprite load; TS applies weather via global GPU pass, not per-palette-slot on sprite creation
- [ ] `ApplyWeatherColorMapToPal` / `ApplyWeatherColorMapIfIdle` / `ApplyWeatherColorMapIfIdle_Gradual` — direct palette manipulation APIs called by other subsystems (field effects, scripts); no TS equivalents exposed
- [ ] `SetRainStrengthFromSoundEffect` / `PlayRainStoppingSoundEffect` — rain audio strength tracking; audio system not implemented
- [ ] `SetCurrentAndNextWeatherNoDelay` — the `readyForInit = TRUE` override for instant weather init without fade delay; not separately exposed in TS
- [ ] `Task_WeatherInit` / `Task_WeatherMain` — GBA task system wrappers; TS uses the game loop directly, so these are structurally replaced but the `readyForInit` gate logic is not fully replicated
- [ ] `sBasePaletteColorMapTypes[32]` slot table — the exact per-slot NONE/CONTRAST/DARK_CONTRAST assignments for all 32 GBA palettes are not represented in TS (no GBA palette buffer architecture)
- [ ] `sDroughtWeatherColors` lookup tables — 6 × 4096-entry precomputed drought color LUTs; not ported (browser uses shader math instead)

#### Notes
- The GBA palette buffer architecture (`gPlttBufferUnfaded`/`gPlttBufferFaded`, 32 slots × 16 colors) has no direct browser equivalent. The TS port uses WebGL shader-based color transforms (`WeatherColorWebGLPass.ts`) that achieve visually similar results but bypass the slot-level `COLOR_MAP_NONE/CONTRAST/DARK_CONTRAST` dispatch entirely.
- `generate-weather-runtime.cjs` reads this file and `field_weather_effect.c` to emit `src/weather/runtime.gen.ts`, which captures weather constants and transition data.
- Audio (rain strength, stop sound effects) is entirely absent from the TS port.
- The fog sprite lightening subsystem (`MarkFogSpritePalToLighten`, `LightenSpritePaletteInFog`, `lightenedFogSpritePals[]`) is GBA-specific and has no TS equivalent; the `FogLayerEffect.ts` applies a uniform tint instead.
- `UNDERWATER` weather reuses `FogHorizontal` callbacks in C; TS has `UnderwaterBubblesEffect.ts` as a separate class.

## batch: src/field_weather_effect.c

### `src/field_weather_effect.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/weather/effects/CloudLayerEffect.ts`, `src/weather/effects/RainEffect.ts`, `src/weather/effects/SnowEffect.ts`, `src/weather/effects/SandstormEffect.ts`, `src/weather/effects/FogLayerEffect.ts`, `src/weather/effects/AshEffect.ts`, `src/weather/effects/UnderwaterBubblesEffect.ts`, `src/weather/effects/AbnormalWeatherEffect.ts`, `src/weather/WeatherManager.ts`, `src/weather/runtime.gen.ts`
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-weather-runtime.cjs` (reads `src/field_weather.c` and `src/field_weather_effect.c`)

#### Implemented
- [x] `Clouds_InitVars` / `Clouds_InitAll` / `Clouds_Main` / `Clouds_Finish` — cloud weather lifecycle, covered by CloudLayerEffect
- [x] `Sunny_InitVars` / `Sunny_Main` / `Sunny_Finish` — no-op sunny weather, covered by ColorTintEffect/NoopWeatherEffect
- [x] `Rain_InitVars` / `Rain_InitAll` / `Rain_Main` / `Rain_Finish` — rain sprite lifecycle, covered by RainEffect
- [x] `Snow_InitVars` / `Snow_InitAll` / `Snow_Main` / `Snow_Finish` — snow sprite lifecycle, covered by SnowEffect
- [x] `Ash_InitVars` / `Ash_InitAll` / `Ash_Main` / `Ash_Finish` — ash sprite lifecycle, covered by AshEffect
- [x] `FogHorizontal_InitVars` / `FogHorizontal_InitAll` / `FogHorizontal_Main` / `FogHorizontal_Finish` — horizontal fog lifecycle, covered by FogLayerEffect
- [x] `FogDiagonal_InitVars` / `FogDiagonal_InitAll` / `FogDiagonal_Main` / `FogDiagonal_Finish` — diagonal fog lifecycle, covered by FogLayerEffect
- [x] `Sandstorm_InitVars` / `Sandstorm_InitAll` / `Sandstorm_Main` / `Sandstorm_Finish` — sandstorm lifecycle, covered by SandstormEffect
- [x] `Shade_InitVars` / `Shade_Main` / `Shade_Finish` — shade (palette-only) weather, covered by ColorTintEffect
- [x] `Bubbles_InitVars` / `Bubbles_InitAll` / `Bubbles_Main` / `Bubbles_Finish` — underwater bubbles lifecycle, covered by UnderwaterBubblesEffect
- [x] `SetSavedWeather` / `GetSavedWeather` / `SetSavedWeatherFromCurrMapHeader` / `SetWeather` — weather persistence, covered by WeatherManager
- [x] `TranslateWeatherNum` — maps weather constants including cycle routes, covered in runtime.gen.ts / WeatherManager
- [x] `UpdateWeatherPerDay` — advances weather cycle stage, covered by WeatherManager
- [x] `DoCurrentWeather` / `ResumePausedWeather` — apply saved weather to next/current, covered by WeatherManager

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `Thunderstorm_InitVars` / `Thunderstorm_InitAll` / `Thunderstorm_Main` / `Thunderstorm_Finish` — thunderstorm logic exists in RainEffect but the multi-state thunder bolt sequencer (short/long bolt pattern, `EnqueueThunder`, `UpdateThunderSound`) is not replicated; TS uses simplified thunder flash
- [-] `Downpour_InitVars` / `Downpour_InitAll` — downpour init ported, but shares thunderstorm logic gaps above
- [-] `UpdateRainSprite` / `StartRainSpriteFall` / sprite movement with Q28.4 fixed-point — TS RainEffect uses CSS/canvas animation rather than per-sprite fixed-point GBA movement; functional approximation only
- [-] `UpdateSnowflakeSprite` / `InitSnowflakeSpriteMovement` — TS SnowEffect approximates the sine-wave lateral drift and tPosY fixed-point; exact GBA reset coordinates (y=250, y=163 thresholds) not matched
- [-] `UpdateSandstormSwirlSprite` / `CreateSwirlSandstormSprites` — regular sandstorm tiles ported; swirly spiral sub-sprites with `tRadius` growth and `gSineTable` orbit not present in TS SandstormEffect
- [-] `UpdateAshSprite` — ash scroll logic ported; exact `ashBaseSpritesX` coordinate wrapping math may differ
- [-] `Task_DoAbnormalWeather` / `CreateAbnormalWeatherTask` — alternating drought/downpour task ported in AbnormalWeatherEffect, but GBA task scheduling replaced with TS timer logic

#### Missing
- [ ] `Drought_InitVars` / `Drought_InitAll` / `Drought_Main` / `Drought_Finish` — drought palette loading state machine (`ResetDroughtWeatherPaletteLoading`, `LoadDroughtWeatherPalettes`, `DroughtStateInit`, `DroughtStateRun`) not implemented; TS has no drought palette brightening effect
- [ ] `StartDroughtWeatherBlend` / `UpdateDroughtBlend` — GBA GPU register manipulation for BLDCNT/BLDY bright-flash then fade-back on drought intro; no TS equivalent
- [ ] `UpdateThunderSound` / `EnqueueThunder` — GBA SE sound queuing for `SE_THUNDER` / `SE_THUNDER2` with random delay; no audio system in TS port
- [ ] `SetRainStrengthFromSoundEffect` (called from Rain/Thunderstorm/Downpour InitVars) — sets rain sound effect; no audio in TS
- [ ] `UpdateRainCounter` — increments `GAME_STAT_GOT_RAINED_ON` game stat; game stat tracking not implemented
- [ ] `WaitSnowflakeSprite` — `gWeatherPtr->snowflakeTimer` is noted as never incremented in C (dead wait); irrelevant but not ported
- [ ] `WaitSandSwirlSpriteEntrance` — entrance delay countdown for swirl sprites; missing with swirl sprites
- [ ] `FogHorizontalSpriteCallback` (sprite-level x/y2 repositioning using `fogHScrollPosX`) — TS FogLayerEffect uses CSS transform rather than per-sprite GBA coord offsets; functionally approximated but not structurally equivalent
- [ ] `UpdateFogDiagonalSprite` — per-sprite repositioning using `fogDBaseSpritesX`/`fogDPosY`; same approximation caveat
- [ ] `UpdateCloudSprite` — moves cloud 1px left every 2 frames using `coordOffsetEnabled`; TS CloudLayerEffect uses canvas scroll instead
- [ ] `sWeatherCycleRoute119` / `sWeatherCycleRoute123` data tables — generated into runtime.gen.ts via generator script; covered

#### Notes
- The generator script (`generate-weather-runtime.cjs`) reads this file and `field_weather.c` together, extracting palette and state data into `src/weather/runtime.gen.ts`. Per-sprite GBA animation callbacks are replaced by canvas/CSS-based particle systems in TS — the visual intent is preserved but the per-sprite fixed-point arithmetic and `coordOffsetEnabled` GBA flag mechanics are not replicated 1:1.
- All GBA hardware registers (`REG_BLDCNT`, `REG_BLDY`, `REG_WININ`) in `UpdateDroughtBlend` have no TS equivalent; drought is the most under-ported weather type.
- `WEATHER_UNDERWATER` reuses `FogHorizontal_*` functions directly in C; TS FogLayerEffect handles both.
- Sound effects (`SE_RAIN`, `SE_THUNDERSTORM`, `SE_DOWNPOUR`, `SE_THUNDER`, `SE_THUNDER2`) are all omitted as the TS port has no audio system.

- [-] `InitBattlePyramidMap` — Battle Pyramid floor layout generation (`GenerateBattlePyramidFloorLayout`) has no direct TS equivalent; Trainer Hill analog exists but Battle Pyramid is unimplemented
- [-] `SaveMapView` / `LoadSavedMapView` / `MoveMapViewToBackup` — saved map view (destructible tiles persisted across map transitions) has no clear equivalent; `SaveManager` / `SaveStateStore` handle general save data but not the per-tile `mapView` buffer logic
- [-] `CopyMapTilesetsToVram` / `CopyPrimaryTilesetToVram` / `CopySecondaryTilesetToVram` — tileset upload is handled by `TilesetUploader.ts` / `WebGLTextureManager.ts` but the primary/secondary split logic is in `WorldManager` rather than a dedicated fieldmap equivalent

#### Missing
- [ ] `InitBackupMapLayoutData` — copies raw map data into backup buffer with MAP_OFFSET padding; TS map loading doesn't replicate this exact border-padded flat buffer structure (uses a different coordinate model)
- [ ] `SavedMapViewIsEmpty` / `ClearSavedMapView` — helpers for the mapView dirty-tile persistence system; not ported
- [ ] `SkipCopyingMetatileFromSavedMap` — skips restoring tiles that are part of a large breakable decoration; not ported (large decorations not fully implemented)
- [ ] `LoadTilesetPalette` / `LoadPrimaryTilesetPalette` / `LoadSecondaryTilesetPalette` / `LoadMapTilesetPalettes` — GBA palette slot management; browser uses CSS/WebGL texture palettes, no direct equivalent
- [ ] `CopyTilesetToVram` / `CopyTilesetToVramUsingHeap` — GBA VRAM tile copy; not applicable to browser (handled by `TilesetUploader.ts` at a higher abstraction)
- [ ] `GetIncomingConnection` / `IsPosInIncomingConnectingMap` / `IsCoordInIncomingConnectingMap` — internal helpers for `CameraMove` to find the matching connection when crossing a border; the TS `mapConnections.ts` implements equivalent logic but by a different code path
- [ ] `SetPositionFromConnection` — repositions player when crossing into adjacent map; logic exists implicitly in `WarpHandler.ts` / `CameraController.ts` but not as a named function

#### Notes
- The C code uses a flat `sBackupMapData` buffer with `MAP_OFFSET` (7-tile) borders on all sides. The TS port uses a coordinate system starting at (0,0) with no border padding (see MEMORY.md MAP_OFFSET trap). This is the most significant architectural divergence — many C index calculations using `MAP_OFFSET` have no direct TS counterpart.
- `SaveMapView` / `LoadSavedMapView` implement the Gen3 "destructible tile persistence" system (e.g., Cut bushes that stay cut after map re-entry). The TS port saves flag/var state but not the per-tile mapView buffer, so tile modifications may not persist correctly across map transitions.
- GBA hardware functions (`CpuFastFill16`, `CpuCopy16`, `LoadBgTiles`, `DecompressAndCopyTileDataToVram`, `LoadPalette`) are all intentionally omitted — they have no browser equivalent.
- `InitSecretBaseAppearance`, `SetOccupiedSecretBaseEntranceMetatiles`, `UpdateTVScreensOnMap` called from `InitMap`/`InitMapFromSavedGame` are not ported (Secret Base and TV features out of scope).
- `ClearMirageTowerPulseBlendEffect` called from `CameraMove` has a TS equivalent in `mirageTowerCollapseRuntime.ts` / `TransientMetatilePulseManager.ts`.

- [-] `SetCutGrassMetatiles` — long-grass root/top tile fixup logic (Fortree Secret Base variants, bottom-row root propagation) is not fully ported; basic tall→plain grass swap exists but not the directional top/bottom edge repair

- [ ] `StartCutGrassFieldEffect` / `StartCutTreeFieldEffect` — field effect active-list management and `SE_M_CUT` sound playback not wired up

#### Notes

- `IncrementGameStat` (GAME_STAT_USED_CUT) has no TS equivalent; game stats are not tracked in the port.
- The Hyper Cutter ability 5×5 area expansion is a notable missing gameplay mechanic — the port only handles the standard case.
- `FieldEffectStart` / `FieldEffectActiveListRemove` task scheduling maps conceptually to `FieldEffectManager.ts` but the Cut-specific task flow is not wired.
- Metatile label constants (`METATILE_General_TallGrass`, `METATILE_Fortree_LongGrass_Root`, etc.) are available via `metatileLabels.gen.ts`.

## batch: src/fldeff_escalator.c

### `src/fldeff_escalator.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `StartEscalator` — initiates the escalator animation task with direction flag
- [ ] `StopEscalator` — destroys the escalator animation task
- [ ] `IsEscalatorMoving` — returns whether escalator animation is still in progress
- [ ] `Task_DrawEscalator` — per-frame task cycling through 7 metatile sections (1F tiles 0–3, 2F tiles 0–2), advancing `tTransitionStage` after each full cycle
- [ ] `SetEscalatorMetatile` — scans 3×3 grid around player coords, replaces matching metatile at current transition stage with next stage (direction-aware for going up vs down)
- [ ] `CreateEscalatorTask` — allocates task, captures player dest coords, initialises state fields, runs first draw step

#### Notes
The escalator effect animates a 3×3 metatile region centered on the player's destination tile, cycling through 3 animation frames across 7 metatile "sections" (4 on 1F, 3 on 2F) per frame tick. Direction (`goingUp`) reverses the frame order. The TS port has `TransientMetatilePulseManager` and `tilesetAnimations` infrastructure but no Pokemon Center escalator-specific runtime. This is a niche field effect needed only when entering/exiting Pokemon Center 2F via escalator; it would fit as a special in `ScriptFieldEffectAnimationManager` or a dedicated `EscalatorEffect` module. The metatile IDs (`METATILE_PokemonCenter_Escalator*`) are covered by `src/data/metatileLabels.gen.ts`.

- [-] `TrySweetScentEncounter` — task loop waiting for palette fade then triggering encounter; wild encounter flow exists but the 64-frame wait loop with `ClearMirageTowerPulseBlendEffect` / `TryStartMirageTowerPulseBlendEffect` bracketing is not ported (`mirageTowerSpecials.ts` has the pulse effect but Sweet Scent doesn't hook into it)
- [-] `FailSweetScentEncounter` — restores palette and runs `EventScript_FailSweetScent`; the script execution path exists via `ScriptRunner.ts` but the palette restore + `SetWeatherPalStateIdle` equivalent on failure path is not wired up

The Sweet Scent field move is a composite of: HM/move use setup → palette fx sequence → wild encounter attempt → fail recovery script. The TS port has all three subsystems (field actions, wild encounters, script execution) but they are not wired together specifically for Sweet Scent. The Mirage Tower pulse suppression during the effect (`ClearMirageTowerPulseBlendEffect` / `TryStartMirageTowerPulseBlendEffect`) is a GBA-specific rendering concern that exists in `mirageTowerSpecials.ts` but is not called from a Sweet Scent flow. ## batch: src/fonts.c

### `src/fonts.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/core/prompt/PromptPrinterEngine.ts`, `src/core/prompt/textLayout.ts`, `src/core/prompt/PromptWindowProfiles.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] gFontNormalLatinGlyphs / gFontSmallLatinGlyphs (glyph bitmaps) — font rendering handled via canvas text rendering in `PromptPrinterEngine.ts`; proportional spacing logic present in `textLayout.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] gFontNormalLatinGlyphWidths / gFontSmallLatinGlyphWidths / gFontNarrowLatinGlyphWidths / gFontShortLatinGlyphWidths — per-glyph advance widths exist in GBA charset; TS uses canvas `measureText` rather than per-glyph lookup tables, so exact GBA pixel-width parity is approximated, not reproduced from these tables
- [-] gFontSmallNarrowLatinGlyphs — "small narrow" variant used in battle textboxes; `BattleTextboxSkin.ts` references a font variant but doesn't replicate the exact narrow glyph metrics

#### Missing
- [ ] gFontSmallJapaneseGlyphs / gFontNormalJapaneseGlyphs — Japanese kana/kanji glyph bitmaps; not ported (JP locale not targeted)
- [ ] gFontFRLGMaleJapaneseGlyphs / gFontFRLGMaleJapaneseGlyphWidths — FRLG-style full-width Japanese male font; not ported
- [ ] gFontFRLGFemaleJapaneseGlyphs / gFontFRLGFemaleJapaneseGlyphWidths — FRLG-style full-width Japanese female font; not ported
- [ ] gFontShortJapaneseGlyphs / gFontShortJapaneseGlyphWidths — short-height Japanese font; not ported

#### Notes
The GBA font system embeds raw 1bpp glyph bitmaps and per-character pixel-width tables used for proportional rendering. The TS port delegates all text rendering to the browser canvas API (`fillText`/`measureText`) using a custom GBA-style bitmap font loaded as a spritesheet, so these exact width tables are never consulted at runtime. Japanese locale support is intentionally omitted. The four Latin glyph-width tables (small-narrow, small, narrow, short, normal) represent distinct font variants used in different window contexts (battle box vs. field dialog vs. menus); the TS port approximates this via `PromptWindowProfiles` font size selection rather than switching literal glyph tables.

*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `ShowFrontierPass` — entry point: allocates data, sets main callback to init sequence
- [ ] `AllocateFrontierPassData` — allocates & initializes pass state (cursor pos, battle points, trainer stars, facility symbols from flags)
- [ ] `FreeFrontierPassData` — frees pass state
- [ ] `AllocateFrontierPassGfx` / `FreeFrontierPassGfx` — allocates/frees GFX struct (tilemaps, sprite refs)
- [ ] `InitFrontierPass` — 11-step init state machine: resets GPU/sprites, loads tilemaps, inits BGs/windows, palette fade-in
- [ ] `HideFrontierPass` — 6-step teardown state machine: fade-out, free sprites/tilemaps, reset GPU
- [ ] `CB2_InitFrontierPass` / `CB2_FrontierPass` / `CB2_HideFrontierPass` — main loop callbacks
- [ ] `CB2_ReshowFrontierPass` — re-enters pass after returning from map/card/record sub-screen
- [ ] `CB2_ReturnFromRecord` — callback after watching recorded battle; restores pass state, replays BGM
- [ ] `CB2_ShowFrontierPassFeature` — dispatches to map / recorded battle / trainer card after zoom-out
- [ ] `Task_HandleFrontierPassInput` — main input task: D-pad moves cursor sprite, A selects area, B cancels
- [ ] `GetCursorAreaFromCoords` — hit-tests cursor (x,y) against `sPassAreasLayout` table; returns cursor area enum
- [ ] `TryCallPassAreaFunction` — on A-press, dispatches to zoom task or record viewer based on cursor area
- [ ] `Task_PassAreaZoom` — 3-state zoom animation for Map/Card areas using BG affine transform + palette fade
- [ ] `ShowAndPrintWindows` — fills all pass windows (symbols label, battle record label, BP count, description)
- [ ] `PrintAreaDescription` — prints contextual description text for current cursor area
- [ ] `UpdateAreaHighlight` — swaps tilemap rects to highlight/unhighlight Map/Card/Record/Cancel areas
- [ ] `DrawFrontierPassBg` — initial BG tilemap draw + highlight + zoom-area setup
- [ ] `LoadCursorAndSymbolSprites` — loads cursor sprite + medal sprites for earned facility symbols
- [ ] `FreeCursorAndSymbolSprites` — destroys cursor and medal sprites, frees palettes/tiles
- [ ] `ShowHideZoomingArea` — copies/clears affine-BG tilemap rect for the zooming Map or Card area; sets BG affine
- [ ] `VBlankCB_FrontierPass` — VBlank: updates BG2 affine during zoom, flushes OAM/palettes
- [ ] `ShowFrontierMap` — allocates map data, creates frontier map task
- [ ] `FreeFrontierMap` — frees map data struct, restores callback
- [ ] `InitFrontierMap` — 8-step init for the facility map sub-screen (BGs, windows, tilemap, sprites, fade-in)
- [ ] `ExitFrontierMap` — 6-step teardown for facility map (fade-out, destroy sprites, reset GPU)
- [ ] `Task_HandleFrontierMap` — input task for facility map: B exits, D-pad scrolls cursor between 7 facilities
- [ ] `HandleFrontierMapCursorMove` — updates cursor position, facility name highlights, map indicator sprite, description text
- [ ] `PrintOnFrontierMap` — prints all facility names (selected in red, others white) + description of selected
- [ ] `InitFrontierMapSprites` — creates cursor sprite, map-indicator sprite, and player-head sprite (positioned from save data)
- [ ] `MapNumToFrontierFacilityId` — maps current map number to one of the 7 frontier facility IDs
- [ ] `SpriteCB_PlayerHead` — no-op sprite callback for the player head icon on the map

#### Notes
The Frontier Pass is a post-game UI feature (accessed after reaching the Battle Frontier) showing earned facility symbols, battle points, trainer card, a recorded battle viewer, and an interactive facility map. The TS port has `src/scripting/runtime/frontierState.ts` which stores frontier-related flags/vars, but no UI rendering or input handling for the Frontier Pass screen exists. The entire screen — GBA-specific multi-BG layering, affine zoom animations, sprite-based cursor, and sub-screen transitions — has no browser equivalent. Battle Frontier content is generally out of scope for the MVP (as noted in project memory), so this is an intentional omission. The facility symbol flags (`FLAG_SYS_TOWER_SILVER`, `FLAG_SYS_TOWER_GOLD`, etc.) are present in the generated `src/data/flagVarMaps.gen.ts`.

#### Notes
- The entire Battle Frontier is unimplemented in the TS port. `src/scripting/runtime/frontierState.ts` exists as a placeholder but contains no real logic.
- All 23 entries in `sFrontierUtilFuncs` dispatch table are called as `special` commands from generated map scripts across all Battle Frontier facility `.gen.ts` files — these will silently no-op or error at runtime until implemented.
- Link cable features (`BATTLE_TYPE_LINK_MULTIS`, e-Reader trainer, `gLinkPlayers`, record mixing, apprentice system) are intentionally out of scope for browser port.
- UI rendering functions (`ShowTowerResultsWindow` etc.) use GBA window/tilemap system with no direct browser equivalent — would need reimplementing as React/Canvas UI.
- `SaveGameFrontier` uses GBA flash save (`TrySavingData`) — the TS save system (`src/save/SaveManager.ts`) would need a frontier-specific save path.
- The `gFrontierBannedSpecies` list (Mew, Mewtwo, Ho-Oh, Lugia, Celebi, Kyogre, Groudon, Rayquaza, Jirachi, Deoxys) is needed by `CheckPartyIneligibility` and would likely be a small data constant when implemented.

## batch: src/gpu_regs.c

### `src/gpu_regs.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `InitGpuRegManager` — initializes buffered register state and waiting list
- [ ] `CopyBufferedValueToGpuReg` — flushes a single buffered register value to hardware
- [ ] `CopyBufferedValuesToGpuRegs` — flushes all pending buffered registers to hardware (called from VBlank)
- [ ] `SetGpuReg` — writes to register buffer, defers or immediately applies based on VCount/forced-blank
- [ ] `SetGpuReg_ForcedBlank` — like `SetGpuReg` but only copies immediately when forced-blank is active
- [ ] `GetGpuReg` — reads DISPSTAT/VCOUNT from hardware, all others from buffer
- [ ] `SetGpuRegBits` — RMW set bits on a buffered register
- [ ] `ClearGpuRegBits` — RMW clear bits on a buffered register
- [ ] `EnableInterrupts` — sets bits in shadow IE register and syncs to hardware
- [ ] `DisableInterrupts` — clears bits in shadow IE register and syncs to hardware
- [ ] `SyncRegIE` — atomically writes shadow IE to REG_IE via IME disable/enable
- [ ] `UpdateRegDispstatIntrBits` — mirrors VBLANK/HBLANK IE flags into DISPSTAT

#### Notes
This entire file is GBA hardware register management — VCount-synchronized buffered writes to memory-mapped I/O registers (REG_DISPCNT, REG_DISPSTAT, REG_IE, etc.), interrupt enable management, and VBlank deferred flushing. None of this has a browser equivalent: the browser port uses WebGL/Canvas and the browser's own event loop with `requestAnimationFrame`. All rendering state is managed through WebGL context calls (`WebGLContext.ts`, `WebGLRenderPipeline.ts`) and the `GameLoop.ts`/`AnimationTimer.ts` engine layer. The interrupt system (`EnableInterrupts`/`DisableInterrupts`/`SyncRegIE`) is intentionally omitted — the browser has no hardware interrupt model. This file is a complete intentional omission with no missing gameplay logic.

- [ ] `gCableCarBg_*` / `gCableCar_*` — cable car scene graphics; cable car cutscene not implemented (visual assets missing though `LavaridgeWarpSequencer.ts` exists for the warp logic)
- [ ] `gUnionRoomChat_*` — Union Room chat graphics; Union Room is link-cable feature, intentionally omitted
- [ ] `gSummaryMoveSelect_*` — summary screen move-select overlay; partial summary screen exists but move select cursor is missing

- [ ] `gMonMarkingsMenu_*` — Pokémon markings menu; not implemented
- [ ] `gSwapLineGfx` / `gSwapLinePal` — item swap line; not implemented
- [ ] `gPokenavRibbonsSummaryBg_*` — PokéNav ribbons; not implemented

- [ ] `gUnused*` / `gBattleAnimUnused*` — all unused/leftover graphics; intentionally omitted

#### Notes
- `src/graphics.c` contains **no executable logic** — it is purely a binary data blob registry for all graphical assets loaded via `INCBIN_*` macros. The GBA stores everything compiled-in; the browser port loads assets as HTTP resources from `public/` at runtime.
- The TS port does not replicate this file at all — asset paths are referenced as URL strings in TypeScript modules (`src/data/spriteMetadata.ts`, `src/utils/assetLoader.ts`, etc.).
- All battle animation sprites (~200 entries) are present as PNG files under `public/graphics/` but the battle animation playback system is unimplemented — `BattleScriptRuntime.ts` exists but does not drive sprite rendering.
- Contest, Battle Frontier, Pokédex, PokéNav, naming screen, storage system, link cable features (trade, Berry Blender, Berry Crush, Union Room) are all out of scope for the current MVP and their graphics are intentionally not ported.
- `gTilesetTiles_General` / `gTilesetPalettes_General` are the only runtime-critical assets here and they are loaded correctly via the map rendering pipeline.

#### Notes
- The Hall of Fame screen (post-Elite Four) and the in-game HOF PC viewer are both entirely absent from the TS port.
- `StartCredits` → `CB2_StartCreditsSequence` (credits roll) is also unimplemented.
- Flash-sector save logic (`SAVE_HALL_OF_FAME`, `LoadGameSave`, `TrySavingData`) has no direct TS equivalent; the port uses `SaveManager`/`localStorage` but has no HOF-specific save slot.
- Hardware concerns (GBA BG layers, DMA palette fills, affine OAM matrices) are intentionally irrelevant to the browser port, but all the gameplay-facing logic (team recording, sequential display, player info) is still missing.
- `DoDomeConfetti` / `ConfettiUtil` is Battle Frontier–specific; Battle Frontier is out of scope for the current MVP.

## batch: src/heal_location.c

### `src/heal_location.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/WorldManager.ts`, `src/save/SaveManager.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [-] GetHealLocationIndexByMap — partial: heal location lookup logic exists implicitly in warp/respawn handling but no dedicated indexed lookup table

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] GetHealLocationByMap — warp destinations and Pokemon Center respawn points are handled in `WorldManager.ts` and `WarpExecutor.ts`, but not via a formal `sHealLocations` table lookup; the data is embedded in map warp data rather than a separate heal location registry

#### Missing
- [ ] GetHealLocation — no TS equivalent for index-based heal location retrieval; `sHealLocations` data table from `data/heal_locations.h` has no generated or hand-ported equivalent
- [ ] sHealLocations data table — the static array mapping map groups/nums to respawn coordinates is not generated or ported; no generator script consumes `data/heal_locations.h` or `include/constants/heal_locations.h`

#### Notes
The heal location system (white-out respawn, Fly destination logic) is not formally implemented. Pokemon Centers set a "last heal point" in the C engine via `SetHealLocation`/`GetHealLocation` which feeds into the white-out warp and Fly HM destination. The TS port lacks a dedicated heal location registry; Pokemon Center healing scripts exist in generated `.gen.ts` files but the respawn coordinate lookup is absent. This is a blocker for correct white-out behavior and Fly destination selection.

Hall of Fame PC is post-game content (accessed from the PC in the player's room after entering the Hall of Fame). The full Hall of Fame recording and playback system (`hall_of_fame.c`, `CB2_DoHallOfFamePC`) is also unimplemented. The PC menu system (`ScriptMenu_CreatePCMultichoice`, `ScriptMenu_DisplayPCStartupPrompt`) has partial scripting coverage via `src/scripting/menu/ScriptMenuHost.ts`, but the HoF-specific PC flow does not exist. Palette fade task pattern is available in `src/field/FadeController.ts` but the specific callback chain here has no equivalent.

## batch: src/image_processing_effects.c

### `src/image_processing_effects.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `ApplyImageProcessingEffects` — dispatch function applying one of 12 image effects to a canvas pixel buffer
- [ ] `ConvertImageProcessingToGBA` — converts processed canvas pixel data into GBA tile format (4bpp or 8bpp)
- [ ] `ApplyImageProcessingQuantization` — dispatch function for palette quantization effects
- [ ] `ApplyImageEffect_Pointillism` — applies pointillism dot pattern using precomputed point table
- [ ] `ApplyImageEffect_Blur` — vertical blur pass over canvas pixel rows
- [ ] `ApplyImageEffect_BlackOutline` — darkens pixels adjacent to transparent pixels to create black outline
- [ ] `ApplyImageEffect_Invert` — inverts all RGB channels per pixel
- [ ] `ApplyImageEffect_BlackAndWhite` — thresholds each pixel to pure black or white
- [ ] `ApplyImageEffect_BlurRight` — horizontal motion blur pass
- [ ] `ApplyImageEffect_BlurDown` — vertical motion blur pass
- [ ] `ApplyImageEffect_Shimmer` — invert → double blur → invert to create light-outline shimmer
- [ ] `ApplyImageEffect_Grayscale` — converts pixels to grayscale using luminance weights
- [ ] `ApplyImageEffect_PersonalityColor` — replaces dark pixels with a personality-derived color, others white
- [ ] `ApplyImageEffect_RedChannelGrayscale` — creates grayscale from red channel with brightness delta
- [ ] `ApplyImageEffect_RedChannelGrayscaleHighlight` — red-channel grayscale with highlight clamping
- [ ] `AddPointillismPoints` — applies color variation along a diagonal run of pixels
- [ ] `ConvertColorToGrayscale` — luminance-weighted grayscale conversion (0.3R + 0.59G + 0.11B)
- [ ] `QuantizePixel_PersonalityColor` — maps dark pixel to personality color, light pixel to white
- [ ] `GetColorFromPersonality` — maps personality byte to one of 6 hue variants at 3 strengths
- [ ] `QuantizePixel_BlackAndWhite` — thresholds pixel to RGB_BLACK or RGB_WHITE
- [ ] `QuantizePixel_BlackOutline` — returns black if neighbor is alpha, preserves otherwise
- [ ] `QuantizePixel_Invert` — per-channel 31-x inversion
- [ ] `QuantizePixel_MotionBlur` — darkens current pixel toward prev based on channel diff, skips light pixels
- [ ] `QuantizePixel_Blur` — softens pixel based on luminance diff with neighbors (soft factor)
- [ ] `QuantizePixel_BlurHard` — same as Blur but uses full diff (no halving) for harsher darkening
- [ ] `QuantizePixel_Standard` — quantizes channels to multiples of 4, clamps to [6, 30]
- [ ] `QuantizePixel_PrimaryColors` — maps pixel to nearest index in a 16-color primary palette
- [ ] `QuantizePixel_GrayscaleSmall` — maps pixel to 14-step small grayscale palette index
- [ ] `QuantizePixel_Grayscale` — maps pixel to 32-step grayscale palette index
- [ ] `QuantizePalette_Standard` — builds palette by scanning canvas, assigns palette indices to pixels
- [ ] `QuantizePalette_BlackAndWhite` — maps all canvas pixels to 2-color B&W palette indices
- [ ] `QuantizePalette_GrayscaleSmall` — maps all canvas pixels to small grayscale palette indices
- [ ] `QuantizePalette_Grayscale` — maps all canvas pixels to full 32-step grayscale palette indices
- [ ] `QuantizePalette_PrimaryColors` — maps all canvas pixels to primary color palette indices
- [ ] `SetPresetPalette_PrimaryColors` — writes 16-entry primary color preset into canvas palette
- [ ] `SetPresetPalette_Grayscale` — writes 33-entry full grayscale preset into canvas palette
- [ ] `SetPresetPalette_GrayscaleSmall` — writes 16-entry small grayscale preset into canvas palette
- [ ] `SetPresetPalette_BlackAndWhite` — writes 3-entry B&W preset into canvas palette

#### Notes
This file implements the Contest Painting image processing pipeline — all effects shown on Pokémon paintings in the Lilycove Contest Hall. The entire system is unused by the browser port: there is no contest painting feature, no GBA tile packing (`ConvertImageProcessingToGBA`), and no canvas pixel buffer infrastructure. This is a reasonable intentional omission as contest paintings are a cosmetic post-game feature with no gameplay impact on the critical path.

## batch: src/international_string_util.c

### `src/international_string_util.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/core/prompt/textLayout.ts`, `src/core/prompt/PromptPrinterEngine.ts`, `src/components/dialog/textPagination.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `GetStringCenterAlignXOffset` — center alignment offset calculation; covered by text layout logic in `PromptPrinterEngine.ts`/`textLayout.ts`
- [x] `GetStringRightAlignXOffset` — right-align offset; analogous logic present in prompt/text layout
- [x] `GetStringCenterAlignXOffsetWithLetterSpacing` — variant with letter spacing; handled in text layout engine
- [x] `GetStringWidthDifference` — computes leftover width; underlying primitive used in text layout

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `GetMaxWidthInMenuTable` / `GetMaxWidthInSubsetOfMenuTable` — dynamic menu width sizing based on string widths; menu layout (`src/menu/hooks/useMenuLayout.ts`) does compute widths but uses fixed tile grid rather than per-glyph measurement against `MenuAction` tables
- [-] `Intl_GetListMenuWidth` — auto-sizing list menus by max item width; `OptionMenu.tsx`/`ScriptChoiceMenuContent.tsx` render lists but do not dynamically measure glyph widths to clamp to 28 tiles

#### Missing
- [ ] `CopyMonCategoryText` — appends " Pokémon" category string for Pokédex display; Pokédex not yet implemented
- [ ] `GetStringClearToWidth` — pads a string with an EXT_CTRL_CODE_CLEAR escape to fill to a fixed width; GBA font escape codes not used in browser port
- [ ] `PadNameString` — pads player name to `PLAYER_NAME_LENGTH-1` with a pad character or font-reset codes; name padding with GBA escape codes not implemented
- [ ] `ConvertInternationalPlayerName` — converts short player names to Japanese encoding; multi-language encoding not needed in browser port
- [ ] `ConvertInternationalPlayerNameStripChar` — variant that strips a trailing char after conversion; same as above
- [ ] `ConvertInternationalContestantName` — appends ENG escape code after JPN block for contest contestants; Pokémon Contests not implemented
- [ ] `TVShowConvertInternationalString` — copies and converts string for TV show display; TV shows not implemented
- [ ] `GetNicknameLanguage` — detects Japanese vs English nickname by leading escape code; no multi-language nickname support
- [ ] `FillWindowTilesByRow` — clears rows of GBA window tile data with 4BPP fill; GBA tile window system has no browser equivalent

#### Notes
The browser port uses HTML/Canvas/WebGL text rendering rather than GBA font tiles and escape-code sequences (`EXT_CTRL_CODE_BEGIN`, `EXT_CTRL_CODE_CLEAR`, `EXT_CTRL_CODE_JPN/ENG`), so all functions that manipulate raw GBA font escape codes (`PadNameString`, `GetStringClearToWidth`, the `ConvertInternational*` family, `FillWindowTilesByRow`) are intentionally omitted — they have no meaningful browser analog. Multi-language/Japanese support and Pokémon Contests/TV Shows are out of scope for the current MVP.

- [ ] `Task_Scene3_LoadKyogre` / `Task_Scene3_Kyogre` — Kyogre scene with bubble sprites, circular movement, cry

- [ ] `SpriteCB_Volbeat` — Volbeat figure-8 flight pattern using sin/cos
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Torchic` — Torchic run/walk/trip animation states
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Manectric` — Manectric enter and circular run-off
- [ ] `SpriteCB_PlayerOnBicycle` — player sprite x/y movement states during bike ride
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Flygon` — Flygon entry arc using sin offset
- [ ] `CreateGroudonRockSprites` / `SpriteCB_GroudonRocks` — floating rock sprites for Groudon scene
- [ ] `CreateKyogreBubbleSprites_Body` / `CreateKyogreBubbleSprites_Fins` / `SpriteCB_KyogreBubbles` — bubble animation for Kyogre scene
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Lightning` — lightning bolt sprite with palette cycling
- [ ] `SpriteCB_RayquazaOrb` — Rayquaza energy orb with affine scale pulse
- [ ] `PanFadeAndZoomScreen` — BG affine pan/zoom via `BgAffineSet` (GBA hardware-specific)
- [ ] `Task_BlendLogoIn` / `Task_BlendLogoOut` — BLDALPHA-based logo blend in/out
- [ ] `IntroResetGpuRegs` / `VBlankCB_Intro` / `MainCB2_Intro` — GBA hardware register management and VBlank callback
- [ ] `SerialCB_CopyrightScreen` / `GameCubeMultiBoot_*` — GameCube Colosseum multiboot (intentionally omitted; no browser equivalent)
- [ ] `CB2_InitCopyrightScreenAfterTitleScreen` — re-entry point from title screen back to copyright

#### Notes
- The entire intro cinematic (`src/intro.c`) has no TypeScript equivalent. The browser port skips directly to the title screen.
- All GBA hardware-specific code (GPU registers, DMA, VBlank callbacks, OAM matrix manipulation, scanline effects, affine BG transforms, palette buffer management) has no browser equivalent and is intentionally omitted.
- GameCube multiboot / Pokémon Colosseum link functionality is intentionally omitted (no browser equivalent).
- `gIntroFrameCounter` is a global frame timer driving the entire cinematic — not ported.
- The intro music (`MUS_INTRO`, `MUS_INTRO_BATTLE`) and cries (`PlayCryInternal`) are not implemented.
- `sIntroCharacterGender` (random gender selection for bike rider) is not ported; gender selection exists elsewhere in the new game flow.
- If intro cinematics are desired, they would need to be reimplemented as canvas/WebGL animations using sprite sheets extracted from the ROM assets.
