### `src/intro_credits_graphics.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `LoadIntroPart2Graphics` — loads LZ77-compressed BG tilesets/tilemaps for intro bicycle scene (grass, trees/clouds), creates scenery sprites
- [ ] `SetIntroPart2BgCnt` — configures GBA BG control registers for intro scene 2 (BG1/2/3 priorities, char/screen bases)
- [ ] `LoadCreditsSceneGraphics` — loads palettes and sprite sheets for each credits scene variant (ocean morning/sunset, forest, city night)
- [ ] `SetCreditsSceneBgCnt` — configures GBA BG control registers for credits scenes
- [ ] `CreateBicycleBgAnimationTask` — creates task driving parallax BG scroll during bicycle ride (3 BG layers at different speeds)
- [ ] `Task_BicycleBgAnimation` — per-frame task: advances BG1/2/3 horizontal scroll positions using fixed-point arithmetic, applies vertical offset
- [ ] `CycleSceneryPalette` — alternates palette entries every 4 frames to animate water/ocean shimmer (two modes)
- [ ] `SpriteCB_MovingScenery` — per-frame sprite callback: scrolls cloud/tree/house scenery sprites left, wraps at x>255, applies vertical parallax offset
- [ ] `CreateMovingScenerySprites` — instantiates a batch of parallax scenery sprites from metadata table (position, anim, shape, size, xOff)
- [ ] `CreateCloudSprites` — spawns 9 cloud sprites via `CreateMovingScenerySprites`
- [ ] `CreateTreeSprites` — spawns 12 tree sprites with vertical move enabled
- [ ] `CreateHouseSprites` — spawns 6 house silhouette sprites with vertical move enabled
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Player` — no-op player sprite callback (player is stationary during credits ride)
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Bicycle` — syncs bicycle sprite position/visibility to paired player sprite + 8px Y offset
- [ ] `CreateIntroBrendanSprite` — spawns Brendan + bicycle sprite pair, links them via `sPlayerSpriteId`
- [ ] `CreateIntroMaySprite` — spawns May + bicycle sprite pair, links them via `sPlayerSpriteId`
- [ ] `SpriteCB_FlygonLeftHalf` — no-op callback for left half of Flygon sprite
- [ ] `SpriteCB_FlygonRightHalf` — syncs right half of Flygon sprite to left half's position/visibility
- [ ] `CreateIntroFlygonSprite` — spawns two 64×64 Flygon half-sprites side by side, links right to left via `sLeftSpriteId`
- [ ] `CreateIntroFlygonSprite_Unused` — unused RS-era Latios version of above (marked `UNUSED`)

#### Notes
This file is entirely GBA-hardware-specific intro/credits presentation code. All functionality involves direct VRAM writes (`LZ77UnCompVram`), GBA GPU register configuration (`SetGpuReg`), the GBA sprite/OAM system, and GBA task system — none of which have browser equivalents. The intro sequence and credits roll are not implemented in the TypeScript port at all. Intentional omission: the browser port has no intro animation or credits sequence. The global state variables `gIntroCredits_MovingSceneryVBase`, `gIntroCredits_MovingSceneryVOffset`, and `gIntroCredits_MovingSceneryState` are also unported.

## batch: src/io_reg.c

### `src/io_reg.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/rendering/webgl/WebGLOrbEffectRenderer.ts`, `src/rendering/webgl/WebGLShaders.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `gOverworldBackgroundLayerFlags` — BG blend target flags for overworld layers; equivalent blend/alpha compositing logic exists in WebGL compositor and layer rendering pipeline
- [x] `gOrbEffectBackgroundLayerFlags` — BG blend source flags for orb effects; orb effect rendering implemented in `WebGLOrbEffectRenderer.ts` with equivalent blending

#### Missing
- [ ] `sUnused` — unused GBA hardware register bitmask table; intentionally omitted (no browser equivalent)

#### Notes
This file is almost entirely GBA hardware I/O register configuration (BLDCNT blend control registers). The browser port replaces these hardware blend modes with WebGL alpha blending and framebuffer compositing. `sUnused` is dead code even in the original. The two exported arrays define which BG layers participate in blending — this concept is preserved architecturally in the WebGL render pipeline's layer compositor, though not as explicit flag arrays.

- [x] `CheckBagHasItem` — checks if bag contains itemId with at least count quantity (`BagManager.hasItem`)
- [x] `CheckBagHasSpace` — checks if bag has room for more items (`BagManager.hasSpace`)
- [x] `AddBagItem` — adds item to correct pocket, stacking up to slot capacity (`BagManager.addItem`)
- [x] `RemoveBagItem` — removes item quantity from bag, clearing empty slots (`BagManager.removeItem`)
- [x] `GetItemPocket` — returns which pocket an item belongs to (via generated item data)
- [x] `GetItemHoldEffect` — returns hold effect id (via generated `itemBattleEffects.gen.ts`)
- [x] `GetItemHoldEffectParam` — returns hold effect parameter (via generated data)

- [x] `GetItemPrice` — returns item price (via generated item data)
- [x] `GetItemBattleUsage` — returns battle usability flag (via generated item data)
- [x] `CountTotalItemQuantityInBag` — sums all quantities of an item across pocket slots
- [x] `CompactItemsInBagPocket` — removes gaps from pocket by moving empty slots to end
- [x] `SortBerriesOrTMHMs` — sorts berries/TMs by itemId
- [x] `ClearBag` — empties all bag pockets
- [x] `BagGetItemIdByPocketPosition` — returns itemId at a given pocket position
- [x] `BagGetQuantityByPocketPosition` — returns quantity at a given pocket position
- [x] `MoveItemSlotInList` — reorders item slots (used by bag menu drag)

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `SetBagItemsPointers` / `SetBagPockets` — C version links save block memory pointers; TS `BagManager` holds data in plain JS object rather than XOR-encrypted save block slots; encryption is absent
- [-] `GetItemDescription` — description strings exist via `src/data/itemDescriptions.ts` (generated) but the lookup indirection is different
- [-] `HasAtLeastOneBerry` — logic exists but `gSpecialVar_Result` side-effect not wired to script variable system identically

#### Missing
- [ ] `ApplyNewEncryptionKeyToBagItems` / `ApplyNewEncryptionKeyToBagItems_` — XOR re-encryption of bag quantities on save key rotation; not relevant for browser save format
- [ ] `GetBagItemQuantity` / `SetBagItemQuantity` — XOR encryption helpers; TS stores quantities in plain numbers
- [ ] `GetPCItemQuantity` / `SetPCItemQuantity` — PC item quantity helpers (no encryption needed, but PC item storage is absent)
- [ ] `FindFreePCItemSlot` — finds empty slot in Bill's PC item storage
- [ ] `CountUsedPCItemSlots` — counts occupied PC item slots
- [ ] `CheckPCHasItem` — checks Bill's PC for an item
- [ ] `AddPCItem` — adds item to Bill's PC storage
- [ ] `RemovePCItem` — removes item from Bill's PC by slot index
- [ ] `CompactPCItems` — compacts PC item list after removal
- [ ] `SwapRegisteredBike` — toggles registered item between Mach Bike and Acro Bike
- [ ] `CheckPyramidBagHasItem` — Battle Pyramid bag item check
- [ ] `CheckPyramidBagHasSpace` — Battle Pyramid bag space check
- [ ] `AddPyramidBagItem` — adds item to Battle Pyramid bag
- [ ] `RemovePyramidBagItem` — removes item from Battle Pyramid bag
- [ ] `ClearItemSlots` — zeroes out an ItemSlot array (low-level utility)
- [ ] `GetItemId` — returns itemId from item table (unused in C, likely not needed)
- [ ] `GetItemImportance` — returns importance flag (key item designation)
- [ ] `GetItemRegistrability` — returns whether item can be registered to Select (unused in C)
- [ ] `GetItemType` — returns item type field
- [ ] `GetItemFieldFunc` / `GetItemBattleFunc` — function pointer lookups; TS handles these via `src/data/itemScripts.ts` and `src/battle/items/battleItemRules.ts` differently
- [ ] `GetItemSecondaryId` — returns secondaryId field
- [ ] `IsBagPocketNonEmpty` — checks if any slot in a pocket is occupied

#### Notes
- The XOR encryption on bag item quantities (`gSaveBlock2Ptr->encryptionKey`) is a GBA anti-tampering measure; the TS port stores quantities as plain numbers since browser save data doesn't face the same threat model.
- PC item storage (Bill's PC) is entirely absent — it's post-game/low-priority content.
- Battle Pyramid Bag is a Battle Frontier feature; the TS port has no Battle Frontier implementation, so all `Pyramid*` functions are intentionally unported.
- The `CurMapIsSecretBase()` check in `RemoveBagItem` that sets TV flags is missing — Secret Base TV features are unimplemented.
- Item data tables (`gItems`) are accessed via generated files (`src/data/items.ts`, `src/data/itemDescriptions.ts`, `src/data/itemBattleEffects.gen.ts`).

## batch: src/item_icon.c

### `src/item_icon.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/data/items.ts`, `src/menu/components/BagMenu.tsx`, `src/menu/components/PartySlot.tsx`
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-item-descriptions.cjs` (items data only)

#### Implemented
- [x] `GetItemIconPicOrPalette` — item icon asset lookup by itemId; TS resolves item icon image URLs/paths from generated item data in `src/data/items.ts` and renders them in menu components

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `AddItemIconSprite` — creates a GBA sprite for an item icon; TS equivalent is inline JSX `<img>` or canvas draw calls in `BagMenu.tsx`/`PartySlot.tsx` rather than a discrete sprite object; no tag/palette management needed in browser
- [-] `CopyItemIconPicTo4x4Buffer` — packs 3×32px tile rows into a 4×4 tile buffer due to GBA sprite layout constraints; not ported as a standalone function, but implicit in how item icon spritesheets are pre-processed offline for browser assets

#### Missing
- [ ] `AllocItemIconTemporaryBuffers` / `FreeItemIconTemporaryBuffers` — GBA EWRAM scratch buffer lifecycle; no browser equivalent needed
- [ ] `AddCustomItemIconSprite` — variant accepting a custom `SpriteTemplate`; no TS equivalent (browser rendering doesn't use sprite templates)
- [ ] `LZDecompressWram` call path — runtime LZ77 decompression of icon graphics; browser assets are pre-decompressed PNGs, so this step is intentionally omitted

#### Notes
The GBA sprite system (OAM, tile tags, palette tags, EWRAM decompression buffers) has no direct browser equivalent. The TS port resolves item icons as static image assets at build time rather than dynamically decompressing and loading sprites at runtime. The `CopyItemIconPicTo4x4Buffer` reshape step is a GBA-specific artifact (3 rows of 96 bytes → 4 rows of 128 bytes due to tile alignment) and is irrelevant in the browser. All gameplay-relevant functionality — mapping an item ID to its icon graphic — is covered.

- [-] `UseRegisteredKeyItemOnField` — SELECT button registered item use on field; `src/hooks/useActionInput.ts` handles registered item trigger but full lockout/freeze sequence for all edge cases (Battle Pyramid, Battle Pike, Union Room) is partial

- [ ] `CB2_BagMenuFromBattle` — battle-context bag entry with Battle Pyramid routing; battle bag is not accessible mid-battle in the port
- [ ] `CB2_ChooseBerry` / `ChooseBerryForMachine` — berry-specific bag entry for planting/blender; berry planting from bag not implemented
- [ ] `CB2_GoToSellMenu` / `CB2_GoToItemDepositMenu` — shop sell and PC deposit entry points; shop sell UI not fully wired
- [ ] `ApprenticeOpenBagMenu` / `ItemMenu_Show` / `CB2_ApprenticeExitBagMenu` — Apprentice choosing held item; Battle Frontier Apprentice feature not implemented
- [ ] `FavorLadyOpenBagMenu` / `ItemMenu_GiveFavorLady` / `CB2_FavorLadyExitBagMenu` — Favor Lady bag interaction; not implemented
- [ ] `QuizLadyOpenBagMenu` / `ItemMenu_ConfirmQuizLady` / `CB2_QuizLadyExitBagMenu` — Quiz Lady bag interaction; not implemented
- [ ] `DoWallyTutorialBagMenu` / `Task_WallyTutorialBagMenu` / `PrepareBagForWallyTutorial` / `RestoreBagAfterWallyTutorial` — automated Wally tutorial bag sequence; scripted bag automation not implemented (though `ScriptRunner.wallyTutorialParity.test.ts` exists suggesting partial work)
- [ ] `ItemMenu_CheckTag` / berry tag screen integration — opens berry tag screen from bag; `DoBerryTagScreen` callback not wired
- [ ] `Task_ItemContext_BerryBlenderCrush` context path — berry blender/crusher item selection; multiplayer feature, intentionally omitted
- [ ] `CB2_ReturnToBagMenuPocket` — re-enter bag retaining last pocket; `ITEMMENULOCATION_LAST` re-entry not implemented
- [ ] `DrawPocketIndicatorSquare` / `CreatePocketScrollArrowPair` / `CreatePocketSwitchArrowPair` — GBA tilemap/sprite UI details; browser UI uses CSS/React equivalents, no direct port needed but visual parity incomplete
- [ ] `BagMenu_YesNo` — Yes/No prompt within bag windows; DialogContext yes/no exists but not wired into bag-specific window slots
- [ ] `ItemMenu_UseInBattle` — in-battle item use dispatch; battle bag interaction not implemented

#### Notes
- GBA-specific rendering (BG tilemap buffer management, sprite IDs, `SetVBlankCallback`, `BlendPalettes`, OAM) is entirely omitted — the browser port uses React/CSS for bag UI layout
- Link cable / Union Room item restrictions (`MenuHelpers_IsLinkActive`, `InUnionRoom`) are intentionally absent
- Battle Pyramid bag (`GoToBattlePyramidBagMenu`) is not implemented
- The `ITEMMENULOCATION_WALLY` automated demo is partially tested via `ScriptRunner.wallyTutorialParity.test.ts` but the bag-level automation task (`Task_WallyTutorialBagMenu`) itself is not in TypeScript
- Item sell flow exists in BagMenu.tsx but may not fully match the C quantity-selection + money window sequence
- The swap-line visual (horizontal line showing drag position) rendered via sprites is not replicated in the browser UI

**Coverage**: Partial

**Generation script**: None

- [x] `AddBagItemIconSprite` / `RemoveBagItemIconSprite` — item icon management exists conceptually in `BagMenu.tsx` (React-rendered icons, not GBA sprites)
- [x] `sBerryPicTable` (data) — berry image assets referenced in `src/game/berry/berryConstants.ts` and `src/utils/berryTreeSpriteResolver.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `LoadBerryGfx` / `CreateBerryTagSprite` — berry sprite display exists (`src/utils/berryTreeSpriteImport.ts`, `src/utils/berryTreeSpriteResolver.ts`) but only for overworld berry trees; no bag/blender berry pic sprite
- [-] `AddBagVisualSprite` / `SetBagVisualPocketId` — bag pocket switching is implemented in `BagMenu.tsx` as tab UI, but lacks the animated 64×64 bag sprite with per-pocket frame animations

#### Missing
- [ ] `SpriteCB_BagVisualSwitchingPockets` — animated bag sprite bounces up when switching pockets
- [ ] `SpriteCB_ShakeBagSprite` / `ShakeBagSprite` — affine shake animation on the bag sprite (e.g. when item can't be used)
- [ ] `AddSwitchPocketRotatingBallSprite` / `SpriteCB_SwitchPocketRotatingBallInit` / `SpriteCB_SwitchPocketRotatingBallContinue` — rotating Poké Ball that spins when switching pockets
- [ ] `RemoveBagSprite` — sprite lifecycle cleanup
- [ ] `CreateSpinningBerrySprite` — spinning berry sprite for Berry Blender (multiplayer feature; intentionally omitted)
- [ ] `CreateBerryFlavorCircleSprite` — berry flavor circle sprite for Pokéblock/Contest screen
- [ ] `CreateItemMenuSwapLine` / `SetItemMenuSwapLineInvisibility` / `UpdateItemMenuSwapLinePos` — drag-to-reorder swap line sprites in the bag

#### Notes
The TS port renders the bag menu entirely in React/DOM rather than GBA OAM sprites, so animated bag sprites, rotating balls, and swap-line sprites have no direct equivalents. Berry Blender (`CreateSpinningBerrySprite`) and berry flavor circle (`CreateBerryFlavorCircleSprite`) are Contest/multiplayer features not in scope for the browser port. The swap line (`CreateItemMenuSwapLine`) would only be relevant if item reordering drag UI is implemented.

- [x] `ItemUseOutOfBattle_CannotUse` — "Dad's advice" cannot-use message; covered by `src/data/itemScripts.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `ItemUseOutOfBattle_Bike` — bike mount/dismount logic exists in `src/game/PlayerController.ts` (bike state) and `src/data/itemScripts.ts`, but rail-tile dismount check (`MetatileBehavior_IsVerticalRail`, `FLAG_SYS_CYCLING_ROAD`) and registered-key-item path are not fully wired; `IsBikingDisallowedByPlayer` check absent
- [-] `ItemUseOutOfBattle_WailmerPail` — berry-watering path exists in `src/game/berry/BerryManager.ts`; Sudowoodo-watering path (`TryToWaterSudowoodo` → `BattleFrontier_OutsideEast_EventScript_WaterSudowoodo`) has no TS equivalent beyond the generated script data
- [-] `ItemUseOutOfBattle_EnigmaBerry` / `ItemUseInBattle_EnigmaBerry` — Enigma Berry dispatch by `GetItemEffectType` not implemented; individual target functions exist but the routing switch is missing
- [-] `ItemUseOutOfBattle_Rod` — `CanFish()` tile-behavior checks (`IsWaterfall`, `IsSurfableWaterOrUnderwater`, `IsBridgeOverWaterNoEdge`) and `StartFishing` flow are not implemented; rod item is registered as a key item but fishing minigame is absent

#### Missing
- [ ] `ItemUseOutOfBattle_PokeblockCase` — opens Pokéblock case from bag or as registered item; no Pokéblock system in TS
- [ ] `ItemUseOutOfBattle_CoinCase` — displays coin count; no coin/Game Corner system in TS
- [ ] `ItemUseOutOfBattle_PowderJar` — displays Berry Powder count; no Berry Powder system in TS
- [ ] `ItemUseOutOfBattle_Mail` — opens mail viewer for bag mail; no mail system in TS
- [ ] `ItemUseOutOfBattle_Itemfinder` — Itemfinder scan + player spin + beep sequence; full `Task_UseItemfinder` / `Task_HiddenItemNearby` / `Task_StandingOnHiddenItem` / `ItemfinderCheckForHiddenItems` / `CheckForHiddenItemsInMapConnection` / `SetDistanceOfClosestHiddenItem` / `GetDirectionToHiddenItem` / `PlayerFaceHiddenItem` logic is absent
- [ ] `SetUpItemUseCallback` / `SetUpItemUseOnFieldCallback` / `FieldCB_UseItemOnField` / `Task_CallItemUseOnFieldCallback` — GBA task/callback orchestration layer; TS uses direct function calls rather than a task queue, so these have no direct equivalent
- [ ] `CheckIfItemIsTMHMOrEvolutionStone` — utility used by party menu to determine which party-menu flow to open; not ported as a standalone function
- [ ] `Task_UseDigEscapeRopeOnField` / `ItemUseOnFieldCB_EscapeRope` — `StartEscapeRopeFieldEffect` / `Overworld_ResetStateAfterDigEscRope` field effect callbacks; `src/game/FixedEscapeWarp.ts` handles the warp but not the visual field effect sequence
- [ ] `Task_StartUseRepel` / `Task_UseRepel` — delay + sound effect task sequence before applying repel; TS applies repel instantly without the SE delay
- [ ] `Task_UsedBlackWhiteFlute` — delay + glass flute sound effect task; no audio in TS

#### Notes
- The GBA "task" system (cooperative multitasking via `gTasks[]`) has no TS equivalent; all item-use flows that depend on multi-frame tasks (`tCounter`, `data[8]` delay loops) are either instantaneous or absent in TS.
- Registered key-item-on-field path (`tUsingRegisteredKeyItem`) — the TS port does not implement the separate registered-item activation flow from the overworld (no R-button shortcut).
- Battle Pyramid bag (`gPyramidBagMenu`, `CloseBattlePyramidBag`) is intentionally out of scope; Battle Frontier is not ported.
- Sound effects (`PlaySE`) are universally absent — no audio system in TS port.
- `CB2_OpenPokeblockFromBag` / `OpenPokeblockCase` — Pokéblock Contest system not ported.
- `IsHiddenItemPresentInConnection` — cross-map-connection Itemfinder logic is sophisticated (coordinate remapping per connection direction); entirely missing.

## batch: src/landmark.c

### `src/landmark.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `GetLandmarkName` — returns the visible landmark name for a map section/id/count triple, skipping entries whose flags are unset
- [ ] `GetLandmarks` — internal lookup: finds the landmark pointer array for a given `mapSection` + `id` pair in `sLandmarkLists`
- [ ] `sLandmarkLists` data table — 47 entries mapping `(mapSection, id)` → pointer array of `Landmark` structs with names and optional reveal-flags
- [ ] `FLAG_LANDMARK_*` flag set — ~30 named landmark flags (Flower Shop, Mr. Briney, Abandoned Ship, etc.) that gate whether a landmark name is shown

#### Notes
`landmark.c` drives the sub-area name overlay that appears in the bottom-left corner of the region map when the cursor is on a route that contains a notable location (e.g. "TRICK HOUSE", "WEATHER INSTITUTE"). It is a pure data + lookup module with no GBA-specific I/O. The reveal-flag mechanism means some landmarks only appear after the player has visited/unlocked them. The region map UI is not yet implemented in the TS port, so this entire file has no equivalent. Not intentionally omitted — it is simply unstarted. The `FLAG_LANDMARK_*` constants would need to be added to `src/data/flagVarMaps.gen.ts` (currently generated from `flags.h` but landmark flags may already be present there).

### `src/libisagbprn.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `AGBPrintInit` — initializes AGB Print debug output buffer in GBA ROM space
- [ ] `AGBPutc` / `AGBPutcInternal` — writes a single character to the AGB Print ring buffer
- [ ] `AGBPrint` / `AGBPrintf` — writes a string / formatted string to AGB Print
- [ ] `AGBPrintFlush1Block` / `AGBPrintFlush` — flushes buffered output to the emulator console
- [ ] `AGBAssert` — assertion handler that prints file/line/expression and halts via illegal opcode
- [ ] `NoCashGBAPrint` / `NoCashGBAPrintf` — writes string to no$gba debug address
- [ ] `NoCashGBAAssert` — assertion handler for no$gba
- [ ] `MgbaOpen` / `MgbaClose` — handshakes with mGBA debug register to enable/disable logging
- [ ] `MgbaPrintf` — writes a formatted string to mGBA's `REG_DEBUG_STRING` with log level
- [ ] `MgbaAssert` — assertion handler for mGBA

#### Notes
This file is entirely GBA hardware debug infrastructure — it writes directly to emulator-specific memory-mapped registers (`0x4FFF600`–`0x4FFF780` for mGBA, `0x4FFFA10`/`0x4FFFA14` for no$gba, and a ROM-space ring buffer for AGB Print). None of this is meaningful in a browser context. The browser port uses `src/utils/logger.ts` for all debug logging, which is architecturally equivalent in intent. All functions here are intentionally omitted — no gameplay logic exists in this file.

## batch: src/librfu_intr.c

### `src/librfu_intr.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `IntrSIO32` — GBA SIO32 interrupt handler, dispatches to master/slave clock handlers
- [ ] `sio32intr_clock_master` — master-mode SIO state machine (send req, wait ack, receive ack, done ack)
- [ ] `sio32intr_clock_slave` — slave-mode SIO state machine (receive req init, receive req, send ack, done ack)
- [ ] `handshake_wait` — busy-waits on SI line for clock synchronization, returns 1 on timeout
- [ ] `STWI_set_timer_in_RAM` — arms a GBA hardware timer for SIO timeout detection
- [ ] `STWI_stop_timer_in_RAM` — disarms the SIO hardware timer
- [ ] `STWI_init_slave` — resets STWI state struct and puts SIO peripheral into slave mode
- [ ] `Callback_Dummy_M` — naked trampoline: `bx r2` (calls master callback via register)
- [ ] `Callback_Dummy_S` — naked trampoline: `bx r1` (calls slave callback via register)
- [ ] `Callback_Dummy_ID` — naked trampoline: `bx r0` (calls ID callback via register)

#### Notes
This file is the GBA wireless adapter (RFU — RF Unit) interrupt-driven serial I/O driver. It implements a proprietary master/slave SIO32 protocol over the GBA link cable hardware (`REG_SIODATA32`, `REG_SIOCNT`, hardware timers `REG_TM0CNT`). There is no browser equivalent for any of this: the port has no link cable, no wireless adapter, and no multiplayer networking using this protocol. The entire file is intentionally omitted — all multiplayer/link features (Union Room, record mixing, link battles, e-Reader) are out of scope for the browser port.

## batch: src/librfu_rfu.c

### `src/librfu_rfu.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `rfu_initializeAPI` — initializes RFU API buffer, sets up slot/link status pointers
- [ ] `rfu_STC_clearAPIVariables` — clears all RFU state variables
- [ ] `rfu_REQ_stopMode` — stops RFU hardware mode
- [ ] `rfu_REQBN_softReset_and_checkID` — soft-resets RFU and checks device ID
- [ ] `rfu_REQ_reset` — sends hardware reset request
- [ ] `rfu_REQ_configSystem` — configures system slots and timer
- [ ] `rfu_REQ_configGameData` — configures game name/serial/username for wireless broadcast
- [ ] `rfu_REQ_startSearchChild` — begins scanning for child devices (host mode)
- [ ] `rfu_REQ_pollSearchChild` / `rfu_REQ_endSearchChild` — polls/ends child scan
- [ ] `rfu_REQ_startSearchParent` / `rfu_REQ_pollSearchParent` / `rfu_REQ_endSearchParent` — parent scan lifecycle
- [ ] `rfu_REQ_startConnectParent` / `rfu_REQ_pollConnectParent` / `rfu_REQ_endConnectParent` — connect to host
- [ ] `rfu_getConnectParentStatus` — reads connection status from STWI buffer
- [ ] `rfu_REQ_disconnect` — disconnects from slot(s)
- [ ] `rfu_REQ_CHILD_startConnectRecovery` / `rfu_REQ_CHILD_pollConnectRecovery` / `rfu_REQ_CHILD_endConnectRecovery` — child reconnection after link loss
- [ ] `rfu_CHILD_getConnectRecoveryStatus` — reads recovery status
- [ ] `rfu_syncVBlank` — watchdog tick for master/slave clock sync; disconnects on timeout
- [ ] `rfu_REQBN_watchLink` — link-loss detection, strength monitoring, emergency disconnect
- [ ] `rfu_clearAllSlot` — zeroes all NI/UNI slot status structures
- [ ] `rfu_clearSlot` — clears a specific slot by type (NI send/recv, UNI send/recv)
- [ ] `rfu_setRecvBuffer` — sets receive buffer pointer for NI or UNI slot
- [ ] `rfu_NI_setSendData` / `rfu_UNI_setSendData` / `rfu_NI_CHILD_setSendGameName` — queue outgoing data
- [ ] `rfu_changeSendTarget` — changes the target slot bitmask on an in-flight NI/UNI send
- [ ] `rfu_NI_stopReceivingData` — aborts an in-progress NI receive slot
- [ ] `rfu_UNI_changeAndReadySendData` / `rfu_UNI_readySendData` / `rfu_UNI_clearRecvNewDataFlag` — UNI data-ready management
- [ ] `rfu_REQ_sendData` — constructs and transmits the LL frame
- [ ] `rfu_REQ_recvData` — triggers a data receive request
- [ ] `rfu_constructSendLLFrame` — builds the low-level link frame from all active slots
- [ ] `rfu_STC_NI_constructLLSF` / `rfu_STC_UNI_constructLLSF` — per-slot LL sub-frame construction
- [ ] `rfu_STC_PARENT_analyzeRecvPacket` / `rfu_STC_CHILD_analyzeRecvPacket` — parse incoming LL packet
- [ ] `rfu_STC_analyzeLLSF` — decode a single LL sub-frame and dispatch to UNI/NI receivers
- [ ] `rfu_STC_UNI_receive` — copy UNI payload into receive buffer
- [ ] `rfu_STC_NI_receive_Sender` — update sender-side ACK state machine
- [ ] `rfu_STC_NI_receive_Receiver` — copy NI payload into receive buffer, update sequence numbers
- [ ] `rfu_STC_NI_initSlot_asRecvControllData` / `rfu_STC_NI_initSlot_asRecvDataEntity` — initialize NI recv slot for control vs. data transfer
- [ ] `rfu_NI_checkCommFailCounter` — increments per-slot fail counters when no ACK received in a frame
- [ ] `rfu_getMasterSlave` / `rfu_REQ_changeMasterSlave` — clock master/slave role query and swap
- [ ] `rfu_REQ_RFUStatus` / `rfu_getRFUStatus` — read hardware status register
- [ ] `rfu_MBOOT_CHILD_inheritanceLinkStatus` — inherit link status from multiboot IWRAM payload
- [ ] `rfu_UNI_PARENT_getDRAC_ACK` — read DRAC ACK flag from STWI buffer
- [ ] `rfu_setTimerInterrupt` / `rfu_setMSCCallback` / `rfu_setREQCallback` — register hardware interrupt/callback hooks
- [ ] `rfu_waitREQComplete` — spin-wait for current STWI request to finish
- [ ] `rfu_REQ_noise` — send RF test-mode noise request
- [ ] All `rfu_CB_*` static callbacks — internal STWI completion handlers (14 functions)
- [ ] `rfu_STC_removeLinkData` / `rfu_STC_clearLinkStatus` / `rfu_STC_releaseFrame` — internal link-state cleanup helpers
- [ ] `rfu_STC_readChildList` / `rfu_STC_readParentCandidateList` — parse STWI discovery buffers into `gRfuLinkStatus`
- [ ] `rfu_STC_fastCopy` — fast byte-copy routine copied to RAM for DMA-safe use

#### Notes
This file implements the **GBA Wireless Adapter (RFU) library** — the hardware driver for the Game Boy Advance's wireless link accessory (used for multi-player over radio). It has zero relevance to the browser port:

- All functionality depends on GBA-specific hardware registers (`REG_IME`, `REG_SIOCNT`, `REG_TMCNT`), IWRAM/EWRAM memory-mapped addresses, STWI (Serial Transfer with Interrupt) hardware, DMA, and timer interrupts.
- The browser port has no multiplayer networking feature, no wireless adapter emulation, and no plans to add link-play functionality.
- This is correctly and **intentionally omitted** — it is a hardware peripheral driver, not gameplay logic.
- The only gameplay-adjacent data it carries is `gRfuLinkStatus->my.serialNo/gname/uname` (player identification for wireless rooms), which the browser port does not need.

### `src/librfu_sio32id.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `AgbRFU_checkID` — GBA Wireless Adapter RFU hardware ID handshake via SIO32
- [ ] `Sio32IDInit` — initializes SIO32 hardware registers for RFU detection
- [ ] `Sio32IDMain` — state machine driving the RFU ID exchange protocol

This file implements GBA link hardware detection for the Wireless Adapter (RFU — RF Unit). It drives raw SIO32 hardware registers (`REG_SIOCNT`, `REG_SIODATA32`, `REG_IE`, `REG_IME`, timers) with interrupt-level code. There is no browser equivalent for GBA serial hardware; the wireless adapter and link cable features are intentional omissions in the browser port. No gameplay logic depends on this file — it is purely a hardware detection utility used before multiplayer sessions.

This file implements the GBA Wireless Adapter (RFU) Serial Transfer With Interrupt (STWI) protocol layer: SIO32 register initialization, hardware timer management, packet framing, master/slave clock negotiation, and all wireless REQ/ACK command sends. It is entirely GBA hardware-specific (REG_SIOCNT, REG_RCNT, REG_SIODATA32, DMA, interrupt tables) with no gameplay logic. The browser port has no wireless link cable / wireless adapter feature, so this entire file is an intentional omission.

- [ ] `ClearQuizLadyPlayerAnswer` — resets player answer to `EC_EMPTY_WORD`
- [ ] `Script_QuizLadyOpenBagMenu` — script special: opens bag menu for quiz prize item
- [ ] `QuizLadyPickNewQuestion` — records previous question ID and picks a new random question
- [ ] `ClearQuizLadyQuestionAndAnswer` — blanks out question words and correct answer
- [ ] `QuizLadySetCustomQuestion` — opens easy-chat screen in SET_QUESTION mode
- [ ] `QuizLadyTakePrizeForCustomQuiz` — removes prize item from player's bag
- [ ] `QuizLadyRecordCustomQuizData` — saves trainer ID, player name, prize after custom quiz setup
- [ ] `QuizLadySetWaitingForChallenger` — sets `waitingForChallenger = TRUE`
- [ ] `BufferQuizCorrectAnswer` — copies correct easy-chat word to `gStringVar3`
- [ ] `FieldCallback_QuizLadyEnableScriptContexts` — re-enables script contexts after easy-chat screen closes
- [ ] `QuizLadyClearQuestionForRecordMix` — avoids repeating the previous question after record mix by re-rolling
- [ ] `GivePokeblockToContestLady` — checks pokeblock flavor vs category; increments good/bad counters; tracks max sheen and player name
- [ ] `BufferContestLadyCategoryAndMonName` / `BufferContestLadyMonName` — copies category name and contest mon name to string vars
- [ ] `BufferContestLadyPlayerName` / `BufferContestLadyLanguage` — copies player name / language stored in contest lady save data
- [ ] `BufferContestName` — copies contest category name by index
- [ ] `GetContestLadyPokeblockState` — returns GOOD/NORMAL/BAD based on numGoodPokeblocksGiven threshold
- [ ] `HasPlayerGivenContestLadyPokeblock` — returns `givenPokeblock` flag
- [ ] `ShouldContestLadyShowGoOnAir` — returns true if either good or other pokeblock threshold is met
- [ ] `Script_BufferContestLadyCategoryAndMonName` — script special wrapper
- [ ] `OpenPokeblockCaseForContestLady` — opens pokeblock case in GIVE mode with field return callback
- [ ] `SetContestLadyGivenPokeblock` — marks `givenPokeblock = TRUE`
- [ ] `GetContestLadyMonSpecies` — writes contest lady's mon species to `gSpecialVar_0x8005`
- [ ] `GetContestLadyCategory` — returns `sContestLadyPtr->category`

#### Notes
This file implements the three Lilycove Department Store lady minigames (Quiz Lady, Favor Lady, Contest Lady), all of which depend on save-block state (`gSaveBlock1Ptr->lilycoveLady`), the Easy Chat system, the Pokeblock system, and record mixing (link cable). None of these subsystems exist in the TypeScript port. The entire feature is post-game / link-cable dependent content and is intentionally omitted. Script specials called from generated `.gen.ts` map scripts (LilycoveCity_DepartmentStore floors) that reference these functions will be unresolvable until stubs are provided.

#### Notes
This file implements the full GBA link-cable (SIO multi-player) and wireless RFU multiplayer subsystem. The browser port has no equivalent — link cable, Berry Blender, trading, multi-battle, and wireless adapter are all GBA hardware features with no web counterpart. All functions are intentionally omitted. The only indirectly related TS code is the save format parser (`src/save/native/Gen3SaveParser.ts`) which reads trainer IDs, but that does not cover any link logic.

- [ ] `InitializeRfuLinkManager_LinkLeader` / `_JoinGroup` / `_EnterUnionRoom` — top-level connection init

#### Notes
This file implements the entire GBA Wireless Adapter (RFU) multiplayer stack: connection management, player exchange, reliable block transfer, union room chat, link-loss recovery, and Berry Blender key sync. It has no TypeScript equivalent because the browser port has no wireless multiplayer system — there is no `RecordCorner`, union room, colosseum battle, or Berry Blender wireless support planned. The GBA RFU hardware (serial registers, DMA, interrupt tables) is entirely non-applicable to a browser environment. This file is intentionally out of scope for the browser port.

The charset conversion tables (`sWireless_ASCIItoRSETable`, `sWireless_RSEtoASCIITable`) are GBA-side charset translation for wireless string exchange; the TS port handles charset conversion in `src/save/native/Gen3Charset.ts` for save file purposes only. All `UNUSED`-marked functions (`RfuUnusedQueue_*`, `PopulateArrayWithSequence`, `PkmnStrToASCII`, `ASCIIToPkmnStr`) were dead code even in the original.

- [-] `AddScrollIndicatorArrowPair` / `Task_ScrollIndicatorArrowPair` — scroll arrow visibility logic (hide top arrow at top, hide bottom arrow at bottom) — `src/field/ArrowOverlay.ts` and `src/hooks/useArrowOverlay.ts` exist but are for overworld arrows, not menu scroll indicators; no animated bouncing scroll indicators in menus

- [ ] `ChangeListMenuCoords` — runtime reposition of window; no TS equivalent

#### Notes
The TS port replaces GBA task/sprite/window machinery entirely with React components and CSS. Core list navigation semantics (up/down selection, scroll-into-view, A/B confirm/cancel, item callbacks) are covered. All GBA-specific visual cursor types (red outline, red arrow, animated scroll indicators with sprite sine bounce) are not ported — the browser UI uses CSS highlight/cursor equivalents. `DoMysteryGiftListMenu` is intentionally omitted as Mystery Gift / wireless link is out of scope for the browser port.

## batch: src/load_save.c

### `src/load_save.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/save/SaveManager.ts`, `src/save/SaveStateStore.ts`, `src/save/native/Gen3SaveParser.ts`, `src/game/GameFlags.ts`, `src/game/GameVariables.ts`, `src/game/BagManager.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `SavePlayerParty` / `LoadPlayerParty` — party serialization/deserialization covered by `Gen3SaveParser.ts` and save/load flow in `SaveManager.ts`
- [x] `SaveObjectEvents` / `LoadObjectEvents` — object event state persisted via `SaveStateStore.ts` snapshot mechanism
- [x] `CopyPartyAndObjectsToSave` / `CopyPartyAndObjectsFromSave` — composite save/load orchestration handled in `SaveManager.ts`
- [x] `LoadPlayerBag` / `SavePlayerBag` — bag pocket serialization covered by `BagManager.ts` and `Gen3SaveParser.ts`
- [x] `UseContinueGameWarp` / `SetContinueGameWarpStatus` / `ClearContinueGameWarpStatus` — continue-game warp flags tracked in `SaveStateStore.ts` / `GameFlags.ts`; `SetContinueGameWarpStatusToDynamicWarp` covered by `DynamicWarp.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `ApplyNewEncryptionKeyToAllEncryptedData` / `ApplyNewEncryptionKeyToHword` / `ApplyNewEncryptionKeyToWord` — XOR encryption of money, coins, bag items exists conceptually in `Gen3SaveParser.ts` for reading native `.sav` files, but the TS save format uses JSON and does not re-encrypt on mutation; encryption key rotation on save is not replicated
- [-] `SavePlayerBag` encryption key swap — the double-write of `encryptionKey` and `ApplyNewEncryptionKeyToBagItems` logic is partially present in `Gen3SaveParser.ts` for decryption on load, but the symmetric re-encryption on save is absent

#### Missing
- [ ] `CheckForFlashMemory` — GBA flash hardware detection; intentionally omitted (no flash chip in browser)
- [ ] `ClearSav2` / `ClearSav1` — zero-fill of EWRAM save blocks; TS uses `SaveStateStore` reset instead, not a direct equivalent
- [ ] `SetSaveBlocksPointers` — ASLR-style pointer randomisation using trainer ID + `Random()`; no equivalent (browser save is not memory-mapped)
- [ ] `MoveSaveBlocks_ResetHeap` — full ASLR re-randomisation: copy to heap, shift pointers, restore, re-encrypt; intentionally omitted (GBA-specific anti-tampering, no browser equivalent)
- [ ] `ClearContinueGameWarpStatus2` — duplicate of `ClearContinueGameWarpStatus`; trivially omitted

#### Notes
The C file's core purpose is twofold: (1) GBA hardware concerns — flash memory detection, EWRAM ASLR pointer randomisation, heap reset, XOR encryption key rotation — all intentionally omitted as browser-irrelevant; (2) logical save data marshalling (party, object events, bag) — this is well-covered by the TS save layer, though the TS format is JSON-based rather than a binary struct overlay. The `Gen3SaveParser.ts` handles decryption of native `.sav` imports but does not replicate the full encryption lifecycle for the internal TS save format.

- Prize table (`sLotteryPrizes`: PP Up, Exp Share, Max Revive, Master Ball) and the 5-digit trailing-match algorithm are both simple and self-contained — straightforward to port when needed.
- The `special` command calls `PickLotteryCornerTicket` and `RetrieveLotteryNumber` from map scripts; both are currently unimplemented `special` handlers in `ScriptRunner`.

#### Missing
- [ ] `m4aSoundInit` — initializes GBA sound hardware, music players, and Pokémon cry players
- [ ] `m4aSoundMain` — main sound update tick (called each VBlank)
- [ ] `m4aSongNumStart` — starts a BGM track by song table index
- [ ] `m4aSongNumStartOrChange` — starts BGM only if different from currently playing song
- [ ] `m4aSongNumStartOrContinue` — starts or unpauses BGM by song index
- [ ] `m4aSongNumStop` — stops a specific song by index
- [ ] `m4aSongNumContinue` — resumes a paused song by index
- [ ] `m4aMPlayAllStop` — stops all music players and all Pokémon cry players
- [ ] `m4aMPlayContinue` / `m4aMPlayAllContinue` — resumes one or all music players
- [ ] `m4aMPlayFadeOut` / `m4aMPlayFadeOutTemporarily` — fades out BGM (permanent or temporary)
- [ ] `m4aMPlayFadeIn` — fades in BGM
- [ ] `m4aMPlayImmInit` — immediately re-initializes all tracks of a music player
- [ ] `m4aMPlayTempoControl` — runtime tempo scaling for a music player
- [ ] `m4aMPlayVolumeControl` — per-track volume override
- [ ] `m4aMPlayPitchControl` — per-track pitch shift
- [ ] `m4aMPlayPanpotControl` — per-track stereo pan override
- [ ] `m4aMPlayModDepthSet` / `m4aMPlayLFOSpeedSet` — LFO modulation depth and speed control
- [ ] `MPlayStart` / `m4aMPlayStop` — start/stop a music player with a song header
- [ ] `MPlayContinue` / `MPlayFadeOut` — low-level player resume and fade-out
- [ ] `MPlayOpen` — registers a music player into the sound engine linked list
- [ ] `FadeOutBody` — per-frame fade-out volume step applied to all tracks
- [ ] `TrkVolPitSet` — applies volume/pan/pitch values to a track's hardware registers
- [ ] `MidiKeyToFreq` — converts MIDI key + fine-adjust to DirectSound sample frequency
- [ ] `MidiKeyToCgbFreq` — converts MIDI key to CGB hardware frequency register value
- [ ] `CgbSound` — full CGB channel ADSR envelope state machine, runs each frame
- [ ] `CgbOscOff` — silences a CGB hardware oscillator channel
- [ ] `CgbModVol` — computes envelope goal and pan for a CGB channel
- [ ] `MPlayExtender` — patches CGB support into the sound engine jump table
- [ ] `SoundInit` — full sound hardware initialization (DMA, timers, jump table copy)
- [ ] `SoundClear` — stops all DirectSound and CGB channels
- [ ] `SampleFreqSet` — sets PCM sample rate and reprograms Timer 0
- [ ] `m4aSoundMode` — configures reverb, max channels, master volume, DA bits, sample freq
- [ ] `m4aSoundVSyncOff` / `m4aSoundVSyncOn` — disables/re-enables DMA-driven PCM output
- [ ] `SetPokemonCryTone` — allocates a Pokémon cry music player slot and starts the cry
- [ ] `SetPokemonCryVolume/Panpot/Pitch/Length/Release/Progress/Chorus/Stereo/Priority` — parameterize Pokémon cry playback
- [ ] `IsPokemonCryPlaying` — checks whether a cry music player's channel is still active
- [ ] `ply_memacc` — music script command: memory-area conditional/arithmetic operations
- [ ] `ply_xcmd` / `ply_xxx` — extended music script command dispatch
- [ ] `ply_xwave/xtype/xatta/xdeca/xsust/xrele/xiecv/xiecl/xleng/xswee` — per-track tone parameter override commands
- [ ] `ply_xcmd_0C` / `ply_xcmd_0D` — loop-counter and parameter extended commands
- [ ] `MusicPlayerJumpTableCopy` / `ClearChain` / `Clear64byte` — SWI-based utility stubs used by sound engine

#### Notes
`m4a.c` is the entire GBA M4A (MusicPlayer2000) sound engine — DMA PCM mixing, CGB hardware channel ADSR, MIDI-to-frequency lookup, music player state machines, and Pokémon cry synthesis. None of this has a browser-side equivalent. The port has no audio system at all yet; music and sound effects are completely absent. When audio is eventually added it will almost certainly be implemented via the Web Audio API rather than porting this GBA-hardware-specific engine. All functions here are intentional omissions due to platform incompatibility, not missed gameplay logic.

- [ ] `gScaleTable` — MIDI note-to-frequency scale lookup table (180 entries, PCM channels)
- [ ] `gFreqTable` — base sample frequency multipliers for 12 semitones (u32, 12 entries)
- [ ] `gPcmSamplesPerVBlankTable` — PCM buffer size per VBlank at each supported sample rate (12 entries)
- [ ] `gCgbScaleTable` — CGB (Game Boy hardware channel) note scale table (132 entries)
- [ ] `gCgbFreqTable` — CGB frequency register deltas for 12 semitones (s16, 12 entries)
- [ ] `gNoiseTable` — CGB noise channel register values (64 entries)
- [ ] `gCgb3Vol` — CGB channel 3 (wave) volume mapping table (16 entries)
- [ ] `gClockTable` — M4A clock/timing table for note duration values (49 entries)
- [ ] `gPokemonCrySongTemplate` — template `PokemonCrySong` struct for cry playback (hardcoded M4A commands)
- [ ] `gXcmdTable` — M4A extended command (`XCMD`) dispatch table (14 function pointers)

#### Notes
This file is pure GBA M4A audio engine data — hardware-specific to the GBA's ARM7 sound mixer and CGB channels. The browser port has no audio system at all; there are no audio files anywhere in the TS file tree. All entries here are intentionally omitted: the GBA sound hardware (DMA-driven PCM mixer, CGB tone/wave/noise channels, M4A sequencer) has no browser equivalent in this port. If audio is ever added, a Web Audio API implementation would be written from scratch rather than porting these tables directly, though `gDeltaEncodingTable`, `gFreqTable`, and `gScaleTable` contain data that could inform a faithful reimplementation.

Intentional omission is reasonable given the port's current focus; mail is a post-game/link-cable-adjacent feature with no story dependency.

- [x] `CallCallbacks` — update/render callback dispatch covered by `UpdateCoordinator.ts` and `useUnifiedGameLoop.ts`
- [x] `ReadKeys` — input reading and key repeat logic covered by `src/core/InputMap.ts` and `src/hooks/useInput.ts`
- [x] `InitKeys` — key state initialization covered in `InputMap.ts`
- [x] `SeedRngAndSetTrainerId` / `SeedRngWithRtc` — RNG seeding covered by `src/battle/engine/BattleRng.ts`

- [-] `ReadKeys` L=A remapping — `InputMap.ts` handles key mapping but the L=A option mode from `gSaveBlock2Ptr->optionsButtonMode` is not wired to save data options
- [-] `DoSoftReset` — no soft reset concept; page reload serves this purpose but no explicit soft-reset hotkey (A+B+Start+Select) is implemented

#### Missing
- [ ] `VBlankIntr` / `HBlankIntr` / `VCountIntr` — GBA interrupt handlers; no browser equivalent needed (requestAnimationFrame replaces VBlank sync)
- [ ] `WaitForVBlank` — VBlank spin-wait; replaced by rAF scheduling, no direct equivalent
- [ ] `InitIntrHandlers` — GBA interrupt table setup; no browser equivalent
- [ ] `EnableVCountIntrAtLine150` — scanline interrupt; no browser equivalent
- [ ] `StartTimer1` / hardware timer registers — GBA hardware timers; no browser equivalent
- [ ] `InitFlashTimer` — flash memory timer; no browser equivalent (save uses localStorage)
- [ ] `m4aSoundInit` / `MapMusicMain` / audio init — audio system entirely missing
- [ ] `PlayTimeCounter_Update` — play time tracking not implemented
- [ ] `LinkVSync` / `RfuVSync` / `HandleLinkConnection` / link cable functions — link cable/RFU multiplayer intentionally omitted (browser has no SIO)
- [ ] `ClearPokemonCrySongs` — Pokémon cry audio system not implemented
- [ ] `UpdateWirelessStatusIndicatorSprite` — wireless indicator; intentionally omitted

#### Notes
- The GBA main loop structure (interrupt handlers, VBlank sync, DMA, hardware registers) has no browser equivalent and is intentionally replaced by the browser's `requestAnimationFrame` + event model.
- Link cable / RFU wireless (multiplayer) is fully omitted — browser port is single-player only.
- Audio (`m4a` sound system, Pokémon cries, map music) is listed as missing in project memory — `MapMusicMain` / `m4aSoundInit` have no TS equivalent yet.
- `PlayTimeCounter_Update` (play time tracking for save display) is not implemented but would be a small addition to `SaveManager.ts`.
- The trainer ID seeding (`SeedRngAndSetTrainerId`) is partially relevant — `BattleRng.ts` handles RNG but trainer ID generation from hardware timer is approximated.

- Mystery Gift, Mystery Events, and e-Reader (`CB2_InitMysteryGift`, `CB2_InitMysteryEventMenu`, `CB2_InitEReader`) are intentionally omitted — GBA wireless/link-cable features with no browser equivalent.
- The Birch speech scene in TS (`BirchSpeechState`, `birchSceneLayout.ts`) covers the narrative flow but approximates GBA hardware effects (OBJ blend mode, affine animation, BG1 scroll, palette fade tasks) rather than reproducing them cycle-accurately.
- The naming screen (`DoNamingScreen` → `C2_NamingScreen`) is handled separately; the return callback `CB2_NewGameBirchSpeech_ReturnFromNamingScreen` is wired up but the full GBA reinit sequence is collapsed.
- `sCurrItemAndOptionMenuCheck` with `OPTION_MENU_FLAG` (bit 15) for returning from options menu is replicated in `MainMenuState` state persistence.

The GBA-specific `gHeap` EWRAM buffer and `MALLOC_SYSTEM_ID` magic sentinel have no browser equivalent and should never be ported.

- [ ] `MatchCall_DrawWindow` — draws border tiles, creates spinning Pokénav icon task

- [ ] `MatchCall_PrintIntro` — prints ellipsis, then calls `SelectMatchCallMessage` to fill `gStringVar4`

- [ ] `SelectMatchCallMessage` — top-level message selector: same-route rematch > different-route request > battle topic > general topic
- [ ] `GetTrainerMatchCallId` — linear search of `sMatchCallTrainers` by trainer ID
- [ ] `GetSameRouteMatchCallText` — looks up same-route battle-request text from topic/id encoded in `sameRouteMatchCallTextId`
- [ ] `GetDifferentRouteMatchCallText` — same for different-route request text
- [ ] `GetBattleMatchCallText` — randomly picks one of 3 battle topics (wild/negative/positive) for the trainer
- [ ] `GetGeneralMatchCallText` — 50% chance: picks a frontier facility with a win streak and uses a streak text; otherwise uses trainer's personal text
- [ ] `GetFrontierStreakInfo` — reads record win streaks from SaveBlock2 frontier data per facility
- [ ] `ShouldTrainerRequestBattle` — deterministic RNG check (requires ≥5 badges, uses day count ^ dewford rand ^ trainer ID)
- [ ] `TrainerIsEligibleForRematch` — checks `SaveBlock1.trainerRematches[matchCallId] > 0`
- [ ] `GetRematchTrainerLocation` — resolves trainer's home map section via `gRematchTable`
- [ ] `GetNumRematchTrainersFought` — counts how many rematch-table trainers have been defeated at least once
- [ ] `GetNthRematchTrainerFought` — returns rematch table index of nth defeated trainer
- [ ] `BuildMatchCallString` — populates string vars then calls `StringExpandPlaceholders`
- [ ] `PopulateMatchCallStringVars` — dispatches up to 3 string var populate calls based on `stringVarFuncIds`
- [ ] `PopulateTrainerName` — uses override table for multi-trainer names (Kira&Dan, Amy&Liv, etc.), otherwise trainer name
- [ ] `PopulateMapName` — calls `GetMapName` for the trainer's home map section
- [ ] `PopulateSpeciesFromTrainerLocation` — looks up a random wild encounter species from the trainer's route
- [ ] `PopulateSpeciesFromTrainerParty` — picks a random species from the trainer's current rematch party
- [ ] `PopulateBattleFrontierFacilityName` — copies facility name string for the chosen frontier facility
- [ ] `PopulateBattleFrontierStreak` — converts `sBattleFrontierStreakInfo.streak` to decimal string
- [ ] `GetLandEncounterSlot` / `GetWaterEncounterSlot` — weighted random slot selection matching the overworld encounter rates
- [ ] `GetNumOwnedBadges` — counts badges from `sBadgeFlags` for `ShouldTrainerRequestBattle` gating
- [ ] `GetPokedexRatingLevel` — maps caught count to one of 21 rating tiers (with Deoxys/Jirachi exclusion logic)
- [ ] `BufferPokedexRatingForMatchCall` — builds Birch's Pokédex rating speech string including optional National Dex paragraph
- [ ] `LoadMatchCallWindowGfx` — public API to load the match call window tiles/palette into an arbitrary BG
- [ ] `DrawMatchCallTextBoxBorder` — public wrapper around `DrawMatchCallTextBoxBorder_Internal`
- [ ] `sMatchCallTrainers[]` — 64-entry table mapping trainer IDs to all their text IDs (battle topics, general, same/diff route)
- [ ] All `sMatchCallWildBattleTexts`, `sMatchCallNegativeBattleTexts`, `sMatchCallPositiveBattleTexts`, `sMatchCallSameRouteBattleRequestTexts`, `sMatchCallDifferentRouteBattleRequestTexts`, `sMatchCallPersonalizedTexts`, `sMatchCallBattleFrontierStreakTexts`, `sMatchCallBattleFrontierRecordStreakTexts`, `sMatchCallBattleDomeTexts`, `sMatchCallBattlePikeTexts`, `sMatchCallBattlePyramidTexts` text tables

#### Notes
- This is the **Pokénav Match Call / Trainer Phone** system — entirely absent from the TS port.
- Depends on the rematch system (`gRematchTable`, `trainerRematches` in SaveBlock1, `TRAINER_REGISTERED_FLAGS_START` flags), which is also unimplemented.
- The UI is GBA-specific (BG tile animation, task-based sliding window, palette loading) and would need a full browser replacement (e.g., an overlay component), but the data-layer logic (trainer registration, message selection, step/time gating, rematch eligibility) is all portable.
- `BufferPokedexRatingForMatchCall` is used by Birch's post-game Pokédex rating call — partially overlaps with the Birch speech state (`src/states/BirchSpeechState.ts`) but is distinct content.
- The GBA `Special` function `TryStartMatchCall` is invoked from the overworld step callback; the TS `StepCallbackManager` would be its integration point.
- Battle Frontier save data (`SaveBlock2.frontier.*`) is not present in the TS save system (`src/save/`), making `GetFrontierStreakInfo` and `ShouldTrainerRequestBattle` (partially) unimplementable until frontier state is added.
- Link Cable / e-Reader content is not relevant here; this is a single-player feature.

## batch: src/math_util.c

### `src/math_util.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `MathUtil_Mul16` — fixed-point 8.8 multiply (s16 × s16 / 256)
- [ ] `MathUtil_Mul16Shift` — fixed-point multiply with variable shift
- [ ] `MathUtil_Mul32` — fixed-point 8.8 multiply (s32 × s32 / 256)
- [ ] `MathUtil_Div16` — fixed-point divide returning (x << 8) / y
- [ ] `MathUtil_Div16Shift` — fixed-point divide with variable shift
- [ ] `MathUtil_Div32` — fixed-point divide (x × 256 / y, 64-bit intermediate)
- [ ] `MathUtil_Inv16` — fixed-point reciprocal 0x10000 / y → s16
- [ ] `MathUtil_Inv16Shift` — fixed-point reciprocal with variable shift
- [ ] `MathUtil_Inv32` — fixed-point reciprocal 0x10000 / y → s32

#### Notes
These are GBA fixed-point math helpers (8.8 and variable-shift formats) used primarily by battle animations, affine sprite transforms, and the 3D camera/rotation system. The browser port uses native JavaScript `number` (IEEE 754 double), so no fixed-point arithmetic library is needed — call sites inline the equivalent expressions directly. None of these functions are needed as standalone utilities in TS. Intentional omission; not a gap.

- [ ] `InitGiddyTaleList` — shuffles question list and picks random Easy Chat words for Giddy's tale list
- [ ] `ResetMauvilleOldManFlag` — resets per-man "already interacted" flag; calls `SetMauvilleOldManObjEventGfx`
- [ ] `SetMauvilleOldManObjEventGfx` — sets `VAR_OBJ_GFX_ID_0` to bard sprite
- [ ] `StorytellerSetup` / `Storyteller_ResetFlag` — initializes/resets Storyteller save data
- [ ] `StorytellerStoryListMenu` / `Task_StoryListMenu` — shows scrollable list of recorded stories as a menu task
- [ ] `Script_StorytellerDisplayStory` — displays selected story text via `ShowFieldMessage`
- [ ] `StorytellerGetFreeStorySlot` — returns index of first empty story slot
- [ ] `StorytellerUpdateStat` — checks if tracked stat has increased; updates record
- [ ] `HasStorytellerAlreadyRecorded` — returns `alreadyRecorded` flag
- [ ] `Script_StorytellerInitializeRandomStat` — picks a random qualifying game stat for a new Storyteller story
- [ ] `SanitizeMauvilleOldManForRuby` / `SanitizeReceivedEmeraldOldMan` / `SanitizeReceivedRubyOldMan` — link-trade language sanitization helpers (intentional omission candidate)
- [ ] `sStorytellerStories` table — 35-entry table mapping game stats to story title/action/text strings

#### Notes
- The entire Mauville Old Man feature (Bard, Hipster, Trader, Storyteller, Giddy) has no TypeScript equivalent. This is a mid-game optional side feature in Mauville City's Pokémon Center.
- The Bard's phoneme-based audio system (`BardSing`, `Task_BardSong`) is fundamentally tied to GBA m4a audio and has no browser audio equivalent; audio output would need to be redesigned entirely (Web Audio API or simply omitted).
- Easy Chat system (`CopyEasyChatWord`, `UnlockRandomTrendySaying`, `GetRandomEasyChatWordFromUnlockedGroup`) is also unimplemented — it's a prerequisite for Bard and Giddy.
- Link-cable functions (`SanitizeMauvilleOldManForRuby`, `SanitizeReceivedRubyOldMan`, etc.) are intentional omissions — link trading is out of scope for a browser port.
- The Storyteller's story data is keyed from `MauvilleCity_PokemonCenter_1F` script text strings which are covered by `src/data/scripts/MauvilleCity_PokemonCenter_1F.gen.ts`, but the runtime logic to display and update them is missing.
- `GameVariables` / `GameFlags` infrastructure exists in TS, so the `VAR_OBJ_GFX_ID_0` / `FLAG_*` parts could be wired up relatively easily once the character selection logic is implemented.

## batch: src/menu.c

### `src/menu.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/components/dialog/DialogContext.tsx`, `src/components/dialog/DialogBox.tsx`, `src/components/dialog/OptionMenu.tsx`, `src/menu/components/StartMenu.tsx`, `src/menu/MenuStateManager.ts`, `src/core/prompt/PromptController.ts`, `src/core/prompt/PromptPrinterEngine.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `InitStandardTextBoxWindows` / `InitTextBoxGfxAndPrinters` — dialog system initialization covered by DialogContext + PromptController setup
- [x] `DrawDialogueFrame` / `DrawStdWindowFrame` — dialog/standard window frame rendering handled by DialogFrame.tsx and PromptWindowSkin/PromptCanvasRenderer
- [x] `DisplayYesNoMenuDefaultYes` / `DisplayYesNoMenuWithDefault` — Yes/No prompts implemented in DialogContext (`showYesNo`) and OptionMenu
- [x] `CreateYesNoMenu` — covered by DialogContext yes/no flow
- [x] `GetPlayerTextSpeedDelay` / `GetPlayerTextSpeed` — text speed logic implemented in `src/core/prompt/textSpeed.ts`
- [x] `AddStartMenuWindow` / `RemoveStartMenuWindow` — start menu window lifecycle in `src/menu/components/StartMenu.tsx` and `src/menu/MenuStateManager.ts`
- [x] `Menu_ProcessInput` / `Menu_ProcessInputNoWrap` — menu cursor input handled in `src/menu/hooks/useMenuState.ts` and `src/menu/hooks/useMenuLayout.ts`
- [x] `Menu_MoveCursor` / `Menu_MoveCursorNoWrapAround` — cursor movement logic in `useMenuState.ts`
- [x] `Menu_GetCursorPos` — cursor position tracking in menu state
- [x] `InitMenuInUpperLeftCornerNormal` / `InitMenuInUpperLeftCorner` — standard menu initialization covered by MenuStateManager
- [x] `PrintMenuTable` / `PrintMenuActionTexts` / `PrintMenuActionTextsAtPos` — menu item text rendering handled by React-based menu components
- [x] `EraseYesNoWindow` / `ClearDialogWindowAndFrame` / `ClearStdWindowAndFrame` — dialog teardown in DialogContext
- [x] `AddTextPrinterForMessage` / `AddTextPrinterParameterized2` — text printing covered by PromptPrinterEngine and DialogText
- [x] `RunTextPrintersAndIsPrinter0Active` — active-printer check equivalent in PromptController
- [x] `BufferSaveMenuText` (SAVE_MENU_LOCATION, SAVE_MENU_PLAY_TIME, SAVE_MENU_BADGES, SAVE_MENU_CAUGHT, SAVE_MENU_NAME) — partially covered by `src/components/SaveLoadButtons.tsx` and SaveManager for save data display
- [x] `BlitMenuInfoIcon` — type/power/accuracy icons rendered in `src/menu/moves/MoveRow.tsx` and `src/menu/components/PokemonSummaryContent.tsx`
- [x] `ListMenuLoadStdPalAt` — palette loading abstracted away (browser uses CSS/canvas, no GBA palette slots)

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `InitMenuNormal` / `InitMenuGrid` / `InitMenuActionGrid` — single-column menus covered; grid menu layout (`columns × rows` cursor wrapping) not fully generalized — `src/menu/hooks/useMenuLayout.ts` handles linear lists but the 2D grid cursor logic (`ChangeMenuGridCursorPosition`, `ChangeGridMenuCursorPosition`) has no direct equivalent
- [-] `Menu_ProcessGridInput` / `ChangeMenuGridCursorPosition` / `ChangeGridMenuCursorPosition` — 2D grid input (used for Pokémon summary multi-page, bag grid, etc.) not fully ported; bag menu uses a list layout rather than a true 2D grid
- [-] `PrintMenuActionGrid` / `PrintMenuGridTable` — grid-layout text printing not generalized; bag/party menus use custom React layouts
- [-] `AddMapNamePopUpWindow` / `RemoveMapNamePopUpWindow` / `GetMapNamePopUpWindowId` — map name popup exists as UI element but lifecycle management differs from C window system

#### Missing
- [ ] `HofPCTopBar_AddWindow` / `HofPCTopBar_Print` / `HofPCTopBar_PrintPair` / `HofPCTopBar_RemoveWindow` — Hall of Fame PC top bar UI; Hall of Fame not implemented
- [ ] `ScheduleBgCopyTilemapToVram` / `DoScheduledBgTilemapCopiesToVram` / `ClearScheduledBgCopiesToVram` — GBA BG tilemap DMA scheduling; not applicable in browser (WebGL handles this differently), intentionally omitted
- [ ] `ResetTempTileDataBuffers` / `FreeTempTileDataBuffersIfPossible` / `DecompressAndCopyTileDataToVram` / `DecompressAndLoadBgGfxUsingHeap` / `malloc_and_decompress` / `copy_decompressed_tile_data_to_vram` — GBA LZ77 decompression + DMA tile upload pipeline; browser loads assets via fetch/canvas, intentionally omitted
- [ ] `task_free_buf_after_copying_tile_data_to_vram` — GBA task system memory cleanup; no equivalent needed
- [ ] `SetBgTilemapPalette` / `CopyToBufferFromBgTilemap` / `AddValToTilemapBuffer` / `ResetBgPositions` / `BgDmaFill` — GBA BG hardware manipulation; intentionally omitted (browser WebGL pipeline)
- [ ] `LoadMessageBoxAndBorderGfx` / `LoadMessageBoxAndFrameGfx` / `Menu_LoadStdPal` / `Menu_LoadStdPalAt` — GBA VRAM palette/tile loading; browser uses CSS/canvas assets, intentionally omitted
- [ ] `WindowFunc_DrawStandardFrame` / `WindowFunc_DrawDialogueFrame` / `WindowFunc_ClearStdWindowAndFrame` / `WindowFunc_ClearDialogWindowAndFrame` / `WindowFunc_DrawDialogFrameWithCustomTileAndPalette` / `WindowFunc_ClearDialogWindowAndFrameNullPalette` / `WindowFunc_DrawStdFrameWithCustomTileAndPalette` / `WindowFunc_ClearStdWindowAndFrameToTransparent` — GBA BG tilemap frame-drawing helpers; browser renders frames via canvas/CSS, no direct equivalent
- [ ] `DrawDialogFrameWithCustomTileAndPalette` / `DrawStdFrameWithCustomTileAndPalette` / `ClearDialogWindowAndFrameToTransparent` / `ClearStdWindowAndFrameToTransparent` / `SetStandardWindowBorderStyle` — custom tile/palette frame variants; browser dialog system uses CSS-based skinning
- [ ] `AddWindowParameterized` / `SetWindowTemplateFields` / `CreateWindowTemplate` — GBA window template system; no direct TS equivalent (browser uses React component layout)
- [ ] `FreeAllOverworldWindowBuffers` / `EraseFieldMessageBox` — GBA window buffer management; not applicable
- [ ] `RedrawMenuCursor` — GBA tile-based cursor arrow blit; browser menus use CSS highlight/React state
- [ ] `ProcessMenuInput_other` / `Menu_ProcessInputNoWrapAround_other` — repeat-input variants (JOY_REPEAT); partial analog exists in useMenuState but repeat-key handling not fully parity-matched
- [ ] `PrintPlayerNameOnWindow` — player name expansion + text print; name display covered ad-hoc in menus but no general utility
- [ ] `DisplayItemMessageOnField` — field item message with task callback chaining; item messages handled differently in `src/game/messages/itemMessages.ts`
- [ ] `AddTextPrinterWithCallbackForMessage` / `AddTextPrinterWithCustomSpeedForMessage` / `AddTextPrinterForMessage_2` — callback/speed variants of message printer; PromptController handles speed but callback chaining API differs

#### Notes
- The C `menu.c` is a low-level GBA windowing + text-printing library. The TypeScript port replaces the entire GBA BG/window hardware layer with React components + Canvas/WebGL, so most hardware-manipulation functions (`BgDmaFill`, `ScheduleBgCopyTilemapToVram`, VRAM loaders, LZ77 decompressors) are intentionally omitted.
- The cursor/menu state machine (`sMenu` struct with `InitMenu`, `Menu_ProcessInput`, etc.) is semantically ported via `useMenuState.ts` and `MenuStateManager.ts`, but the GBA-specific tile-blit cursor arrow (`RedrawMenuCursor`) is replaced by React selection state.
- Grid menus (2D cursor, `columns × rows`) are partially missing — the bag and party screens use custom React layouts rather than a generalized grid-cursor system.
- `HofPCTopBar` functions are entirely missing because the Hall of Fame feature hasn't been implemented.
- `BufferSaveMenuText` is partially covered: name, play time, and location display exist in save UI, but the exact string-encoding format (using `EXT_CTRL_CODE_BEGIN` color codes) is not replicated since the browser uses CSS coloring.

## batch: src/menu_helpers.c

### `src/menu_helpers.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/menu/MenuStateManager.ts`, `src/components/dialog/DialogContext.tsx`, `src/menu/components/StartMenu.tsx`, `src/menu/hooks/useMenuState.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `DisplayMessageAndContinueTask` — dialog display with continuation callback; covered by `DialogContext.showMessage` + callback chaining
- [x] `DoYesNoFuncWithChoice` / `CreateYesNoMenuWithCallbacks` — yes/no prompt; covered by `DialogContext.showYesNo`
- [x] `Task_CallYesOrNoCallback` — yes/no input processing; handled inside `DialogContext` / `OptionMenu`
- [x] `AdjustQuantityAccordingToDPadInput` — D-pad quantity adjustment (up/down ±1, left/right ±10); covered in `BagMenu.tsx` quantity selector logic
- [x] `SetItemListPerPageCount` — counts non-empty slots + 1 for Cancel, clamps to maxPerPage; equivalent logic in `BagMenu.tsx` / `useMenuState`
- [x] `SetCursorWithinListBounds` — clamps scroll+cursor to list bounds; equivalent in list-menu navigation hooks

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `GetLRKeysPressed` / `GetLRKeysPressedAndHeld` — L/R button mode check respects `optionsButtonMode`; TS `InputMap` handles L/R but does not gate on the save-block `OPTIONS_BUTTON_MODE_LR` option setting
- [-] `SetCursorScrollWithinListBounds` — scroll-centering logic for odd/even `maxShownItems`; partial equivalent in menu scroll logic but the exact half-page centering algorithm may not be faithfully reproduced

#### Missing
- [ ] `ResetVramOamAndBgCntRegs` — zeros VRAM/OAM/palette registers; intentionally omitted (no GBA hardware)
- [ ] `ResetAllBgsCoordinates` — resets all four BG scroll registers to 0; intentionally omitted (WebGL handles layers)
- [ ] `SetVBlankHBlankCallbacksToNull` — clears VBlank/HBlank interrupt callbacks; intentionally omitted (no GBA interrupts)
- [ ] `RunTextPrintersRetIsActive` — polls text printer active state; GBA text engine concept, no direct equivalent
- [ ] `Task_ContinueTaskAfterMessagePrints` — task that waits for text printer to finish; GBA task system, replaced by Promise/callback in TS dialog
- [ ] `IsHoldingItemAllowed` — blocks Enigma Berry in Trade Center / Union Room; link-play feature, intentionally omitted
- [ ] `IsWritingMailAllowed` — blocks mail writing during link; link-play feature, intentionally omitted
- [ ] `MenuHelpers_IsLinkActive` — checks overworld link or remote players connected; link-play, intentionally omitted
- [ ] `IsActiveOverworldLinkBusy` / `MenuHelpers_ShouldWaitForLinkRecv` — link receive queue saturation guard; link-play, intentionally omitted
- [ ] `LoadListMenuSwapLineGfx` — loads compressed swap-line sprite sheet/palette; GBA sprite system, no equivalent
- [ ] `CreateSwapLineSprites` / `DestroySwapLineSprites` — spawn/destroy the swap-line OAM sprites used in party reorder; GBA OAM, no TS equivalent
- [ ] `SetSwapLineSpritesInvisibility` / `UpdateSwapLineSpritesPos` — show/hide and position swap-line sprites; GBA OAM, no TS equivalent

#### Notes
- Link-cable functions (`IsHoldingItemAllowed`, `IsWritingMailAllowed`, `MenuHelpers_IsLinkActive`, queue guards) are intentionally out of scope for the browser port.
- GBA hardware reset/register functions (`ResetVramOamAndBgCntRegs`, `ResetAllBgsCoordinates`, `SetVBlankHBlankCallbacksToNull`) have no meaning in a WebGL renderer and are correctly omitted.
- The swap-line sprite set (used for party Pokémon reorder drag indicator) has no TS implementation yet; the party menu exists (`PartyMenuContent.tsx`) but the visual swap-line feedback is absent.
- The GBA task-continuation pattern (`sMessageNextTask`, `Task_ContinueTaskAfterMessagePrints`) is architecturally replaced by Promise chains and React state in the TS dialog system.

#### Implemented
- [x] `InitMoveRelearnerWindows` — window setup for move relearner; equivalent React component/menu lifecycle in `MoveForgetMenuContent.tsx`
- [x] `LoadMoveRelearnerMovesList` — populates move list; covered by `MoveListModel.ts` / `MoveRow.tsx`
- [x] `MoveRelearnerPrintMessage` — prints message in relearner msg window; handled by `DialogContext` prompt system

- [x] `MoveRelearnerCreateYesNoMenu` — yes/no confirmation; covered by `DialogContext.showYesNo`
- [x] `DrawLevelUpWindowPg1` — renders stat diff (+/-) after level up; covered by level-up flow in `EvolutionState.ts` / `EvolutionRenderer.ts`
- [x] `DrawLevelUpWindowPg2` — renders current stats after level up; same as above
- [x] `GetMonLevelUpWindowStats` — reads HP/ATK/DEF/SPD/SPATK/SPDEF from mon; equivalent in `src/pokemon/stats.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `MoveRelearnerCursorCallback` — cursor move plays SE_SELECT and reloads both battle and contest descriptions; SE_SELECT sound is absent (no audio system), contest description (`MoveRelearnerMenuLoadContestMoveDescription`) not implemented
- [-] `MoveRelearnerLoadBattleMoveDescription` — prints move type/PP/power/accuracy/desc in relearner window; battle stat display exists in `MoveRow.tsx` but the full pixel-level window rendering differs; contest window counterpart absent

#### Missing
- [ ] `MailboxMenu_Alloc` — allocates mailbox list (PC mailbox feature)
- [ ] `MailboxMenu_AddWindow` — adds mailbox window; part of PC mailbox UI
- [ ] `MailboxMenu_RemoveWindow` — removes mailbox window
- [ ] `MailboxMenu_GetWindowId` — (UNUSED) returns window id for mailbox slot
- [ ] `MailboxMenu_ItemPrintFunc` — renders player name for each mail item
- [ ] `MailboxMenu_CreateList` — builds scrollable mailbox list menu
- [ ] `MailboxMenu_MoveCursorFunc` — plays SE_SELECT on cursor move in mailbox
- [ ] `MailboxMenu_AddScrollArrows` — adds scroll indicator arrows to mailbox list
- [ ] `MailboxMenu_Free` — frees mailbox list allocation
- [ ] `ConditionGraph_Init` — initializes the pentagon condition graph state
- [ ] `ConditionGraph_SetNewPositions` — interpolates graph vertices between two Pokémon
- [ ] `ConditionGraph_TryUpdate` — advances interpolation step counter
- [ ] `ConditionGraph_InitResetScanline` — resets scanline state machine for graph
- [ ] `ConditionGraph_ResetScanline` — runs scanline effect reset (GBA-specific WIN0H scanline DMA)
- [ ] `ConditionGraph_Draw` — writes scanline buffers for pentagon graph using WIN0H hardware trick
- [ ] `ConditionGraph_InitWindow` — sets WIN0/WIN1 GPU registers for graph masking (GBA hardware)
- [ ] `ConditionGraph_Update` — copies interpolated positions into curPositions each frame
- [ ] `ConditionGraph_CalcLine` — rasterizes one edge of the pentagon into scanline arrays
- [ ] `ConditionGraph_CalcRightHalf` — computes right-half scanlines (Cool→Beauty→Cute→Smart edges)
- [ ] `ConditionGraph_CalcLeftHalf` — computes left-half scanlines (Cool→Tough→Smart edges)
- [ ] `ConditionGraph_CalcPositions` — converts raw condition values to screen-space pentagon vertex coords
- [ ] `GetBoxOrPartyMonData` — unified accessor for party or box mon data
- [ ] `GetConditionMenuMonNameAndLocString` — builds name+location string for condition menu slot
- [ ] `GetConditionMenuMonString` — builds nickname/gender/level string for condition menu
- [ ] `BufferConditionMenuSpacedStringN` — pads string to fixed width with spaces
- [ ] `GetConditionMenuMonConditions` — reads Cool/Tough/Smart/Cute/Beauty/Sheen from mon for graph
- [ ] `GetConditionMenuMonGfx` — loads front sprite tiles and palette for condition menu
- [ ] `MoveConditionMonOnscreen` / `MoveConditionMonOffscreen` — slide mon sprite horizontally in/out
- [ ] `ConditionMenu_UpdateMonEnter` / `ConditionMenu_UpdateMonExit` — combined graph+sprite transition helpers
- [ ] `LoadConditionMonPicTemplate` — sets up sprite sheet/template/pal structs for condition mon pic
- [ ] `LoadConditionSelectionIcons` — loads pokeball/placeholder/cancel icons for condition selection UI
- [ ] `LoadConditionSparkle` — loads sparkle sprite sheet and palette
- [ ] `CreateConditionSparkleSprites` — creates and links up to 10 sparkle sprites around the mon
- [ ] `DestroyConditionSparkleSprites` / `FreeConditionSparkles` — cleanup sparkle sprites and VRAM
- [ ] `ResetConditionSparkleSprites` — nulls sparkle sprite pointer array
- [ ] `InitConditionSparkles` — initializes sparkle timing/state per sprite
- [ ] `SetConditionSparklePosition` — positions sparkle relative to mon sprite using polar coords table
- [ ] `SetNextConditionSparkle` / `ShowAllConditionSparkles` — sparkle sequencing callbacks
- [ ] `SpriteCB_ConditionSparkle` / `SpriteCB_ConditionSparkle_WaitForAllAnim` / `SpriteCB_ConditionSparkle_DoNextAfterDelay` — sparkle animation sprite callbacks

#### Notes
- **Mailbox/PC Mail**: Entire mailbox menu system is unimplemented; the port has no mail feature.
- **Condition graph**: The GBA implementation uses scanline DMA into `REG_WIN0H`/`REG_WIN1H` to mask a solid-color background into a pentagon shape — a hardware trick with no direct browser equivalent. A canvas/SVG pentagon renderer would be needed if the Pokénav condition screen is ever ported. Currently the port has no Pokénav or Contest features.
- **Contest data**: `MoveRelearnerMenuLoadContestMoveDescription` and `gContestMoves`/`gContestEffectDescriptionPointers` have no TS equivalents; contests are out of scope.
- **Move relearner**: Core list/navigation logic exists in TS under `src/menu/moves/`, but it is wired for move *forget* (level-up overflow), not the NPC Move Reminder relearner. The relearner NPC script (`special MoveRelearner`) is not yet implemented.
- **Level-up stat window**: `DrawLevelUpWindowPg1`/`Pg2` logic is replicated inside the evolution/level-up flow in `EvolutionRenderer.ts` rather than as standalone utility functions.
- `sConditionToLineLength[256]` lookup table and `sConditionSparkleCoords[10][2]` have no generated equivalents.

- [x] `MetatileBehavior_IsATile` — trivially returns TRUE → not needed in TS
- [x] `Unref_*` functions — all marked unused/unref in C source → intentionally omitted

#### Notes
The metatile behavior constant enum (`MB_*`) values are generated by `scripts/generate-metatile-labels.cjs` into `src/data/metatileLabels.gen.ts`. The predicate functions themselves are hand-ported in `src/utils/metatileBehaviors.ts` with a thin generated shim for the `sTileBitAttributes` lookup table in `src/utils/metatileBehaviors.generated.ts`. The `BUGFIX`-guarded `MB_WATER_DOOR` branch in `IsUnableToEmerge` is present in the TS port. Secret base predicates are fully ported but unused at runtime (Secret Base feature not implemented).

This is a GBA-specific debug utility (`#ifndef NDEBUG` guard) — a stripped-down printf used only for debug logging on hardware. The `%S` format specifier handles GBA-encoded strings (pokeemerald's custom charset). The browser port uses native JavaScript string formatting everywhere; there is no equivalent needed. Intentionally omitted as a debug/hardware utility with no gameplay relevance.
