The browser port has no link-cable or wireless adapter layer, so the synchronized-timer path (`gReceivedRemoteLinkPlayers`, `Rfu_SendPacket`) is intentionally irrelevant. The animated countdown visuals themselves could theoretically be ported as a pure CSS/canvas animation if wireless minigames are ever added, but none of those minigames are implemented, making this file a low-priority intentional omission.

## batch: src/mirage_tower.c

### `src/mirage_tower.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/scriptEffects/mirageTowerCollapseRuntime.ts`, `src/scripting/specials/mirageTowerSpecials.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `IsMirageTowerVisible` — checks map + flag; implemented in `mirageTowerSpecials.ts`
- [x] `SetMirageTowerVisibility` — random visibility with flag override; implemented in `mirageTowerSpecials.ts`
- [x] `SetInvisibleMirageTowerMetatiles` — replaces tower footprint with sand metatiles; implemented in `mirageTowerCollapseRuntime.ts` (with MAP_OFFSET subtraction from baked-in coords)
- [x] `TryStartMirageTowerPulseBlendEffect` / `ClearMirageTowerPulseBlendEffect` — palette pulse blend lifecycle; implemented as `TransientMetatilePulseManager` / `mirageTowerSpecials.ts` pulse hook
- [x] `StartMirageTowerShake` / `InitMirageTowerShake` — loads tower BG overlay, shakes camera; implemented in `mirageTowerCollapseRuntime.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `DoMirageTowerDisintegration` — pixel-level tile disintegration via `UpdateDisintegrationEffect` randomized erasure of the BG tile buffer; the high-level sequence (shake → collapse → script resume) is ported, but the exact per-pixel 4bpp tile-nibble erasure algorithm (`UpdateDisintegrationEffect`) is not reproduced — TS uses a fade/dissolve approximation
- [-] `Task_FossilFallAndSink` / `SpriteCB_FallingFossil` — fossil sprite falling and pixel disintegration on landing; the fall animation exists in `mirageTowerCollapseRuntime.ts` but the per-pixel `UpdateDisintegrationEffect` disintegration loop for the fossil sprite is approximated, not exact
- [-] `DoMirageTowerCeilingCrumble` / `CreateCeilingCrumbleSprites` / `SpriteCB_CeilingCrumble` — 16 falling debris sprites with player-palette tint and screen shake; TS has a simplified crumble effect but does not replicate the exact 8-position debris sprite layout or the inadvertent PALSLOT_PLAYER color tint behavior

#### Missing
- [ ] `UpdateDisintegrationEffect` — core 4bpp tile-nibble erasure algorithm; randomized pixel-by-pixel GBA VRAM tile destruction has no direct browser equivalent and is not ported
- [ ] `PlayerDescendMirageTower` — duplicate object event of player animated falling down tower during disintegration; the falling-player object event is not replicated
- [ ] `StartScreenShake` / `DoScreenShake` — camera pan oscillation using `SetCameraPanning`; screen shake during crumble uses a different path in TS (`FadeController`/`CameraController`) and may not match exact amplitude/frequency
- [ ] `UpdateMirageTowerPulseBlend` (task) / `ClearMirageTowerPulseBlend` — raw task-based per-frame palette pulse update; TS pulse blend lifecycle differs architecturally (no direct GBA palette slot manipulation)
- [ ] `UpdateBgShake` — per-frame BG0 HOFS register oscillation during shake; GBA register writes have no TS equivalent (TS uses CSS/canvas transforms)
- [ ] `SetBgShakeOffsets` — writes `REG_BG0HOFS`/`REG_BG0VOFS`; GBA hardware register, intentionally omitted
- [ ] `gMirageTowerPulseBlendSettings` data — exact pulse blend parameters (blendColor `RGB(27,25,16)`, paletteOffset `BG_PLTT_ID(6)+1`, 15 colors, delay 5, maxCoeff 11); not reproduced in TS
- [ ] `sInvisibleMirageTowerMetatiles` baked coords — used with `MAP_OFFSET` subtraction; coords are baked in TS equivalent but should be verified against the MAP_OFFSET trap documented in MEMORY.md

#### Notes
- The core gameplay flow (visibility toggle, shake sequence, metatile replacement, script resume) is ported. The visual fidelity gap is the `UpdateDisintegrationEffect` pixel-erasure algorithm, which manipulates raw 4bpp GBA tile data in VRAM — this has no meaningful browser canvas equivalent and is intentionally approximated.
- `sMirageTowerCrumbles_Palette` uses `PALSLOT_PLAYER` (an apparent GBA bug causing gender-dependent green tint on crumble sprites); the TS port does not replicate this artifact.
- The BG overlay approach (loading `mirage_tower.4bpp` / `mirage_tower.bin` onto BG0) is architecturally replaced in TS by canvas/WebGL sprite layering — exact visual match is not expected.
- `sInvisibleMirageTowerMetatiles` coords are baked with content-space values (no MAP_OFFSET); the C source adds `MAP_OFFSET` at call time in `SetInvisibleMirageTowerMetatiles` — TS must NOT add MAP_OFFSET (per MEMORY.md trap).

- [ ] `CreateMarkingComboSprite` — shared internal sprite builder for both combo variants

#### Notes
This file implements the Pokémon box markings UI (circle/square/triangle/heart toggles) used in the PC, party summary, and Pokénav. The port has no equivalent — `src/pokemon/types.ts` likely carries a `markings` field on the Pokémon struct, but no interactive marking-toggle menu exists in TypeScript. The heavy use of GBA OBJ sprites, DMA tile loading, and hardware sprite animation frames are all irrelevant in browser context; the UI would need to be reimplemented as a React overlay component. This feature is gameplay-optional (markings have no battle effect) so omission is low priority.

The XOR encryption key behavior is preserved in the TS port since it reads/writes the same save format as the native Gen 3 binary save (`Gen3SaveParser.ts`).

- [x] Move learning confirmation prompts — `moveLearningPromptAdapter.ts` drives yes/no confirm → teach/stop flow

- [x] `RemoveMonPPBonus` — PP bonus clearing on move overwrite handled in move learning logic

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] Full state machine (`DoMoveRelearnerMain`, 33 states) — TS covers the core teach/forget/cancel branches but the battle vs. contest display toggle (left/right arrows switching between `MENU_STATE_IDLE_BATTLE_MODE` / `MENU_STATE_IDLE_CONTEST_MODE`) is absent; contest mode not implemented
- [-] Move list scrolling UI — `MoveListModel.ts` + `useMoveListNavigation.ts` provide a list model but no equivalent of the GBA scroll-arrow system (`AddScrollArrows`/`RemoveScrollArrows`)
- [-] `CreateLearnableMovesList` — learnset filtering exists but the full relearner move pool (all previously learnable level-up moves the mon has forgotten) is not computed identically to `GetMoveRelearnerMoves`

#### Missing
- [ ] `TeachMoveRelearnerMove` entry point — no equivalent field-script callable entry that fades out, sets callback, and returns VAR_0x8004 result to the script engine
- [ ] `CB2_InitLearnMove` / `CB2_InitLearnMoveReturnFromSelectMove` — GBA-style callback setup not needed, but the return-from-summary-screen re-entry path has no TS equivalent
- [ ] `MoveRelearnerShowHideHearts` — contest appeal/jam heart sprites; contest info entirely absent
- [ ] `HideHeartSpritesAndShowTeachMoveText` / `ShowTeachMoveText` — contest-mode display switching
- [ ] `HandleInput` left/right mode toggle — switching between battle and contest info panels
- [ ] `InitMoveRelearnerBackgroundLayers` — GBA BG setup; intentionally omitted (browser rendering)
- [ ] `VBlankCB_MoveRelearner` — GBA VBlank handler; intentionally omitted
- [ ] `CreateUISprites` / heart sprite creation (16 appeal/jam heart sprites) — contest UI sprites not present
- [ ] Double-fanfare forgot-move sequence (`MENU_STATE_DOUBLE_FANFARE_FORGOT_MOVE`) — fanfare audio not implemented
- [ ] `gSpecialVar_0x8004` result write-back — the relearner does not currently signal success/failure back to the script engine via game variables

#### Notes
- The TS port has a working move-forget flow triggered from level-up (`openMoveForgetMenu.ts`) but the **relearner-specific** flow (accessed via Move Reminder NPC, consuming a Heart Scale) is not wired as a standalone scene callable from a map script.
- Contest mode (appeal/jam hearts, `showContestInfo` flag) is entirely absent from the TS port — this is an intentional omission consistent with no contest system existing.
- The GBA version's `CB2_InitLearnMoveReturnFromSelectMove` re-entry pattern (preserving `listOffset`/`listRow` across a full callback swap) has no equivalent; the TS menu is retained in React state and doesn't need it.
- `VAR_0x8004` result signaling (TRUE = taught, FALSE = gave up) needed for `ScriptRunner` integration is missing.

Intentionally omitted. This file implements GBA link cable multi-boot (single-pak multiplayer ROM distribution via SIO hardware registers `REG_SIOCNT`, `REG_SIODATA8`, `REG_SIOMULTI`). The browser port has no link cable, no hardware serial I/O, and no mechanism to distribute a ROM to other clients. This entire subsystem is irrelevant to a single-player browser emulation context.

- [ ] `MEScrCmd_setmsg` — script command: conditionally expands a localized message into gStringVar4
- [ ] `MEScrCmd_runscript` — script command: runs a standard field script immediately
- [ ] `MEScrCmd_setenigmaberry` — script command: loads Enigma Berry data into save and sets VAR_ENIGMA_BERRY_AVAILABLE
- [ ] `MEScrCmd_giveribbon` — script command: gives a gift ribbon to the player's party
- [ ] `MEScrCmd_initramscript` — script command: installs a RAM script for a specific map/object
- [ ] `MEScrCmd_givenationaldex` — script command: unlocks National Pokédex
- [ ] `MEScrCmd_addrareword` — script command: unlocks a Trendy Saying entry

- [ ] `MEScrCmd_givepokemon` — script command: adds a Mystery Gift Pokémon (with optional mail) to the party
- [ ] `MEScrCmd_addtrainer` — script command: loads an e-Reader trainer into the Battle Frontier save slot
- [ ] `MEScrCmd_enableresetrtc` — script command: enables RTC reset capability
- [ ] `MEScrCmd_checksum` — script command: validates a byte-array checksum; invalidates context on mismatch
- [ ] `MEScrCmd_crc` — script command: validates a CRC-16; invalidates context on mismatch

#### Notes
This entire subsystem is the **e-Reader / Mystery Event** infrastructure (GBA hardware peripheral). It has no browser equivalent and is intentionally out of scope for the port: there is no wireless adapter, no e-Reader card reader, no link cable, and the GBA version-mask checks are hardware-specific. The only tangentially relevant concept is the `recordMixingGift` save field and `enigmaBerry`, but those are driven by this peripheral and are not reachable via normal gameplay in the browser port. Safe to mark as intentionally omitted.

Mystery Gift is a GBA wireless adapter / e-Reader feature for receiving Wonder News and Wonder Cards over link cable or infrared. It has no browser equivalent — there is no wireless hardware, no link cable, and the browser port has no plans to emulate the Mystery Gift distribution system. The save-block data structures (`mysteryGift` in `SaveBlock1`) are technically present in the Gen3 save format parsed by `src/save/native/Gen3SaveParser.ts`, but the runtime logic is entirely absent. This is an intentional omission: the feature is post-game, requires external hardware/servers, and is out of scope for the browser port.

- [ ] `Client_RunMysteryEventScript` — runs mystery event script context commands
- [ ] `Client_RunBufferScript` — executes arbitrary code from decompression buffer (e-Reader trainer injection)

#### Notes
Entire Mystery Gift / Wonder Card / Wonder News subsystem is absent from the TS port. This is intentional: the system requires GBA wireless adapter or link cable hardware (SIO), server-side distribution infrastructure, and an e-Reader interface — none of which have browser equivalents. `Client_RunBufferScript` executes arbitrary native code from a receive buffer, which has no meaningful browser analogue. The save-side effects (`SaveWonderCard`, `SaveWonderNews`, `MysteryGift_TrySaveStamp`, `InitRamScript_NoObjectEvent`) are also unimplemented. Low priority for a gameplay port; can remain omitted.

Mystery Gift as a whole (wonder card download, e-Reader, friend delivery) is post-game/connectivity content with no planned TS implementation.

The Wonder Card/News storage and save-data structures (`wonder_card.c`, `wonder_news.c`) are also unported. This is an intentional omission: wireless Mystery Gift distribution is not feasible in a browser port, and the feature is post-game / side-content that does not block the main story. The only dependency the main game flow has on this file is the `CB2_InitMysteryGift` entry point reachable from the main menu; that path simply does not exist in the TS port.

- [ ] `sServerScript_ClientCanceledCard` — server script: send dynamic error with canceled-reading text, return CLIENT_CANCELED
- [ ] `sServerScript_HasNews` — server script: tell client it already has news, return HAS_NEWS

#### Notes
Mystery Gift (Wonder Card / Wonder News) is entirely unimplemented. This feature requires:
- Link cable or wireless adapter communication (hardware-specific, no browser equivalent without a custom multiplayer backend)
- e-Reader / Nintendo WFC integration (no longer operational)
- RAM script injection (`CLI_SAVE_RAM_SCRIPT` / `SVR_LOAD_RAM_SCRIPT`) which is a security-sensitive mechanism

The entire Mystery Gift subsystem is an intentional omission — it depends on GBA link cable / wireless adapter protocol (SIO registers, JOY bus), which has no direct browser equivalent. Online distribution infrastructure (Nintendo WFC) is also defunct. None of the client/server script interpreter machinery (`mystery_gift_client.c`, `mystery_gift_server.c`) has been ported.

This entire subsystem is an **intentional omission**: it has no gameplay impact on a single-player offline browser port and depends entirely on absent multiplayer infrastructure.

- [ ] `WonderNews_Exit` — 6-state machine: fade out, clear WIN registers, clear BGs, remove windows/scroll arrows, restore header, fade in
- [ ] `WonderNews_RemoveScrollIndicatorArrowPair` — removes scroll arrow task (called when entering sub-menu)
- [ ] `WonderNews_AddScrollIndicatorArrowPair` — re-adds scroll arrow task (called when returning from sub-menu)

#### Notes
Mystery Gift / Wonder Card / Wonder News is a link-cable/e-Reader feature with no browser equivalent. The entire system depends on GBA hardware registers (WIN0, BG scroll via `ChangeBgY`), VRAM tile decompression, and wireless RFU link communication — none of which have browser ports. This is an intentional omission; the feature is post-game and requires external connectivity infrastructure that is out of scope for the browser port.

- [ ] `Task_HandlePageSwapAnim` + `PageSwapAnimState_*` — 4-state sinusoidal BG scroll animation for page flip
- [ ] `CreateButtonFlashTask` / `TryStartButtonFlash` / `Task_UpdateButtonFlash` — palette-based button highlight flash system
- [ ] `CreateSprites` + all `Create*Sprite` helpers — cursor, page-swap button, back/OK buttons, text-entry underscores, input arrow, PC/player/mon icons

- [ ] `SpriteCB_InputArrow` — bouncing input-arrow animation (x offsets cycling)

- [ ] `SetCursorPos` / `GetCursorPos` / `MoveCursorToOKButton` / `SetCursorInvisibility` / `SetCursorFlashing` — cursor position/visibility management

- [ ] `Task_HandleInput` / `Input_Enabled` / `Input_Disabled` / `Input_Override` — input task with 3 states
- [ ] `HandleDpadMovement` — cursor movement with key/button column transition and row wrap logic
- [ ] `KeyboardKeyHandler_Character` / `_Page` / `_Backspace` / `_OK` — per-key-role action handlers
- [ ] `SwapKeyboardPage` — triggers page-swap state
- [ ] `AddTextCharacter` / `DeleteTextCharacter` / `BufferCharacter` / `SaveInputText` — text buffer management
- [ ] `GetTextEntryPosition` / `GetPreviousTextCaretPosition` / `GetCharAtKeyboardPos` — text buffer utilities
- [ ] `DrawTextEntry` — renders current text buffer with wide-letter handling and gender icon
- [ ] `DrawTextEntryBox` / `DrawNormalTextEntryBox` / `DrawMonTextEntryBox` — title/species header above text entry
- [ ] `DrawGenderIcon` / `TryDrawGenderIcon` — renders ♂/♀ in coloured font next to entered name
- [ ] `PrintKeyboardKeys` / `DrawKeyboardPageOnDeck` / `PrintControls` — keyboard BG text printing
- [ ] `NamingScreen_Create*Icon` family — player overworld sprite, PC icon, mon icon, Walda's dad icon
- [ ] `VBlankCB_NamingScreen` — VBlank: transfers OAM/palette, sets BG scroll regs and BG priority regs for page-swap
- [ ] All sprite/palette/template data tables (`sSpriteSheets`, `sSpritePalettes`, `sNamingScreenTemplates`, `sKeyboardChars`, `sPageColumnCounts`, etc.)

#### Notes
The naming screen is entirely absent from the TypeScript port. It is a prerequisite for:
- **New-game player naming** (`NAMING_SCREEN_PLAYER`) — called from `src/game/NewGameFlow.ts` which currently has no naming step.
- **Caught-mon nicknaming** (`NAMING_SCREEN_CAUGHT_MON` / `NAMING_SCREEN_NICKNAME`) — needed after catch flow.
- **PC box naming** (`NAMING_SCREEN_BOX`).
- **Walda phrase input** (`NAMING_SCREEN_WALDA`).

The GBA implementation is heavily hardware-specific (GBA BG layers, OAM, DMA, VBlank ISR, palette fade hardware), but the gameplay logic (keyboard layout, cursor navigation, page-swap, character buffering) is fully portable to a browser React component. The existing `PromptCanvasRenderer` / `PromptService` infrastructure provides some analogous text-window primitives but does not implement a full character-selection keyboard UI. A new `NamingScreen` React component or modal state would be the natural TS equivalent, invoking the existing `DialogContext` or a new `GameState` (analogous to `BirchSpeechState`). The `SeedRngAndSetTrainerId()` call on exit ties into save-block initialisation which already exists in `src/save/`.

- [-] `InitPlayerTrainerId` — random trainer ID generated at save creation in `src/save/SaveManager.ts`, but uses JS `Math.random()` rather than the GBA `Random()` / `GetGeneratedTrainerIdLower()` split; functional parity close but not exact

- [ ] `Sav2_ClearSetDefault` — explicit SaveBlock2 clear + set defaults; no direct equivalent (save init is implicit)

- [ ] `ClearPokedexFlags` — Pokédex owned/seen bitfields; Pokédex system not implemented

- [ ] `ClearFrontierRecord` — Battle Frontier save data; Frontier not implemented

- [ ] `ZeroEnemyPartyMons` — enemy party zeroing; battle init handles this ad-hoc but no explicit call

- [ ] `InitEasyChatPhrases` — EasyChat not implemented
- [ ] `SetMauvilleOldMan` — Mauville old man story not implemented
- [ ] `InitDewfordTrend` — Dewford trend system not implemented
- [ ] `ResetFanClub` — Pokémon Fan Club not implemented
- [ ] `ResetLotteryCorner` — Lottery Corner not implemented

#### Notes
- The browser port's new-game path is intentionally minimal: it warps to the truck and runs the truck cutscene, then transitions to Birch's speech. The ~30 missing sub-systems are post-game, multiplayer (link cable/RFU), or cosmetic features (Contest, Pokéblocks, Decorations) that are out of scope for the current MVP.
- `gDifferentSaveFile` flag has no TS equivalent; it controls whether certain legacy reset paths run and is not needed in the browser save model.
- `EventScript_ResetAllMapFlags` is a script constant covered by generated data (`src/data/newGameFlags.gen.ts`); its execution is approximated by the flag-initialization loop in `NewGameFlow.ts`.

The GBA-specific rendering code (BG tilemap, palette slots, GBA window registers) is intentionally omitted; a browser equivalent would be a React settings overlay. The `ButtonMode` "L=A" option is GBA hardware-specific and has no browser equivalent. `SetPokemonCryStereo` (called from `Sound_ProcessInput`) has no audio system to route to.

- [x] `SetDynamicWarp` / `SetDynamicWarpWithCoords` / `SetWarpDestinationToDynamicWarp` — `src/game/DynamicWarp.ts`
- [x] `SetFixedDiveWarp` / `SetDiveWarpEmerge` / `SetDiveWarpDive` — `src/game/FixedDiveWarp.ts`, `src/game/dive/DiveWarpResolver.ts`
- [x] `SetFixedHoleWarp` / `SetWarpDestinationToFixedHoleWarp` — `src/game/FixedHoleWarp.ts`
- [x] `SetEscapeWarp` / `SetWarpDestinationToEscapeWarp` — `src/game/FixedEscapeWarp.ts`
- [x] `SetWarpDestinationToHealLocation` / `SetWarpDestinationToLastHealLocation` / `SetLastHealLocationWarp` — warp destination helpers in warp system
- [x] `SetContinueGameWarp` / `SetContinueGameWarpToHealLocation` / `SetContinueGameWarpToDynamicWarp` — continue-game warp in save/load flow
- [x] `WarpIntoMap` / `ApplyCurrentWarp` — `src/game/WarpExecutor.ts`, `src/game/overworld/warp/performWarpTransition.ts`
- [x] `IsMapTypeOutdoors` / `IsMapTypeIndoors` / `Overworld_MapTypeAllowsTeleportAndFly` — `src/game/mapTypeUtils.ts`
- [x] `GetCurrentMapType` / `GetMapTypeByGroupAndId` — `src/game/mapTypeUtils.ts`
- [x] `Overworld_IsBikingAllowed` — checked in `PlayerController.ts` via map header `allowCycling`
- [x] `SetDefaultFlashLevel` / `SetFlashLevel` / `GetFlashLevel` — `src/game/flash/FlashController.ts`
- [x] `GetMapConnection` — `src/game/mapConnections.ts`
- [x] `ResetInitialPlayerAvatarState` / `StoreInitialPlayerAvatarState` / `GetInitialPlayerAvatarState` — `src/game/overworld/load/loadSelectedOverworldMap.ts` player avatar state on warp
- [x] `GetAdjustedInitialTransitionFlags` / `GetAdjustedInitialDirection` — initial direction/avatar mode logic in `loadSelectedOverworldMap.ts`
- [x] `LoadObjEventTemplatesFromHeader` / `LoadSaveblockObjEventScripts` / `SetObjEventTemplateCoords` / `SetObjEventTemplateMovementType` — `src/game/ObjectEventManager.ts`, `src/game/applyObjectEventOverridesForMap.ts`
- [x] `GetLocationMusic` / `GetCurrLocationDefaultMusic` / `GetWarpDestinationMusic` — music selection logic in `WorldManager.ts` / overworld state
- [x] `SetDefaultFlashLevel` — `FlashController.ts`
- [x] `UpdateEscapeWarp` — `src/game/FixedEscapeWarp.ts`
- [x] `IncrementGameStat` / `GetGameStat` / `SetGameStat` — `src/game/GameVariables.ts` (game stats)
- [x] `CB2_NewGame` — `src/game/NewGameFlow.ts`, `src/game/TruckSequence.ts`
- [x] `CB2_ContinueSavedGame` — save/load continue flow in `src/save/SaveManager.ts`
- [x] `LoadMapFromCameraTransition` — seam/camera transition map loading in `src/game/overworld/seam/seamTransitionScripts.ts`
- [x] `gDirectionToVectors` — direction vector table in `src/utils/direction.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `CB2_WhiteOut` — white out state transition exists but 120-frame wait and full music/reset sequence not fully implemented as discrete callback state machine
- [-] `UpdateAmbientCry` / `ChooseAmbientCrySpecies` / `PlayAmbientCry` — ambient cry species selection from wild encounter data exists but audio playback (`PlayCry_NormalNoDucking`) is not implemented (no audio system)
- [-] `LoadMapFromWarp` — map loading pipeline partially ported in `loadSelectedOverworldMap.ts` but steps like `TrySetMapSaveWarpStatus`, `CheckLeftFriendsSecretBase`, `TryUpdateRandomTrainerRematches`, `UpdateTVScreensOnMap`, `InitSecretBaseAppearance` are absent
- [-] `ResumeMap` — partial in `loadSelectedOverworldMap.ts`; `RotatingGate_InitPuzzleAndGraphics`, `TryStartMirageTowerPulseBlendEffect`, `ResumePausedWeather` partially covered but not all tasks reset/re-init identically
- [-] `GetAdjustedInitialTransitionFlags` / `GetAdjustedInitialDirection` — surf/bike/underwater transitions handled but some metatile edge cases (deep south warp, ladder) may differ
- [-] `ShouldLegendaryMusicPlayAtLocation` / `NoMusicInSotopolisWithLegendaries` / `IsInfiltratedWeatherInstitute` / `IsInflitratedSpaceCenter` — special music override logic exists in `WorldManager.ts` but coverage of all conditional branches (Weather Institute infiltration, Space Center states) is incomplete
- [-] `TryFadeOutOldMapMusic` / `Overworld_FadeOutMapMusic` / `TransitionMapMusic` — music fade on warp partially handled; the Surf/Sky Pillar Sootopolis special case not implemented

#### Missing
- [ ] `ResetGameStats` / `ApplyNewEncryptionKeyToGameStats` — game stats encryption/reset (save encryption not implemented)
- [ ] `CB2_ReturnToFieldContestHall` / `CB2_ReturnToFieldCableClub` / `CB2_ReturnToFieldFromMultiplayer` / `CB2_ReturnToFieldWithOpenMenu` — multiplayer/contest return callbacks (link/cable club not in scope)
- [ ] `Overworld_ResetStateAfterWhiteOut` — abnormal weather VAR reset on white out not implemented
- [ ] `UpdateMiscOverworldStates` — `ResetCyclingRoadChallengeData`, `UpdateLocationHistoryForRoamer`, `RoamerMoveToOtherLocationSet` not implemented (roamer system absent)
- [ ] `CB2_LoadMap` / `CB2_LoadMap2` / `CB2_DoChangeMap` — these GBA callback state machine steps have no direct equivalent (TS uses async/await pattern instead)
- [ ] `SetFieldVBlankCallback` / `VBlankCB_Field` / `FieldClearVBlankHBlankCallbacks` — GBA VBlank/HBlank hardware interrupt management; browser equivalent not needed
- [ ] `InitOverworldBgs` / `CleanupOverworldWindowsAndTilemaps` / `InitOverworldGraphicsRegisters` / `InitViewGraphics` — GBA BG register setup; WebGL pipeline replaces this entirely
- [ ] `InitCurrentFlashLevelScanlineEffect` / `ResetScreenForMapLoad` — GBA scanline DMA effects; flash overlay handled differently in TS
- [ ] `LoadMapInStepsLocal` / `LoadMapInStepsLink` / `ReturnToFieldLocal` / `ReturnToFieldLink` / `DoMapLoadLoop` — GBA step-based map load state machines; replaced by async loading in TS
- [ ] `InitObjectEventsLink` / `SpawnLinkPlayers` / `CreateLinkPlayerSprites` / `SpawnLinkPlayerObjectEvent` / `InitLinkPlayerObjectEventPos` — all multiplayer link player object management (link cable/RFU out of scope)
- [ ] `CB1_OverworldLink` / `ResetAllMultiplayerState` / `SetKeyInterceptCallback` / all `KeyInterCB_*` callbacks — link multiplayer input interception system (out of scope)
- [ ] `UpdateAllLinkPlayers` / `HandleLinkPlayerKeyInput` / `GetLinkPlayerIdAt` / `SetPlayerFacingDirection` / `MovementEventModeCB_*` / `FacingHandler_*` / `MovementStatusHandler_*` — link player movement/collision system (out of scope)
- [ ] `CreateLinkPlayerSprite` / `SpriteCB_LinkPlayer` — link player sprite rendering (out of scope)
- [ ] `GetCableClubPartnersReady` / `SetInCableClubSeat` / `SetLinkWaitingForScript` / `QueueExitLinkRoomKey` / `SetStartedCableClubActivity` — cable club seat/ready protocol (out of scope)
- [ ] `Overworld_IsRecvQueueAtMax` / `Overworld_RecvKeysFromLinkIsRunning` / `Overworld_SendKeysToLinkIsRunning` / `IsSendingKeysOverCable` — link queue state queries (out of scope)
- [ ] `LoadMapFromSavedGame` / `InitMapFromSavedGame` — continue-game specific map init variant (partially handled in save load but not as named function)
- [ ] `GetCurrentMapBattleScene` — battle scene type query for map header (not wired up)
- [ ] `GetSavedWarpRegionMapSectionId` / `GetCurrentRegionMapSectionId` — region map section queries (region map not implemented)
- [ ] `Overworld_ResetMapMusic` / `Overworld_PlaySpecialMapMusic` / `Overworld_SetSavedMusic` / `Overworld_ClearSavedMusic` / `Overworld_ChangeMusicToDefault` / `Overworld_ChangeMusicTo` / `BGMusicStopped` / `GetMapMusicFadeoutSpeed` — full music system (no audio implementation)
- [ ] `SetCurrentMapLayout` / `SetObjectEventLoadFlag` / `GetMapLayout` — save block map layout management helpers
- [ ] `ClearLinkPlayerObjectEvents` / `ZeroLinkPlayerObjectEvent` / `ZeroObjectEvent` — link player cleanup (out of scope)
- [ ] `ResetMirageTowerAndSaveBlockPtrs` — `ClearMirageTowerPulseBlend` + `MoveSaveBlocks_ResetHeap` combo (heap management not applicable)
- [ ] `OffsetCameraFocusByLinkPlayerId` / `SetCameraToTrackGuestPlayer` / `SetCameraToTrackGuestPlayer_2` — link camera tracking (out of scope)
- [ ] `InitLinkRoomStartMenuScript` / `RunConfirmLeaveCableClubScript` / `RunTerminateLinkScript` / `RunInteractLocalPlayerScript` / `InitMenuBasedScript` / `InitLinkPlayerQueueScript` — cable club script launchers (out of scope)
- [ ] `GetDirectionForEventScript` — cable club colosseum/trade seat facing lookup (out of scope)

#### Notes
- The entire link/cable club/RFU multiplayer subsystem (~40% of this file by line count) is intentionally omitted — browser port is single-player only.
- GBA hardware callbacks (`CB1_Overworld`, `CB2_Overworld`, `VBlankCB_Field`, etc.) have no direct equivalents; the TS port uses a React game loop (`useGameLoop`, `useUnifiedGameLoop`) and WebGL rendering pipeline instead.
- Map loading steps are async/promise-based in TS rather than the GBA's per-frame state machine.
- Audio system (`MUS_*`, `PlayNewMapMusic`, `FadeOutMapMusic`, ambient cries) is entirely absent — no audio implementation exists.
- Roamer system (`UpdateLocationHistoryForRoamer`, `RoamerMove`) is not implemented.
- `gDirectionToVectors` is ported as direction utility functions in `src/utils/direction.ts`.
- Game stats encryption (`encryptionKey` XOR) is not implemented — TS uses plain localStorage.
- Warp/location data structures (`WarpData`, `sWarpDestination`, `sFixedDiveWarp`, etc.) are distributed across several TS files rather than a single global state struct.

## batch: src/palette.c

### `src/palette.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/field/FadeController.ts`, `src/rendering/webgl/WebGLFadeRenderer.ts`, `src/rendering/Canvas2DFadeRenderer.ts`, `src/rendering/IFadeRenderer.ts`, `src/weather/WeatherColorPipeline.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `BeginNormalPaletteFade` — fade controller entry point; mapped to `FadeController` start/begin methods
- [x] `BeginFastPaletteFade` / `BeginFastPaletteFadeInternal` — fast black/white in-out fades; covered by `WebGLFadeRenderer` and `Canvas2DFadeRenderer` submodes
- [x] `UpdatePaletteFade` — per-frame fade step; driven by `FadeController.update()` / `WebGLFadeRenderer` frame loop
- [x] `UpdateNormalPaletteFade` — incremental blend toward target Y; implemented in fade renderer step logic
- [x] `UpdateFastPaletteFade` — fast ±2 per-channel RGB approach; implemented in WebGL/Canvas2D fade renderers (FAST_FADE_IN_FROM_BLACK, FAST_FADE_OUT_TO_WHITE, etc.)
- [x] `BlendPalettes` / `BlendPalette` — palette blending with coeff+color; handled by `WeatherColorPipeline` and WebGL tint passes
- [x] `TintPalette_GrayScale` — grayscale conversion using luminance weights; used in evolution/flash effects via `WeatherColorPipeline`
- [x] `TintPalette_CustomTone` — custom RGB tone palette tint; mapped to `WeatherColorPipeline` tint operations
- [x] `ResetPaletteFadeControl` / `ResetPaletteFade` — reset fade state; `FadeController` reset paths

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `BlendPalettesGradually` / `Task_BlendPalettesGradually` — task-based gradual blend used for Groudon/Kyogre lightning flash; `WebGLOrbEffectRenderer` covers orb flash but the generic task-based gradual blending pipeline is not a direct port — no general `BlendPalettesGradually` equivalent exists for arbitrary palette slots
- [-] `TintPalette_GrayScale2` — uses `sRoundedDownGrayscaleMap` rounding table; grayscale is implemented but the specific 5-step quantization table rounding is not replicated
- [-] `TintPalette_SepiaTone` — sepia conversion; not directly ported but custom-tone equivalent covers it if called with sepia coefficients; no dedicated sepia path exists
- [-] `BeginHardwarePaletteFade` / `UpdateHardwarePaletteFade` — GBA BLDCNT/BLDY hardware blend registers; approximated via CSS/WebGL blend ops but hardware register semantics (`REG_OFFSET_BLDCNT`, `REG_OFFSET_BLDY`) are replaced with shader uniforms; `shouldResetBlendRegisters` flag not ported

#### Missing
- [ ] `LoadCompressedPalette` — LZ-decompresses palette data into unfaded/faded buffers; no direct equivalent (palette data is handled via PNG sprite sheets, not raw GBA palette buffers)
- [ ] `LoadPalette` / `FillPalette` — raw palette slot writes to `gPlttBufferUnfaded`/`gPlttBufferFaded`; GBA PLTT buffer abstraction does not exist in TS (replaced by WebGL texture/uniform system)
- [ ] `TransferPlttBuffer` — DMA copies faded buffer to hardware PLTT; no equivalent (WebGL uploads textures directly)
- [ ] `UnfadePlttBuffer` / `InvertPlttBuffer` / `TintPlttBuffer` — direct in-place mutations of the faded palette buffer; not ported (no equivalent mutable palette buffer structure)
- [ ] `PaletteStruct_*` family (16 functions) — the `sPaletteStructs[16]` animation system (animated palette cycling with timing); entirely unused in pokeemerald itself (marked UNUSED) and not ported
- [ ] `BlendPalettesUnfaded` — DMA-copies unfaded→faded then blends; no equivalent
- [ ] `ReadPlttIntoBuffers` — reads hardware PLTT back into buffers; GBA-hardware-only, intentionally omitted

#### Notes
- The GBA palette system (`gPlttBufferUnfaded`/`gPlttBufferFaded`, 512-entry u16 arrays, DMA to `PLTT`) has no direct TS equivalent. The browser port replaces it with WebGL texture uploads, CSS transitions, and shader-based tinting.
- `BeginHardwarePaletteFade` (BLDCNT/BLDY registers) is approximated via WebGL blend uniforms; exact per-scanline hardware blend semantics are not replicated but visual results are close enough.
- The `PaletteStruct` animation system is explicitly marked `UNUSED` in the C source and is intentionally omitted.
- `BlendPalettesGradually` is used only for the Groudon/Kyogre climax scene — the orb effect renderer (`WebGLOrbEffectRenderer`) handles that specific case but is not a general-purpose port of the task-based gradual blend.

## batch: src/palette_util.c

### `src/palette_util.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/scriptEffects/orbEffectRuntime.ts`, `src/rendering/webgl/WebGLOrbEffectRenderer.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `UpdatePulseBlend` (type 1 / fade-in-out) — the pulse-blend fade-in/out logic is replicated for the orb/Mirage Tower effect in `orbEffectRuntime.ts` and rendered via `WebGLOrbEffectRenderer`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `InitPulseBlend` / `InitPulseBlendPaletteSettings` — slot-allocation logic exists implicitly inside `orbEffectRuntime.ts` but not as a general multi-slot palette manager
- [-] `UpdatePulseBlend` (type 0 / wrap-around, type 2 / flip) — only fade-type 1 (in-out) is used in the orb/Mirage Tower path; types 0 and 2 are unused in vanilla and not ported

#### Missing
- [ ] `RouletteFlash_Reset` / `RouletteFlash_Add` / `RouletteFlash_Run` / `RouletteFlash_Enable` / `RouletteFlash_Stop` — full Roulette wheel palette-flash system; Roulette minigame is not ported
- [ ] `RouletteFlash_FadePalette` / `RouletteFlash_FlashPalette` — internal fade/flash helpers for Roulette; not ported
- [ ] `MarkUsedPulseBlendPalettes` / `UnmarkUsedPulseBlendPalettes` / `UnloadUsedPulseBlendPalettes` — general pulse-blend slot lifecycle management; no direct TS equivalent (orbEffectRuntime manages its own state ad-hoc)
- [ ] `ClearPulseBlendPalettesSettings` — palette restore-on-unload logic; not ported
- [ ] `FillTilemapRect` / `SetTilemapRect` — GBA tilemap fill/blit helpers used by Roulette grid; not ported (no GBA VRAM tilemap in browser renderer)

#### Notes
- The `RouletteFlash_*` family exists solely for the Roulette minigame UI, which is not implemented in the TS port — intentional omission.
- `FillTilemapRect`/`SetTilemapRect` operate on GBA VRAM tilemap memory (32-tile-wide rows); the WebGL renderer has no equivalent structure, so these are intentionally omitted.
- The `PulseBlend` system is partially replicated specifically for the Mirage Tower collapse orb effect; the general-purpose 16-slot manager has not been ported as a reusable utility.
- `UpdatePulseBlend` fade-type 0 has a known vanilla bug (signed/unsigned comparison) and is dead code; not porting it is correct.

- [ ] `UseSacredAsh` / `Task_SacredAshLoop` / `Task_SacredAshDisplayHPRestored` — Sacred Ash full-party revive loop
- [ ] `ItemUseCB_ReduceEV` — EV-reducing berry use callback

- [ ] `ShiftMoveSlot` — reorder move slots after forget
- [ ] `SetSelectedMoveForPPItem` / `TryUsePPItem` — PP restore item target move selection
- [ ] `ReturnGiveItemToBagOrPC` — return item to bag or PC when bag is full

- [ ] `DrawHeldItemIconsForTrade` / `CreateHeldItemSpriteForTrade` — held item sprites in the trade screen

#### Notes
- The TypeScript port has a functional party menu (`PartyMenuContent.tsx`, `PartySlot.tsx`, `HPBar.tsx`) covering the basic field use case (viewing, switching, using items), but is missing the full action-popup system, all secondary menus (mail, register, trade, minigame, contest), the two-column double-layout navigation, Battle Frontier modes, and the GBA sprite/palette animation system.
- Link cable, Union Room, and wireless minigame features are intentionally omitted.
- Contest participation is not ported.
- The GBA-specific hardware rendering (BG tilemaps, OAM sprites, palette loads, DMA) has no direct equivalent and is replaced by React/CSS rendering; those functions are structurally omitted.
- `GetAilmentFromStatus` logic is duplicated/reimplemented in multiple TS files rather than centralised.
- Sacred Ash, EV-reducing berries, PP items, and Rare Candy level-up flows are missing from the party menu layer (though some item effects exist in `fieldItemEffects.ts`).

**Generation script**: None

- [x] `ItemUseCB_TMHM` — TM/HM teaching flow: eligibility check, give move, prompt to replace if full; covered by `src/pokemon/moveLearning.ts` + `src/menu/moves/openMoveForgetMenu.ts`
- [x] `ItemIdToBattleMoveId` — maps item ID to move ID via TM/HM table; covered in `src/data/moves.ts` (generated) + move learning logic

- [x] `Task_ReplaceMoveYesNo` / `Task_ShowSummaryScreenToForgetMove` / `Task_PartyMenuReplaceMove` — forget-move flow after moveset is full; `src/menu/moves/openMoveForgetMenu.ts` + `src/pokemon/moveLearningPromptAdapter.ts`
- [x] `StopLearningMovePrompt` / `Task_HandleStopLearningMoveYesNoInput` — "stop learning?" yes/no prompt; `src/pokemon/moveLearningPromptAdapter.ts`
- [x] `ItemUseCB_PPRecovery` / `ItemUseCB_PPUp` — PP restore item flows showing move select window; `src/pokemon/fieldItemEffects.ts`
- [x] `TryUsePPItem` — applies PP item effect and shows result message; `src/pokemon/fieldItemEffects.ts`
- [x] `ItemUseCB_RareCandy` — applies Rare Candy, buffers stats, triggers level-up fanfare; `src/pokemon/fieldItemEffects.ts`
- [x] `Task_TryLearnNewMoves` / `Task_TryLearningNextMove` — post-level-up move learning loop; `src/pokemon/moveLearning.ts`
- [x] `PartyMenuTryEvolution` — triggers evolution scene after level-up; `src/states/EvolutionState.ts` + `src/pokemon/evolution.ts`
- [x] `ItemUseCB_SacredAsh` / `UseSacredAsh` / `Task_SacredAshLoop` — Sacred Ash full-party revive loop; `src/pokemon/fieldItemEffects.ts`
- [x] `ItemUseCB_EvolutionStone` — applies evolution stone, triggers evolution; `src/pokemon/fieldItemEffects.ts`
- [x] `GetItemEffectType` — classifies item by its effect byte flags; `src/data/items.ts` (partially via generated item data) and `src/pokemon/fieldItemEffects.ts`
- [x] `MoveDeleterForgetMove` / `ShiftMoveSlot` — remove a move and shift remaining slots; `src/menu/moves/openMoveForgetMenu.ts`
- [x] `BufferMoveDeleterNicknameAndMove` — buffers nickname + move name for move deleter dialogue; handled inline in scripting specials
- [x] `GetNumMovesSelectedMonHas` — counts non-NONE moves on selected mon; inline in scripting
- [x] `MoveDeleterChooseMoveToForget` — opens summary screen in move-select mode; `src/menu/moves/openMoveForgetMenu.ts`
- [x] `IsSelectedMonEgg` — checks if selected party mon is an egg; `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts` special handling

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `ShowMoveSelectWindow` / `Task_HandleWhichMoveInput` / `SetSelectedMoveForPPItem` — move selection UI for PP items exists functionally but the in-party move-select window is not a dedicated component; routed through `ScriptChoiceMenuContent.tsx` or dialog choice rather than a dedicated move-window overlay
- [-] `Task_DisplayLevelUpStatsPg1` / `Task_DisplayLevelUpStatsPg2` / `DisplayLevelUpStatsPg1/2` — level-up stat comparison window (before/after) partially implemented; `src/menu/components/HPBar.tsx` shows current stats but the two-page before/after diff window is not fully rendered
- [-] `UpdateMonDisplayInfoAfterRareCandy` — HP bar / level / status gfx refresh after Rare Candy; HP bar updates present in React state but sprite animation (`AnimatePartySlot`) has no direct equivalent
- [-] `TryTutorSelectedMon` — move tutor flow: eligibility check and teach are present but the full in-field tutor callback chain (`ChooseMonForMoveTutor`) is only partially wired in `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts`
- [-] `CB2_WriteMailToGiveMonFromBag` / `CB2_ReturnToPartyOrBagMenuFromWritingMail` — mail writing flow; no mail/EasyChat system implemented
- [-] `TryGiveItemOrMailToSelectedMon` / `GiveItemOrMailToSelectedMon` — give-item from bag flow handles non-mail items; mail branch unimplemented
- [-] `IsLastMonThatKnowsSurf` — checks party + PC storage for last Surf user; PC storage check (`AnyStorageMonWithMove`) not implemented

#### Missing
- [ ] `CB2_PartyMenuFromStartMenu` — entry point opening party menu from Start menu with standard field layout
- [ ] `CB2_ChooseMonToGiveItem` — entry point for giving an item to a mon from the bag menu
- [ ] `Task_SwitchItemsFromBagYesNo` / `Task_HandleSwitchItemsFromBagYesNoInput` — yes/no to swap a currently held item when giving from bag
- [ ] `DisplayItemMustBeRemovedFirstMessage` — error when trying to give item to mon holding mail
- [ ] `ChooseMonToGiveMailFromMailbox` / `TryGiveMailToSelectedMon` — mailbox → party mail assignment flow
- [ ] `InitChooseHalfPartyForBattle` / `Task_ValidateChosenHalfParty` / `CheckBattleEntriesAndGetMessage` — Battle Frontier half-party selection with eligibility validation
- [ ] `GetBattleEntryEligibility` — checks species bans, level caps, held items for Frontier entry
- [ ] `GetMaxBattleEntries` / `GetMinBattleEntries` / `GetBattleEntryLevelCap` — Frontier facility-specific party size/level limits
- [ ] `GetFacilityCancelString` — facility-specific cancel button label
- [ ] `ChooseMonForTradingBoard` — party select for Union Room trading
- [ ] `ChooseMonForWirelessMinigame` — party select for wireless minigames
- [ ] `OpenPartyMenuInBattle` / `ChooseMonForInBattleItem` — in-battle party menu entry points
- [ ] `GetPartyMenuActionsTypeInBattle` — determines which action buttons show in-battle (send out / shift / summary only)
- [ ] `TrySwitchInPokemon` — validates and executes in-battle switch with all guard conditions (multi-battle ally, fainted, already in battle, same as prev, ability lock, forced stay)
- [ ] `BufferBattlePartyCurrentOrder` / `BufferBattlePartyOrder` / `BufferBattlePartyCurrentOrderBySide` / `BufferBattlePartyOrderBySide` — packs party order into nibble-packed bytes for battle slot tracking
- [ ] `SwitchPartyOrderLinkMulti` — adjusts nibble-packed party order for multi-battle link scenario
- [ ] `GetPartyIdFromBattleSlot` / `SetPartyIdAtBattleSlot` / `SwitchPartyMonSlots` / `GetPartyIdFromBattlePartyId` — low-level battle party order slot accessors
- [ ] `UpdatePartyToBattleOrder` / `UpdatePartyToFieldOrder` — reorders `gPlayerParty` array to match battle or field slot order
- [ ] `ShowPartyMenuToShowcaseMultiBattleParty` / `Task_InitMultiPartnerPartySlideIn` / `Task_MultiPartnerPartySlideIn` — multi-battle partner party slide-in showcase animation
- [ ] `ChooseMonForDaycare` — party select entry point for Day Care
- [ ] `ChooseContestMon` / `Task_ChooseContestMon` / `CB2_ChooseContestMon` — party select for Contests
- [ ] `ChoosePartyMon` / `Task_ChoosePartyMon` — generic "show mon to NPC" party select (in-game trades, move deleter)
- [ ] `ChooseMonForMoveRelearner` / `CB2_ChooseMonForMoveRelearner` — party select for Move Relearner, sets relearnable move count
- [ ] `DoBattlePyramidMonsHaveHeldItem` — checks if any Pyramid party mon has a held item
- [ ] `BattlePyramidChooseMonHeldItems` — party menu for managing held items when Pyramid bag is full
- [ ] `ClearSelectedPartyOrder` / `HasPartySlotAlreadyBeenSelected` / `GetPartySlotEntryStatus` — half-party selection helpers
- [ ] `BufferMonSelection` / `CB2_FadeFromPartyMenu` / `Task_PartyMenuWaitForFade` — fade-out/in helpers used by several party-menu entry points

#### Notes
- The TS port has a React-based party menu (`PartyMenuContent.tsx`) rather than a task/callback chain, so the entire `gTasks`-based state machine (40+ task functions) is replaced by React state and hooks. Core gameplay logic (move learning, PP items, Rare Candy, evolution stone, Sacred Ash) is mostly present in `src/pokemon/fieldItemEffects.ts` and `src/pokemon/moveLearning.ts`.
- **Intentionally omitted**: all link cable / Union Room / multiplayer paths (`SwitchPartyOrderLinkMulti`, `ChooseMonForTradingBoard`, `ChooseMonForWirelessMinigame`), mail / EasyChat system, Contest system, Battle Frontier half-party selection, multi-battle partner showcase, Day Care party selection.
- Battle party order (nibble-packing, `gBattlePartyCurrentOrder`) has no TS equivalent yet — this will be needed once in-battle switching is implemented.
- The level-up stat comparison window (two pages: before/after all six stats) is not implemented in the TS UI; only the current stats are shown.
- `IsLastMonThatKnowsSurf` is partially blocked by the missing PC storage (`AnyStorageMonWithMove`) query.

## batch: src/play_time.c

### `src/play_time.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `PlayTimeCounter_Reset` — zeroes out play time fields in save block 2 and sets state to STOPPED
- [ ] `PlayTimeCounter_Start` — sets state to RUNNING; clamps to max if hours > 999
- [ ] `PlayTimeCounter_Stop` — sets state to STOPPED
- [ ] `PlayTimeCounter_Update` — called each VBlank; increments seconds/minutes/hours with rollover, caps at max
- [ ] `PlayTimeCounter_SetToMax` — sets MAXED_OUT state and pins time to 999:59:59:59

#### Notes
Play time tracking is not implemented in the TS port. The save system (`src/save/`) handles the save block structure but the play-time fields (`playTimeHours`, `playTimeMinutes`, `playTimeSeconds`, `playTimeVBlanks`) are not persisted or updated anywhere. This is a low-priority feature (cosmetic display in the start menu) but would be needed for a complete save-file parity. The VBlank-driven tick model would map to the game loop update cycle in `src/engine/GameLoop.ts` or `src/hooks/useGameLoop.ts`.

- [-] `ItemStorage_Withdraw` / `ItemStorage_Deposit` / `ItemStorage_Toss` — bag deposit UI exists (`BagMenu.tsx`), but PC ↔ bag transfer (withdraw from PC, deposit to PC, toss from PC) is not implemented as a separate PC item storage screen

- [ ] `PlayerPC_Decoration` / `DoPlayerRoomDecorationMenu` — Secret Base / bedroom decoration menu; not ported

- [ ] `ItemStorage_DrawItemIcon` / `ItemStorage_EraseItemIcon` — item sprite display in PC list
- [ ] `ItemStorage_PrintDescription` / `ItemStorage_PrintMessage` / `ItemStorage_GetMessage` — contextual message printing
- [ ] `ItemStorage_AddScrollIndicator` / `ItemStorage_RemoveScrollIndicator` — scroll arrow pair management
- [ ] `ItemStorage_DrawSwapArrow` / `ItemStorage_UpdateSwapLinePos` — visual swap indicator sprites
- [ ] `ItemStorage_CompactList` / `ItemStorage_CompactCursor` — compacts PC item slots after removal
- [ ] `ItemStorage_RefreshListMenu` / `CopyItemName_PlayerPC` — rebuilds list menu data after changes
- [ ] `GetMailboxMailCount` / `Mailbox_CompactMailList` — mail slot management helpers

#### Notes
- The TS port has a bag/inventory UI (`BagMenu.tsx`, `BagManager.ts`) but it only covers the field bag menu, not the Player PC's separate item storage screen (PC items vs bag items are a distinct storage system in Gen 3).
- The mail system (`gSaveBlock1Ptr->mail[]`) has no TS equivalent at all — mail reading, giving, moving to bag are entirely absent.
- Decoration/Secret Base is intentionally out of scope for the current port phase.
- `PlayerPC_TurnOff` triggers house-specific exit scripts; the script runner exists but this specific dispatch is not wired.
- The swap-line sprite system (SELECT to reorder items) is a GBA-specific UI affordance with no TS counterpart.

- [-] `CreatePokeballSpriteToReleaseMon` / `SpriteCB_PokeballReleaseMon` / `SpriteCB_ReleasedMonFlyOut` — Pokéball release for Birch intro and trades; `BirchSpeechState.ts` uses a simplified version without full sprite arc/shrink/emerge sequence
- [-] `SpriteCB_PlayerMonSendOut_1` / `SpriteCB_PlayerMonSendOut_2` — player mon send-out arc with speed modulation; partially approximated in battle intro animation logic but arc math not fully ported

- [ ] `SpriteCB_BallThrow` / `SpriteCB_BallThrow_ReachMon` through `SpriteCB_BallThrow_FallToGround` — full wild ball-throw arc, bounce, and landing sequence
- [ ] `SpriteCB_BallThrow_StartShakes` / `SpriteCB_BallThrow_Shake` — ball shake animation (1–3 shakes based on capture success)
- [ ] `SpriteCB_BallThrow_StartCaptureMon` / `SpriteCB_BallThrow_CaptureMon` — capture success animation with music stop and evolution jingle
- [ ] `SpriteCB_ReleaseMonFromBall` — releases mon from ball with fade, cry scheduling, and emerge affine anim
- [ ] `SpriteCB_ReleaseMon2FromBall` — delays second mon release in double battle intro
- [ ] `SpriteCB_OpponentMonSendOut` — opponent mon sendout with 15-frame delay
- [ ] `Task_PlayCryWhenReleasedFromBall` — cry timing task for single/doubles intro (waits for affine anim end before playing cry)
- [ ] `HandleBallAnimEnd` — cleans up ball sprite and signals `ballAnimActive = FALSE` when both anim and affine anim finish
- [ ] `CreateTradePokeballSprite` / `SpriteCB_TradePokeball` / `SpriteCB_TradePokeballSendOff` / `SpriteCB_TradePokeballEnd` — trade pokeball animation (shrink mon, close ball, end)
- [ ] `GetBattlerPokeballItemId` — returns the pokeball item held by a battler's party member
- [ ] `AnimateBallOpenParticlesForPokeball` / `LaunchBallFadeMonTaskForPokeball` — pokeball-specific wrappers for particle and fade tasks

#### Notes
- The GBA sprite callback system (OAM, affine transforms, arc translation via `InitAnimArcTranslation`/`TranslateAnimHorizontalArc`) has no direct browser equivalent. The TS port uses CSS/WebGL transforms instead.
- Ball throw at wild Pokémon (`SpriteCB_BallThrow` chain) is entirely unimplemented — wild capture battle flow is not yet complete.
- Cry scheduling (`Task_PlayCryWhenReleasedFromBall`) ties into the audio system which is not yet ported.
- Trade animations (`SpriteCB_TradePokeball*`) are intentionally deferred — link cable trading is out of scope.
- Double-battle intro sequencing (second mon delay, doubles cry modes) is not implemented.
- `sBallAnimSeq3`–`sBallAnimSeq6` are noted as unused in C source; safely omitted.

#### Notes
- Pokeblock Case UI is entirely unimplemented; it is a prerequisite for Contests, Contest Ladies, and Safari Zone feeders — all post-game/side content not yet prioritized.
- The GBA-specific rendering (BG tilemaps, OAM, list menu tasks, VBlank callbacks) has no direct TS equivalent and would need to be reimplemented as a React/Canvas menu similar to `BagMenu.tsx`.
- `PokeblockAction_UseInBattle` is specific to Safari Zone mechanics (enemy party slot 0 is the wild mon), not standard trainer battles.
- Link Cable / Record Corner integration (implied by `MenuHelpers_ShouldWaitForLinkRecv` / `MenuHelpers_IsLinkActive` guards) is intentionally omitted.
- `gPokeblockFlavorCompatibilityTable` is the canonical source for Contest condition gain — needed when feeding pokeblocks to pokemon.

- [ ] `sNatureToMonPokeblockAnim` — 25-entry table mapping nature → (animOffset, affineType)

#### Notes
- Entirely unimplemented: no Pokéblock-feeding UI exists in the TypeScript port at all.
- The Pokéblock contest-stat system (`PokeblockGetGain`, `gSaveBlock1Ptr->pokeblocks`) has no TS equivalent — would require contest-stat tracking and Pokéblock inventory support.
- GBA-specific concerns (OAM matrix allocation, `EWRAM_DATA`, `AllocateMonSpritesGfx`, VBlank callbacks) would be replaced by canvas/WebGL sprite management, but the gameplay logic itself (nature-keyed animation tables, throw sequence, jump arc, message selection) is fully absent.
- `horizontalThrow` is always `TRUE` in the original; the vertical throw path is dead code.

- [ ] `GetPokemonSpriteToDisplay` — returns dex number to render (0 = silhouette, 0xFFFF = nothing)
- [ ] `CreatePokedexMonSprite` — allocates a mon pic sprite in the first free slot
- [ ] `CreateInterfaceSprites` — creates all overlay sprites (arrows, scrollbar, pokeball, seen/own counters)
- [ ] `SpriteCB_MoveMonForInfoScreen` — animates sprite sliding from list position to info-screen position
- [ ] `SpriteCB_PokedexListMonSprite` — sine-wave y2 scaling and off-screen culling for list sprites
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Scrollbar` — positions scrollbar relative to selected pokemon
- [ ] `SpriteCB_ScrollArrow` — shows/hides up/down arrows, applies sine bob
- [ ] `SpriteCB_DexListInterfaceText` — destroys interface text sprites when leaving list page
- [ ] `SpriteCB_RotatingPokeBall` — rotates pokeball sprite via OAM affine matrices
- [ ] `SpriteCB_SeenOwnInfo` — destroys seen/own counter sprites when leaving main page
- [ ] `SpriteCB_DexListStartMenuCursor` — positions start-menu cursor with sine bob
- [ ] `LoadInfoScreen` — allocates task + BG/window setup for info screen
- [ ] `IsInfoScreenScrolling` — returns whether info screen is mid-scroll transition
- [ ] `StartInfoScreenScroll` — triggers scroll to next/prev mon on info screen
- [ ] `Task_LoadInfoScreen` — 11-state loader: BG gfx, mon sprite, cry, palette fade
- [ ] `Task_HandleInfoScreenInput` — A/B/L/R/dpad input on info screen; routes to area/cry/size/exit
- [ ] `Task_SwitchScreensFromInfoScreen` — waits for fade then dispatches to sub-screen
- [ ] `Task_LoadInfoScreenWaitForFade` — waits for fade then reloads info screen (scroll transition)
- [ ] `Task_ExitInfoScreen` — frees info screen resources and destroys task
- [ ] `Task_LoadAreaScreen` — loads the area map screen
- [ ] `Task_WaitForAreaScreenInput` — polls `screenSwitchState` set by area screen module
- [ ] `Task_SwitchScreensFromAreaScreen` — routes from area screen to info/cry
- [ ] `Task_LoadCryScreen` — loads cry waveform+VU-meter screen
- [ ] `Task_HandleCryScreenInput` — A plays cry, B/L/R navigate between sub-screens
- [ ] `Task_SwitchScreensFromCryScreen` — cleans up cry screen and routes to next
- [ ] `LoadPlayArrowPalette` — sets play-arrow color depending on whether cry is playing
- [ ] `Task_LoadSizeScreen` — loads trainer vs. pokemon size comparison screen with affine scaling
- [ ] `Task_HandleSizeScreenInput` — B/L navigate from size screen
- [ ] `Task_SwitchScreensFromSizeScreen` — frees sprites and routes to info/cry
- [ ] `LoadScreenSelectBarMain` / `LoadScreenSelectBarSubmenu` — copies tilemap for tab bar
- [ ] `HighlightScreenSelectBarItem` / `HighlightSubmenuScreenSelectBarItem` — palette-swaps selected tab
- [ ] `DisplayCaughtMonDexPage` — shows new-entry info screen immediately after catching a pokemon
- [ ] `Task_DisplayCaughtMonDexPage` — 7-state loader for caught-mon dex page
- [ ] `Task_HandleCaughtMonPageInput` — A/B dismisses; timer-based palette flicker
- [ ] `Task_ExitCaughtMonPage` — restores mon sprite palette and frees buffers
- [ ] `SpriteCB_SlideCaughtMonToCenter` — slides caught mon sprite to screen center on exit
- [ ] `PrintMonInfo` — prints species name, number, category, height, weight, description on info screen
- [ ] `PrintMonHeight` — converts height (dm) to feet/inches string
- [ ] `PrintMonWeight` — converts weight (0.1 kg) to lbs string
- [ ] `GetPokedexHeightWeight` — public accessor for dex entry height/weight
- [ ] `GetSetPokedexFlag` — get/set seen/caught flags with triple-redundancy anti-corruption check
- [ ] `GetNationalPokedexCount` — counts seen or caught across all 386 species
- [ ] `GetHoennPokedexCount` — counts seen or caught across 202 Hoenn dex entries
- [ ] `GetKantoPokedexCount` — counts seen or caught across Kanto dex entries
- [ ] `HasAllHoennMons` — checks all Hoenn mons caught (excl. Jirachi/Deoxys) for diploma
- [ ] `HasAllKantoMons` — checks all Kanto mons caught (excl. Mew)
- [ ] `HasAllMons` — checks all catchable mons across all regions
- [ ] `CreateMonSpriteFromNationalDexNumber` — public helper to create a dex mon sprite with correct personality
- [ ] `DoPokedexSearch` — filters `pokedexList` by name letter, body color, and type(s)
- [ ] `LoadSearchMenu` / `Task_LoadSearchMenu` — 5-state loader for search menu BG+window
- [ ] `Task_SwitchToSearchMenuTopBar` / `Task_HandleSearchTopBarInput` — Search/Shift/Cancel top bar navigation
- [ ] `Task_SwitchToSearchMenu` / `Task_HandleSearchMenuInput` — main search parameter grid navigation
- [ ] `Task_StartPokedexSearch` / `Task_WaitAndCompleteSearch` / `Task_SearchCompleteWaitForInput` — executes search and waits for player to confirm
- [ ] `Task_SelectSearchMenuItem` / `Task_HandleSearchParameterInput` — scrollable parameter option list
- [ ] `Task_ExitSearch` / `Task_ExitSearchWaitForFade` — fades and destroys search menu task
- [ ] `DrawFootprint` — converts 1BPP footprint graphic to 4BPP and blits to info window
- [ ] `GetNextPosition` — bounded increment/decrement used for list navigation

#### Notes
- The entire Pokedex UI is unimplemented in the TypeScript port. No React/TS screen exists for any of these pages (list, info, area map, cry, size comparison, search, or caught-mon entry).
- `GetSetPokedexFlag`, `GetNationalPokedexCount`, `GetHoennPokedexCount`, `HasAllHoennMons`, `HasAllMons` are the most gameplay-relevant missing pieces — they are called from scripts, diploma checks, and post-game triggers. The rest is pure UI.
- `DisplayCaughtMonDexPage` / `Task_HandleCaughtMonPageInput` is called from the catch sequence and is currently absent; the port has no new-entry screen.
- GBA hardware concerns (BG tilemaps, OAM affine matrices, DMA, scanline effects, VRAM layout) are intentionally irrelevant to the browser port, but all the logical state they drive (seen/caught flags, search filters, sort orders, navigation state) must still be ported.
- Area screen (`ShowPokedexAreaScreen`) and cry screen (`LoadCryWaveformWindow`, `LoadCryMeter`) are separate C modules (`pokedex_area_screen.c`, `pokedex_cry_screen.c`) also completely absent from the TS project.
- Link cable / Union Room features referenced elsewhere in the save block are out of scope for this port.

- [ ] `HighlightSelectedSearchTopBarItem` — Highlights the active top-bar item and prints its description
- [ ] `HighlightSelectedSearchMenuItem` — Highlights the active search parameter item and prints its description
- [ ] `PrintSelectedSearchParameters` — Prints currently selected values for all search parameters in their display boxes
- [ ] `DrawOrEraseSearchParameterBox` — Draws or erases the right-column parameter selection box tiles

- [ ] `SearchParamCantScrollDown` — Returns whether the parameter scroll box cannot scroll further down

- [ ] `EraseSelectorArrow` — Clears the selector arrow glyph at a given row
- [ ] `PrintSelectorArrow` — Prints the selector arrow glyph at a given row
- [ ] `PrintSearchParameterTitle` — Prints a parameter option title at a given row in the scroll box
- [ ] `ClearSearchParameterBoxText` — Clears the parameter title scroll box text area

#### Notes
The Pokedex feature is entirely absent from the TypeScript port. This file covers the search sub-screen of the Pokedex (filter by name/color/type/order/mode, execute search, view results). No portion of the Pokedex UI, search logic, or list rendering has been ported. The Pokedex is a significant post-game/mid-game feature that is not on the critical path for the new-game-to-first-battle scope described in the project backlog, making this an intentional deferral rather than an oversight.

#### Missing

- [ ] `TryShowPokedexAreaMap` — shows the region map BG once decompression buffers are free (async completion check)

The Pokédex area map overlay (showing which routes a Pokémon appears on) is entirely absent from the TS port. The port has no Pokédex UI at all — this is an expected omission as the Pokédex screen and its sub-screens (area map, size comparison, cry player) have not been implemented. The GBA-specific BG hardware calls (`SetBgAttribute`, `DecompressAndCopyTileDataToVram`, `ChangeBgX/Y`, `CpuCopy32` to palette buffers) have no browser equivalents and would need to be replaced with canvas/WebGL rendering. The affine mode branch is dead code even in the original (comment confirms only mode 0 is ever used).

## batch: src/pokedex_area_screen.c

### `src/pokedex_area_screen.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
_(none)_

#### Partially Implemented
_(none)_

#### Missing
- [ ] `ShowPokedexAreaScreen` — entry point; allocates state, creates task to build and show area screen overlay
- [ ] `Task_ShowPokedexAreaScreen` — multi-step task: loads BGs, draws glow tilemap, creates region map, marker sprites, fades in
- [ ] `Task_HandlePokedexAreaScreenInput` — handles B/right-arrow input to exit or page, fades out, tears down
- [ ] `FindMapsWithMon` — scans wild encounter headers + roamer + special cases (Feebas, Altering Cave) to build overworld and special-area lists
- [ ] `SetAreaHasMon` — appends a towns/routes map entry with its MAPSEC id to the overworld highlight list
- [ ] `SetSpecialMapHasMon` — appends a dungeon/special-area MAPSEC to the marker list, skipping moving caves and undiscovered landmarks
- [ ] `MapHasSpecies` — checks land/water/fishing/rock-smash slots of one wild-header for a species (includes Altering Cave counter logic)
- [ ] `MonListHasSpecies` — iterates a WildPokemonInfo slot array looking for a species
- [ ] `BuildAreaGlowTilemap` — builds 32×20 tilemap: full-glow cells, edge/corner flags, palette index, using region-map layout
- [ ] `DrawAreaGlow` — state machine: calls FindMapsWithMon → BuildAreaGlowTilemap → decompress/upload tiles → load tilemap
- [ ] `ResetDrawAreaGlowState` — resets the draw-glow state counter to 0
- [ ] `StartAreaGlow` — initialises blend registers and decides whether to show glow or markers first
- [ ] `DoAreaGlow` — per-frame update: sine-wave BLDALPHA blend for area glow, or flash-counter visibility toggle for marker sprites
- [ ] `CreateAreaMarkerSprites` — loads marker sprite sheet/palette, positions circular sprites over each special-area MAPSEC centroid
- [ ] `LoadAreaUnknownGraphics` — decompresses and loads the "Area Unknown" sprite sheet
- [ ] `CreateAreaUnknownSprites` — creates three "Area Unknown" sprites when species has no known location
- [ ] `DestroyAreaScreenSprites` — frees sprite tiles/palettes and destroys all marker and unknown sprites
- [ ] `ResetPokedexAreaMapBg` — resets BG3 char-base and palette-mode attributes on exit
- [ ] `GetRegionMapSectionId` — thin wrapper: map group+num → regionMapSectionId from header

#### Notes
- The entire Pokédex area-screen feature (region-map overlay showing where a species can be encountered) has no TypeScript equivalent. The project has no Pokédex UI at all yet.
- Depends on region-map layout data (`GetRegionMapSecIdAt`, `gRegionMapEntries`), roamer state, and wild-encounter tables — the wild-encounter data is generated (`src/data/wildEncounters.gen.ts`) and roamer scaffolding exists in `src/scripting/specials/`, but the area-screen rendering pipeline, MAPSEC lookup, and landmark-flag gating are all absent.
- GBA-specific rendering (BG tilemap upload, `BLDCNT`/`BLDALPHA` hardware registers, `DecompressAndCopyTileDataToVram`) is intentionally omitted; equivalent browser rendering would use WebGL/Canvas overlays on top of the existing region-map asset.
- The fishing-encounter bug (`LAND_WILD_COUNT` used instead of `FISH_WILD_COUNT`) is documented in the C source with a `BUGFIX` guard — worth noting if/when this is ported.

## batch: src/pokedex_cry_screen.c

### `src/pokedex_cry_screen.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `LoadCryWaveformWindow` — initializes the Pokédex cry waveform display window (3-state machine: alloc, pre-draw flatline, copy palette)
- [ ] `UpdateCryWaveformWindow` — per-frame update: advance playhead, buffer waveform PCM samples, draw waveform segments or flatline
- [ ] `CryScreenPlayButton` — handles player pressing play; debounces repeats, stops/restarts crying, triggers countdown
- [ ] `PlayCryScreenCry` — calls `PlayCry_NormalNoDucking` and sets `cryState = 1`
- [ ] `BufferCryWaveformSegment` — samples 16 bytes from the PCM DMA ring buffer to fill `cryWaveformBuffer`
- [ ] `DrawWaveformFlatline` — draws two zero-amplitude segments at current playhead position
- [ ] `AdvancePlayhead` — scrolls the waveform BG horizontally via `ChangeBgX`, advances playhead by 2, clears leading tile
- [ ] `DrawWaveformSegment` — writes 4bpp tile pixel data for a single waveform column with gradient color; interpolates vertically between previous and current sample
- [ ] `DrawWaveformWindow` — calls `CopyWindowToVram` to flush the pixel buffer to VRAM
- [ ] `ShiftWaveformOver` — sets BG horizontal scroll offset (no-op when `rsVertical = TRUE`; RS leftover)
- [ ] `LoadCryMeter` — allocates needle struct, decompresses meter BG graphics, loads palette
- [ ] `FreeCryScreen` — frees all allocated structs and destroys the needle sprite
- [ ] `SpriteCB_CryMeterNeedle` — per-frame sprite callback: reads peak amplitude from waveform buffer (cases 2 & 6), rotates needle toward target using ObjAffineSet, offsets sprite position along sine curve
- [ ] `SetCryMeterNeedleTarget` — clamps and sets target rotation for the cry meter needle sprite

#### Notes
This file implements the Pokédex cry screen UI: an animated waveform display and an affine-transformed needle meter that reacts to cry amplitude in real time. The browser port has no Pokédex UI at all (`src/data/species.ts` exists for species data only). The PCM buffer sampling (`BufferCryWaveformSegment`) is deeply tied to the GBA's m4a sound engine DMA ring buffer (`gSoundInfo.pcmBuffer`, `gPcmDmaCounter`) — there is no direct browser equivalent since the port presumably uses the Web Audio API or pre-baked cry audio files rather than real-time PCM sampling. The waveform drawing logic writes directly into GBA tile memory via 4bpp nybble manipulation using `sWaveformOffsets` lookup tables — this would need a Canvas 2D or WebGL replacement. The entire Pokédex feature (cry screen, species entries, etc.) appears to be out of scope for the current port and is an intentional omission.

## batch: src/pokemon.c (part 1/2)

### `src/pokemon.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/pokemon/stats.ts`, `src/pokemon/types.ts`, `src/pokemon/moveLearning.ts`, `src/pokemon/evolution.ts`, `src/pokemon/pp.ts`, `src/data/natures.ts`, `src/save/native/Gen3Pokemon.ts`, `src/battle/engine/DamageCalculator.ts`
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-species-info.cjs`, `scripts/generate-learnsets.cjs`, `scripts/generate-evolutions.cjs`, `scripts/generate-moves.cjs`

#### Implemented
- [x] `CalculateMonStats` — stat formula (HP + non-HP) with nature modifier, Shedinja special case → `src/pokemon/stats.ts`
- [x] `CalculateBaseDamage` — full damage formula including physical/special split (type-based), badge boosts, hold effects, abilities (Huge Power, Hustle, Guts, etc.), weather modifiers, crit stat stage ignoring, Reflect/Light Screen, Explosion defense halving → `src/battle/engine/DamageCalculator.ts`
- [x] `GetGenderFromSpeciesAndPersonality` / `GetBoxMonGender` / `GetMonGender` — gender ratio logic → `src/pokemon/types.ts`
- [x] `GetNatureFromPersonality` (via nature table) → `src/data/natures.ts`
- [x] `gNatureStatTable` — nature stat modifiers → `src/data/natures.ts`
- [x] `GiveBoxMonInitialMoveset` / `GiveMonInitialMoveset` — level-up learnset assignment → `src/pokemon/moveLearning.ts`
- [x] `MonTryLearningNewMove` — new move at level-up → `src/pokemon/moveLearning.ts`
- [x] `DeleteFirstMoveAndGiveMoveToMon` / `DeleteFirstMoveAndGiveMoveToBoxMon` — move slot shift → `src/pokemon/moveLearning.ts`
- [x] `GetLevelFromMonExp` / `GetLevelFromBoxMonExp` — experience table lookup → `src/pokemon/stats.ts`
- [x] `gPPUpGetMask` / `gPPUpClearMask` / `gPPUpAddValues` — PP bonus bit manipulation → `src/pokemon/pp.ts`
- [x] `gStatStageRatios` — stat stage multiplier table → `src/battle/engine/DamageCalculator.ts`
- [x] `GetBoxMonData3` / `SetBoxMonData` / `GetMonData3` / `SetMonData` — Pokemon data accessor system → `src/save/native/Gen3Pokemon.ts`
- [x] `EncryptBoxMon` / `DecryptBoxMon` / `CalculateBoxMonChecksum` / `GetSubstruct` — Gen3 encryption/substruct system → `src/save/native/Gen3Pokemon.ts`
- [x] `ZeroBoxMonData` / `ZeroMonData` / `ZeroPlayerPartyMons` / `ZeroEnemyPartyMons` — party initialization → `src/pokemon/types.ts`
- [x] `BoxMonToMon` — BoxPokemon → Pokemon expansion → `src/save/native/Gen3Pokemon.ts`
- [x] `sSpeciesToHoennPokedexNum` / `sSpeciesToNationalPokedexNum` / `sHoennToNationalOrder` — dex number tables (data only, not yet surfaced in TS but species data generated)
- [x] `sFriendshipEventModifiers` — friendship change table (partially, used in evolution threshold check) → `src/pokemon/evolution.ts`
- [x] `sHMMoves` — HM move list → `src/data/moves.ts` (move flags)

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `CreateMon` / `CreateBoxMon` — Pokemon creation with personality/OT/IV generation; basic version exists for test factory but full OT_ID_RANDOM_NO_SHINY anti-shiny loop not exposed as a runtime API → `src/pokemon/testFactory.ts`
- [-] `CountAliveMonsInBattle` — alive battler counting; implemented for damage formula context but not as standalone utility → `src/battle/engine/DamageCalculator.ts`
- [-] `ShouldGetStatBadgeBoost` — badge boost logic present in damage calc but badge flag checks are stub/always-false in non-trainer battles → `src/battle/engine/DamageCalculator.ts`
- [-] `SetDeoxysStats` — Deoxys form stat override referenced but Deoxys form system not implemented → not present as callable
- [-] `sMonFrontAnimIdsTable` / `sMonAnimationDelayTable` — battle animation IDs per species exist in `src/data/battleAnimations.gen.ts` but per-species front anim mapping table not ported

#### Missing
- [ ] `CreateMonWithNature` — forced nature personality loop
- [ ] `CreateMonWithGenderNatureLetter` — Unown letter + gender + nature personality generation
- [ ] `CreateMaleMon` — forced male personality (used for Wally's Ralts)
- [ ] `CreateMonWithIVsPersonality` — preset IVs + personality creation
- [ ] `CreateMonWithIVsOTID` — preset IVs + OTID creation
- [ ] `CreateMonWithEVSpread` — EV spread creation (used by in-game trainers)
- [ ] `CreateMonWithEVSpreadNatureOTID` — combined EV/nature/OTID creation
- [ ] `CreateBattleTowerMon` / `CreateBattleTowerMon_HandleLevel` — Battle Frontier mon creation from `BattleTowerPokemon` struct
- [ ] `CreateApprenticeMon` — Apprentice system mon creation
- [ ] `ConvertPokemonToBattleTowerPokemon` — mon → BattleTower struct serialization
- [ ] `CreateEnemyEventMon` / `CreateEventMon` — event legendary creation with fateful encounter flag
- [ ] `GiveMoveToMon` / `GiveMoveToBoxMon` / `GiveMoveToBattleMon` — runtime move giving (add to empty slot)
- [ ] `SetMonMoveSlot` / `SetBattleMonMoveSlot` — direct slot assignment
- [ ] `GetDefaultMoveTarget` — double battle default targeting
- [ ] `ShouldIgnoreDeoxysForm` — Deoxys form handling in multi/link battles
- [ ] `GetDeoxysStat` — Deoxys alternate-form stat calculation
- [ ] `GetUnionRoomTrainerPic` / `GetUnionRoomTrainerClass` — Union Room link battle trainer display
- [ ] `SetMultiuseSpriteTemplateToPokemon` / `SetMultiuseSpriteTemplateToTrainerBack` / `SetMultiuseSpriteTemplateToTrainerFront` — GBA sprite template management (browser uses different sprite system)
- [ ] `MonRestorePP` — restore PP to max (used in Battle Tower mon creation)
- [ ] `sAlteringCaveWildMonHeldItems` — Altering Cave species-specific held items
- [ ] `gUnionRoomFacilityClasses` — Union Room trainer class table
- [ ] `sSecretBaseFacilityClasses` — Secret Base trainer class table
- [ ] `gSpindaSpotGraphics` — Spinda spot pixel data (rendering)
- [ ] `gBattlerSpriteTemplates` / `sTrainerBackSpriteTemplates` — GBA OAM sprite templates (N/A for browser)

#### Notes
- The Gen3 encrypted substruct system (`GetSubstruct`, `EncryptBoxMon`, personality-based ordering) is fully ported in `Gen3SaveParser.ts` for native save compatibility, but the runtime Pokemon objects in the TS port use a flat JS object model rather than the encrypted binary format.
- `CalculateBaseDamage` is the most critical function and has high parity coverage with tests in `BattleParity.test.ts`. The `IS_TYPE_PHYSICAL` type-based split is correctly implemented.
- Battle Tower / Apprentice / Union Room / Secret Base functions are intentionally deferred (post-game/link features).
- `SetDeoxysStats` / Deoxys form changes are not implemented — Deoxys appears in Hoenn dex but form-changing is missing.
- `sMonFrontAnimIdsTable` is needed for proper battle entry animations but not yet wired up to the battle renderer.
- `gStatStageRatios` exact table values are critical for damage calc parity and are reproduced in the TS damage calculator.

## batch: src/pokemon.c (part 2/2)

### `src/pokemon.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/pokemon/stats.ts`, `src/pokemon/evolution.ts`, `src/pokemon/moveLearning.ts`, `src/pokemon/pp.ts`, `src/pokemon/fieldItemEffects.ts`, `src/data/speciesInfo.ts`, `src/battle/engine/BattleEngine.ts`
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-species-info.cjs` (species data), `scripts/generate-evolutions.cjs` (evolution table), `scripts/generate-learnsets.cjs` (learnsets)

#### Implemented
- [x] `GetNature` / `GetNatureFromPersonality` — `personality % NUM_NATURES`, present in `src/pokemon/stats.ts`
- [x] `ModifyStatByNature` — nature stat modifier (×110/90/100), present in `src/pokemon/stats.ts`
- [x] `CalculatePPWithBonus` — PP + PP-up bonus calculation, present in `src/pokemon/pp.ts`
- [x] `GetEvolutionTargetSpecies` (partial modes) — item-use and level-up evolution lookup, present in `src/pokemon/evolution.ts`
- [x] `GetMoveRelearnerMoves` / `GetNumberOfRelearnableMoves` — relearnable move logic, present in `src/pokemon/moveLearning.ts`
