- [x] `TryIncrementMonLevel` — level-up via EXP threshold check, present in `src/pokemon/stats.ts`
- [x] `IsMonShiny` / `IsShinyOtIdPersonality` — shiny value calc `GET_SHINY_VALUE`, present in `src/pokemon/stats.ts` or `src/pokemon/types.ts`
- [x] `GetMonAbility` / `GetAbilityBySpecies` — ability lookup from species + ability slot, present in `src/data/speciesInfo.ts` / battle engine
- [x] `CopyPlayerPartyMonToBattleData` — party→battle struct copy, present in `src/battle/engine/BattleEngine.ts` (battle setup)
- [x] `HealStatusConditions` — status-bit clearing, present in `src/battle/engine/StatusEffects.ts`
- [x] `MonRestorePP` / `BoxMonRestorePP` — restore PP to bonus-adjusted max, present in `src/pokemon/pp.ts`
- [x] `GetMonEVCount` — sum of all EVs, present in `src/pokemon/stats.ts`
- [x] `CalculatePlayerPartyCount` / `CalculateEnemyPartyCount` — count non-NONE party slots, present in battle engine / party context
- [x] `IsTradedMon` / `IsOtherTrainer` — OT ID + name mismatch check, present in `src/pokemon/types.ts` or save layer
- [x] `AdjustFriendship` — walking/battle/level-up friendship deltas with Soothe Bell/Luxury Ball, present in `src/pokemon/fieldItemEffects.ts`
- [x] `PokemonUseItemEffects` (field HP/PP/status/EV/friendship cases) — item effect dispatch for out-of-battle use, present in `src/pokemon/fieldItemEffects.ts`
- [x] `EvolutionRenameMon` — rename on evolution if nickname == species name, present in `src/pokemon/evolution.ts`
- [x] `GetBattleBGM` — BGM selection by battle type/trainer class, present in `src/battle/engine/BattleEngine.ts` (stub/partial)

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `GiveMonToPlayer` — party add present; PC-box overflow path (`CopyMonToPC`) not implemented (no box storage system)
- [-] `GetEvolutionTargetSpecies` — `EVO_MODE_NORMAL` friendship/day/night/beauty/Wurmple/Shedinja branches; day/night require RTC; trade-evolution modes absent (no link cable)
- [-] `MonGainEVs` — EV yield logic present but Pokerus multiplier and Macho Brace hold-effect path not fully verified
- [-] `PokemonUseItemEffects` — in-battle stat-stage X-item cases (ITEM0–ITEM3 battle stat raises, Guard Spec mist timer, Dire Hit focus energy) not hooked to battle engine item use; Sacred Ash, Revive in-battle battler resurrection incomplete
- [-] `SetWildMonHeldItem` — common/rare held item assignment on encounter present in `src/game/encounters/wildEncounterService.ts`; Altering Cave special table and Compound Eyes probability adjustment not implemented

#### Missing
- [ ] `CopyMonToPC` — box storage: find empty slot across all boxes, store BoxPokemon
- [ ] `GetMonsStateToDoubles` / `GetMonsStateToDoubles_2` — count alive non-egg party members for double-battle eligibility check
- [ ] `CreateSecretBaseEnemyParty` — build enemy party from SecretBase record (Secret Base battles not implemented)
- [ ] `GetSecretBaseTrainerPicIndex` / `GetSecretBaseTrainerClass` — facility-class lookup for secret base opponent
- [ ] `IsPlayerPartyAndPokemonStorageFull` / `IsPokemonStorageFull` — check all 14 boxes for empty slots
- [ ] `GetItemEffectParamOffset` — compute byte offset into itemEffect array for a given flag bit (used by battle AI)
- [ ] `UseStatIncreaseItem` — format battle text for X-item stat rise messages
- [ ] `RandomlyGivePartyPokerus` — 1-in-21845 chance to infect random party member
- [ ] `CheckPartyPokerus` / `CheckPartyHasHadPokerus` — bitmask check for active/past Pokerus
- [ ] `UpdatePartyPokerusTime` / `PartySpreadPokerus` — daily Pokerus countdown and adjacent spread
- [ ] `CanMonLearnTMHM` / `CanSpeciesLearnTMHM` — TM/HM learnset bitmask lookup
- [ ] `HoennPokedexNumToSpecies` / `NationalPokedexNumToSpecies` / `NationalToHoennOrder` / `SpeciesToNationalPokedexNum` / `SpeciesToHoennPokedexNum` / `HoennToNationalOrder` — Pokédex number conversion tables
- [ ] `SpeciesToCryId` — map species to cry table index
- [ ] `DrawSpindaSpots` — personality-seeded spot rendering on Spinda's front sprite pixels
- [ ] `GetPlayerFlankId` / `GetLinkTrainerFlankId` / `GetBattlerMultiplayerId` — link multi-battle flank assignment (link cable, not applicable)
- [ ] `GetTrainerEncounterMusicId` — encounter music selection (Battle Pyramid / Trainer Hill variants)
- [ ] `SetMonPreventsSwitchingString` — format "X's Y prevents switching" battle text
- [ ] `GetMonFrontSpritePal` / `GetMonSpritePalFromSpeciesAndPersonality` / `GetMonSpritePalStruct` / `GetMonSpritePalStructFromOtIdPersonality` — shiny/normal palette selection (GBA palette data; handled differently in WebGL renderer)
- [ ] `IsHMMove2` — check if move is an HM (for forgetting restrictions)
- [ ] `IsMonSpriteNotFlipped` — `noFlip` species flag for sprite rendering
- [ ] `GetMonFlavorRelation` / `GetFlavorRelationByPersonality` — Pokéblock flavor compat by nature
- [ ] `HandleSetPokedexFlag` — set seen/caught Pokédex flag with Unown/Spinda personality recording
- [ ] `GetTrainerClassNameFromId` / `GetTrainerNameFromId` — trainer data string accessors
- [ ] `HasTwoFramesAnimation` — species two-frame front-anim eligibility
- [ ] `FacilityClassToPicIndex` / `PlayerGenderToFrontTrainerPicId` — facility class → sprite index
- [ ] `GetBattlerMultiplayerId` — link player ID lookup
- [ ] `GetTrainerPartnerName` — in-game partner name (Steven / Frontier partner)
- [ ] `DoMonFrontSpriteAnimation` / `BattleAnimateFrontSprite` / `BattleAnimateBackSprite` / `PokemonSummaryDoMonAnimation` / `StopPokemonAnimationDelayTask` — GBA sprite animation task system (not applicable to WebGL renderer)
- [ ] `CreateMonSpritesGfxManager` / `DestroyMonSpritesGfxManager` / `MonSpritesGfxManager_GetSpritePtr` — GBA sprite GFX allocation manager (not applicable)
- [ ] `PlayBattleBGM` / `PlayMapChosenOrBattleBGM` / `CreateTask_PlayMapChosenOrBattleBGM` — audio playback (no audio system implemented)
- [ ] `SpeciesToPokedexNum` / `IsSpeciesInHoennDex` — Pokédex number resolution
- [ ] `ClearBattleMonForms` — reset battler form array

#### Notes
- Link cable functions (`GetPlayerFlankId`, multi-battle flank, `GetOpposingLinkMultiBattlerId`) are intentionally N/A — no link system in browser port.
- GBA sprite/palette/animation tasks (`DrawSpindaSpots`, `DoMonFrontSpriteAnimation`, `MonSpritesGfxManager`) have no direct equivalent; sprites are handled by WebGL renderer with separate sprite sheets.
- Audio functions (`PlayBattleBGM`, etc.) are all missing — no audio system exists yet.
- PC box storage (`CopyMonToPC`, `IsPokemonStorageFull`) is a significant missing subsystem.
- Pokerus system entirely absent.
- Pokédex flag/number conversion tables not ported.
- `PokemonUseItemEffects` field-item path (HP, PP, EVs, friendship) is largely covered by `src/pokemon/fieldItemEffects.ts`; in-battle path is incomplete.

*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `LaunchAnimationTaskForFrontSprite` — starts a front-sprite intro animation task for a battler sprite
- [ ] `LaunchAnimationTaskForBackSprite` — starts a back-sprite animation task, selecting variant by nature
- [ ] `StartMonSummaryAnimation` — triggers an animation on the Summary Screen sprite
- [ ] `SetSpriteCB_MonAnimDummy` — sets sprite callback to a no-op (used to stop animation)
- [ ] `GetSpeciesBackAnimSet` — looks up which BACK_ANIM set a species uses from `sSpeciesToBackAnimSet`
- [ ] `Task_HandleMonAnimation` — task wrapper that dispatches to `sMonAnimFunctions`, restores battler data on completion
- [ ] `sSpeciesToBackAnimSet[]` — per-species back-animation set assignment table (all 386 species)
- [ ] `sBackAnimationIds[]` — maps each BACK_ANIM set × nature-mod-index to a concrete ANIM constant
- [ ] `sBackAnimNatureModTable[]` — maps each nature to index 0/1/2 to select anim speed variant
- [ ] `sMonAnimFunctions[]` — dispatch table mapping ANIM_* constants to sprite callbacks (~150 entries)
- [ ] `sYellowFlashData[]` — timing table for the flash-yellow palette blend sequence
- [ ] `sVerticalShakeData[]` — timing/amplitude table for two-phase vertical shake
- [ ] `sZigzagData[]` — delta/timer table for the zigzag path animation
- [ ] `sBounceRotateToSidesData[]` — per-step x/y/time data for bounce-rotate animation (2 variants)
- [ ] `sTriangleDownData[]` — delta/timer table for the triangle-down path animation
- [ ] `sShakeYellowFlashData_Fast/Normal/Slow[]` — flash-yellow timing arrays for shake+flash variants
- [ ] All ~150 `Anim_*` sprite-callback functions — individual intro animation implementations (squish-bounce, vibrate, slide, rotate, glow, flip, spring, arc, jolt, shrink-grow, etc.)
- [ ] All shared inner helpers (`VerticalSquishBounce`, `HorizontalSlide`, `VerticalSlide`, `Zigzag`, `HorizontalShake`, `VerticalShake`, `Twist`, `Spin`, `CircleCounterclockwise`, `BounceRotateToSides`, `SwingConcave`, `SwingConvex`, `RotateToSides`, `DeepVerticalSquishBounce`, `VerticalJumps`, `ShrinkGrow`, `HorizontalSpring`, `HorizontalRepeatedSpring`, `VerticalStretchBothEnds`, `HorizontalStretchFar`, `GrowStutter`, `VerticalShakeLowTwice`, `VerticalShakeBack`, `TriangleDown`, `Grow`, `ConcaveArc`, `ConvexDoubleArc`, `ShrinkGrowVibrate`, `JoltRight`, `ShakeFlashYellow`, `TumblingFrontFlip`, `Figure8`, `VerticalShakeTwice`, `VerticalSlideWobble`, `RisingWobble`, `SetHorizontalDip`, etc.)
- [ ] Affine/palette helper group (`SetAffineData`, `HandleStartAffineAnim`, `HandleSetAffineData`, `TryFlipX`, `ResetSpriteAfterAnim`, `SetPosForRotation`, `InitAnimData`, `AddNewAnim`) — GBA OAM affine matrix manipulation
- [ ] `GlowColor` macro — palette-blend glow effect used by `Anim_GlowBlack/Orange/Red/Blue/Yellow/Purple`

#### Notes
- This entire file is GBA sprite-animation code (OAM affine transforms, palette blending via `BlendPalette`, task system). There is no TypeScript equivalent at all — the battle render layer (`src/battle/render/`) handles sprite display but does not implement per-species intro animations.
- The port's battle renderer uses CSS/WebGL sprite positioning; a future implementation would need to re-express each animation as a JS frame-based animation driver operating on a `BattlerSprite` abstraction rather than GBA `struct Sprite` fields.
- `sSpeciesToBackAnimSet` and `sBackAnimationIds` are pure data tables that could be code-generated if intro animations are ever prioritised.
- GBA-specific concerns (OAM matrix num, `gOamMatrices`, `ObjAffineSet`, `CalcCenterToCornerVec`, `FreeOamMatrix`, `gAffineAnims_BattleSpriteOpponentSide`) have no browser equivalent and would need to be replaced with CSS transform or WebGL matrix math.
- The `sIsSummaryAnim` flag distinguishes Summary Screen animations from battle animations — both paths would need handling in a TS port.

## batch: src/pokemon_animation.c (part 2/2)

### `src/pokemon_animation.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `Anim_ShakeFlashYellow_Slow` — entry-point wrapper initializing palette ID + counters then calling ShakeFlashYellow each frame
- [ ] `ShakeGlow_Blend` — per-frame palette blend toward a color using Sin(data[2], 12); resets blend to 0 and transitions to WaitAnimEnd when counter > 127
- [ ] `ShakeGlow_Move` — lateral shake via `x2 = sign * Sin((step * 384 / period) % 256, 6)` with TryFlipX bookending each frame; counts full oscillation cycles
- [ ] `Anim_ShakeGlowRed_Fast` — ShakeGlow entry-point: period=10, cycles=2, color=RED
- [ ] `Anim_ShakeGlowRed` — ShakeGlow entry-point: period=20, cycles=1, color=RED
- [ ] `Anim_ShakeGlowRed_Slow` — ShakeGlow entry-point: period=80, cycles=1, color=RED
- [ ] `Anim_ShakeGlowGreen_Fast` — ShakeGlow entry-point: period=10, cycles=2, color=GREEN
- [ ] `Anim_ShakeGlowGreen` — ShakeGlow entry-point: period=20, cycles=1, color=GREEN
- [ ] `Anim_ShakeGlowGreen_Slow` — ShakeGlow entry-point: period=80, cycles=1, color=GREEN
- [ ] `Anim_ShakeGlowBlue_Fast` — ShakeGlow entry-point: period=10, cycles=2, color=BLUE
- [ ] `Anim_ShakeGlowBlue` — ShakeGlow entry-point: period=20, cycles=1, color=BLUE
- [ ] `Anim_ShakeGlowBlue_Slow` — ShakeGlow entry-point: period=80, cycles=1, color=BLUE
- [ ] `WaitAnimEnd` — polls `sprite->animEnded`; transitions to `SpriteCallbackDummy` when the frame animation finishes

#### Notes
This file implements idle/intro sprite animations for Pokémon in battle (the "shake and glow" entry animations). The port has `src/battle/render/BattleSpriteLoader.ts` and `BattleLayout.ts` for sprite positioning but no equivalent of the GBA sprite callback / palette-blend animation system. Browser-side palette blending (`BlendPalette`) would need to be implemented as a WebGL uniform or canvas filter effect. The GBA `OBJ_PLTT_ID` / hardware palette slot concept has no direct browser equivalent. These animations are cosmetic and not required for battle correctness, but should be implemented for visual parity.

## batch: src/pokemon_icon.c

### `src/pokemon_icon.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/pokemon/icons.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `gMonIconTable` — species → icon image pointer table; TS equivalent maps species IDs to icon asset paths in `src/pokemon/icons.ts`
- [x] `gMonIconPaletteIndices` — species → palette index (0/1/2); TS equivalent encodes palette slot per species in icons.ts
- [x] `GetIconSpecies` — resolves Unown letter from personality, clamps invalid species to question mark; ported in icons.ts icon resolver
- [x] `GetUnownLetterByPersonality` — extracts Unown form index from personality bits; ported alongside GetIconSpecies
- [x] `GetMonIconPtr` — returns icon tile pointer for a species+personality; TS equivalent returns asset URL

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `UpdateMonIconFrame` — 2-frame flip animation (frame 0↔1 at configurable speed); icon animation timing is present in the party menu / summary screen but the full 5-speed `sMonIconAnims` table (6/8/14/22/29 frame intervals) is not explicitly modelled — icons animate at a fixed rate
- [-] `GetIconSpeciesNoPersonality` — handles mail species remapping via `MailSpeciesToSpecies`; species resolution without personality is ported, but the mail-species path is not (mail system not implemented)
- [-] `CreateMonIcon` / `CreateMonIconNoPersonality` — GBA sprite creation wrappers; conceptually replaced by React/WebGL icon rendering, but the affine-shrink animation (`sAffineAnim_1`) used during party-menu selection is not reproduced

#### Missing
- [ ] `LoadMonIconPalettes` / `LoadMonIconPalette` / `FreeMonIconPalettes` / `FreeMonIconPalette` — GBA palette slot management; not needed (WebGL handles palettes)
- [ ] `TryLoadAllMonIconPalettesAtOffset` — loads all 3 icon palettes into BG palette at a given offset; GBA-specific, no equivalent
- [ ] `GetValidMonIconPalIndex` / `GetMonIconPaletteIndexFromSpecies` / `GetValidMonIconPalettePtr` — palette index/pointer accessors used by contest/summary screens; not ported
- [ ] `SpriteCB_MonIcon` — GBA sprite callback that drives animation each frame; replaced by React render loop
- [ ] `GetMonIconTiles` — returns raw tile pointer with Deoxys Speed-form offset hack (`+0x400`); Deoxys form icon switching not implemented
- [ ] `SetPartyHPBarSprite` — resets animation state on the HP bar sprite used in party menu; HP bar rendering is custom in TS, this specific reset is not reproduced
- [ ] `CreateMonIconSprite` / `FreeAndDestroyMonIconSprite_` — GBA OAM sprite lifecycle; no equivalent needed in browser
- [ ] `SafeLoadMonIconPalette` / `SafeFreeMonIconPalette` — guarded palette load/free (unused in C too); not ported
- [ ] `gMonIconPaletteTable` — maps palette index to actual 4bpp colour data + tag; palette data is embedded in PNG assets rather than raw GBA palette tables

#### Notes
- The 3-palette system (indices 0/1/2) is a GBA VRAM constraint — the browser port uses full-colour PNGs so palette management is entirely absent.
- Icon animation (2-frame bob) is visually reproduced in the UI but the exact frame-counter/speed-tier logic from `sMonIconAnims` is simplified.
- Deoxys form icon selection via the `+0x400` pointer offset has no TS equivalent; all Deoxys forms currently show the same icon.
- Mail species remapping (`MailSpeciesToSpecies`) is intentionally omitted — the mail/decoration system is not implemented.
- `gMonIconTable` and `gMonIconPaletteIndices` are the only data structures with a direct TS counterpart; everything else is GBA hardware plumbing.

**Coverage**: None

#### Partially Implemented

#### Missing
- [ ] `StartPokemonJump` — entry point: allocates state, sets up multiplayer link, launches minigame
- [ ] `InitGame` / `ResetForNewGame` — initializes vine state, scores, player array, vine speed
- [ ] `InitPlayerAndJumpTypes` — maps each player's species to JUMP_TYPE_NORMAL/FAST/SLOW via `sPokeJumpMons[]`
- [ ] `Task_PokemonJump_Leader` / `Task_PokemonJump_Member` — per-frame update loops for leader and member roles
- [ ] `GameIntro_Leader` / `GameIntro_Member` — countdown/intro sequence state machines
- [ ] `WaitRound_Leader` / `WaitRound_Member` — inter-round synchronisation via link
- [ ] `GameRound_Leader` / `GameRound_Member` — main gameplay loop: vine swing + jump input + hit detection
- [ ] `GameOver_Leader` / `GameOver_Member` — vine-hit effect, branching to prize/save/play-again
- [ ] `AskPlayAgain_Leader` / `AskPlayAgain_Member` — yes/no prompt, record update, link sync
- [ ] `ResetGame_Leader` / `ResetGame_Member` — resets score display and game state for replay
- [ ] `ExitGame` — fade out, close link, return to exit callback
- [ ] `GivePrize_Leader` / `GivePrize_Member` — prize item selection, bag add, full/no-room messaging
- [ ] `SavePokeJump` — link-safe full save during minigame
- [ ] `UpdateVineState` — advances vine swing position using vineStateTimer + vineSpeed
- [ ] `GetVineSpeed` / `UpdateVineSpeed` — speed ramp using `sVineBaseSpeeds[]`, delay, stage progression
- [ ] `PokeJumpRandom` — deterministic RNG via ISO_RANDOMIZE1 seeded from leader
- [ ] `HandleSwingRound` — reads A_BUTTON input, transitions player mon state to JUMP
- [ ] `HandleMonState` — per-frame: moves sprites for NORMAL/JUMP/HIT states, plays SFX
- [ ] `UpdateJump` — animates jump arc using `sJumpOffsets[jumpType][]` table
- [ ] `TryUpdateScore` — awards 10pts per cleared vine, bonus for synchronised jumps, tracks excellents-in-a-row
- [ ] `UpdateVineHitStates` — determines if player was hit (jumpOffset==0 at VINE_UPSWING_LOWER)
- [ ] `AllPlayersJumpedOrHit` / `DidAllPlayersClearVine` / `ShouldPlayAgain` — round-end predicates
- [ ] `AddJumpScore` / `GetScoreBonus` / `TryUpdateExcellentsRecord` — score accumulation helpers
- [ ] `GetPlayersAtJumpPeak` / `GetNumPlayersForBonus` — synchronous-jump bonus detection
- [ ] `HasEnoughScoreForPrize` / `GetPrizeData` / `GetPrizeItemId` / `GetPrizeQuantity` — prize item selection from `sPrizeItems[]` / `sPrizeQuantityData[]`
- [ ] `TryUpdateRecords` / `IncrementGamesWithMaxPlayers` — updates `SaveBlock2.pokeJump` records
- [ ] `ResetPokemonJumpRecords` / `ShowPokemonJumpRecords` — public API for save reset and UI display
- [ ] `IsSpeciesAllowedInPokemonJump` / `IsPokemonJumpSpeciesInParty` — `special` command hooks checking `sPokeJumpMons[]`
- [ ] `Task_CommunicateMonInfo` — link exchange of species/OT/personality before game starts
- [ ] All link packet send/recv functions (`SendPacket_LeaderState`, `RecvPacket_LeaderState`, etc.) — RFU wireless protocol
- [ ] All GFX functions (`LoadPokeJumpGfx`, `UpdateVineSwing`, `UpdateVineAnim`, `CreateJumpMonSprites`, etc.) — BG/sprite rendering for the minigame scene
- [ ] All sprite callbacks (`SpriteCB_Star`, `SpriteCB_MonHitShake`, `SpriteCB_MonHitFlash`, `SpriteCB_MonIntroBounce`) — sprite animations
- [ ] `ShowBonus` / `Task_UpdateBonus` / `DoSameJumpTimeBonus` — bonus BG scroll animation
- [ ] `PrintScore` / `PrintJumpsInRow` / `PrintScoreSuffixes` — digit-sprite score display
- [ ] `Task_ShowPokemonJumpRecords` / `PrintRecordsText` — records screen UI
- [ ] `sPokeJumpMons[]` data table — 100-entry species→jump-type mapping (not generated)
- [ ] `sJumpOffsets[3][48]` data table — per-jump-type Y-offset curves
- [ ] `sVineBaseSpeeds[]` / `sVineSpeedDelays[]` / `sPrizeItems[]` / `sPrizeQuantityData[]` / `sScoreBonuses[]` — all gameplay constants

#### Notes
- Pokemon Jump is a GBA Wireless Adapter (RFU) multiplayer minigame; the port has no multiplayer/link subsystem at all, making this entirely out of scope for the current single-player browser port.
- The GBA-specific subsystems used here (RFU packets, GBA BG hardware, OAM DMA, VBlank callbacks, task system, DMA3) have no equivalents in the TS architecture.
- `IsPokemonJumpSpeciesInParty` is called via a `special` script command — the `special` dispatch in `ScriptRunner.ts` would need a stub returning FALSE, but no such stub exists.
- `ResetPokemonJumpRecords` is called from new-game init in C; the TS `NewGameFlow.ts` omits this record reset (no `SaveBlock2.pokeJump` equivalent in the TS save schema).
- The entire file could reasonably be deferred as a post-game multiplayer feature.

#### Missing
- [ ] `GetMonSizeHash` — derives a 16-bit size hash from a Pokémon's personality and IVs
- [ ] `TranslateBigMonSizeTableIndex` — maps the size hash into an index in `sBigMonSizeTable`
- [ ] `GetMonSize` — computes actual display size (cm) from species Pokédex height × table scalar
- [ ] `FormatMonSizeRecord` — formats size as a decimal string (supports imperial conversion)
- [ ] `CompareMonSize` — compares a party mon's computed size against the stored village record; sets `gSpecialVar_Result`
- [ ] `GetMonSizeRecordInfo` — populates `gStringVar1/2/3` with species name, record holder name, and formatted size
- [ ] `InitSeedotSizeRecord` / `InitLotadSizeRecord` — reset the VAR size record to `DEFAULT_MAX_SIZE`
- [ ] `GetSeedotSizeRecordInfo` / `GetLotadSizeRecordInfo` — public wrappers calling `GetMonSizeRecordInfo` for the two species
- [ ] `CompareSeedotSize` / `CompareLotadSize` — public script specials that call `CompareMonSize` for the two species
- [ ] `GiveGiftRibbonToParty` — writes a gift ribbon ID to `gSaveBlock1` and sets the ribbon data on every non-egg party member; sets `FLAG_SYS_RIBBON_GET`
- [ ] `sBigMonSizeTable` — 16-entry lookup table mapping size-hash ranges to scalar/offset values

#### Notes
This file implements the Sootopolis City Lotad/Seedot size-contest minigame (NPC size records) and the gift-ribbon distribution mechanic (e.g. Pokémon Center Japan ribbon events). Neither feature has been ported. The size-contest specials (`CompareSeedotSize`, `GetSeedotSizeRecordInfo`, etc.) are called from map scripts via the `special` command — they are referenced in the generated script data but the underlying logic is absent. `GiveGiftRibbonToParty` would require ribbon fields in the Gen3 Pokémon save structure (`Gen3Pokemon.ts`) and a `giftRibbons` array in save block 1 (`SaveManager.ts`), neither of which currently exists. The imperial-units `#ifdef` branch is intentionally a no-op for a browser port.

- [ ] Box scroll sprite callbacks: `SpriteCB_BoxMonIconScrollIn` / `SpriteCB_BoxMonIconScrollOut` / `DestroyBoxMonIconsInColumn` / `CreateBoxMonIconsInColumn` / `InitBoxMonIconScroll` / `UpdateBoxMonIconScroll`
- [ ] `CreatePartyMonsSprites` / `CompactPartySprites` / `MovePartySprites` / `DestroyAllPartyMonIcons` — party sprite management

- [ ] `MoveShiftingMons` / `SaveMonSpriteAtPos` — shift animation (two mons swap with sine oscillation)
- [ ] Item icon subsystem: `CreateItemIconSprites` / `TryLoadItemIconAtPos` / `TakeItemFromMon` / `GiveItemToMon` / `SwapItemsWithMon` / `MoveItemFromMonToBag` / `MoveItemFromCursorToBag` / `SetItemIconCallback` / 7 item icon sprite callbacks
- [ ] Cursor subsystem: `CreateCursorSprites` / `InitCursor` / `UpdateCursorPos` / `DoCursorNewPosUpdate` / `GetCursorCoordsByPos` / `SetCursorInParty` / `SetCursorBoxPosition` / `SaveCursorPos` / `GetSavedCursorPos` / `TrySetCursorFistAnim`
- [ ] Mon place-change: `InitMonPlaceChange` / `DoMonPlaceChange` / `MonPlaceChange_Grab` / `MonPlaceChange_Place` / `MonPlaceChange_Shift` + multi variants
- [ ] Box scroll: `SetUpScrollToBox` / `ScrollToBox` / `DetermineBoxScrollDirection` / `SetCurrentBox`
- [ ] Box title sprites: `InitBoxTitle` / `CreateIncomingBoxTitle` / `CycleBoxTitleSprites` / `CycleBoxTitleColor` + callbacks
- [ ] Wallpaper: `SetWallpaperForCurrentBox` / `DoWallpaperGfxChange` / `LoadWallpaperGfx` / `WaitForWallpaperGfxLoad` / `DrawWallpaper` / `GetBoxWallpaper` / `SetBoxWallpaper`
- [ ] Display mon: `RefreshDisplayMon` / `SetDisplayMonData` / `LoadDisplayMonGfx` / `PrintDisplayMonInfo` / `StartDisplayMonMosaicEffect` / `CreateDisplayMonSprite`
- [ ] `MoveMon` / `PlaceMon` / `PurgeMonOrBoxMon` / `SetMovingMonData` / `SetPlacedMonData` / `SetShiftedMonData` / `TryStorePartyMonInBox` / `ReleaseMon` / `InitReleaseMon` / `RunCanReleaseMon` / `AtLeastThreeUsableMons`
- [ ] `PrintMessage` — message printer with dynamic placeholder expansion
- [ ] `DrawTextWindowAndBufferTiles` — renders text into tile buffer (used for box titles)
- [ ] `StringCopyAndFillWithSpaces` — pads string with spaces to fixed width
- [ ] Party menu slide: `ShowPartyMenu` / `HidePartyMenu` / `SetUpShowPartyMenu` / `SetUpHidePartyMenu` / `DoShowPartyMenu`
- [ ] `UpdateCloseBoxButtonFlash` / `StartFlashingCloseBoxButton` / `StopFlashingCloseBoxButton`
- [ ] `SetPartySlotTilemaps` / `UpdatePartySlotColors` — party slot filled/empty tilemap state
- [ ] `UpdateBoxToSendMons` — updates VAR_PC_BOX_TO_SEND_MON on box change
- [ ] TilemapUtil subsystem (8 functions) — supplemental tilemap management for party/close-button overlays
- [ ] `InitSummaryScreenData` / `SetSelectionAfterSummaryScreen` — state save/restore around summary screen
- [ ] `GiveChosenBagItem` — applies bag-selected item to mon on return from bag menu

#### Notes
- The PC Pokemon Storage System (Bill's PC) is entirely absent from the TypeScript port. No box management UI, no deposit/withdraw, no move-mons/move-items modes exist.
- The port has a `BagMenu` and party display in menus (`PartyMenuContent.tsx`) but no box system.
- Link cable trading (`TradeCenter.gen.ts` script data exists but runtime is absent) and record mixing are intentionally omitted.
- `ResetPokemonStorageSystem` is relevant to new-game initialization — the port's `NewGameFlow.ts` would need to call an equivalent to initialize box storage.
- The Walda wallpaper unlock (`IsWaldaWallpaperUnlocked`) depends on a special mini-game that is also unimplemented.
- `CountPartyMons` / `CountPartyNonEggMons` logic is partially covered inline in various TS files but no dedicated utility module exists for it.

- [ ] `SpriteCB_ItemIcon_*` (7 callbacks) — item icon affine animations (appear/disappear/pick-up/put-down/swap)
- [ ] `PrintItemDescription` / `InitItemInfoWindow` / `UpdateItemInfoWindowSlideIn` / `UpdateItemInfoWindowSlideOut` / `DrawItemInfoWindow` — item info panel

#### Notes
The PC Pokémon Storage System (Bill's PC) is entirely unimplemented in the TypeScript port. This is a large, self-contained GBA UI with hardware BG scrolling, OAM sprite management, DMA, palette fades, and compressed tilemap data — none of which map directly to browser equivalents. The underlying data model (`gPokemonStoragePtr->boxes[14][30]`) is similarly absent; the port's `SaveManager`/`Gen3SaveParser` reads Gen 3 save files but does not expose a live box-management interface. The Walda wallpaper, multi-mon move mode, item move mode, release legality checks, and summary screen integration are all missing. The `CompactPartySlots` and `AnyStorageMonWithMove` utility functions are the most likely candidates for eventual porting as they support field logic (release guard, party management).

- [ ] `IsInGamePartnerMon` — suppresses trainer memo for in-game partner party slots (multi battle)
- [ ] `DoesMonOTMatchOwner` / `DidMonComeFromGBAGames` / `DidMonComeFromRSE` — OT ownership checks for trainer memo text selection

#### Notes
- The TS port splits summary screen functionality across `PokemonSummaryContent` (read-only display), `MoveForgetMenuContent` (move replacement flow), and `MoveListModel` (move reorder). The C file is a single monolithic screen controller with 4 sub-pages.
- Contest moves page is intentionally out of scope for the browser port.
- GBA-specific rendering (BG tilemaps, OAM sprites, palette fade, DMA scheduling) has no TS equivalent — the TS uses React/CSS/Canvas rendering instead.
- Multi-battle and Battle Frontier rental display paths are not implemented.
- The move-reorder-within-summary mode (`SwitchToMovePositionSwitchMode`) may not be accessible in TS; move reordering likely handled differently (drag or separate menu).
- Egg-state text thresholds (friendship ≤ 5/10/40 steps) should be straightforward to add but appear missing.

**Coverage**: None

**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented

#### Missing
- [ ] `CB2_InitPokeNav` — main entry point, allocates resources, sets up tasks/callbacks for Pokénav
- [ ] `OpenPokenavForTutorial` / `CB2_InitPokenavForTutorial` — tutorial-mode Pokénav entry (called from Wally tutorial script)
- [ ] `Task_Pokenav` — top-level state machine: opens main menu, routes to sub-menus, handles exit/shutdown
- [ ] `SetActivePokenavMenu` — activates a sub-menu by index, inits/opens its looped tasks
- [ ] `CreateLoopedTask` / `IsLoopedTaskActive` / `FuncIsActiveLoopedTask` — GBA task scheduler wrappers for looped tasks
- [ ] `Task_RunLoopedTask` / `Task_RunLoopedTask_LinkMode` — looped-task runner (state-machine stepping, LT_* actions)
- [ ] `InitPokenavResources` / `FreePokenavResources` — resource alloc/free lifecycle
- [ ] `AnyMonHasRibbon` — scans party + PC boxes for any ribbon-bearing Pokémon
- [ ] `AllocSubstruct` / `GetSubstructPtr` / `FreePokenavSubstruct` — sub-struct heap management (15 slots)
- [ ] `GetPokenavMode` / `SetPokenavMode` — tutorial vs normal mode flag
- [ ] `SetSelectedConditionSearch` / `GetSelectedConditionSearch` — tracks which condition-search category is selected
- [ ] `CanViewRibbonsMenu` — returns whether any mon has a ribbon (gates Ribbons menu option)
- [ ] `VBlankCB_Pokenav` / `CB2_Pokenav` — GBA render-loop callbacks (palette, OAM, sprite animation)
- [ ] `PokenavMenuCallbacks[15]` — full dispatch table for 15 sub-menus (map, condition, match call, ribbons, etc.)

#### Notes
The Pokénav is entirely unimplemented in the TypeScript port. It is a substantial post-new-game feature (region map, Pokémon condition graphs, Match Call / trainer rematches, ribbons browser). The GBA-specific infrastructure (`LoopedTask`, VBlank callbacks, task scheduler, EWRAM allocation) has no direct browser equivalent and would need to be rewritten as React state / hooks. The looped-task pattern could map to async generators or a step-callback queue. The ribbon-check logic (`AnyMonHasRibbon`) depends on PC box data which exists in `src/save/native/Gen3SaveParser.ts` but box ribbon counts are not currently surfaced. Match Call / trainer rematch scheduling is also absent. This entire system is intentionally deferred — it is not on the critical path to first-battle parity.

- [ ] `GetMonListCount` — returns total list count (including Cancel entry)
- [ ] `GetConditionGraphCurrentListIndex` — returns currIndex in the mon list

- [ ] `GetConditionGraphMenuToLoadListIndex` — returns toLoadListIndex (next list index to prefetch)
- [ ] `GetConditionMonPicGfx` — returns pointer to mon picture GFX buffer for a slot
- [ ] `GetConditionMonPal` — returns pointer to mon palette buffer for a slot

- [ ] `GetConditionMonLocationText` — returns pointer to locationText string buffer for a slot
- [ ] `GetConditionMonDataBuffer` — returns the `data` field of the currently selected mon entry

#### Notes
The Pokénav Condition Graph is entirely unimplemented in the TypeScript port. This is a contest-condition visualization feature (showing Cool/Tough/Smart/Cute/Beauty stats as a pentagon graph with sparkles). The port has no contest system, no Pokénav UI beyond what's needed for basic gameplay, and no `PokenavMonList`/`ConditionGraph` equivalents. The triple-buffer slot pattern (`loadId`/`nextLoadIdUp`/`nextLoadIdDown`) is GBA-specific to allow smooth scrolling of mon sprites. The markings system (`MonMarkings`) also has no TS equivalent. This entire file can be considered intentionally deferred — it's post-game/side-content with no impact on the core Emerald story playthrough.

The entire Pokénav Condition Graph menu is unimplemented. This is a post-game / optional feature (Contest condition viewer) with no counterpart anywhere in the TS codebase. It depends on a full Pokénav subsystem (`pokenav.c`, `pokenav_conditions.c`, `condition_graph.c`, etc.) that is also absent. The GBA-specific machinery (looped tasks, BG templates, DMA tilemap transfers, scanline HBlank effects, `AllocSubstruct`/`GetSubstructPtr` arena) has no direct TS equivalent; the port would need a React/WebGL-based Pokénav UI layer before this could be addressed. Intentional omission — not on the critical path for the main-game playthrough.

- [ ] `HandleConditionSearchInput_WaitSetup` — waits for async setup looped task to finish before accepting input
- [ ] `HandleConditionSearchInput` — D-pad/A/B input handler for the search results list
- [ ] `ReturnToConditionSearchList` — transitions back to the condition search menu
- [ ] `OpenConditionGraphFromSearchList` — transitions to the condition graph screen for selected mon
- [ ] `GetReturningFromGraph` — accessor: whether we returned from the graph view
- [ ] `GetSearchResultsMonDataList` — accessor: pointer to the sorted mon list array
- [ ] `GetSearchResultsMonListCount` — accessor: number of mons in search results
- [ ] `GetSearchResultsSelectedMonRank` — returns the rank value of the currently highlighted mon
- [ ] `GetSearchResultsCurrentListIndex` — accessor: current scroll/cursor index in the list
- [ ] `GetConditionSearchLoopedTask` — dispatcher for the async build-results looped task chain
- [ ] `BuildPartyMonSearchResults` — scans party Pokémon and inserts condition data into sorted list
- [ ] `InitBoxMonSearchResults` — initializes box scan state (boxId/monId counters)
- [ ] `BuildBoxMonSearchResults` — scans all PC boxes and inserts condition data into sorted list (batched/yielding)
- [ ] `ConvertConditionsToListRanks` — replaces raw condition values with rank integers (1-based, ties share rank)
- [ ] `InsertMonListItem` — binary-search insertion into descending-sorted mon list
- [ ] `OpenConditionSearchResults` — allocates GFX substruct and kicks off open looped task (fresh entry)
- [ ] `OpenConditionSearchListFromGraph` — same but sets `fromGraph = TRUE` (skip left-header reload)
- [ ] `CreateSearchResultsLoopedTask` — starts a specific looped task for cursor/page movement actions
- [ ] `IsSearchResultLoopedTaskActive` — polls whether current GFX looped task is still running
- [ ] `GetSearchResultCurrentLoopedTaskActive` — inner poll used as `gfx->callback`
- [ ] `FreeSearchResultSubstruct2` — destroys Pokénav list, removes window, frees GFX substruct
- [ ] `LoopedTask_OpenConditionSearchResults` — 6-state async sequence: BG setup → decompress → palette/list creation → fade in
- [ ] `LoopedTask_MoveSearchListCursorUp` — async cursor-up: move list, play SFX, reprint rank label
- [ ] `LoopedTask_MoveSearchListCursorDown` — async cursor-down: same pattern
- [ ] `LoopedTask_MoveSearchListPageUp` — async page-up scroll
- [ ] `LoopedTask_MoveSearchListPageDown` — async page-down scroll
- [ ] `LoopedTask_ExitConditionSearchMenu` — async exit: play SFX, fade to black, slide header down
- [ ] `LoopedTask_SelectSearchResult` — async A-press: play SFX, fade to black before entering graph
- [ ] `AddSearchResultListMenuWindow` — allocates BG window and triggers initial rank label print
- [ ] `PrintSearchResultListMenuItems` — renders current mon's rank number into the rank window
- [ ] `CreateSearchResultsList` — builds `PokenavListTemplate` and calls `CreatePokenavList` for the scrolling list
- [ ] `BufferSearchMonListItem` — formats a single list row: nickname, gender symbol, level from party or PC box

#### Notes
This file implements the Pokénav **Condition Search Results** screen — the ranked list of Pokémon by a contest condition (Cool/Beauty/Cute/Smart/Tough) shown after running a condition search. The entire Pokénav feature set (condition search, condition graph, Pokémon lists, left/right header GFX system, looped-task framework) has no equivalent in the TS port. This is explicitly post-game/side-content (Pokémon Contests) and is not part of the main-game-to-first-battle critical path tracked in the project backlog. Intentional omission is appropriate for the current MVP scope.

- [ ] `PokenavList_MoveCursorDown` — moves cursor down one entry, scrolls list if at bottom of viewport

- [ ] `PokenavList_PageDown` — scrolls list down one full page or moves cursor to bottom

- [ ] `PokenavList_DrawCurrentItemIcon` — redraws the icon for the currently selected entry
- [ ] `LoopedTask_EraseListForCheckPage` — async: hides arrows, erases rows, scrolls BG to align selected entry at top
- [ ] `LoopedTask_PrintCheckPageInfo` — async: prints trainer name + three flavor-text fields to the check-page window
- [ ] `LoopedTask_ReshowListFromCheckPage` — async: erases check-page, scrolls BG back, reprints all visible list items
- [ ] `EraseListEntry` — clears pixel rows in the window tile buffer for N entries at a given offset (handles wrap-around in 16-row ring buffer)

- [ ] `PrintMatchCallListTrainerName` — re-renders trainer name with plain background (restores after check-page exit)
- [ ] `PrintMatchCallFieldNames` — renders "Strategy / Trainer's Pokémon / Self Introduction" red field labels
- [ ] `PrintMatchCallFlavorText` — renders the trainer's flavor text strings for the three check-page fields
- [ ] `LoadListArrowGfx` — decompresses and loads arrow sprite sheet + palette
- [ ] `CreateListArrowSprites` — creates right-arrow, up-arrow, and down-arrow sprites at correct positions
- [ ] `DestroyListArrows` — destroys all three arrow sprites and frees their tiles/palette
- [ ] `ToggleListArrows` — shows or hides all three arrows (used during check-page transitions)
- [ ] `SpriteCB_RightArrow` — tracks `y2` to the selected cursor row each frame
- [ ] `SpriteCB_DownArrow` — shows/hides based on `ShouldShowDownArrow`, bounces Y by ±offset
- [ ] `SpriteCB_UpArrow` — shows/hides based on `ShouldShowUpArrow`, bounces Y by ∓offset
- [ ] `PokenavList_ToggleVerticalArrows` — sets the `sInvisible` flag on up/down arrows (suppresses them during list erase)
- [ ] `InitPokenavListWindowState` — computes `windowTopIndex`, `selectedIndexOffset`, `entriesOffscreen` from template
- [ ] `CopyPokenavListMenuTemplate` — copies BG/window/callback params from template into substruct, calls `AddWindow`

#### Notes
The Pokénav (PokéNav) feature — including the Match Call list and trainer check-pages — is entirely unimplemented in the TypeScript port. The port has no `PokenavState`, no equivalent of `pokenav.c`/`pokenav_list.c`, and no TS menu component for trainer phone calls, rematch scheduling, or the Match Call flavor-text system. This is post-game / side-feature content; it is not on the critical path to the main story. The GBA-specific machinery (looped tasks, DMA BG copy, OAM sprites, hardware BG scroll registers) would be replaced by React/canvas equivalents, but no such replacement exists yet. The `src/menu/` tree covers the start-menu and bag, but has no Pokénav subtree.

#### Notes
The Pokénav is a post-game/secondary feature (accessible via the SELECT button in Hoenn). The port has no Pokénav implementation at all — there is no equivalent menu system, no spinning nav-gear sprite, no help bar system, and no sub-menu routing infrastructure. The entire file's runtime is GBA-hardware-specific (BG registers, DMA3, OBJ VRAM, sprite system) and the UI is driven by looped tasks — none of which have browser equivalents. This is an intentional omission appropriate to the current MVP scope, which focuses on core overworld, battles, and story progression. The Pokénav (map navigation, Match Call, Condition, Ribbons) is a non-critical quality-of-life feature that can be deferred indefinitely.

#### Notes
The PokeNav is a post-game / mid-game feature (registered after obtaining the device). The entire match-call subsystem — contact registry, flag-gated dialogue selection, rematch availability, Trainer's Check page — has no TypeScript equivalent. The `special SetMatchCallRegisteredFlag` hook would need to fire from the script runner when trainers register their number. The `trainerRematches` save field referenced here maps to `GameFlags`/`GameVariables` but the byte array itself is not surfaced in the TS save model. This is a non-trivial feature block that depends on: save data (trainerRematches), the rematch table (`include/constants/rematches.h`, partially covered by `generate-flag-var-maps.cjs`), and PokeNav UI (entirely absent).

Match Call is not on the critical path for the early-game story flow, so omission is expected. No generator script consumes this file.

The rematch flag infrastructure (`GameFlags.ts`) exists and could support `IsRematchEntryRegistered`, but no higher-level Pokénav UI or match-call logic has been built.

- [ ] `HandleMainMenuInputTutorial` — tutorial mode: only Match Call is selectable; other buttons play failure SFX
- [ ] `HandleMainMenuInputEndTutorial` — post-tutorial mode: only Match Call or Switch Off allowed
- [ ] `HandleCantOpenRibbonsInput` — handles input when Ribbons is selected but no ribbon winners exist
- [ ] `HandleConditionMenuInput` — Condition sub-menu navigation and A/B dispatch
- [ ] `HandleConditionSearchMenuInput` — Condition Search sub-menu navigation, sets `SelectedConditionSearch`
- [ ] `CB2_ReturnToMainMenu` / `CB2_ReturnToConditionMenu` — deferred cursor-move callbacks that restore parent menus
- [ ] `SetMenuIdAndCB` / `GetMenuId` — one-shot callback trick to return a menu feature ID after cursor animation
- [ ] `ReturnToMainMenu` / `ReturnToConditionMenu` — restore parent menu state (type, cursor, callback)
- [ ] `UpdateMenuCursorPos` — wrapping up/down D-pad navigation over `sLastCursorPositions`
- [ ] `GetPokenavMenuType` / `GetPokenavCursorPos` / `GetCurrentMenuItemId` / `GetHelpBarTextId` — state-read accessors used by rendering/help-bar subsystems
- [ ] `sLastCursorPositions[]` / `sMenuItems[][]` — menu layout tables (cursor wrap bounds and item-ID arrays per menu type)

#### Notes
The Pokénav (in-game map/condition/match-call device) has no TypeScript equivalent at all. The entire subsystem — including the Pokénav UI shell (`pokenav.h`), the region map, Match Call / trainer-rematch list, Condition graphs, and Ribbon browser — is unimplemented. This is a significant post-early-game feature; it is not on the critical path for the new-game-to-first-battle scope described in the project backlog, so the omission is intentional for the current phase.

- [ ] `FreeAndDestroyMainMenuSprites` — frees sprite tiles/palettes and destroys all menu option sprites

- [ ] `DestroyMenuOptionSprites` — destroys all menu-option subsprites and frees OAM matrices
- [ ] `DrawCurrentMenuOptionLabels` — sets tile/palette/position data on all option subsprites for current menu type

- [ ] `StartOptionAnimations_Exit` — unselected options slide out; selected option zooms and blends away
- [ ] `AreMenuOptionSpritesMoving` — polls sprite callbacks and blend counter to determine if animations are done
- [ ] `StartOptionSlide` — sets up velocity-based linear slide for a group of subsprites
- [ ] `StartOptionZoom` — sets up affine zoom + alpha blend task for the selected option when entering a feature
- [ ] `SetOptionInvisibility` — sets/clears `invisible` flag on a group of subsprites
- [ ] `SpriteCB_OptionSlide` — per-frame slide callback: integrates speed, stops at target X
- [ ] `SpriteCB_OptionZoom` — per-frame zoom callback: affine scale-up with per-subsprite x2 spread, ends invisible
- [ ] `Task_OptionBlend` — task that fades blended option sprites from opaque to transparent over 12 frames
- [ ] `CreateMatchCallBlueLightSprite` — creates blue-light sprite; sets blinking callback if rematches are nearby
- [ ] `DestroyRematchBlueLightSprite` — destroys blue-light sprite
- [ ] `SpriteCB_BlinkingBlueLight` — blinks the blue light sprite every 8 frames
- [ ] `AreAnyTrainerRematchesNearby` — checks rematch table for same map section with pending rematches
- [ ] `AddOptionDescriptionWindow` — creates BG1 text window for option descriptions
- [ ] `PrintCurrentOptionDescription` — clears window, centers and prints description string for current menu item
- [ ] `PrintNoRibbonWinners` — clears window, centers "no ribbon winners" text
- [ ] `CreateMovingBgDotsTask` / `DestroyMovingDotsBgTask` / `Task_MoveBgDots` — scrolls BG3 dot pattern rightward each frame
- [ ] `CreateBgDotPurplePalTask` / `CreateBgDotLightBluePalTask` / `ChangeBgDotsColorToPurple` — palette crossfade tasks for BG dot color (light blue ↔ purple when entering/leaving condition menu)
- [ ] `Task_UpdateBgDotsPalette` — interpolates two 2-color palette entries over 12 frames
- [ ] `SetupPokenavMenuScanlineEffects` — configures WIN0/BLDCNT/scanline-DMA for per-row highlight glow on selected option
- [ ] `DestroyMenuOptionGlowTask` — tears down glow task and clears BLDCNT/WIN0 registers
- [ ] `InitMenuOptionGlow` / `SetMenuOptionGlow` — fills scanline effect buffers with a horizontal highlight band at the cursor row
- [ ] `Task_CurrentMenuOptionGlow` — pulsates BLDY via sine table each frame for glow animation
- [ ] `VBlankCB_PokenavMainMenu` — VBlank handler: transfers palettes, loads OAM, processes sprite copy requests, triggers scanline DMA

#### Notes
The Pokénav is entirely absent from the TypeScript port. It is a post-early-game feature (unlocked after defeating Roxanne) consisting of the Region Map, Condition checker, Match Call, and Ribbons viewer. This file is pure GBA rendering/animation logic (BG layers, OAM affine, DMA scanline effects, palette fades) with no direct browser-rendering analogue. Porting would require a full Pokénav UI system — none of the substruct pattern, looped-task coroutine model, or sprite-sheet management is replicated in TS. The rematch-nearby check (`AreAnyTrainerRematchesNearby`) touches `gSaveBlock1Ptr->trainerRematches` which also has no TS equivalent. This is a significant intentional omission appropriate for a "complete main story" MVP scope.

The Pokénav region map is entirely unimplemented in the TS port. This covers the in-game map accessible from the Pokénav menu — a full-screen interactive region map with zoom in/out to city-level detail, landmark name display, and a cursor. The port has no Pokénav UI at all; this is firmly post-game/UI polish scope. GBA-specific subsystems (looped tasks, BG hardware registers, DMA, OAM) have no direct browser equivalents, but the gameplay logic (map section type detection, city map lookup, landmark listing) would need TS equivalents when this feature is implemented.

#### Notes
This file implements the Pokénav "Ribbons" feature — a scrollable list of all party and PC-boxed Pokémon that have at least one ribbon, sorted descending by ribbon count, with a detail link to the ribbon summary screen. The entire Pokénav UI subsystem is absent from the TypeScript port; the port has no concept of ribbons, PC boxes, or the Pokénav as a navigable app. This is expected — the Pokénav is a post-game/optional feature and the port focuses on the core overworld/battle loop. No GBA hardware specifics are relevant here beyond VRAM/DMA (ignored per guidelines); the omission is intentional scope, not a technical blocker.

Ribbon data is stored in the Gen3 save format (`src/save/native/Gen3Pokemon.ts`) but the ribbon bitfield is parsed only for save round-trip purposes, not for display. The entire Pokénav subsystem (map, condition, ribbons, match call) is absent. This screen is post-game/side-content and not on the critical path to completing the main story.

## batch: src/random.c

### `src/random.c`
**Coverage**: Full
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/battle/engine/BattleRng.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `Random` — linear congruential RNG step using ISO_RANDOMIZE1, returns upper 16 bits of state; mirrored in `BattleRng.ts`
- [x] `SeedRng` — seeds primary RNG state `gRngValue`; seeding support present in `BattleRng.ts`
- [x] `Random2` — secondary RNG using `gRng2Value`; secondary RNG present in `BattleRng.ts`
- [x] `SeedRng2` — seeds secondary RNG state `gRng2Value`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `sRandCount` / `sUnknown` — debug/diagnostic counters not tracked in TS (no gameplay impact)

#### Missing
- (none — all gameplay-relevant logic is covered)

#### Notes
`BattleRng.ts` implements the same ISO_RANDOMIZE1 LCG (`state = state * 0x41C64E6D + 0x6073` or equivalent GBA formula) for battle parity. The overworld uses the same RNG via `ScriptRunner.ts` for wild encounter rolls. `sRandCount` and `sUnknown` are diagnostic/debug fields with no gameplay effect and are intentionally omitted.

- [ ] `SpriteCB_ChasesAway_DuoRingPush` / `ChasesAway_PushDuoBack` — brief knockback of Groudon/Kyogre when Rayquaza's hyper voice ring fires
- [ ] `Task_ChasesAway_AnimateBg` — flickering BLDALPHA for light-ray behind Rayquaza
- [ ] `Task_ChasesAway_AnimateRing` — BG affine expanding ring from Rayquaza's shout (two pulses)
- [ ] `SetWindowsHideVertBorders` / `ResetWindowDimensions` — GBA WIN0 registers to crop scene to narrow letterbox view

#### Notes
The debug page `Rayquaza3DDebugPage.tsx` is a static 3D model viewer (likely for the `RAY_ANIM_DESCENDS` or `RAY_ANIM_CHARGES` Rayquaza sprite), not a playable cutscene runner. The entire 5-scene Rayquaza cutscene system — triggered in-game when the player arrives at Sootopolis during the Groudon/Kyogre conflict — is unimplemented. This is a story-critical sequence gating the Cave of Origin endgame. The GBA-specific techniques (scanline HBlank DMA for cloud parallax, OAM affine for smoke scaling, BG affine scaling for the "Takes Flight" scene, hardware window registers for letterboxing) would all need browser-equivalent replacements (CSS/canvas transforms, WebGL shaders, or pre-composited sprite sheets).

### `src/record_mixing.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `RecordMixingPlayerSpotTriggered` — entry point triggered when player steps on record mixing spot
- [ ] `SetSrcLookupPointers` — caches save block pointers for record mixing session
- [ ] `PrepareExchangePacket` / `PrepareExchangePacketForRubySapphire` / `PrepareUnknownExchangePacket` — serializes player's local data (secret bases, TV shows, Dewford trends, daycare mail, Battle Tower records, Lilycove Lady, apprentices, hall records) into a network packet
- [ ] `ReceiveExchangePacket` — dispatches received record data to per-subsystem receive handlers
- [ ] `Task_RecordMixing_Main` — top-level GBA task: allocates buffers, drives the full mixing state machine, shows completion text
- [ ] `Task_MixingRecordsRecv` — waits for all link players to connect, then initiates packet exchange
- [ ] `Task_SendPacket` — chunks and sends the local record buffer over the link cable (200 bytes/chunk)
- [ ] `Task_CopyReceiveBuffer` — copies received link-block data into the per-player receive buffer as chunks arrive
- [ ] `Task_WaitReceivePacket` / `Task_ReceivePacket` / `Task_SendPacket_SwitchToReceive` — link-state machine helpers
- [ ] `Task_DoRecordMixing` — post-receive task: triggers full save, closes link, handles RS vs Emerald save paths
- [ ] `Task_RecordMixing_SoundEffect` — plays periodic SE during mixing animation
- [ ] `ShufflePlayerIndices` — deterministically permutes 2–4 player indices using link master's trainer ID
- [ ] `ReceiveOldManData` — picks shuffled partner's old man data, sanitizes for version/language, overwrites save
- [ ] `ReceiveBattleTowerData` — copies shuffled partner's Battle Tower record, converts RS↔Emerald format, recalculates checksum
- [ ] `ReceiveLilycoveLadyData` — swaps Lilycove Lady data with a shuffled partner, resets fan-club quiz for record mix
- [ ] `CalculateDaycareMailRandSum` / `GetDaycareMailRandSum` — computes a deterministic sum over TV show bytes used as daycare-mail swap seed
- [ ] `SwapDaycareMail` — swaps daycare mail structs between two specific player/slot pairs
- [ ] `ReceiveDaycareMailData` — full daycare mail mixing: language sanitization, determines which slots can hold items, performs 2/3/4-player swap using seeded RNG, writes result to save
- [ ] `ReceiveGiftItem` — grants key item gift (e.g. Eon Ticket) from link master, sets `VAR_TEMP_RECORD_MIX_GIFT_ITEM`
- [ ] `GetSavedApprentices` — selects up to 2 apprentice records to pass on, preferring non-self apprentices
- [ ] `ReceiveApprenticeData` — saves up to 2 new apprentices from mixed records into save block 2
- [ ] `GetPlayerHallRecords` — populates a `PlayerHallRecords` struct from save block 2 frontier streak data
- [ ] `GetNewHallRecords` — merges partner hall records with local records, keeping best win streaks per facility/level
- [ ] `FillWinStreakRecords1P` / `FillWinStreakRecords2P` — sorts merged hall records by win streak descending, picks top N
- [ ] `SaveHighestWinStreakRecords` — writes best merged hall records back into save block 2
- [ ] `ReceiveRankingHallRecords` — orchestrates hall record merge and save
- [ ] `GetRecordMixingDaycareMail` — reads current daycare mail from save and initializes the mix mail struct
- [ ] `SanitizeDaycareMailForRuby` — converts daycare mail OT/nickname strings for RS compatibility
- [ ] `SanitizeEmeraldBattleTowerRecord` — strips extended control codes from Battle Tower Pokémon nicknames, recalculates checksum
- [ ] `SanitizeRubyBattleTowerRecord` — stub (empty in C source)
- [ ] `PrintTextOnRecordMixing` — draws dialogue frame and prints status text during mixing
- [ ] `LoadPtrFromTaskData` / `StorePtrInTaskData` / `GetMultiplayerId_` / `GetPlayerRecvBuffer` — low-level GBA task/link helpers

#### Notes
This entire file implements the **multiplayer Record Mixing** feature, which requires a GBA link cable (serial IO) or wireless adapter. It has no browser equivalent and is intentionally out of scope for this port. Dependencies on `secret_base`, `tv`, `battle_tower`, `lilycove_lady`, `mauville_old_man`, `dewford_trend`, `daycare`, and `apprentice` subsystems are all link-multiplayer-only features. No portion of this logic is ported or generated.

#### Notes
This entire file is unported. The recorded battle system is a GBA-era feature for saving/replaying battles to a dedicated flash save sector and sharing via record mixing (link cable). Key dependencies that don't exist in the TS port include: link cable multiplayer (`BATTLE_TYPE_LINK`, `gLinkPlayers`), the special flash save sector (`SECTOR_ID_RECORDED_BATTLE`, `TryWriteSpecialSaveSector`), frontier pass/apprentice systems, and the Easy Chat speech system. The feature is post-game Battle Frontier content. The browser port's `SaveManager`/`Gen3SaveParser` handles its own save format and does not model the special sectors. This is a reasonable intentional omission for the current scope of the port.

- The mapsec layout data (`sRegionMap_MapSectionLayout`, `gRegionMapEntries`) lives in `data/region_map/region_map_layout.h` and `data/region_map/region_map_entries.h` — neither is generated nor imported in TS.
- `sRegionMap_SpecialPlaceLocations` (underwater→surface remapping) and `sTerraOrMarineCaveMapSecIds` / `sMarineCaveLocationCoords` (weather-cave positions) are data tables with no TS equivalent.
- `sMapHealLocations` (mapsec → heal-location warp target table) is missing; warp targets for Fly landing are hardcoded inline in `CB_ExitFlyMap`.
- Intentional omissions: GBA hardware affine register writes (`BG2PA` etc.), VBlank callback, OAM/DMA transfers — these have no browser equivalent.
- The Pokénav region map (`pokenav_region_map.c`), Pokédex area screen, and field region map (PC wall) are separate files not analyzed here but equally unported.

- [ ] `RegisterRamReset` / `REG_IME` manipulation — GBA hardware; intentionally omitted (no browser equivalent)

This file's primary purpose is post-link-error recovery — a GBA multiplayer concern with no browser equivalent. The general "load save and continue" path is covered by the TS save system, but the atomic hardware-level reset sequence (`RegisterRamReset`, IME disable/restore, heap reinit) is intentionally omitted. Audio options from save data (`optionsSound`) are not consumed by the port's audio system since audio is not yet implemented.

#### Notes
This file implements a GBA-specific RTC reset utility screen launched from the main menu when the "Reset RTC" option is selected (triggered by holding Select+B+Down on the title screen in the original game). It is a standalone hardware maintenance screen heavily tied to GBA RTC (`rtc.h`), DMA, GBA register writes, and sprite OAM. The browser port has no concept of a hardware RTC or RTC drift correction. The underlying data it manipulates (`gSaveBlock2Ptr->lastBerryTreeUpdate`, `VAR_DAYS`) does have TS equivalents in `GameVariables`/`SaveManager`, but the screen itself has no TS equivalent and is an intentional omission — browser saves don't have RTC skew issues.

- GBA-specific operations (`ScanlineEffect_Clear`, `CpuFastFill` to VRAM, `SetGpuReg`, `BattleInitBgsAndWindows`, `FreeAllSpritePalettes`) are intentionally omitted as they have no browser equivalent.
- Safari Battle and Wally Tutorial special-case sprite/healthbox paths exist in TS types but the reshow-specific branches for these modes are unimplemented.

- The shadow sprite reload (`LoadAndCreateEnemyShadowSprites`, `SetBattlerShadowSpriteCallback`) for opponent battlers is not present in the TS reshow flow.

**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented

#### Missing
- [ ] `ClearRoamerData` — zeroes roamer save data, sets species to Latias
- [ ] `ClearRoamerLocationData` — zeroes location history and current location arrays
- [ ] `CreateInitialRoamerMon` — creates Latias or Latios at level 40 with random IVs, sets initial random route location
- [ ] `InitRoamer` — entry point called from TV event; clears data then calls `CreateInitialRoamerMon` based on player's multichoice answer (Red=Latias, Blue=Latios)
- [ ] `UpdateLocationHistoryForRoamer` — shifts 3-slot location history ring buffer on each map transition
- [ ] `RoamerMoveToOtherLocationSet` — picks a new starting route from `sRoamerLocations` different from current map
- [ ] `RoamerMove` — called on map transition; 1/16 chance teleports to new location set, otherwise moves within current set's route list while avoiding recent history
- [ ] `IsRoamerAt` — checks if roamer is active and on the given map group/num

- [ ] `UpdateRoamerHPStatus` — persists HP/status back to roamer struct after battle, then moves roamer to new location set
- [ ] `SetRoamerInactive` — marks roamer as caught/defeated

- [ ] `sRoamerLocations` table — 20 route-sets defining legal roamer movement graph across Routes 110–134

#### Notes
Roaming Pokémon (Latias/Latios) are post-game content gated behind the TV event multichoice. The entire roamer subsystem — save-data struct (`gSaveBlock1Ptr->roamer`), location tracking, encounter injection, and post-battle persistence — has no TypeScript equivalent. `wildEncounterService.ts` handles wild encounters but has no hook for roamer injection before the normal encounter roll. The roamer save struct fields (`species`, `level`, `ivs`, `personality`, `hp`, `status`, `active`, contest stats) would need to be added to the save state. The `sLocationHistory` ring buffer and `sRoamerLocation` are runtime-only (EWRAM) and would live in `WorldManager` or a new `RoamerManager`. The `TryStartRoamerEncounter` call site is in `src/wild_encounter.c` (not shown here) and would need wiring into `overworldWildEncounterFlow.ts`.

**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `sGFRomHeader` — ROM header struct exposing save block offsets, species/move/item/ability data pointers, and game constants for external tool access (PKHex, Colosseum, streaming HUDs)

#### Notes
This file is GBA-ROM-specific infrastructure: a statically allocated struct placed in `.text.consts` that exposes internal save block offsets and data table pointers via a fixed memory address so external applications (Colosseum, XD, PKHex) can locate save data and graphics without reverse-engineering the binary. The browser port has no equivalent concept — save data is accessed directly via `Gen3SaveParser.ts`, species/move/item data is accessed via generated TypeScript modules, and there is no external binary API surface. Intentionally omitted; not applicable to a browser runtime.

The TS port uses `RotatingGateManager.ts` for game-logic (collision, orientation state) but the visual rotation animation using GBA affine sprites is not ported — rendering the gates as static or procedurally-drawn overlays would be needed.
- The 20 affine animation sequences (idle at 4 orientations + 8 slow rotations + 8 fast rotations) are GBA hardware specific and have no equivalent.
- Orientation state persists in `VAR_TEMP_0` (resets on map exit), which the TS equivalent maps to `GameVariables` temp var storage.
- The `BUGFIX` collision check (`!= 0` vs `== 1`) is a known decompilation correctness fix; TS should use the fixed version.

## batch: src/rotating_tile_puzzle.c

### `src/rotating_tile_puzzle.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/RotatingGateManager.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `InitRotatingTilePuzzle` — allocates puzzle state; TS equivalent initializes rotating gate state in `RotatingGateManager`
- [x] `FreeRotatingTilePuzzle` — tears down puzzle state and unfreezes object events; TS equivalent cleans up gate manager state

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `MoveRotatingTileObjects` — scans object templates on arrow metatiles and slides them one tile; TS has object movement but the metatile-based arrow detection and per-color puzzle filtering logic may not be fully replicated
- [-] `TurnRotatingTileObjects` — rotates NPC facing direction counter-clockwise after sliding; TS `RotatingGateManager` handles gate rotation but the exact `prevPuzzleTileNum` tracking / tileDifference → rotation mapping may differ

#### Missing
- [ ] `SaveRotatingTileObject` — records which template index and arrow tile an object was on before moving (internal bookkeeping needed for `TurnRotatingTileObjects`)
- [ ] `TurnUnsavedRotatingTileObject` — functionally dead in retail (off-map object edge case), but rotation logic is otherwise identical to the saved path

#### Notes
- The C file handles two puzzle contexts via `isTrickHouse`: Mossdeep Gym (yellow arrows, `METATILE_MossdeepGym_YellowArrow_Right`) and Trick House puzzles (`METATILE_TrickHousePuzzle_Arrow_YellowOnWhite_Right`). Both puzzle types need to be verified in `RotatingGateManager`.
- The `tileDifference` → rotation logic in `TurnRotatingTileObjects` always resolves to `ROTATE_COUNTERCLOCKWISE` by design (a known quirk/UB in the original); the TS port should replicate counter-clockwise-only rotation regardless.
- MAP_OFFSET (+7) is applied to `objectEvents[i].x/y` before `MapGridGetMetatileIdAt` — TS port must NOT add this offset given the project's coordinate convention (see MEMORY.md MAP_OFFSET trap).
- Movement scripts (`sMovement_ShiftRight` etc.) are inline byte arrays; TS equivalent likely uses the ScriptRunner movement command infrastructure rather than raw movement arrays.

- [ ] `SetBallStuck` — handles stuck ball: determines quadrant, picks Shroomish vs Taillow, selects target slot with party-influenced 75% bet-favor chance

- [ ] `CreateShroomishSprite` / `CreateTaillowSprite` — spawn Shroomish/Taillow + shadows
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Shroomish` / `SpriteCB_ShroomishFall` / `SpriteCB_ShroomishShakeScreen` / `SpriteCB_ShroomishExit` — full Shroomish animation sequence with screen shake
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Taillow` / `SpriteCB_Taillow_FlyIn` / `SpriteCB_Taillow_PickUpBall` / `SpriteCB_Taillow_FlyAway` / `SpriteCB_TaillowShadow_FlyIn` / `SpriteCB_TaillowShadow_Flash` — full Taillow animation sequence
- [ ] `SpriteCB_WheelIcon` — rotates wheel icon sprites using affine matrix each frame
- [ ] `SpriteCB_WheelCenter` — syncs center sprite affine matrix to wheel rotation
- [ ] `SpriteCB_GridSquare` — offsets grid sprites by `gridX` scroll value
- [ ] `CreateWheelIconSprites` / `CreateWheelBallSprites` / `CreateWheelCenterSprite` / `CreateGridSprites` / `CreateGridBallSprites` / `CreateInterfaceSprites` — all sprite creation (wheel, grid, UI)
- [ ] `ShowHideGridIcons` / `ShowHideGridBalls` / `ShowHideWinSlotCursor` / `HideWheelBalls` — visibility management
- [ ] All sprite/palette/tilemap data constants (`sRouletteTables`, `sGridSelections`, `sRouletteSlots`, `sFlashData_Colors`, `sFlashData_PokeIcons`, all `sSpriteTemplate_*`) — game configuration tables

#### Notes
- Roulette is the Mauville Game Corner minigame — entirely absent from the TypeScript port. No file, no stub, no partial implementation.
- The feature depends on GBA-specific hardware (BG affine rotation, DMA, palette blending, OAM matrix updates) but all of that is abstractable into Canvas/WebGL; the main blocker is simply that the feature has never been started.
- `RouletteFlash_*` utilities referenced here live in a separate `roulette_util.c` — also not ported.
- Party species check (Shroomish/Taillow influencing ball physics) is a notable gameplay mechanic that would need to hook into the existing party system (`PartyContext`).
- Coin management would integrate with the existing `MoneyManager` / coins API already used by `ScriptRunner` specials.
- The `gSpecialVar_0x8004` communication protocol (table ID, special rate flag, result) would need to be wired through `GameVariables`.
- This is a non-critical post-game/side-content feature; intentional omission is reasonable for MVP scope.

## batch: src/rtc.c

### `src/rtc.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/GameVariables.ts`, `src/save/native/Gen3SaveParser.ts`, `src/save/SaveManager.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `IsLeapYear` — leap year calculation; logic implicitly available via JS `Date`
- [x] `ConvertDateToDayCount` — day count from date; implicitly handled by JS `Date` arithmetic in time-related save/variable code
- [x] `RtcCalcLocalTime` / `RtcCalcTimeDifference` — local time delta from saved offset; partially replicated via JS `Date.now()` in `SaveManager`/`GameVariables` for berry growth, day-cycle features

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `RtcGetMinuteCount` — returns total minutes since epoch; used for berry timers and Pokémon Day Care. JS `Date` is used instead of GBA RTC, but the specific minute-count interface is not explicitly ported as a named function
- [-] `RtcCalcLocalTimeOffset` / `RtcInitLocalTimeOffset` — sets offset stored in SaveBlock2 (`localTimeOffset`); save-block fields exist in `Gen3SaveParser.ts` but the offset calculation/write path is not fully wired up

#### Missing
- [ ] `ConvertBcdToBinary` — BCD-to-binary conversion; GBA RTC hardware returns BCD, irrelevant in browser (JS `Date` gives binary directly), intentionally omitted
- [ ] `RtcDisableInterrupts` / `RtcRestoreInterrupts` — GBA interrupt masking, hardware-only, no browser equivalent
- [ ] `RtcInit` — probes GBA RTC hardware via SiiRtc driver, hardware-only
- [ ] `RtcGetErrorStatus` — returns hardware error flags, hardware-only
- [ ] `RtcGetInfo` / `RtcGetRawInfo` / `RtcGetDateTime` / `RtcGetStatus` — GBA SiiRtc driver reads, hardware-only
- [ ] `RtcCheckInfo` — validates BCD fields from hardware RTC, hardware-only
- [ ] `RtcReset` — resets GBA RTC chip, hardware-only
- [ ] `RtcGetDayCount` — extracts day count from raw BCD RTC struct; only needed when parsing real GBA RTC data
- [ ] `CalcTimeDifference` — generic struct Time subtraction; not explicitly ported (JS arithmetic used directly)
- [ ] `RtcGetLocalDayCount` — returns cached day count from last `RtcGetInfo` call; not ported
- [ ] `FormatDecimalTime` / `FormatHexTime` / `FormatHexRtcTime` / `FormatDecimalDate` / `FormatHexDate` — GBA string formatting utilities for debug/clock display, no browser equivalent needed

#### Notes
- The GBA RTC hardware layer (`SiiRtcProbe`, `SiiRtcGetDateTime`, etc.) has no browser equivalent and is entirely omitted by design. The browser port uses `Date.now()` directly for all time-dependent features (berries, day/night, Pokémon Day Care).
- The `struct Time` / `gLocalTime` / `localTimeOffset` pattern from SaveBlock2 is partially reflected in the Gen3 save parser for native save compatibility, but the live RTC offset recalculation loop is not implemented.
- `RtcGetMinuteCount` is the most gameplay-critical function (berry growth uses it directly); a JS equivalent is needed but not yet explicitly named/exposed.

- Pokeblock feeder system (`sPokeblockFeeders[10]`) is entirely absent — this affects wild encounter encounter-rate modifiers inside the Safari Zone.
- `TryPutSafariFanClubOnAir` (TV system) is intentionally omitted as the TV/fan-club broadcast system is not ported.
- Safari Zone map scripts (`SafariZone_EventScript_TimesUp` etc.) are generated via `scripts/generate-scripts.cjs` and exist as `.gen.ts` files, but the runtime trigger hooks (`SafariZoneTakeStep` calling `ScriptContext_SetupScript`) are not connected.
- `CB2_EndSafariBattle` depends on battle outcome routing (`B_OUTCOME_NO_SAFARI_BALLS`, `B_OUTCOME_CAUGHT`) which exists in the battle engine types but the Safari-specific post-battle flow is unimplemented.

## batch: src/save.c

### `src/save.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/save/SaveManager.ts`, `src/save/SaveStateStore.ts`, `src/save/native/Gen3SaveParser.ts`, `src/save/native/Gen3LayoutProfiles.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `CalculateChecksum` — 32-bit accumulator folded to 16-bit; implemented in `Gen3SaveParser.ts`
- [x] `GetSaveValidStatus` — dual-slot validity check with counter comparison; implemented in `Gen3SaveParser.ts`
- [x] `CopySaveSlotData` — reads sectors and copies validated data into save buffers; implemented in `Gen3SaveParser.ts`
- [x] `TryLoadSaveSlot` / `LoadGameSave(SAVE_NORMAL)` — full-slot load with status; `Gen3SaveParser.ts` + `SaveManager.ts`
- [x] `sSaveSlotLayout` — sector chunk layout (SaveBlock2, SaveBlock1×4, PokemonStorage×9); mirrored in `Gen3LayoutProfiles.ts`
- [x] `Save_ResetSaveCounters` — counter reset on new game; handled in `SaveManager.ts`
- [x] `HandleSavingData(SAVE_NORMAL)` / `TrySavingData` — normal save path; `SaveManager.ts` persists to localStorage/IndexedDB

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `HandleSavingData(SAVE_HALL_OF_FAME)` — HOF sector write (`SECTOR_ID_HOF_1/2`) not implemented; browser save ignores HOF sectors
- [-] `HandleSavingData(SAVE_LINK)` / `WriteSaveBlock2` / `WriteSaveBlock1Sector` — incremental link-save path exists in C but no link cable in browser; the TS save is atomic, not incremental-sector-based

#### Missing
- [ ] `ClearSaveData` — erases all 32 flash sectors; no browser equivalent (localStorage clear is done differently)
- [ ] `HandleWriteSector` / `HandleReplaceSector` / `TryWriteSector` — low-level flash sector write with rotation logic; browser uses JSON/binary blob, no sector rotation
- [ ] `HandleWriteSectorNBytes` — raw N-byte sector write used for HOF; not ported
- [ ] `RestoreSaveBackupVarsAndIncrement` / `RestoreSaveBackupVars` — incremental save state backup; not needed without sector rotation
- [ ] `HandleWriteIncrementalSector` — incremental sector-by-sector write for link save; not ported
- [ ] `HandleReplaceSectorAndVerify` / `WriteSectorSignatureByte` / `WriteSectorSignatureByte_NoOffset` / `CopySectorSignatureByte` — signature byte manipulation for atomic sector replacement; flash-specific, not ported
- [ ] `TryLoadSaveSector` — load a single named sector (used for HOF); not ported
- [ ] `TryReadSpecialSaveSector` / `TryWriteSpecialSaveSector` — Trainer Hill / Recorded Battle special sectors; not ported
- [ ] `GetSaveBlocksPointersBaseOffset` — derives pointer base offset from trainer ID for pointer-based save addressing; not needed (TS uses direct struct parsing)
- [ ] `ReadFlashSector` — hardware flash read; replaced by binary ArrayBuffer parsing in `Gen3SaveParser.ts`
- [ ] `Task_LinkFullSave` — task-based incremental link save with standby states; link cable not implemented, entire task omitted
- [ ] `LinkFullSave_Init` / `LinkFullSave_WriteSector` / `LinkFullSave_ReplaceLastSector` / `LinkFullSave_SetLastSectorSignature` — link full-save protocol; not ported
- [ ] `WriteSaveBlock2` / `WriteSaveBlock1Sector` — partial link-save helpers; not ported
- [ ] `SetDamagedSectorBits` — tracks flash wear/damage per sector; no browser equivalent

#### Notes
- The GBA save format uses 32 flash sectors (4 KB each), rotating between two save slots with counter-based selection. The TS port reads the binary `.sav` file format via `Gen3SaveParser.ts` for import/export compatibility with real GBA saves, but the browser's runtime save uses `SaveStateStore.ts` (localStorage/IndexedDB JSON), completely bypassing the sector/flash layer.
- Link cable save paths (`SAVE_LINK`, `SAVE_EREADER`, `Task_LinkFullSave`) are intentionally omitted — no multiplayer in browser.
- Hall of Fame (`SAVE_HALL_OF_FAME`) sectors and Recorded Battle sector are not implemented.
- Trainer Hill special sector (`SECTOR_ID_TRAINER_HILL`) has runtime data (`src/game/trainerHillRuntime.ts`) but no save-sector persistence.
- The `gLastWrittenSector` rotation scheme (wear leveling) is irrelevant to the browser implementation.

## batch: src/save_failed_screen.c

### `src/save_failed_screen.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `DoSaveFailedScreen` — entry point; sets up save-failed state machine
- [ ] `CB2_SaveFailedScreen` — initializes BG/OBJ/window layout with Birch grass backdrop and clock sprite
- [ ] `CB2_WipeSave` — iterates `gDamagedSaveSectors`, calls `WipeSectors`/`HandleSavingData` up to 3 times
- [ ] `CB2_GameplayCannotBeContinued` — waits for A-press, shows "gameplay cannot be continued" message
- [ ] `CB2_FadeAndReturnToTitleScreen` — waits for A-press, fades to black
- [ ] `CB2_ReturnToTitleScreen` — after fade, either soft-resets or calls `gGameContinueCallback`
- [ ] `VBlankCB_UpdateClockGraphics` — animates spinning clock OAM sprite during save wipe
- [ ] `WipeSectors` / `WipeSector` / `VerifySectorWipe` — low-level flash-sector erase and verify loop
- [ ] `SaveFailedScreenTextPrint` — local text printer helper used by the screen

#### Notes
This file is entirely GBA-hardware-specific: it manages flash-memory sector wiping (`ProgramFlashByte`, `ReadFlash`), DMA fills into VRAM/OAM/PLTT, and a VBlank-driven spinning-clock sprite. None of these concepts exist in the browser port. The browser port's `SaveManager` / `SaveStateStore` use `localStorage` (no flash sectors, no damaged-sector bitmask), so there is no analogous "save failed" recovery flow. The screen can be omitted entirely or replaced with a simple error toast if `localStorage` write fails.

## batch: src/scanline_effect.c

### `src/scanline_effect.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/rendering/webgl/WebGLScanlineRenderer.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `ScanlineEffect_InitWave` — per-scanline wave distortion effect; approximated in `WebGLScanlineRenderer.ts` via sine-based horizontal offset applied in the WebGL pass

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `TaskFunc_UpdateWavePerFrame` — wave animation update loop; the TS renderer animates the wave each frame but does not replicate the double-buffered scanline register model or `tApplyBattleBgOffsets` battle-scroll offsets (BG0–BG3 H/VOFS integration is absent)
- [-] `GenerateWave` — sine-table wave generation; equivalent math exists in `WebGLScanlineRenderer.ts` but uses `Math.sin` rather than the GBA sine lookup table, so phase/amplitude fidelity may diverge slightly

#### Missing
- [ ] `ScanlineEffect_Stop` — stops DMA and destroys the wave task; no explicit teardown path in the TS renderer
- [ ] `ScanlineEffect_Clear` — resets all DMA/buffer state to zero; TS renderer has no equivalent reset method
- [ ] `ScanlineEffect_SetParams` — configures 16-bit vs 32-bit DMA destination register; hardware-specific, intentionally omitted
- [ ] `ScanlineEffect_InitHBlankDmaTransfer` — HBlank DMA setup per frame; GBA-hardware-only, intentionally omitted
- [ ] `CopyValue16Bit` / `CopyValue32Bit` — direct register writes for first scanline; GBA-hardware-only, intentionally omitted
- [ ] Double-buffer swap logic (`srcBuffer ^= 1`) — GBA DMA double-buffering; not applicable in WebGL

#### Notes
The GBA scanline effect system relies on HBlank DMA to write per-scanline register values directly into `REG_BG*HOFS`/`VOFS` on each raster line — a hardware mechanism with no direct browser equivalent. The TS port approximates the visual result (horizontal wave distortion) in a WebGL shader pass rather than emulating the register-level mechanism. Battle background scroll offsets (`gBattle_BG*_X/Y`) applied inside `TaskFunc_UpdateWavePerFrame` are not wired up in the TS equivalent, so battle-background wave effects may not match C parity. DMA, task system, and interrupt-driven code is intentionally omitted as GBA-only.

- [x] ScrCmd_removeobject / ScrCmd_removeobjectat — despawn NPC
- [x] ScrCmd_addobject / ScrCmd_addobjectat — spawn NPC

- [x] ScrCmd_setobjectxyperm — update NPC template coords (persistent)

- [x] ScrCmd_turnobject — turn NPC to direction

- [x] ScrCmd_releaseall / ScrCmd_release — unfreeze objects, hide message box

- [x] ScrCmd_waitbuttonpress — wait for A/B

- [x] ScrCmd_gotopostbattlescript / ScrCmd_gotobeatenscript — post-battle script routing

- [x] ScrCmd_setberrytree — plant berry tree

- [x] ScrCmd_opendoor / ScrCmd_closedoor / ScrCmd_waitdooranim / ScrCmd_setdooropen / ScrCmd_setdoorclosed — door animation commands

- [x] ScrCmd_givemon / ScrCmd_giveegg — give Pokémon/egg to player
- [x] ScrCmd_checkpartymove — search party for a known move
- [x] ScrCmd_addmoney / ScrCmd_removemoney / ScrCmd_checkmoney — money operations
- [x] ScrCmd_showmoneybox / ScrCmd_hidemoneybox / ScrCmd_updatemoneybox — money HUD

- [x] ScrCmd_setrespawn — set heal location warp
