- [x] ScrCmd_compare_local_to_local / compare_local_to_value / compare_local_to_ptr / compare_ptr_to_local / compare_ptr_to_value / compare_ptr_to_ptr — ctx.data slot comparisons

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] ScrCmd_playbgm / ScrCmd_savebgm / ScrCmd_fadedefaultbgm / ScrCmd_fadenewbgm / ScrCmd_fadeoutbgm / ScrCmd_fadeinbgm — audio commands parsed/dispatched but audio system is a stub (no real BGM playback)
- [-] ScrCmd_playse / ScrCmd_waitse / ScrCmd_playfanfare / ScrCmd_waitfanfare — SE/fanfare commands present but audio unimplemented
- [-] ScrCmd_playmoncry / ScrCmd_waitmoncry — cry commands present; no actual audio
- [-] ScrCmd_bufferstring / ScrCmd_bufferstdstring / ScrCmd_buffercontestname / ScrCmd_bufferboxname / ScrCmd_bufferdecorationname / ScrCmd_buffertrainerclassname / ScrCmd_buffertrainername / ScrCmd_bufferleadmonspeciesname / ScrCmd_bufferitemnameplural — some buffer commands implemented, coverage incomplete
- [-] ScrCmd_vgoto / ScrCmd_vcall / ScrCmd_vgoto_if / ScrCmd_vcall_if / ScrCmd_setvaddress — virtual address (Mystery Event / saved RAM script) commands; address offset mechanism not ported (no e-Reader/Mystery Event support planned)
- [-] ScrCmd_setmonmove — slot overwrite present but PP recalculation may be incomplete
- [-] ScrCmd_showmoneybox / ScrCmd_showcoinsbox / ScrCmd_updatecoinsbox / ScrCmd_hidecoinsbox — money/coins HUD wired but coin display system partially stubbed

#### Missing
- [ ] ScrCmd_gotonative / ScrCmd_callnative — execute arbitrary native function pointer (GBA-specific, intentionally omitted)
- [ ] ScrCmd_returnram / ScrCmd_endram — RAM script return/end (Mystery Event system, not ported)
- [ ] ScrCmd_setmysteryeventstatus / ScrCmd_trywondercardscript — Wonder Card / Mystery Event (not ported)
- [ ] ScrCmd_loadbytefromptr / ScrCmd_setptr / ScrCmd_setptrbyte / ScrCmd_copylocal / ScrCmd_copybyte — raw pointer/ctx slot manipulation commands (low-level, not all present in ScriptRunner)
- [ ] ScrCmd_incrementgamestat — game stat counters (not ported)
- [ ] ScrCmd_animateflash / ScrCmd_setflashlevel — flash level animation (FlashController exists but script command dispatch unclear)
- [ ] ScrCmd_fadescreenswapbuffers — palette swap + fade (GBA palette double-buffer, not meaningful in browser)
- [ ] ScrCmd_initclock / ScrCmd_dotimebasedevents / ScrCmd_gettime — RTC time commands (no real-time clock system in port)
- [ ] ScrCmd_setstepcallback — activate per-step callback by index
- [ ] ScrCmd_setmaplayoutindex — dynamic map layout switching
- [ ] ScrCmd_warpteleport / ScrCmd_warpmossdeepgym — unused RS teleport warps
- [ ] ScrCmd_warpspinenter — spin-enter warp (Union Room, not ported)
- [ ] ScrCmd_warpwhitefade — white fade warp
- [ ] ScrCmd_addpcitem / ScrCmd_checkpcitem — PC item storage
- [ ] ScrCmd_adddecoration / ScrCmd_removedecoration / ScrCmd_checkdecorspace / ScrCmd_checkdecor — Secret Base decorations (not ported)
- [ ] ScrCmd_pokemartdecoration / ScrCmd_pokemartdecoration2 — decoration shop menus (not ported)
- [ ] ScrCmd_playslotmachine — slot machine minigame (not ported)
- [ ] ScrCmd_braillemessage / ScrCmd_closebraillemessage — braille Pokémon text (Regi puzzles)
- [ ] ScrCmd_vmessage / ScrCmd_vbuffermessage / ScrCmd_vbufferstring — virtual-address message/buffer commands (Mystery Event)
- [ ] ScrCmd_buffercontestname — contest name string fill (contests not ported)
- [ ] ScrCmd_showcontestpainting / ScrCmd_choosecontestmon / ScrCmd_startcontest / ScrCmd_showcontestresults / ScrCmd_contestlinktransfer — contest system (not ported)
- [ ] ScrCmd_showcoinsbox / ScrCmd_hidecoinsbox / ScrCmd_updatecoinsbox — coin HUD display commands
- [ ] ScrCmd_addelevmenuitem / ScrCmd_showelevmenu — RS elevator menu (no-ops in Emerald, not ported)
- [ ] ScrCmd_getpokenewsactive — PokéNews system (not ported)
- [ ] ScrCmd_setobjectsubpriority / ScrCmd_resetobjectsubpriority — sprite sub-priority override
- [ ] ScrCmd_copyobjectxytoperm — copy live NPC coords back to template
- [ ] ScrCmd_createvobject / ScrCmd_turnvobject — virtual object events (Birch scene etc.)
- [ ] ScrCmd_pokenavcall / ScrCmd_messageautoscroll / ScrCmd_messageinstant — PokéNav call message, auto-scroll, and instant-print variants
- [ ] ScrCmd_setmodernfatefulencounter / ScrCmd_checkmodernfatefulencounter — fateful encounter bit
- [ ] ScrCmd_setmonmetlocation — override met-location on party Pokémon
- [ ] ScrCmd_bufferboxname — PC box name string fill
- [ ] SetMovingNpcId — exported helper to set sMovingNpcId from outside (not needed in TS architecture)

#### Notes
- `ScriptRunner.ts` implements the ~30 "MVP" commands; coverage of the full ~220-command set is roughly 40–50% of gameplay-relevant commands.
- Audio commands (`playse`, `playbgm`, `playfanfare`, etc.) are parsed and dispatched but the audio subsystem is a stub — they count as partial.
- The virtual-address (`vgoto`, `vcall`, `setvaddress`) and RAM script (`returnram`, `endram`, Mystery Event) family are intentionally omitted — no e-Reader/Mystery Event support.
- Link cable commands (`IsOverworldLinkActive` guards in `lockall`/`lock`) are treated as always-false; the guard logic is present.
- Contest, decoration, slot machine, elevator, PokéNews, and Secret Base command families are not ported.
- `createvobject`/`turnvobject` (virtual object events) are needed for the Birch intro cutscene but are currently missing.
- The MAP_OFFSET handling in `setmetatile`, `opendoor`, `closedoor`, `setdooropen`, `setdoorclosed` is documented in MEMORY.md — the TS side strips MAP_OFFSET before storage, so these commands must NOT re-add it when dispatching.

## batch: src/script.c

### `src/script.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts`, `src/scripting/mapHooks/runMapEntryScripts.ts`, `src/scripting/mapHooks/createMapScriptRunnerContext.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `InitScriptContext` — context initialization with mode/stack/data reset → `ScriptRunner` constructor
- [x] `SetupBytecodeScript` — set script pointer and mode → `ScriptRunner.run()`
- [x] `StopScript` — halt context → `ScriptRunner` stop/terminate logic
- [x] `RunScriptCommand` — bytecode dispatch loop → `ScriptRunner.tick()` / `step()`
- [x] `ScriptPush` / `ScriptPop` — call stack for `call`/`return` → `ScriptRunner` internal call stack
- [x] `ScriptJump` — unconditional jump → `jump` command in `ScriptRunner`
- [x] `ScriptCall` — push return address, jump → `call` command in `ScriptRunner`
- [x] `ScriptReturn` — pop return address → `end`/`return` command in `ScriptRunner`
- [x] `ScriptReadHalfword` / `ScriptReadWord` — little-endian reads from bytecode → handled by generated script data (scripts stored as structured objects, not raw bytecode)
- [x] `LockPlayerFieldControls` / `UnlockPlayerFieldControls` / `ArePlayerFieldControlsLocked` — input lock during scripts → `src/game/overworld/inputLock/scheduleInputUnlock.ts`, `scriptWarpInputGuard.ts`
- [x] `ScriptContext_IsEnabled` / `ScriptContext_Init` / `ScriptContext_RunScript` / `ScriptContext_SetupScript` / `ScriptContext_Stop` / `ScriptContext_Enable` — global context lifecycle → `useHandledStoryScript.ts`, `ScriptRunner` state machine
- [x] `RunScriptImmediately` — synchronous run-to-completion for map header scripts → `runMapEntryScripts.ts` synchronous execution path
- [x] `RunOnLoadMapScript` / `RunOnTransitionMapScript` / `RunOnResumeMapScript` / `RunOnReturnToFieldMapScript` / `RunOnDiveWarpMapScript` — map header script triggers → `runMapEntryScripts.ts`, `runMapDiveScript.ts`
- [x] `TryRunOnFrameMapScript` — per-frame var-check table → `createMapScriptRunnerContext.ts` ON_FRAME_TABLE handling
- [x] `TryRunOnWarpIntoMapScript` — warp-into-map table check → `runMapEntryScripts.ts`
- [x] `MapHeaderGetScriptTable` / `MapHeaderRunScriptType` / `MapHeaderCheckScriptTable` — map header script lookup by tag → generated script registry (`src/data/scripts/index.ts`) + `runMapEntryScripts.ts`

#### Missing
- [ ] `SetupNativeScript` — native C function callback mode; not applicable (no equivalent concept in TS scripting model)
- [ ] `CalculateRamScriptChecksum` — CRC16 checksum for RAM script persistence
- [ ] `ClearRamScript` — wipe RAM script from save block
- [ ] `InitRamScript` — store a runtime-created script into save block with metadata
- [ ] `GetRamScript` — retrieve and validate per-object RAM script override
- [ ] `ValidateSavedRamScript` — validate Mystery Gift/Wonder Card RAM script
- [ ] `GetSavedRamScriptIfValid` — retrieve validated Wonder Card RAM script
- [ ] `InitRamScript_NoObjectEvent` — store a player-context RAM script (Wonder Card / e-Reader)

#### Notes
- The TS port uses structured script objects (generated from `.inc` files) rather than raw GBA bytecode, so `ScriptReadHalfword`/`ScriptReadWord` have no direct equivalent — the parser handles this at generation time.
- `SCRIPT_MODE_NATIVE` has no TS analogue; the TS `ScriptRunner` uses async generator / yield patterns instead of a C function pointer callback.
- RAM script system (`RamScript`, `gSaveBlock1Ptr->ramScript`) is entirely missing — this covers Mystery Gift / e-Reader / Wonder Card injected scripts. Intentional omission for now (post-game / link features).
- The global vs. immediate context distinction (`sGlobalScriptContext` / `sImmediateScriptContext`) is architecturally replicated: `ScriptRunner` for async field scripts, synchronous execution in `runMapEntryScripts.ts` for load/transition hooks.

- [x] `ScriptMenu_YesNo` — covered by `DialogContext.showYesNo`
- [x] `Task_HandleYesNoInput` — async resolution handled in dialog system
- [x] `Task_HandleMultichoiceInput` — async menu input handled in ScriptMenuHost/OptionMenu
- [x] `GetLilycoveSSTidalSelection` — partial: selection index remapping present in scripting specials
- [x] `CreateWindowFromRect` / `ClearToTransparentAndRemoveWindow` — UI lifecycle managed by React/DialogContext; no direct equivalent needed
- [x] `DisplayTextAndGetWidth` / `ConvertPixelWidthToTileWidth` / `ScriptMenu_AdjustLeftCoordFromWidth` — layout arithmetic handled internally by OptionMenu/dialog rendering; no standalone TS equivalent needed

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `ScriptMenu_MultichoiceGrid` — grid-layout multichoice not implemented; standard multichoice used as fallback
- [-] `CreateLilycoveSSTidalMultichoice` — SS Tidal destination list logic (ticket/flag checks for Southern Island, Navel Rock, Birth Island, Faraway Island) not fully ported; basic Slateport/Battle Frontier routing may work but ticket-gated islands are absent
- [-] `ScriptMenu_CreatePCMultichoice` — PC multichoice (Someone's PC / Lanette's PC / Player's PC / Hall of Fame) not fully ported; no Hall of Fame entry, Lanette flag check for name swap absent
- [-] `Task_HandleMultichoiceGridInput` — no grid input handler; blocked by missing `ScriptMenu_MultichoiceGrid`

- [ ] `ScriptMenu_ShowPokemonPic` / `ScriptMenu_HidePokemonPic` / `Task_PokemonPicWindow` — in-menu Pokémon sprite display box (used by move reminder, move deleter, etc.); no TS equivalent
- [ ] `ScriptMenu_DisplayPCStartupPrompt` — "Which PC should be accessed?" message box shown before PC multichoice

- [ ] `DrawLinkServicesMultichoiceMenu` — description text shown below link service multichoice options (wireless/cable club service descriptions); link services intentionally omitted but description rendering is absent even for non-link uses
- [ ] `InitMultichoiceCheckWrap` / `InitMultichoiceNoWrap` — wrap/no-wrap cursor behavior distinction not implemented; TS menus don't differentiate wrap vs. no-wrap based on count

#### Notes
- Link cable / wireless adapter multichoice options (`MULTI_WIRELESS_*`, `MULTI_CABLE_CLUB_*`) are intentionally omitted — no link play in browser port.
- `sLilycoveSSTidalSelections` state array and the scrollable multichoice path (`ShowScrollableMultichoice` / `SCROLL_MULTI_SS_TIDAL_DESTINATION`) are not ported; the scrollable multichoice variant is a separate system not present in TS.
- `ScriptMenu_ShowPokemonPic` is functionally needed for move reminder NPC and move deleter NPC interactions.
- The `generate-multichoice.cjs` generator covers the static data tables (`sMultichoiceLists` / `sLilycoveSSTidalDestinations` etc.) but not the runtime task logic in this file.

## batch: src/script_movement.c

### `src/script_movement.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts`, `src/game/npc/NPCMovementEngine.ts`, `src/game/ObjectEventManager.ts`
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-scripts.cjs` (movement action constants consumed)

#### Implemented
- [x] `ScriptMovement_StartObjectMovementScript` — dispatching scripted movement to an object event; equivalent logic in `ScriptRunner.ts` `applymovement` command handler
- [x] `ScriptMovement_IsObjectMovementFinished` — polling whether a scripted movement sequence completed; equivalent in `ScriptRunner.ts` `waitmovement` / `checkmovement` handlers
- [x] `ScriptMovement_UnfreezeObjectEvents` — clearing all active scripted movement and unfreezing objects; equivalent cleanup in `ScriptRunner.ts` and `ObjectEventManager`
- [x] `ScriptMovement_TakeStep` — advancing one movement action per frame for an object; equivalent step logic in `NPCMovementEngine.ts` movement tick
- [x] `MOVEMENT_ACTION_STEP_END` sentinel detection — terminating a movement script and freezing the object; handled in `NPCMovementEngine.ts` / `ScriptRunner.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `ScriptMovement_MoveObjects` (task loop) — the C version runs all active movement scripts in a single GBA task each frame; the TS port achieves the same result via `NPCMovementEngine` update loop but does not model the slot-based `gTasks` structure; functionally equivalent but the per-slot bookkeeping differs
- [-] `ScriptMovement_TryAddNewMovement` — slot-reuse logic (reassigning a finished slot to a new object) is not explicitly modeled; TS tracks movements per object-event ID directly in a map, so the concept of numbered movement-script slots is absent

#### Missing
- [ ] `sMovementScripts` slot array (16-slot fixed array indexed by `moveScrId`) — C uses a parallel fixed-size array of pointers; TS uses a dynamic Map keyed by object-event ID with no hard slot limit
- [ ] `GetMovementScriptIdFromObjectEventId` / `SetObjectEventIdAtMovementScript` / `LoadObjectEventIdPtrFromMovementScript` — internal slot-management helpers; no direct TS equivalent (slot concept absent)
- [ ] `ClearMovementScriptFinished` / `SetMovementScriptFinished` / `IsMovementScriptFinished` — bitmask-in-task-data finished tracking; TS uses per-entry promise/callback resolution instead

#### Notes
The C implementation is built on GBA task infrastructure (`CreateTask`, `gTasks`, `FindTaskIdByFunc`) with a fixed 16-slot parallel movement table backed by a bitmask. The TS port achieves the same gameplay behaviour (start movement, step each frame, signal completion, unfreeze) through `NPCMovementEngine` and `ScriptRunner`'s `applymovement`/`waitmovement` commands, but the low-level slot/task machinery is intentionally absent — it has no browser equivalent and the higher-level contract is preserved. The movement action byte-sequence format itself is generated by `scripts/generate-scripts.cjs` (movement tables) and consumed at runtime by `NPCMovementEngine`.

- [x] `HealPlayerParty` — full party heal (HP, PP, status clear); covered by Pokemon Center special in `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts` via `ScriptRunner.pokemonCenterSpecials` tests
- [x] `ScriptGiveMon` — create and give mon to player; partial equivalent in `ScriptRunner.ts` `givemon` command handling
- [x] `ScriptGiveEgg` — create egg and give to player; partial equivalent in `ScriptRunner.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `HasEnoughMonsForDoubleBattle` — checks party state for double battle eligibility; `GetMonsStateToDoubles` logic not fully ported, no `PLAYER_HAS_TWO_USABLE_MONS` enum equivalent found in TS
- [-] `CreateScriptedWildMon` — creates scripted wild encounter mon; `wildEncounterService.ts` handles wild encounters but scripted fixed-species encounters (e.g. legendaries) use different path; `overworldWildEncounterFlow.ts` covers random encounters only

- [ ] `CheckPartyMonHasHeldItem` — scans party for a specific held item; no TS equivalent
- [ ] `DoesPartyHaveEnigmaBerry` — checks for Enigma Berry in held items and populates `gStringVar1`; no TS equivalent
- [ ] `ScriptSetMonMoveSlot` — sets a specific move slot on a party mon by index; no TS equivalent
- [ ] `ChooseHalfPartyForBattle` — launches half-party selection UI for multi battles; no TS equivalent (Battle Frontier not implemented)
- [ ] `CB2_ReturnFromChooseHalfParty` — callback after half-party selection; no TS equivalent
- [ ] `ChoosePartyForBattleFrontier` — launches Battle Frontier party selection UI; no TS equivalent

- [ ] `ReducePlayerPartyToSelectedMons` — trims party to only selected mons in chosen order; no TS equivalent

#### Notes
- Battle Frontier party selection (`ChooseHalfPartyForBattle`, `ChoosePartyForBattleFrontier`, `ReducePlayerPartyToSelectedMons`) is intentionally omitted — Battle Frontier is post-game content not in scope.
- `DoesPartyHaveEnigmaBerry` / `CheckPartyMonHasHeldItem` are small utilities that would be straightforward to add to `BagManager.ts` or a party utility module when needed.
- `ScriptSetMonMoveSlot` is used by event scripts (e.g. Wally tutorial giving Ralts a move); the script runner likely needs this for full story parity.
- `HealPlayerParty` is confirmed tested via `ScriptRunner.pokemonCenterSpecials.test.ts`.

## batch: src/secret_base.c

### `src/secret_base.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `ClearSecretBase` / `ClearSecretBases` — zero-fill one or all secret base save slots
- [ ] `SetCurSecretBaseId` / `TrySetCurSecretBaseIndex` — resolve active base index from ID stored in special var
- [ ] `CheckPlayerHasSecretBase` — checks whether player's base slot is occupied
- [ ] `GetSecretBaseTypeInFrontOfPlayer` — maps metatile behavior in front of player to a base type enum
- [ ] `FindMetatileIdMapCoords` — scans map grid to find coordinates of a specific metatile ID
- [ ] `ToggleSecretBaseEntranceMetatile` — swaps closed↔open entrance metatile in front of player and redraws
- [ ] `SetPlayerSecretBase` — writes player's base ID, trainer ID, name, gender into save slot 0
- [ ] `SetOccupiedSecretBaseEntranceMetatiles` — on map load, opens entrances for any occupied bases on the current map
- [ ] `EnterSecretBase` — fade to black, warp player into their own or a friend's secret base map
- [ ] `EnterNewlyCreatedSecretBase` — warp into the newly created base and place player in front of PC
- [ ] `SecretBaseMapPopupEnabled` — suppresses map name popup on uninitialised secret base maps
- [ ] `CurMapIsSecretBase` — checks whether the current location is one of the 24 secret base maps
- [ ] `InitSecretBaseAppearance` — places decoration metatiles and optionally swaps PC type on map load
- [ ] `InitSecretBaseDecorationSprites` — spawns sprite-type decorations as object events with correct positions/scripts
- [ ] `HideSecretBaseDecorationSprites` — removes all decoration sprite object events and sets their hide flags
- [ ] `SetSecretBaseOwnerGfxId` — sets the owner NPC graphic var based on trainer ID + gender
- [ ] `SetCurSecretBaseIdFromPosition` / `WarpIntoSecretBase` — identify which base entrance the player stepped on and launch entry script
- [ ] `TrySetCurSecretBase` — validates and sets current base from special var, returns occupancy flag
- [ ] `Task_WarpOutOfSecretBase` / `WarpOutOfSecretBase` — fade + dynamic-warp back to field from inside a base
- [ ] `IsCurSecretBaseOwnedByAnotherPlayer` — checks whether active base belongs to someone else
- [ ] `GetSecretBaseMapName` / `CopyCurSecretBaseOwnerName_StrVar1` — string helpers for owner name display
- [ ] `SetPlayerSecretBaseParty` — serialises player's non-egg party into the compact `SecretBaseParty` struct in save
- [ ] `ClearAndLeaveSecretBase` / `MoveOutOfSecretBase` / `MoveOutOfSecretBaseFromOutside` — abandon player's base and warp out
- [ ] `GetNumRegisteredSecretBases` / `GetCurSecretBaseRegistrationValidity` / `ToggleCurSecretBaseRegistry` — registry count and toggle logic
- [ ] `ShowSecretBaseDecorationMenu` / `ShowSecretBaseRegistryMenu` — task entrypoints for the two in-base PC menus
- [ ] `Task_ShowSecretBaseRegistryMenu` + 14 helper tasks (BuildRegistryMenuItems, HandleRegistryMenuInput, ShowRegistryMenuActions, etc.) — full scrollable list menu for viewing/deleting registered friend bases
- [ ] `GetSecretBaseOwnerType` — maps trainer ID byte + gender to one of 10 owner sprite types
- [ ] `GetSecretBaseTrainerLoseText` — returns the appropriate defeat text string for the base owner's type
- [ ] `PrepSecretBaseBattleFlags` / `SetBattledOwnerFromResult` / `GetSecretBaseOwnerAndState` — set up and record outcome of a secret base trainer battle
- [ ] `SecretBasePerStepCallback` — per-step task that tracks which decorations the player interacted with (chairs, boards, tents, slides, balloons, mats, doors) for TV stats
- [ ] `ReceiveSecretBasesData` — record-mixing: merges up to 3 friends' base arrays into save, de-duplicates, re-registers, sorts by registry status
- [ ] `SaveRecordMixBases` + 8 record-mix helpers (TrySaveFriendsSecretBase, SortSecretBasesByRegistryStatus, ClearDuplicateOwnedSecretBases, etc.) — full record-mixing merge algorithm
- [ ] `ClearJapaneseSecretBases` — removes Japanese-language bases (R/S international compat)
- [ ] `InitSecretBaseVars` / `CheckLeftFriendsSecretBase` — initialise/reset secret base game vars on entry/exit
- [ ] `CheckInteractedWithFriends*` (Doll, Cushion, Poster, FurnitureBottom/Middle/Top, SandOrnament) — 8 functions that set TV-stat flags when player touches specific decoration metatiles
- [ ] `WonSecretBaseBattle` / `LostSecretBaseBattle` / `DrewSecretBaseBattle` / `DeclinedSecretBaseBattle` — record battle outcome in `VAR_SECRET_BASE_LOW/HIGH_TV_FLAGS`

#### Notes
Secret bases are entirely absent from the TypeScript port. The feature depends on GBA link-cable record mixing (`ReceiveSecretBasesData`), which has no browser equivalent and should be treated as intentionally omitted for multiplayer aspects. However, single-player portions — entering/exiting a base, rendering decorations, the in-base PC menus, the per-step interaction tracking, and trainer battles — are all gameplay-relevant and currently missing. The `SetOccupiedSecretBaseEntranceMetatiles` hook needs to run during map load (analogous to existing `mapEventLoader.ts` logic) to open entrance metatiles for any registered bases. The decoration sprite system would need to integrate with `ObjectEventManager`. No generator script consumes this file.

### `src/shop.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/BagManager.ts`, `src/game/MoneyManager.ts`, `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts`, `src/menu/components/BagMenu.tsx`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `CreatePokemartMenu` / `CreateDecorationShop1Menu` / `CreateDecorationShop2Menu` — entry points invoked from scripts; the ScriptRunner handles `pokemart` command and delegates to shop logic
- [x] `SetShopItemsForSale` — item list loading from script command arguments (covered in ScriptRunner mart flow)
- [x] `BuyMenuSubtractMoney` / `RemoveMoney` — `MoneyManager` handles deducting funds
- [x] `AddBagItem` — `BagManager.addItem()` covers adding purchased items to bag
- [x] `IsEnoughMoney` — `MoneyManager.hasEnough()` equivalent used before purchase confirmation
- [x] `GetItemPrice` / `GetItemDescription` — covered via generated `src/data/items.ts` and `src/data/itemDescriptions.ts`
- [x] `ClearItemPurchases` / `RecordItemPurchase` — purchase history tracking; partially present in ScriptRunner mart flow for smart shopper TV segment

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `Task_BuyMenu` / `Task_BuyHowManyDialogueInit` / `Task_BuyHowManyDialogueHandleInput` — quantity selection and purchase confirmation flow exists in the scripting mart handler but lacks full GBA-parity UI (quantity picker with D-pad, "in bag" counter display, Premier Ball bonus on 10+ Poké Balls)
- [-] `BuyMenuConfirmPurchase` / `BuyMenuTryMakePurchase` — yes/no confirmation is present via `DialogContext.showYesNo`, but decoration purchase path (`DecorationAdd`) is not implemented
- [-] `Task_ShopMenu` / `CreateShopMenu` — buy/sell/quit top-level menu exists in ScriptRunner mart command but sell flow (`CB2_GoToSellMenu`) is not implemented
- [-] `BuyMenuPrintPriceInList` — price display in item list; basic price shown but PokeNews Slateport discount (`IsPokeNewsActive`) not applied

#### Missing
- [ ] `Task_HandleShopMenuSell` / `CB2_GoToSellMenu` / `CB2_ExitSellMenu` — sell menu entirely absent; selling items not implemented
- [ ] `BuyMenuDrawMapGraphics` / `BuyMenuCollectObjectEventData` / `BuyMenuDrawObjectEvents` / `BuyMenuDrawMapBg` — GBA-specific: renders the live overworld map and NPCs behind the buy menu overlay; no browser equivalent needed (different UI paradigm)
- [ ] `BuyMenuCopyMenuBgToBg1TilemapBuffer` / `BuyMenuDrawMapMetatile` / `BuyMenuDrawMapMetatileLayer` / `BuyMenuCheckForOverlapWithMenuBg` / `BuyMenuCheckIfObjectEventOverlapsMenuBg` — GBA BG layer compositing for shop window; intentionally omitted (browser uses HTML/CSS overlay)
- [ ] `BuyMenuInitBgs` / `BuyMenuDecompressBgGraphics` / `BuyMenuInitWindows` / `VBlankCB_BuyMenu` / `CB2_BuyMenu` / `CB2_InitBuyMenu` — GBA hardware setup; intentionally omitted
- [ ] `BuyMenuAddScrollIndicatorArrows` / `BuyMenuRemoveScrollIndicatorArrows` / `BuyMenuPrintCursor` — GBA scroll arrow sprites; browser scrolling handled natively
- [ ] `BuyMenuAddItemIcon` / `BuyMenuRemoveItemIcon` — item icon sprite management in buy list; item icons not shown in current mart UI
- [ ] `CreateDecorationShop1Menu` / `CreateDecorationShop2Menu` runtime — decoration inventory (`DecorationAdd`, `gDecorations`) not implemented; decoration system absent
- [ ] `Task_ReturnToItemListAfterItemPurchase` Premier Ball bonus — buying 10+ Poké Balls awards a Premier Ball; not implemented
- [ ] `TryPutSmartShopperOnAir` — TV news segment triggered on quit; not implemented
- [ ] `IncrementGameStat(GAME_STAT_SHOPPED)` — game stat tracking on purchase; not implemented

#### Notes
The mart/shop flow is invoked via the `pokemart` script command in `ScriptRunner.ts`. The browser port has a functional buy flow (item list → confirm → deduct money → add to bag) using `DialogContext` menus, but it is significantly simplified compared to the C original. Key missing features: sell menu, decoration shops, quantity picker with proper D-pad input, Premier Ball bonus, and the live map background render behind the shop window (a GBA-specific visual that has no equivalent in the browser UI). The map background rendering functions (`BuyMenuDraw*`) are intentionally omitted as architectural non-equivalents.

## batch: src/siirtc.c

### `src/siirtc.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
*(none — intentionally omitted)*

#### Notes
`siirtc.c` implements the SII S-3511A real-time clock chip driver via GBA GPIO port registers (`0x80000C4`/`0x80000C6`/`0x80000C8`). Every function in this file (`SiiRtcUnprotect`, `SiiRtcProtect`, `SiiRtcProbe`, `SiiRtcReset`, `SiiRtcGetStatus`, `SiiRtcSetStatus`, `SiiRtcGetDateTime`, `SiiRtcSetDateTime`, `SiiRtcGetTime`, `SiiRtcSetTime`, `SiiRtcSetAlarm`, plus static helpers `WriteCommand`, `WriteData`, `ReadData`, `EnableGpioPortRead`, `DisableGpioPortRead`) is pure GBA cartridge hardware I/O with no browser equivalent. The browser port uses `Date` / `Date.now()` natively where calendar time is needed (e.g. berry growth, day/night, daily randomisation). No porting is necessary or possible.

#### Implemented

#### Missing
- [ ] `PlaySlotMachine` / `CB2_SlotMachineSetup` / `CB2_SlotMachine` — entry point, setup loop, main game loop
- [ ] `InitSlotMachine` — initializes all slot machine state (coins, lucky game, reel positions, etc.)
- [ ] `Task_SlotMachine` + all `SlotTask_*` (30 states) — full state machine driving the slot game flow
- [ ] `DrawMachineBias` / `TrySelectBias_Special` / `TrySelectBias_Regular` — per-round bias draw system
- [ ] `ShouldTrySpecialBias` — RNG gate on special bias draw based on bet and machine ID
- [ ] `GetReelTimeDraw` / `GetReelTimeSpinProbability` — ReelTime spin count lottery
- [ ] `ShouldReelTimeMachineExplode` — ReelTime explosion RNG
- [ ] `ReelTimeSpeed` — selects reduced reel speed for ReelTime based on net coin loss and consecutive spins
- [ ] `CheckMatch` / `CheckMatch_CenterRow` / `CheckMatch_TopAndBottom` / `CheckMatch_Diagonals` — match detection across all bet lines
- [ ] `GetMatchFromSymbols` — symbol triple → match type lookup
- [ ] `AwardPayout` / `Task_Payout` / `PayoutTask_*` — coin payout state machine
- [ ] `GetSymbolAtRest` / `GetSymbol` / `GetReelTimeSymbol` — reel position → symbol lookup
- [ ] `AdvanceSlotReel` / `AdvanceSlotReelToNextSymbol` / `AdvanceReeltimeReel` / `AdvanceReeltimeReelToNextSymbol` — reel physics
- [ ] `CreateReelTasks` / `Task_Reel` / `ReelTask_*` (5 states) — per-reel spin/stop state machine
- [ ] `DecideStop_Bias_Reel1` / `DecideStop_Bias_Reel1_Bet1` / `DecideStop_Bias_Reel1_Bet2or3` — biased stop logic reel 1
- [ ] `DecideStop_Bias_Reel2` / `DecideStop_Bias_Reel2_Bet1or2` / `DecideStop_Bias_Reel2_Bet3` — biased stop logic reel 2
- [ ] `DecideStop_Bias_Reel3` / `DecideStop_Bias_Reel3_Bet1or2` / `DecideStop_Bias_Reel3_Bet3` — biased stop logic reel 3
- [ ] `DecideStop_NoBias_Reel1..3` + helpers — no-bias forced-loss stop logic (prevents matches, taunts with 7 mismatches)
- [ ] `MismatchedSyms_77` / `MismatchedSyms_777` / `NeitherMatchNor7Mismatch` — 7-mismatch detection helpers
- [ ] `Task_ReelTime` + all `ReelTime_*` (19 states) — full ReelTime mini-game sequence (window animation, Pikachu, explosion, etc.)
- [ ] `LoadReelTimeWindowTilemap` / `ClearReelTimeWindowTilemap` — ReelTime BG tilemap management
- [ ] `OpenInfoBox` / `Task_InfoBox` + `InfoBox_*` (15 states) — Select-button help screen
- [ ] `CreateDigitalDisplayTask` / `CreateDigitalDisplayScene` / `DestroyDigitalDisplayScene` / `IsDigitalDisplayAnimFinished` — right-panel animated display
- [ ] All `SpriteCB_DigitalDisplay_*` — digital display sprite callbacks (stop, reel, smoke, win/lose, bonus, etc.)
- [ ] `CreatePikaPowerBoltTask` / `AddPikaPowerBolt` / `Task_CreatePikaPowerBolt` / `PikaPowerBolt_*` — Pikachu power meter bolt animation
- [ ] `LoadPikaPowerMeter` / `ResetPikaPowerBolts` — power meter BG tilemap management
- [ ] `FlashMatchLine` / `SpriteCB_FlashMatchingLines` / `IsMatchLineDoneFlashingBeforePayout` / `TryStopMatchLinesFlashing` — match line flash sprites
- [ ] `FlashSlotMachineLights` / `Task_FlashSlotMachineLights` / `TryStopSlotMachineLights` — border light flash on win
- [ ] `LightenBetTiles` / `DarkenBetTiles` / `LightenMatchLine` / `DarkenMatchLine` — bet line palette highlighting
- [ ] `PressStopReelButton` / `Task_PressStopReelButton` / `StopReelButton_*` — reel stop button tilemap press animation
- [ ] All reel/ReelTime sprite create/destroy functions (20+ sprite management functions) — sprite lifecycle for all slot machine actors
- [ ] `SlotMachineSetup_InitBgsWindows` / `_InitVRAM` / `_InitOAM` / `_InitGpuRegs` / `_InitTilemaps` / `_LoadGfxAndTilemaps` — GBA hardware setup (intentionally omitted in browser context)
- [ ] All graphics/tilemap load functions (`LoadMenuGfx`, `LoadSlotMachineGfx`, `LoadReelBackground`, etc.) — GBA VRAM loading (browser uses different asset pipeline)

#### Notes
The slot machine is a complete self-contained GBA mini-game (~2500 lines) with no TypeScript equivalent whatsoever. It involves: a 30-state top-level task machine, three independent reel tasks with biased stopping logic, a ReelTime sub-game with 19 states, animated digital display panels, Pikachu power bolt tracking, match line flashing, payout animation, and an info/help overlay. The bias system is notable — the machine actively manipulates reel stops to favour or suppress matches based on per-machine probability tables. GBA hardware setup (`SetGpuReg`, DMA, VBlank, OAM, BG layer configuration) is intentionally irrelevant for the browser port but the gameplay logic (bias, matching, payout, ReelTime RNG) is fully absent. The Game Corner / slot machine is generally considered a post-game optional feature; it is not on the critical path to the first rival battle.

- [ ] `LoadMenuGfx` — load background tileset and palette for the slot machine menu UI

- [ ] Reel-time symbol strip (`sReelTimeSymbols`) — 6-entry strip for the Reel Time bonus game
- [ ] Initial reel positions (`sInitialReelPositions`) — normal-game vs lucky-game starting offsets per reel

- [ ] Reel Time probability tables (`sReelTimeProbabilities_NormalGame`, `sReelTimeProbabilities_LuckyGame`) — 6-spin × 17-bolt weighted draw tables
- [ ] Reel Time speed / explode probability tables (`sReelTimeExplodeProbability`, `sReelTimeSpeed_Probabilities`, `sQuarterSpeed_ProbabilityBoost`)
- [ ] Bias and match mapping tables (`sBiasSymbols`, `sBiasesSpecial`, `sBiasesRegular`, `sSymbolToMatch`, `sSlotMatchFlags`)
- [ ] Payout table (`sSlotPayouts`) — coin payouts for each match type (Cherry=2 … Red7=300)
- [ ] Digital display scene descriptors (`sDigitalDisplay_InsertBet`, `sDigitalDisplay_StopReel`, etc.) and scene/exit callback dispatch tables
- [ ] Sprite template and subsprite layout data for all slot machine sprites (reels, pikachu, reel-time machine, digital display letters, pokéball, etc.)
- [ ] Match line palette tables (`sLitMatchLinePalTable`, `sDarkMatchLinePalTable`, `sMatchLinePalOffsets`, `sBetToMatchLineIds`, `sMatchLinesPerBet`) — bet-count to lit/unlit row/diagonal palette routing
- [ ] Flashing lights palette animation tables (`sFlashingLightsPalTable`)
- [ ] Pokéball shining palette table (`sPokeballShiningPalTable`)

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] All GBA hardware register writes (`SetGpuReg REG_OFFSET_MOSAIC`, `LoadBgTiles`, `LoadBgTilemap`, `LoadPalette`, `HideBg`, OAM subsprite tables, `LZDecompressWram`) — GBA-specific VRAM/BG/OAM management with no direct browser parallel; rendering would be reimplemented via Canvas/WebGL

#### Notes
`src/slot_machine.c` is the Game Corner slot machine minigame — a self-contained feature with its own state machine, sprite system, RNG/probability logic, and digital display animation system. Nothing in this file has been ported; there is no TypeScript equivalent anywhere in the project. The slot machine is a post-game/optional side activity (Mauville Game Corner), so it is a low-priority feature. The gameplay logic (reel stopping, bias draws, payout calculation, Reel Time bonus round) is fully absent. The GBA-specific graphics loading (LZ decompression, direct BG/OAM register manipulation, subsprite tables) would need to be redesigned for WebGL/Canvas, but the core probability and reel-logic data tables could be ported directly.

### `src/sound.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `InitMapMusic` — initializes map music state, clears disable flag
- [ ] `MapMusicMain` — per-frame state machine driving BGM transitions (play, fade-out, fade-in)
- [ ] `ResetMapMusic` — clears all music state variables
- [ ] `GetCurrentMapMusic` — returns active song number
- [ ] `PlayNewMapMusic` — starts a new map BGM immediately
- [ ] `StopMapMusic` — stops BGM and resets state
- [ ] `FadeOutMapMusic` — fades out current BGM
- [ ] `FadeOutAndPlayNewMapMusic` — fade out then switch to new song
- [ ] `FadeOutAndFadeInNewMapMusic` — fade out then fade in replacement song
- [ ] `IsNotWaitingForBGMStop` — returns false while in fade-out/transition states
- [ ] `PlayFanfare` — plays a fanfare stinger by song ID, creates countdown task
- [ ] `PlayFanfareByFanfareNum` — plays fanfare by index into sFanfares table
- [ ] `WaitFanfare` — decrements fanfare counter, optionally resumes BGM when done
- [ ] `IsFanfareTaskInactive` — checks whether the fanfare task is running
- [ ] `Task_Fanfare` — per-frame task that waits for fanfare duration then resumes BGM
- [ ] `PlayBGM` — starts a BGM song (respects gDisableMusic)
- [ ] `FadeInNewBGM` — starts song at volume 0 and fades in
- [ ] `FadeOutBGMTemporarily` — temporarily reduces BGM volume
- [ ] `FadeInBGM` — fades BGM back in from current volume
- [ ] `FadeOutBGM` — fades out BGM
- [ ] `IsBGMStopped` — returns true when BGM track is not playing
- [ ] `IsBGMPausedOrStopped` — returns true when BGM is paused or stopped
- [ ] `IsBGMPlaying` — returns true when BGM is actively playing
- [ ] `PlaySE` — plays a sound effect
- [ ] `PlaySE12WithPanning` — plays SE on channels 1+2 with panning
- [ ] `PlaySE1WithPanning` — plays SE on channel 1 with panning
- [ ] `PlaySE2WithPanning` — plays SE on channel 2 with panning
- [ ] `SE12PanpotControl` — adjusts panning on SE channels 1+2
- [ ] `IsSEPlaying` — checks if any SE channel is active
- [ ] `IsSpecialSEPlaying` — checks if SE channel 3 is active
- [ ] `PlayCryInternal` — core Pokémon cry playback with mode-based parameter overrides
- [ ] `PlayCry_Normal` — plays cry with BGM ducking and volume restore
- [ ] `PlayCry_NormalNoDucking` — plays cry without ducking BGM
- [ ] `PlayCry_ByMode` — plays cry in specified mode, with conditional ducking
- [ ] `PlayCry_ReleaseDouble` — plays cry for multi-release scenario in battle
- [ ] `PlayCry_DuckNoRestore` — ducks BGM for cry but does not schedule restore
- [ ] `PlayCry_Script` — plays cry triggered from a field script
- [ ] `IsCryFinished` — returns true when cry ducking task is done and clears cry songs
- [ ] `IsCryPlaying` — returns true while Pokémon cry audio is active
- [ ] `IsCryPlayingOrClearCrySongs` — checks cry playing, clears if not
- [ ] `StopCry` — stops Pokémon cry channel
- [ ] `StopCryAndClearCrySongs` — stops cry and clears cry song state
- [ ] `Task_DuckBGMForPokemonCry` — per-frame task that restores BGM volume after cry finishes
- [ ] `RestoreBGMVolumeAfterPokemonCry` — creates the BGM-restore task if not already running

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] `FadeInNewBGM` (m4a layer calls) — direct GBA M4A audio engine calls; browser audio uses Web Audio API instead
- [/] `PlayCryInternal` (tone table indexing) — GBA-specific `gCryTable`/`gCryTable_Reverse` ToneData structures with hardware MIDI player; no browser equivalent

#### Notes
Audio is entirely absent from the TypeScript port. The `src/sound.c` file is the sole owner of all BGM, fanfare, SE, and Pokémon cry playback in the C codebase. There is no audio subsystem in the TS project at all — no Web Audio API wrapper, no music state machine, no sound effect dispatcher. The map music state machine (`MapMusicMain`, states 0–7) would need a full TypeScript reimplementation. Fanfare durations in `sFanfares` (frame counts at ~60fps) are browser-portable as millisecond equivalents. Pokémon cry modes (`CRY_MODE_*`) encode pitch/length/chorus parameters — these would need mapping to Web Audio API `AudioBufferSourceNode` playback rate and filters. BGM ducking for cries (85% volume during cry, restore after) is a gameplay-relevant audio feature for battle polish. The GBA M4A engine calls (`m4aSongNumStart`, `m4aMPlayFadeOut`, etc.) have no browser equivalent and would be replaced wholesale by a Web Audio API layer.

## batch: src/sprite.c

### `src/sprite.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/rendering/SpriteBatcher.ts`, `src/game/npc/ObjectEventAffineManager.ts`, `src/rendering/webgl/WebGLSpriteRenderer.ts`, `src/rendering/spriteUtils.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `AnimateSprite` / `BeginAnim` / `ContinueAnim` / `AnimCmd_frame` / `AnimCmd_end` / `AnimCmd_jump` / `AnimCmd_loop` — sprite frame animation sequencing is handled in `src/game/npc/NPCAnimationEngine.ts` and `src/rendering/spriteUtils.ts`
- [x] `CalcCenterToCornerVec` — sprite dimension/offset lookup partially replicated in `spriteUtils.ts` via `sOamDimensions`-equivalent tables
- [x] `SortSprites` — sprite priority/subpriority sorting logic is handled in `src/rendering/SpriteBatcher.ts` and `src/rendering/overworld/renderOverworldSprites.ts`
- [x] `SetSpriteOamFlipBits` — hFlip/vFlip bitmask logic replicated in `spriteUtils.ts`
- [x] `StartSpriteAnim` / `StartSpriteAnimIfDifferent` — animation restart logic exists in `NPCAnimationEngine.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] Affine animation system (`BeginAffineAnim`, `ContinueAffineAnim`, `AffineAnimCmd_*`, `ApplyAffineAnimFrame*`, `ConvertScaleParam`, `SetOamMatrixRotationScaling`) — partial equivalent in `src/game/npc/ObjectEventAffineManager.ts` but the full per-matrix state machine (`sAffineAnimStates`, loop/jump commands) is not completely replicated
- [-] `AddSpriteToOamBuffer` / `AddSubspritesToOamBuffer` — subsprite table support is partially present in `src/rendering/SpriteBatcher.ts` for large objects, but the full subsprite flip/priority logic is not fully mirrored
- [-] Sprite tile allocation (`AllocSpriteTiles`, `AllocSpriteTileRange`, `FreeSpriteTilesByTag`, `FreeSpriteTileRanges`, `GetSpriteTileStartByTag`, `IndexOfSpriteTileTag`) — WebGL texture/tile management is handled differently in `src/rendering/webgl/WebGLTextureManager.ts` and `TilesetUploader.ts`; tag-based bitmap allocator is not directly ported
- [-] Sprite palette management (`LoadSpritePalette`, `LoadSpritePalettes`, `FreeSpritePaletteByTag`, `AllocSpritePalette`, `IndexOfSpritePaletteTag`) — palette slots managed via `WebGLTextureManager.ts` but not as a 16-slot tag array

#### Missing
- [ ] `BuildOamBuffer` / `UpdateOamCoords` / `BuildSpritePriorities` / `CopyMatricesToOamBuffer` / `AddSpritesToOamBuffer` — OAM buffer build pipeline; no direct equivalent (browser renders via WebGL draw calls, not OAM)
- [ ] `SetSpriteMatrixAnchor` / `UpdateSpriteMatrixAnchorPos` / `GetAnchorCoord` — anchor-based scale-position compensation for affine sprites (used by minigame countdown)
- [ ] `SeekSpriteAnim` — seek to specific animation command index mid-sequence
- [ ] `ChangeSpriteAffineAnim` / `ChangeSpriteAffineAnimIfDifferent` — switch affine anim without full reset
- [ ] `SpriteTileAllocBitmapOp` — raw bitmap operation on tile allocator
- [ ] `ProcessSpriteCopyRequests` / `RequestSpriteCopy` / `RequestSpriteFrameImageCopy` — VRAM DMA copy request queue
- [ ] `CopyFromSprites` / `CopyToSprites` — bulk sprite state serialization (used by save/link)
- [ ] `LoadOam` — DMA flush to OAM register
- [ ] `ResetOamRange` — zero OAM entries by range
- [ ] `SetSubspriteTables` — assign subsprite table to sprite

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] `LoadOam` — writes directly to GBA OAM hardware register `(void *)OAM`
- [/] `CpuCopy16` / `CpuCopy32` calls — GBA DMA/CPU copy intrinsics for VRAM
- [/] `OBJ_VRAM0` tile addressing — GBA video memory layout; WebGL uses texture atlases instead
- [/] `ObjAffineSet` — GBA BIOS affine matrix computation call
- [/] `EWRAM_DATA` / `COMMON_DATA` / `iwram bss` — GBA memory section placement attributes
- [/] `sSpriteCopyRequests` queue — VRAM upload batching needed only on GBA; browser GPU upload is immediate
- [/] `gOamLimit` / OAM slot counting — GBA has a hard 128-sprite OAM limit; irrelevant in browser rendering

#### Notes
The GBA sprite system is fundamentally an OAM-driven hardware abstraction. The TypeScript port replaces the entire pipeline with WebGL sprite batching (`SpriteBatcher.ts`, `WebGLSpriteRenderer.ts`). The animation state machine concepts (frame sequences, loop/jump commands, delay counters) are partially replicated in `NPCAnimationEngine.ts` for NPC walking cycles. The affine animation system (rotation/scaling via matrix states) is partially present in `ObjectEventAffineManager.ts` for spinning/scaling object events. Full GBA OAM buffer management, VRAM tile allocation bitmaps, and DMA copy requests have no browser equivalent and are intentionally omitted. Subsprite tables (used for large multi-OAM objects) are handled differently via large object graphics in `src/data/largeObjectGraphics.gen.ts` and `src/game/DynamicObjectGfx.ts`.

- [ ] `SaveForBattleTowerLink` / `Task_WaitForBattleTowerLinkSave` — Battle Tower link save

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] `VBlankCB_LinkBattleSave` — GBA VBlank interrupt callback for palette transfer during link save
- [/] `InitSaveWindowAfterLinkBattle` — reinitializes GBA BG/sprite/DMA/VRAM state before link battle save screen
- [/] `CB2_SaveAfterLinkBattle` — GBA main loop callback running tasks + palette fade for link battle save

#### Notes
- The TS `StartMenu.tsx` implements the normal gameplay menu flow adequately. All frontier, link, Safari Zone, and Union Room context variants are missing — these are post-game or multiplayer features currently out of scope.
- The save info panel (player name, badge count, Pokédex count, play time displayed before confirming save) is not rendered in the TS UI.
- Script-triggered `SaveGame()` (called by `special SaveGame` in `.inc` scripts) is not yet wired through `ScriptRunner`; the save manager exists but the script integration path is incomplete.
- Link cable / wireless functions (`Task_LinkFullSave`, `Link_AnyPartnersPlayingFRLG_JP`, etc.) have no TS equivalent and are intentionally omitted.

## batch: src/starter_choose.c

### `src/starter_choose.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/states/BirchSpeechState.ts`, `src/game/NewGameFlow.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `GetStarterPokemon` — maps choice index (0–2) to species ID (Treecko/Torchic/Mudkip); species constants exist in `src/data/species.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `Task_StarterChoose` / `Task_HandleStarterChooseInput` — starter selection flow exists in `NewGameFlow.ts` / `BirchSpeechState.ts` but the full 3-pokéball cursor UI with left/right navigation is not implemented as a standalone screen; selection is handled via scripted dialog flow
- [-] `Task_AskConfirmStarter` / `Task_HandleConfirmStarterInput` — yes/no confirmation exists via `DialogContext` `showYesNo`, but tied to script flow rather than a dedicated task state machine
- [-] `CreateStarterPokemonLabel` — species name display exists in dialog/party menus but the floating label with category text positioned above each pokéball is not rendered

#### Missing
- [ ] `CB2_ChooseStarter` / `CB2_StarterChoose` — dedicated GBA-style fullscreen starter selection screen with Birch bag + grass background, three pokéball sprites, and selection hand cursor
- [ ] `SpriteCB_SelectionHand` — sinusoidal floating cursor above selected pokéball
- [ ] `SpriteCB_Pokeball` — pokéball spin animation when selected
- [ ] `SpriteCB_StarterPokemon` — pokémon sprite slides to center-top on selection
- [ ] `Task_WaitForStarterSprite` — waits for affine zoom + slide animation to complete before prompting
- [ ] `Task_MoveStarterChooseCursor` / `Task_CreateStarterLabel` — cursor movement with label refresh
- [ ] `Task_DeclineStarter` — returns to label display after declining
- [ ] `CreatePokemonFrontSprite` — affine-scaled front sprite at pokéball position
- [ ] `ClearStarterLabel` — removes name/category label window

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] `VblankCB_StarterChoose` — GBA VBlank OAM/palette DMA transfers
- [/] `CB2_ChooseStarter` hardware init — `SetGpuReg`, `DmaFill`, `LZ77UnCompVram`, BG layer setup, `BLDCNT` darken effect, `WIN0` clipping region for label highlight — all GBA register writes with no browser equivalent
- [/] `sOam_Hand` / `sOam_Pokeball` / `sOam_StarterCircle` / affine anim tables — GBA OAM/affine animation data structures
- [/] `sBgTemplates` / `sWindowTemplates` / `sWindowTemplate_ConfirmStarter` / `sWindowTemplate_StarterLabel` — GBA BG and window system configuration

#### Notes
The TypeScript port handles starter selection through the scripted new-game flow (`NewGameFlow.ts` + `BirchSpeechState.ts`) using dialog choices rather than a standalone animated selection screen. The full GBA presentation — three pokéballs at fixed screen coordinates, an animated hand cursor, affine-zoom-in pokémon sprite, per-starter floating name/category label with WIN0 highlight, and darken blend on unselected elements — has not been ported. `sStarterMon[]` species constants (Treecko=277, Torchic=255, Mudkip=258) are implicitly available via `src/data/species.ts` but the array itself is not explicitly declared in TS. The `gSpecialVar_Result` write on confirm maps to the game variable/flag system in `src/game/GameVariables.ts`.

## batch: src/string_util.c

### `src/string_util.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/save/native/Gen3Charset.ts`, `src/scripting/ScriptRunner.ts` (placeholder expansion), `src/core/prompt/PromptPrinterEngine.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `StringCopy_Nickname` / `StringGet_Nickname` — Gen3 EOS-terminated string copy/scan for nicknames; equivalent logic in `Gen3Charset.ts` when decoding mon names
- [x] `StringCopy_PlayerName` — player name copy with length limit; handled in `Gen3SaveParser.ts` / `Gen3Charset.ts`
- [x] `StringExpandPlaceholders` — placeholder substitution (`{PLAYER}`, `{STR_VAR_1}`, etc.); ported in `ScriptRunner.ts` text expansion and `PromptPrinterEngine.ts`
- [x] `GetExpandedPlaceholder` (PLAYER, STRING_VAR_1/2/3, RIVAL, VERSION, AQUA/MAGMA/ARCHIE/MAXIE/KYOGRE/GROUDON) — all named placeholders resolved in the script runner's text interpolation layer

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `ConvertIntToDecimalStringN` / `ConvertUIntToDecimalStringN` — numeric formatting with leading-zeros / right-align / space-pad modes used in battle HUD and dialog; partial equivalent in `PromptPrinterEngine.ts` for rendering numbers, but the STR_CONV_MODE_RIGHT_ALIGN spacer character and GBA charset encoding are not fully replicated
- [-] `StringBraille` — Braille font encoding for Sealed Chamber; no Braille rendering in TS, but the text content is present in generated scripts; rendering not needed for gameplay
- [-] `StripExtCtrlCodes` / `GetExtCtrlCodeLength` — control-code stripping used when formatting strings for display; partial equivalent in `PromptPrinterEngine.ts` which parses control codes, but stripping (in-place mutation) is not directly ported

#### Missing
- [ ] `ConvertIntToHexStringN` — hex string formatting with configurable width/mode; no TS equivalent
- [ ] `StringCompareWithoutExtCtrlCodes` — comparison that skips embedded control codes; no TS equivalent
- [ ] `ConvertInternationalString` — wraps a string in JPN/ENG control codes for language switching; no TS equivalent (EN-only port)
- [ ] `IsStringJapanese` / `IsStringNJapanese` — detects Japanese character range; no TS equivalent
- [ ] `WriteColorChangeControlCode` — emits color/shadow/highlight control byte sequences; no TS equivalent
- [ ] `StringCopyN_Multibyte` / `StringLength_Multibyte` — multi-byte character handling (CHAR_EXTRA_SYMBOL two-byte chars); no TS equivalent
- [ ] `StringCopyPadded` / `StringFill` / `StringFillWithTerminator` — fixed-width padding helpers; no TS equivalent
- [ ] `StringCopy` / `StringAppend` / `StringCopyN` / `StringAppendN` / `StringLength` / `StringCompare` / `StringCompareN` / `IsStringLengthAtLeast` — basic GBA string primitives operating on `u8*` EOS-terminated buffers; JS native strings are used throughout instead

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] `gStringVar1/2/3/4` / `sUnknownStringVar` — EWRAM global string scratch buffers; JS uses local variables / ScriptRunner state object fields instead
- [/] `sDigits[]` / `sPowersOfTen[]` — lookup tables for the custom GBA number-to-charset conversion; JS uses `String()` / `toString()`

#### Notes
The port does not use GBA charset (Gen 3 encoded bytes) at runtime — all in-game text is decoded to UTF-8 at load time via `Gen3Charset.ts`. As a result, the low-level `u8*`-buffer string primitives (`StringCopy`, `StringAppend`, etc.) have no runtime role; JS string operations replace them entirely. Placeholder expansion is the one area with a real TS equivalent, implemented inside `ScriptRunner.ts` when interpolating `{PLAYER}`, `{STR_VAR_1}` etc. into dialog text. The numeric formatting functions (`ConvertIntToDecimalStringN` with its spacer/leading-zero modes) are relevant for battle HUD money/stat display but are not fully ported with GBA-accurate padding behavior.

- [x] Item pocket names (`gText_ItemsPocket`, `gText_PokeBallsPocket`, etc.) — present in `src/game/BagManager.ts` / bag menu

- [ ] Berry Crush / Pokémon Jump / Dodrio Berry Picking strings — link minigames not implemented
- [ ] Wonder Card / Mystery Gift strings — Mystery Gift not implemented
- [ ] Record mixing strings — link record mixing not implemented
- [ ] Trainer Hill time board strings (`gText_TimeBoard`, `gText_TrainerHill1F`, etc.) — time display exists partially
- [ ] `gText_SafariBallStock` — Safari Zone not implemented
- [ ] Move Deleter/Relearner strings (`gText_MoveRelearner*`) — move relearner NPC not implemented

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] `gText_BatteryRunDry` — GBA internal battery; no browser equivalent
- [/] `gJPText_No1MSubCircuit` — GBA 1M sub-circuit hardware check; no browser equivalent
- [/] Link cable / wireless adapter error strings (`gText_WirelessNotConnected`, `gText_MysteryGiftCantUse`, `gText_CommErrorCheckConnections`, etc.) — GBA link hardware; no browser equivalent
- [/] `gText_SavingDontTurnOff*` — GBA flash write warnings; browser autosave handles differently
- [/] `gText_DontRemoveCableTurnOff` — GBA link cable hardware warning

#### Notes
`src/strings.c` is a monolithic string table covering virtually every system in the game. The TS port does not have a centralized equivalent — strings are inlined at point of use or defined in relevant modules. The implemented fraction covers only the systems that have been ported (battle, menus, items, save). The vast majority of strings belong to unimplemented systems (contests, PokéNav, PC box, Battle Frontier, link features, Pokédex UI) and are simply absent. No generator script targets this file directly; `generate-item-descriptions.cjs` covers a subset. The `gText_SaveFileCorrupted`/`gText_SaveFileErased` messaging is present but uses browser-adapted wording rather than exact GBA strings.

## batch: src/task.c

### `src/task.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `ResetTasks` — initializes the priority-sorted doubly-linked task list
- [ ] `CreateTask` — allocates a free slot, inserts task into sorted list by priority
- [ ] `InsertTask` — inserts new task into sorted linked list by priority
- [ ] `DestroyTask` — marks task inactive, unlinks from priority list
- [ ] `RunTasks` — iterates active tasks in priority order and calls each func
- [ ] `FindFirstActiveTask` — finds head of active task chain
- [ ] `TaskDummy` — no-op task callback
- [ ] `SetTaskFuncWithFollowupFunc` — sets task func and stores followup func pointer in data slots
- [ ] `SwitchTaskToFollowupFunc` — restores stored followup func as current task func
- [ ] `FuncIsActiveTask` — checks if any active task uses a given func pointer
- [ ] `FindTaskIdByFunc` — finds task slot index by func pointer
- [ ] `GetTaskCount` — counts all active tasks
- [ ] `SetWordTaskArg` / `GetWordTaskArg` — pack/unpack 32-bit values across two 16-bit data slots

#### No good TS-equivalent
*(none)*

#### Notes
The GBA task system is a priority-sorted doubly-linked list of up to `NUM_TASKS` (16) slots, each holding a function pointer and 16 half-words of scratch data. The TS port replaces this entirely with React hooks, `GameLoop.ts`, `UpdateCoordinator.ts`, and `AnimationTimer.ts` — a frame-callback scheduler rather than a named-task system. The `SetTaskFuncWithFollowupFunc`/`SwitchTaskToFollowupFunc` pattern (storing a function pointer split across two `s16` data slots) is GBA-specific and has no direct analogue; TS state machines (e.g. `GameStateManager`, `ScriptRunner` continuation passing) cover the same use cases architecturally. None of the C task API is exposed as hand-ported TypeScript.

## batch: src/text.c

### `src/text.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/core/prompt/PromptPrinterEngine.ts`, `src/core/prompt/PromptCanvasRenderer.ts`, `src/core/prompt/PromptWindowProfiles.ts`, `src/core/prompt/textLayout.ts`, `src/core/prompt/textSpeed.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `AddTextPrinter` / `AddTextPrinterParameterized` — text printer initialization and immediate-render path covered by `PromptPrinterEngine` + `PromptController`
- [x] `RunTextPrinters` — per-frame text advancement loop covered by `PromptPrinterEngine.tick()`
- [x] `IsTextPrinterActive` — active-printer check covered by prompt state tracking
- [x] `TextPrinterWaitWithDownArrow` / `TextPrinterDrawDownArrow` / `TextPrinterClearDownArrow` — down-arrow animation covered by `PromptCanvasRenderer` down-arrow rendering
- [x] `TextPrinterInitDownArrowCounters` — arrow counter init covered by prompt state reset
- [x] `TextPrinterWait` / `TextPrinterWaitAutoMode` — wait-for-input and auto-scroll delay covered by prompt await logic
- [x] `GetStringWidth` — string width measurement covered by `src/core/prompt/textLayout.ts`
- [x] `GetFontAttribute` — font metric lookup covered by `PromptWindowProfiles` / `PromptWindowSkin` font descriptors
- [x] `SetDefaultFontsPointer` / `SetFontsPointer` — font registry covered by static font profile tables in TS
- [x] `DeactivateAllTextPrinters` — clearing all printers covered by prompt reset/teardown
- [x] Text speed constants (`sWindowVerticalScrollSpeeds`) — covered by `src/core/prompt/textSpeed.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `RenderText` (the big state-machine) — core character-decode loop (CHAR_NEWLINE, EOS, CHAR_PROMPT_CLEAR, CHAR_PROMPT_SCROLL, delays, pause states) is covered; `EXT_CTRL_CODE_PLAY_BGM`, `EXT_CTRL_CODE_PLAY_SE`, `EXT_CTRL_CODE_WAIT_SE` sound hooks are stubs or absent since audio is not yet implemented; `EXT_CTRL_CODE_PAUSE_MUSIC` / `EXT_CTRL_CODE_RESUME_MUSIC` have no audio equivalent
- [-] `DrawDownArrow` (standalone form used outside text printers) — the prompt canvas handles the common case but the standalone version with external counter/yCoordIndex pointers has no direct TS counterpart

#### Missing
- [ ] `GenerateFontHalfRowLookupTable` — GBA 4bpp palette lookup table generation for blitting glyphs; no TS equivalent needed for canvas rendering but color-slot mapping (fg/bg/shadow) is not explicitly replicated
- [ ] `DecompressGlyphTile` — GBA 4bpp tile decompressor using the half-row lookup table; not needed (fonts rendered via Canvas 2D / CSS)
- [ ] `CopyGlyphToWindow` / `GLYPH_COPY` — blit decompressed glyph pixels into the GBA window tile buffer; not needed
- [ ] `ClearTextSpan` — fill a horizontal span with bg color in tile buffer; not needed
- [ ] `DecompressGlyph_Small/Normal/Short/Narrow/SmallNarrow/Bold` — per-font glyph decoders; not needed
- [ ] `GetGlyphWidth_Small/Normal/Short/Narrow/SmallNarrow` — per-font per-glyph width lookup from binary glyph tables; superseded by canvas `measureText` / TS glyph width tables in `textLayout.ts`
- [ ] `RenderTextHandleBold` — renders a string to a raw pixel buffer using bold/normal glyphs (used for battle HP bars etc.); no direct TS equivalent
- [ ] `DrawKeypadIcon` / `GetKeypadIconTileOffset` / `GetKeypadIconWidth` / `GetKeypadIconHeight` — GBA keypad icon blitting; not ported (button icons rendered differently in TS UI)
- [ ] `GetMenuCursorDimensionByFont` — returns tile dimensions for menu cursor per font; no TS equivalent
- [ ] `GetStringWidthFixedWidthFont` (UNUSED) — fixed-width string measurement; not ported
- [ ] `SaveTextColors` / `RestoreTextColors` — save/restore fg/bg/shadow color globals; not needed in TS (colors are part of printer state objects)

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] `GenerateFontHalfRowLookupTable` — GBA-specific 4bpp palette index encoding for blitting; browser canvas uses CSS colors directly
- [/] `DecompressGlyphTile` / `CopyGlyphToWindow` / `GLYPH_COPY` / `ClearTextSpan` — GBA tile VRAM blit pipeline; entire pipeline replaced by Canvas 2D drawImage/fillText
- [/] `DecompressGlyph_*` family — GBA binary font decompression into VRAM tile format; not applicable to browser
- [/] `DrawKeypadIcon` — blits GBA keypad icon tiles to a VRAM window; browser uses image/SVG assets
- [/] `RenderTextHandleBold` — renders directly to a raw 4bpp pixel buffer for GBA hardware; no browser equivalent

#### Notes
The TS port replaces the GBA window/tile/VRAM text pipeline entirely with a Canvas 2D prompt system (`src/core/prompt/`). The logical behavior — character-by-character printing, scroll/clear prompts, down-arrow animation, text speed, wait-for-input — is faithfully ported. The low-level glyph decompression, tile blitting, and palette lookup machinery is intentionally omitted as it is GBA-hardware-specific. Sound effect hooks (`EXT_CTRL_CODE_PLAY_SE`, `EXT_CTRL_CODE_WAIT_SE`, `EXT_CTRL_CODE_PLAY_BGM`, etc.) are not yet implemented since audio is missing from the port. The multiple font IDs (SMALL, NORMAL, SHORT, NARROW, BOLD, etc.) map to different `PromptWindowProfile` / `PromptWindowSkin` configurations rather than a runtime font-registry lookup.

The TS port replaces the GBA tile-based text window system with HTML/Canvas rendering. The 20 selectable window frame types from `gSaveBlock2Ptr->optionsWindowFrameType` are not implemented — the port uses a single hardcoded frame style. The core 9-slice border layout logic is present in `PromptCanvasRenderer` but is not data-driven from the original `.4bpp` assets. Palette handling is entirely absent as a runtime concern.

- [-] `TilesetAnim_EverGrande` / `QueueAnimTiles_EverGrande_Flowers` — Same 8-column stagger pattern as Mauville/Rustboro

#### Missing
- [ ] `InitTilesetAnim_BattleDome` / `TilesetAnim_BattleDome` / `BlendAnimPalette_BattleDome_FloorLights` — palette blending animation that also detects an in-progress battle transition task and switches to a no-blend variant; no palette-blend tileset animation exists in TS
- [ ] `BlendAnimPalette_BattleDome_FloorLightsNoBlend` / `TilesetAnim_BattleDome2` — countdown self-disabling palette variant
- [ ] `InitTilesetAnim_Petalburg` / `InitTilesetAnim_Fallarbor` / `InitTilesetAnim_Fortree` / `InitTilesetAnim_Lilycove` / `InitTilesetAnim_Mossdeep` — no-op secondary inits (set counter to primary's counter, callback = NULL); these don't produce animations but ensure secondary counter stays in sync with primary

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] `ResetTilesetAnimBuffer` / `AppendTilesetAnimToBuffer` / `TransferTilesetAnimsBuffer` — GBA DMA3 transfer buffer; the TS renderer uploads tile frames directly to WebGL textures each frame without a hardware DMA queue

#### Notes
- The C implementation uses GBA VRAM addresses (`BG_VRAM + TILE_OFFSET_4BPP(...)`) as destinations; the TS port uses named animation slots tied to tileset IDs and tile indices instead. The mapping of VRAM offsets → tile indices is implicit in the C code (e.g. tile 432 = water in general tileset) and must be replicated correctly in `tilesetAnimations.ts`.
- `gTilesetAnims_Sootopolis_StormyWater` uses paired `_kyogre`/`_groudon` frames concatenated per frame (96 tiles total, double-wide for the two weather states); this detail needs verification in the TS asset loading.
- `InitSecondaryTilesetAnimation` (public re-init for secondary only) is a distinct entry point used on map re-entry; confirm `loadTilesetAnimations` handles the same case.
- Battle Dome floor lights are the only animation relying on `gPaletteFade` state and battle transition task detection — this is fundamentally a battle-transition-aware palette animation with no direct TS analogue yet.

## batch: src/tilesets.c

### `src/tilesets.c`
**Coverage**: Full
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/utils/tilesetUtils.ts`, `src/game/loadTilesetAnimations.ts`, `src/data/tilesetAnimations.ts`, `src/rendering/TilesetCanvasCache.ts`
**Generation script**: None (data headers consumed by rendering pipeline; tileset graphics loaded as assets)

#### Implemented
- [x] (data inclusion) `data/tilesets/graphics.h` — tileset pixel data loaded via asset pipeline (`src/utils/assetLoader.ts`, `src/utils/mapLoader.ts`)
- [x] (data inclusion) `data/tilesets/metatiles.h` — metatile attribute data loaded from map JSON assets consumed by `src/utils/tilesetUtils.ts`
- [x] (data inclusion) `data/tilesets/headers.h` — tileset header info (palette slots, is-compressed, secondary flag) reflected in `src/components/map/types.ts` and `src/utils/mapLoader.ts`

#### Notes
`src/tilesets.c` is a pure data-inclusion file — it has no runtime functions of its own; it only `#include`s three data headers that define the `gTilesets_*` arrays. All meaningful content lives in those headers:

- **graphics.h** — raw 4bpp tile graphics; in the TS port these are loaded as PNG spritesheets from `public/` and uploaded to WebGL via `src/rendering/webgl/TilesetUploader.ts`.
- **metatiles.h** — metatile layer/attribute tables; consumed by the map-loading pipeline in `src/utils/mapLoader.ts` and rendered by `src/rendering/webgl/WebGLTileRenderer.ts`.
- **headers.h** — `struct Tileset` descriptors (palette count, secondary flag, animation callbacks); the TS equivalent is the `Tileset` / `TilesetPair` types in `src/components/map/types.ts`, with animation callbacks replaced by the data-driven `src/data/tilesetAnimations.ts` + `src/game/loadTilesetAnimations.ts` system.

No GBA-specific logic (VRAM DMA, OAM, hardware palette registers) is present in this file, so there is nothing to omit.

- [x] `GetMirageRnd` / `SetMirageRnd` — VAR_MIRAGE_RND_H / VAR_MIRAGE_RND_L read/write via `GameVariables` infrastructure
- [x] `IsMirageIslandPresent` — implemented in `src/scripting/specials/legendaryIslandSpecials.ts` (checks party personality words against mirage RNG)
- [x] `WaitWeather` / `Task_WaitWeather` — weather-change completion gating covered by `src/scripting/specials/weatherSpecials.ts` and `WeatherManager`

- [-] `UpdateMirageRnd` — the ISO_RANDOMIZE2 step-by-day logic is referenced in `legendaryIslandSpecials.ts` but real-time day-counting integration (advancing by elapsed days on load) is not wired to an RTC equivalent
- [-] `UpdateShoalTideFlag` — the 24-hour tide table is present in the shoal cave runtime; however `IsMapTypeOutdoors`/`GetLastUsedWarpMapType` guard is not explicitly replicated — the flag is set unconditionally on map entry rather than only when arriving from an outdoor map

#### Missing
- [ ] `InitMirageRnd` — marked unused in C but seeds the mirage RNG; no equivalent initialization call in new-game flow
- [ ] `InitBirchState` / `UpdateBirchState` — VAR_BIRCH_STATE cycling (mod-7 day counter for Birch lab roaming behavior) has no TS equivalent; `BirchSpeechState` does not implement the day-based state variable

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] RTC integration (`RtcCalcLocalTime`, `gLocalTime.hours`) — browser uses `Date` object; the GBA RTC hardware layer is intentionally replaced

#### Notes
- The day-elapsed parameter in `UpdateMirageRnd` and `UpdateBirchState` implies these are called from a daily-tick system (presumably on save load after N days). No equivalent daily-tick driver exists in the TS port yet.
- `FLAG_SYS_SHOAL_TIDE` is generated in `src/data/flagVarMaps.gen.ts`; the runtime set/clear is the only hand-ported piece.

- [ ] `SpriteCB_PressStartCopyrightBanner` — Blinking "Press Start" banner (16-frame on/off toggle)

- [/] `CB2_GoToClearSaveDataScreen` — GBA-specific clear save data screen (hardware flash erase)
- [/] `CB2_GoToResetRtcScreen` — GBA RTC reset screen; no RTC hardware in browser
- [/] `CB2_GoToBerryFixScreen` — GBA-only berry glitch fix program (link cable / e-Reader)
- [/] `ScanlineEffect_InitWave` call — GBA scanline HOFS wave effect on BG1 via DMA; no equivalent in WebGL renderer
- [/] `gTitleScreenAlphaBlend[64]` table — GBA BLDALPHA register values for hardware alpha blend; not needed in browser (CSS/WebGL handle blending)

#### Notes
- The TS title screen (`TitleScreenState.ts`) is a functional stub that handles input and transitions but does not replicate the full 3-phase GBA animation sequence (logo shines, banner slide, BG parallax scroll, Rayquaza silhouette scroll).
- `CB2_GoToCopyrightScreen` (loops back to copyright after BGM ends) is not implemented — the TS version likely just stays on title indefinitely.
- The `gTitleScreenAlphaBlend` table is GBA hardware data with no TS equivalent needed.
- Berry fix program, RTC reset, and link cable features are intentionally omitted as browser-irrelevant.

- [ ] `GetUnionRoomTradeMessageId` — Union Room trade legality check returning specific error message ID
- [ ] `CanRegisterMonForTradingBoard` — validate mon for Union Room trading board registration
- [ ] `CanSpinTradeMon` — validate mon for (unfinished) Spin Trade feature
- [ ] `SetSelectedMon` / `DrawSelectedMonScreen` — animate chosen mon to center; display name and moves panel
- [ ] `PrintPartyNicknames` / `PrintPartyMonNickname` — render nickname text for party slots
- [ ] `PrintPartyLevelsAndGenders` / `PrintLevelAndGender` — render level numbers and gender symbols into tilemap
- [ ] `ComputePartyTradeableFlags` / `ComputePartyHPBarLevels` / `SetTradePartyHPBarSprites` — compute egg/faint/HP bar state for party display
- [ ] `SetActiveMenuOptions` — show/hide party sprite icons based on party counts
- [ ] `TradeMenuMoveCursor` / `GetNewCursorPosition` — cursor navigation using lookup table
- [ ] `QueueAction` / `GetNumQueuedActions` / `DoQueuedActions` — timed message/data action queue
- [ ] `PrintTradeMessage` — render status messages (Standby, Canceled, Only Mon, etc.) to message window

- [ ] `RedrawPartyWindow` / `PrintTradePartnerPartyNicknames` / `RedrawChooseAPokemonWindow` — redraw party panel after returning from sub-screens

- [ ] `GetMonNicknameWidth` — measure nickname pixel width for centering
- [ ] `LoadUISpriteGfx` — load name/cancel/choose text tiles into OBJ VRAM as sprites
- [ ] `LoadTradeBgGfx` — load trade menu BG tiles, tilemaps, palettes
- [ ] `DrawBottomRowText` — render bottom action row text into tile buffer
- [ ] `CB2_LinkTrade` — link trade animation entry: load gfx, sync link, show mon sprites
- [ ] `CB2_InitInGameTrade` — in-game trade animation entry: skip link sync, load mon pics
- [ ] `DoTradeAnim` / `DoTradeAnim_Cable` / `DoTradeAnim_Wireless` — 70+ state animation sequence: slide-in, Poké Ball depart, GBA zoom, crossing scene, receive, fanfare, evolution check
- [ ] `TradeMons` — swap mon structs between player and enemy party, set 70 friendship, apply mail, update Pokédex
- [ ] `UpdatePokedexForReceivedMon` — mark received mon as seen+caught in Pokédex
- [ ] `BufferTradeSceneStrings` — fill gStringVar1-3 with trainer/mon names for trade text
- [ ] `BufferInGameTradeMonName` — buffer in-game trade mon name for post-trade script message
- [ ] `GetInGameTradeMail` — populate Mail struct from InGameTrade data table
- [ ] `CheckPartnersMonForRibbons` — check received mon for special ribbons and award them
- [ ] `CB2_UpdateLinkTrade` / `CB2_WaitTradeComplete` / `CB2_SaveAndEndTrade` / `CB2_SaveAndEndWirelessTrade` / `CB2_FreeTradeAnim` — link trade post-animation: sync finish, save, free memory, return to field
- [ ] `Task_InGameTrade` — task wrapper for in-game trade flow
- [ ] `Task_AnimateWirelessSignal` / `Task_OpenCenterWhiteColumn` / `Task_CloseCenterWhiteColumn` — wireless-specific visual tasks during crossing scene
- [ ] `HandleLinkDataSend` — scheduled link block send with 1-frame delay
- [ ] `TradeAnimInit_LoadGfx` — load battle textbox BG gfx for trade animation window
- [ ] `SetTradeSequenceBgGpuRegs` / `SetTradeBGAffine` / `SetTradeGpuRegs` / `InitTradeSequenceBgGpuRegs` — configure BG modes, affine matrix, scroll regs for each animation phase
- [ ] `LoadTradeSequenceSpriteSheetsAndPalettes` / `LoadTradeMonPic` — load mon sprites and trade-sequence sprite sheets
- [ ] `VBlankCB_TradeMenu` / `VBlankCB_TradeAnim` — VBlank handlers (OAM, palette transfer, BG scroll regs)
- [ ] 8 `SpriteCB_*` functions — sprite animations: link mon glow/shadow, cable end, GBA screen flash, bouncing Poké Ball depart/arrive

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] `SendLinkData` / `RequestLinkData` / `IsLinkTradeTaskFinished` / `_GetBlockReceivedStatus` / `TradeResetReceivedFlags` / `TradeResetReceivedFlag` / `IsWirelessTrade` — GBA SIO/RFU link cable / wireless adapter protocol; no browser equivalent
- [/] `SetTradeLinkStandbyCallback` / `_IsLinkTaskFinished` / `CheckForLinkTimeout` — link state machine helpers tied to GBA link hardware
- [/] `CB2_LinkError` reference — link error callback for hardware disconnect
- [/] `SetTradeBGAffine` / `DoBgAffineSet` calls — GBA affine BG matrix hardware registers for GBA zoom animation
- [/] `VBlankCB_TradeMenu` / `VBlankCB_TradeAnim` — GBA VBlank interrupt handlers
- [/] `Trade_Memcpy` — trivial memcpy wrapper, no equivalent needed

#### Notes
The entire trade system is **not ported**. It falls into two distinct parts:

1. **Trade menu** (`sTradeMenu`): party selection UI with bidirectional link negotiation. This requires a link protocol layer that has no browser equivalent; single-player in-game trades could theoretically be ported but the menu is fully absent.

2. **Trade animation** (`sTradeAnim`): ~70-state GBA-specific visual sequence involving affine BG zoom, GBA console sprites, cable/wireless-specific paths, and sprite animations. The browser port has no visual trade sequence at all.

The **`TradeMons`** data-swap logic (swap party slots, set 70 friendship, apply mail, update Pokédex) is the only gameplay-critical piece and could be ported as a simple utility function. The in-game trade data table (`sIngameTrades`) referenced via `gSpecialVar_0x8004/8005` would be needed for NPC trade scripts — currently missing.

`GetUnionRoomTradeMessageId` and `CanRegisterMonForTradingBoard` are Union Room features that are entirely absent from the TS port.

- [/] `CB2_TryLinkTradeEvolution` — GBA main-callback state machine for link trade evolution sequence
- [/] `HandleLinkDataReceive` — reads link cable block receive buffer for trade handshake commands
- [/] `SpriteCB_BouncingPokeball` — GBA sprite callback: physics sim for trade Pokéball bounce arc
- [/] `SpriteCB_BouncingPokeballDepart` — GBA sprite callback: departure bounce + palette fade
- [/] `SpriteCB_BouncingPokeballDepartEnd` — GBA sprite callback: end of departure animation, triggers fade-out state
- [/] `SpriteCB_BouncingPokeballArrive` — GBA sprite callback: arrival bounce with four sound cues
- [/] `LoadTradeAnimGfx` — loads GBA tile graphics for trade animation
- [/] `DrawTextOnTradeWindow` — draws text into a GBA BG window buffer for the trade UI
- [/] `Task_AnimateWirelessSignal` — animates wireless signal strength palette for wireless trade
- [/] `Task_OpenCenterWhiteColumn` / `Task_CloseCenterWhiteColumn` — GBA WIN0 register animation for wireless trade flash effect
- [/] `CB2_UpdateLinkTrade` — GBA main-callback: drives DoTradeAnim, link data send/receive, mon swap
- [/] `CB2_WaitTradeComplete` — GBA main-callback: synchronizes both players before confirming trade finish
- [/] `CB2_SaveAndEndTrade` — GBA main-callback: multi-stage link-save sequence after cable trade
- [/] `CB2_SaveAndEndWirelessTrade` — GBA main-callback: multi-stage link-save sequence after wireless trade
- [/] `CB2_FreeTradeAnim` — GBA main-callback: frees all BG tile buffers, sprite gfx, and trade anim struct
- [/] `Task_InGameTrade` — GBA task: waits for palette fade then switches to in-game trade callback

#### Notes
`src/trade.c` implements the full GBA link-trade and in-game-trade systems. The link/wireless trade path (`CB2_UpdateLinkTrade`, `CB2_WaitTradeComplete`, `CB2_SaveAndEnd*`, all sprite callbacks, all link-data handlers) is entirely GBA hardware-dependent (SIO, block transfer, OAM, BG windows) and has no browser equivalent.

The in-game trade functions (`GetInGameTradeSpeciesInfo`, `CreateInGameTradePokemonInternal`, `DoInGameTradeScene`, etc.) are gameplay-relevant and are **missing** from the TypeScript port — they would be needed to implement the NPC in-game trades found throughout Hoenn (e.g. Slateport Seedot↔Lotad, etc.). The evolution system invoked post-trade (`GetEvolutionTargetSpecies`, `TradeEvolutionScene`) is partially covered by `src/pokemon/evolution.ts` and `src/evolution/EvolutionRenderer.ts`, but not wired to any trade trigger.

This file implements the **Mauville Old Man Trader** — an Emerald-only NPC who trades decorations with the player. It depends on the decoration system (`decoration.c`, `decoration_inventory.c`), the Mauville Old Man save-block union, and the script-menu/window infrastructure. None of the decoration system, the `MauvilleOldManTrader` save struct, or the decoration category UI exist in the TS port. This is post-game/side-content; the script `special` calls that invoke these functions (e.g. `TraderMenuGetDecoration`, `GetTraderTradedFlag`) would surface as unimplemented `special` stubs in `ScriptRunner.ts` if ever reached.
