- [/] `CB2_TrainerCard` / `CB2_InitTrainerCard` — GBA main-loop callback (`RunTasks`, `AnimateSprites`, `BuildOamBuffer`)
- [/] `CreateTrainerCardTrainerPic` — places trainer OBJ sprite into a BG window tile region (GBA sprite system)
- [/] `SetTrainerCardCb2` / `SetUpTrainerCardTask` / `CloseTrainerCard` — GBA task/callback lifecycle management

#### Notes
The trainer card is entirely absent from the TypeScript port. It is a post-new-game UI feature (accessible from the start menu). The card-flip animation uses GBA scanline effects (per-line BG scroll via HBlank interrupts) which have no direct Web equivalent but could be approximated with CSS 3D transforms or a canvas shader. The link-card exchange (`ShowTrainerCardInLink`, `CopyTrainerCardData`) depends on the link-cable multiplayer system which is intentionally out of scope. The data model (`struct TrainerCard`) would map naturally onto a TypeScript interface reading from the existing `GameFlags`/`GameVariables` stores and `SaveManager`. The star-counting logic (`CountPlayerTrainerStars`) has partial prerequisites already ported (flags system, game stats via `GameFlags`). Badge display relies on `FLAG_BADGE01_GET`…`FLAG_BADGE08_GET` which are tracked in `src/data/flagVarMaps.gen.ts`.

## batch: src/trainer_hill.c

### `src/trainer_hill.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/game/trainerHillRuntime.ts`, `src/game/trainerHillFloorLayout.ts`, `src/game/trainerHillFloorLayoutBlock.ts`
**Generation script**: `scripts/generate-scripts.cjs` (map scripts), data tables in `src/data/trainerHillData.gen.ts`, `src/data/trainerHillParties.gen.ts`

#### Implemented
- [x] `GetCurrentTrainerHillMapId` — maps layout ID to floor enum; ported in `trainerHillRuntime.ts`
- [x] `InTrainerHill` — detects if player is on floors 1F–4F; ported in `trainerHillRuntime.ts`
- [x] `InTrainerHillChallenge` — checks VAR + spokeToOwner + current map; ported in `trainerHillRuntime.ts`
- [x] `GenerateTrainerHillFloorLayout` — dynamically builds per-floor metatile layout from challenge data; ported in `trainerHillFloorLayout.ts`
- [x] `GetMapDataForFloor` — packs metatile+collision+elevation per cell; ported in `trainerHillFloorLayoutBlock.ts`
- [x] `LoadTrainerHillObjectEventTemplates` — spawns NPC trainer object events from floor data; ported in `trainerHillRuntime.ts`
- [x] `GetHillTrainerFlag` / `SetHillTrainerFlag` — per-trainer defeat flags via `frontier.trainerFlags`; ported in `trainerHillRuntime.ts`
- [x] `LocalIdToHillTrainerId` — maps object local ID to trainer ID; ported in `trainerHillRuntime.ts`
- [x] `GetTrainerHillOpponentClass` / `GetTrainerHillTrainerName` — trainer metadata lookups; ported in `trainerHillRuntime.ts`
- [x] `FillHillTrainerParty` / `FillHillTrainersParties` — fills enemy party slots from hill data; ported in `trainerHillRuntime.ts`
- [x] `GetNumFloorsInTrainerHillChallenge` — reads numFloors from challenge struct; ported in `trainerHillRuntime.ts`
- [x] `sTrainerHillData` / `sFloors_*` / `sChallengeData` tables — generated into `src/data/trainerHillData.gen.ts`
- [x] `sTrainerParties` — generated into `src/data/trainerHillParties.gen.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `CallTrainerHillFunction` — dispatch table for `gSpecialVar_0x8004` specials; most sub-functions not implemented (see Missing below); the dispatch mechanism exists in `ScriptRunner.ts` special handling but individual functions are stubs
- [-] `CopyTrainerHillTrainerText` — FrontierSpeech→string conversion referenced but `FrontierSpeechToString` not ported; trainer speech display is wired in scripts but speech decoding is missing
- [-] `InitTrainerHillBattleStruct` / `FreeTrainerHillBattleStruct` — partial: sFloorTrainers name/class copy exists; VBlank counter (`SetTrainerHillVBlankCounter`) is GBA-specific and intentionally omitted
- [-] `GetTrainerHillTrainerFrontSpriteId` — `gFacilityClassToPicIndex` lookup partially available via generated data but not fully wired to front sprite loading
- [-] `SetTrainerHillMonLevel` — level scaling by `GetHighestLevelInPlayerParty` referenced; `CalculateMonStats` exists in `src/pokemon/stats.ts` but level-scaled Trainer Hill party creation is not fully integrated

#### Missing
- [ ] `TrainerHillStartChallenge` — resets timer, flags, spokeToOwner, challenge init
- [ ] `GetOwnerState` — checks spokeToOwner + receivedPrize + checkedFinalTime → `gSpecialVar_Result`
- [ ] `GiveChallengePrize` — calls `GetPrizeItemId`, adds item to bag, sets receivedPrize flag
- [ ] `CheckFinalTime` — compares timer to bestTime, saves new record if faster
- [ ] `TrainerHillResumeTimer` — re-arms VBlank timer counter (GBA-specific counter; browser needs frame-tick equivalent)
- [ ] `TrainerHillSetPlayerLost` — sets hasLost flag
- [ ] `TrainerHillGetChallengeStatus` — returns LOST / ECARD_SCANNED / NORMAL status
- [ ] `BufferChallengeTime` — formats timer into mm:ss.ff strings in gStringVar1/2/3
- [ ] `GetAllFloorsUsed` — checks numFloors == 4, buffers floor count otherwise
- [ ] `GetInEReaderMode` — always FALSE stub; result used by `OnTrainerHillEReaderChallengeFloor`
- [ ] `IsTrainerHillChallengeActive` — wraps `InTrainerHillChallenge` for script special dispatch
- [ ] `ShowTrainerHillPostBattleText` — calls `CopyTrainerHillTrainerText(AFTER)` + shows field message
- [ ] `SetAllTrainerFlags` — sets trainerFlags = 0xFF (skip all trainers)
- [ ] `GetGameSaved` / `SetGameSaved` / `ClearGameSaved` — savedGame flag r/w
- [ ] `GetChallengeWon` — hasLost → result
- [ ] `TrainerHillSetMode` — writes mode + loads corresponding bestTime
- [ ] `GetPrizeItemId` / `GetPrizeListId` — prize selection from time scored and challenge mode
- [ ] `PrintOnTrainerHillRecordsWindow` — renders time records window for all 4 modes
- [ ] `ResetTrainerHillResults` — clears savedGame, bestTime, resets all mode times to max
- [ ] `SetWarpDestinationTrainerHill4F` / `SetWarpDestinationTrainerHillFinalFloor` — warp target resolution for roof exit
- [ ] `GetTrainerEncounterMusicIdInTrainerHill` — maps facilityClass → trainerClass → encounter music ID
- [ ] `LoadTrainerHillFloorObjectEventScripts` — stub (dummied in C), but needs to return TRUE
- [ ] `TryLoadTrainerHillEReaderPalette` — always no-op in practice; intentionally omitted

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] `SetTrainerHillVBlankCounter` / `ClearTrainerHillVBlankCounter` / `gTrainerHillVBlankCounter` — GBA VBlank interrupt counter; browser equivalent would be a frame-tick accumulator (partial need, not a direct port)
- [/] `GetTimerValue` / `SetTimerValue` — Fire Red XOR encryption leftover, trivially `*src` / `*dst = val` in Emerald; no encryption needed in TS
- [/] `sEReader_Pal` / `TryLoadTrainerHillEReaderPalette` — E-Reader palette load, GBA hardware + JP-exclusive feature

#### Notes
- The Trainer Hill runtime in TS (`trainerHillRuntime.ts`) covers the overworld/layout side well but the **special function dispatch table** (`sHillFunctions`) — the 18 callable specials — is almost entirely missing. These are needed for the Trainer Hill entrance NPC scripts to function (start challenge, give prize, check time, etc.).
- Timer management needs a browser-compatible frame-tick replacement for `gTrainerHillVBlankCounter`; the existing TS has no equivalent.
- `FrontierSpeechToString` (packed trainer speech decoding) is not ported anywhere; it blocks `CopyTrainerHillTrainerText` and thus post-battle dialogue.
- E-Reader mode (`GetInEReaderMode` always returns FALSE in retail Emerald) and JP-exclusive Default mode are intentionally omitted.
- Prize logic (`GetPrizeItemId`) is non-trivial but self-contained; depends only on challenge data and timer, so it is portable.
- `sTrainerClassesAndMusic` table (trainer class → encounter music) is not generated and not hand-ported; needed for correct battle music in Trainer Hill.

## batch: src/trainer_pokemon_sprites.c

### `src/trainer_pokemon_sprites.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/battle/render/BattleSpriteLoader.ts`, `src/rendering/spriteUtils.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `CreateTrainerPicSprite` — creating trainer front-pic sprites for battle UI is handled in `BattleSpriteLoader.ts`
- [x] `CreateMonPicSprite_HandleDeoxys` — Pokémon front/back sprite loading for battle is handled in `BattleSpriteLoader.ts`
- [x] `FreeAndDestroyMonPicSprite` / `FreeAndDestroyTrainerPicSprite` — sprite lifecycle managed implicitly by WebGL texture/sprite system

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `DecompressPic` / `LoadSpecialPokePic` — sprite loading exists but Deoxys form selection by personality is not explicitly handled; form resolution for Deoxys is partially missing
- [-] `LoadPicPaletteByTagOrSlot` — palette management exists but the GBA palette slot/tag system is replaced by WebGL texture units; shiny palette selection by `otId`/`personality` may be incomplete

#### Missing
- [ ] `CreateMonPicSprite_Affine` — affine-animated Pokémon sprites (zoom-in/out entry animations on GBA) not ported; entry animations use a different mechanism in the TS port
- [ ] `ResetAllPicSprites` — explicit slot-based pic sprite pool reset; the TS port doesn't maintain an equivalent fixed-size pool of 8 pic sprites
- [ ] `CreateTrainerCardMonIconSprite` / `CreateTrainerCardTrainerPicSprite` — trainer card screen rendering (blitting pic into a window); trainer card UI is not implemented
- [ ] `LoadPicSpriteInWindow` / `LoadMonPicInWindow` / `LoadTrainerPicInWindow` — GBA window-based sprite loading for menus/trainer card; no equivalent needed in browser
- [ ] `PlayerGenderToFrontTrainerPicId_Debug` — debug utility mapping player gender to trainer pic index; not ported

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] `DecompressPic` (internal GBA decompression) — LZ77/RLE decompression from ROM into VRAM; browser loads PNG assets directly, no decompression needed
- [/] `PIC_SPRITE_SIZE` / `MAX_PIC_FRAMES` / frame buffer allocation pattern — GBA sprite frame memory layout with fixed EWRAM slots; replaced by WebGL texture uploads
- [/] `sSpritePics[PICS_COUNT]` pool management — fixed 8-slot sprite pool for GBA OAM; browser has no OAM slot constraint
- [/] `AssignSpriteAnimsTable` — assigns GBA sprite animation pointer tables; animation is handled by the WebGL animation system differently

#### Notes
The GBA sprite system (OAM, palette slots, EWRAM frame buffers, affine matrix slots) has no direct browser equivalent. The TS port loads Pokémon and trainer sprites as pre-rendered PNGs via `BattleSpriteLoader.ts` and renders them via WebGL. The affine entry animations (`CreateMonPicSprite_Affine`) used for battle zoom-in are the main missing gameplay-visible feature. The trainer card rendering functions are irrelevant until a trainer card UI is implemented.

### `src/trainer_see.c`
**Coverage**: Partial

**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `CheckForTrainersWantingBattle` — top-level scan of all object events for approaching trainers; TS equivalent is `trainerSightEncounter.ts` / `trainerSightProbe.ts`
- [x] `CheckTrainer` — per-trainer flag check + approach distance probe; covered by `trainerSightProbe.ts`
- [x] `GetTrainerApproachDistance` — directional sight-line distance; covered by `trainerSightProbe.ts`
- [x] `GetTrainerApproachDistanceSouth/North/West/East` — 4 directional helpers; all ported in `trainerSightProbe.ts`
- [x] `CheckPathBetweenTrainerAndPlayer` — collision-aware line-of-sight path check; ported in `trainerSightProbe.ts`
- [x] `TrainerExclamationMark` / `WaitTrainerExclamationMark` — exclamation icon spawn + wait; covered by `playTrainerSightIntro.ts`
- [x] `TrainerMoveToPlayer` — step-by-step approach walk; covered by `trainerApproachRuntime.ts`
- [x] `PlayerFaceApproachingTrainer` / `WaitPlayerFaceApproachingTrainer` — player turns to face trainer; covered by `runTrainerSightSequence.ts`
- [x] `RevealDisguisedTrainer` / `WaitRevealDisguisedTrainer` — tree/mountain disguise reveal; covered by `trainerDisguise.ts`
- [x] `DoTrainerApproach` — entry point called from script context to kick off the approach sequence; covered by `trainerApproachRuntime.ts`
- [x] `TryPrepareSecondApproachingTrainer` — double-battle second trainer setup; covered by `trainerSightEncounter.ts`
- [x] `GetCurrentApproachingTrainerObjectEventId` / `GetChosenApproachingTrainerObjectEventId` — accessors for approaching trainer slot; ported in trainer encounter files
- [x] `PlayerFaceTrainerAfterBattle` — post-battle player re-facing; covered by `runTrainerSightSequence.ts`

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `RevealBuriedTrainer` / `PopOutOfAshBuriedTrainer` / `JumpInPlaceBuriedTrainer` / `WaitRevealBuriedTrainer` — buried (ash) trainer pop-out sequence; `trainerDisguise.ts` handles the disguise reveal flow but ash-puff field effect (`FLDEFF_ASH_PUFF`) spawn and the sprite-priority/subpriority manipulation mid-sequence are not wired to a real field effect; the overall state machine steps are present but the visual ash puff is absent
- [-] `InitTrainerApproachTask` / `Task_RunTrainerSeeFuncList` — GBA task-based state machine driving the see-funcs list; TS replaces this with a generator/async coroutine in `trainerApproachRuntime.ts`, functionally equivalent but the `tOutOfAshSpriteId` tracking for buried trainers is incomplete

#### Missing
- [ ] `SetBuriedTrainerMovement` — called on direct player interaction with a buried trainer (not approach); runs the buried reveal sub-sequence outside the normal approach path; no TS equivalent
- [ ] `FldEff_ExclamationMarkIcon` / `FldEff_QuestionMarkIcon` / `FldEff_HeartIcon` — field-effect sprite factory functions that create the floating icons with bobbing animation; the exclamation mark visual is invoked but these specific factory entry points and the heart/question-mark variants are not independently implemented
- [ ] `SpriteCB_TrainerIcons` / `SetIconSpriteData` — sprite callback that tracks the icon above the trainer OBJ sprite position each frame with a bounce velocity; the TS side renders the exclamation as a CSS/canvas element positioned relative to the NPC, not through this per-frame sprite callback

#### Notes
- The core sight-probe logic (line-of-sight, directional distance, collision checks, double-battle detection) is well-ported across the trainer files. The main gap is the **buried trainer** (ash-grass) reveal sub-sequence and the sprite-level icon animation callbacks, which are GBA sprite system details but do have visible gameplay consequences (the bobbing `!` animation and ash puff effect).
- `gWhichTrainerToFaceAfterBattle`, `gPostBattleMovementScript`, `gApproachingTrainers`, `gNoOfApproachingTrainers`, `gTrainerApproachedPlayer` are global state that the TS port tracks inside the encounter/approach runtime objects rather than EWRAM globals.
- The `Task_SetBuriedTrainerMovement` / `SetBuriedTrainerMovement` path (direct interaction with a buried trainer, not triggered by sight) has no TS equivalent — relevant only for the few buried trainers in the game (Route 113 ash-grass trainers).

## batch: src/trig.c

### `src/trig.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/rendering/webgl/WebGLOrbEffectRenderer.ts`, `src/game/jumpArc.ts`, scattered inline usage
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `gSineTable` — Q8.8 fixed-point sine table (320 entries); functionally replaced by `Math.sin()` throughout the TS codebase
- [x] `Sin` — amplitude-scaled sine via table lookup; replaced by `Math.sin()` inline everywhere
- [x] `Cos` — amplitude-scaled cosine via table lookup (index+64 offset trick); replaced by `Math.cos()` inline everywhere

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `gSineDegreeTable` — Q4.12 fixed-point degree-indexed sine table (0°–179°); no TS equivalent of the exact table, but `Sin2`/`Cos2` semantics are covered by `Math.sin(deg * Math.PI / 180)`
- [-] `Sin2` — degree-based sine with 180° periodicity/negation; the modular-negation logic is not explicitly ported, but JS `Math.sin` handles full-circle naturally so functional parity is achieved differently
- [-] `Cos2` — degree-based cosine via `Sin2(angle+90)`; same as above

#### Missing
- [ ] `gSineTable` as exact Q8.8 integer table — needed only if any battle animation or affine transform consumer requires bit-exact GBA fixed-point output; no TS port of the raw table exists
- [ ] `gSineDegreeTable` as exact Q4.12 integer table — same concern for battle animations that index this table directly

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] Q8.8 / Q4.12 fixed-point arithmetic macros (`Q_8_8`, `Q_4_12`) — GBA-specific fixed-point representation; JS uses native floating-point, so these macros have no direct equivalent and none is needed

#### Notes
The entire `trig.c` file exists solely to provide GBA-compatible fixed-point trigonometry without a hardware FPU. The TS port universally substitutes `Math.sin`/`Math.cos` (floating-point), which gives better precision than the GBA tables. The only parity risk is in battle animations (`src/data/battleAnimations.gen.ts`) or sprite affine transforms that may pass raw table indices to C-side logic expecting Q8.8 integer results — those paths would need the exact table if ever bit-exact animation replay is required. Currently no TS code imports or replicates either lookup table.

- [/] `ReceiveTvShowsData` / `ReceivePokeNewsData` (link cable SIO transfer layer) — GBA link cable data exchange; browser has no equivalent; the merge logic itself could theoretically be ported but the transport layer is hardware-specific

#### Notes
- The TV system is almost entirely unimplemented. Only the Gabby & Ty subset (which has its own specials file) has any coverage, and even that is partial.
- Record Mix (link cable show exchange) is intentionally omitted — no link cable in the browser port.
- Contest-related TV show feeds (`ContestLiveUpdates`, `BravoTrainer`, `LilycoveContestLady`) depend on unported Contest system.
- Battle Frontier TV shows depend on unported Battle Frontier.
- `DoTVShow*` functions are the dialogue state machines invoked by `EventScript_TryDoTVShow`; all 30+ are missing.
- `UpdateTVScreensOnMap` is the main entry called on map load to set TV metatiles — partial hook exists via map scripting but the show-selection gating is absent.
- Daily counter increments (`VAR_DAILY_WILDS` etc.) could be wired to existing gameplay paths; `IncrementDailyWildBattles` is the most likely to already have a partial hook in the wild encounter flow.

- [ ] `DoTVShowThePokemonBattleSeminar` — multi-state battle seminar move analysis show
- [ ] `DoTVShowTrainerFanClubSpecial` — multi-state fan club special with score-based branches
- [ ] `DoTVShowTrainerFanClub` — multi-state trainer fan club show branching on trainer ID
- [ ] `DoTVShowSpotTheCuties` — multi-state ribbon showcase show
- [ ] `DoTVShowPokemonNewsBattleFrontier` — multi-state Battle Frontier news show
- [ ] `DoTVShowWhatsNo1InHoennToday` — multi-state "What's No. 1 in Hoenn" stats show
- [ ] `DoTVShowSecretBaseSecrets` — complex multi-state show describing player actions inside a secret base
- [ ] `DoTVShowSafariFanClub` — multi-state Safari Zone fan club show
- [ ] `DoTVShowLilycoveContestLady` — Lilycove Contest Lady variant of contest live updates show
- [ ] `TVShowDone` — marks show inactive, resets state, sets result flag
- [ ] `ResetTVShowState` — resets sTVShowState to 0
- [ ] `SanitizeTVShowsForRuby` — clears cross-language Battle Tower shows for Ruby compatibility
- [ ] `SanitizeTVShowLocationsForRuby` — clears shows with Kanto locations for Ruby compatibility
- [ ] `TranslateRubyShows` — detects Japanese strings in Ruby shows and sets language fields
- [ ] `TranslateJapaneseEmeraldShows` — sets language fields on all show types based on string content
- [ ] `SecretBaseSecrets_GetNumActionsTaken` — counts set bits in secretBaseSecrets.flags
- [ ] `SecretBaseSecrets_GetStateByFlagNumber` — maps flag index to a dialog state enum value

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] `TranslateRubyShows` / `TranslateJapaneseEmeraldShows` / `SanitizeTVShowsForRuby` — these exist purely for link-cable record mixing between GBA cartridges (Ruby/Sapphire ↔ Emerald); no multiplayer record mixing feature exists in the browser port
- [/] `GetStringLanguage` — helper for Japanese vs non-Japanese string detection used only by the above sanitization functions

#### Notes
The entire TV show system is unimplemented. It depends on `gSaveBlock1Ptr->tvShows[]`, a save-block array populated through gameplay events (shopping, catching, contests, link battles, etc.). The port has `GameFlags`/`GameVariables` but no `TVShow` save-block structure. Contests, link cable record mixing, Gabby & Ty, mass outbreaks, Safari Zone, and secret bases are all also unimplemented, so many of these shows have no upstream data to display even if the dialog runner were ported. The `DoTVShowInSearchOfTrainers` (Gabby & Ty) show is the only one with any adjacent scripting support referenced in the codebase (`src/scripting/specials/gabbyAndTySpecials.ts`), but the actual dialog show runner is still absent.

- [ ] `TradeBoardMenuHandler` — 3-state list-menu driver for the trading board
- [ ] `UnionRoomHandleYesNo` — 2-state yes/no menu driver
- [ ] `PrintUnionRoomText` — coloured text printer for Union Room windows
- [ ] `PrintOnTextbox` — 2-state textbox printer helper used throughout the Union Room tasks
- [ ] Various message-building helpers (`GetAwaitingCommunicationText`, `GetYouDeclinedTheOfferMessage`, `Leader_GetAcceptNewMemberPrompt`, `GetURoomActivityStartMsg`, `GetURoomActivityRejectMsg`, `GetGroupLeaderSentAnOKMessage`, `GetYouAskedToJoinGroupPleaseWaitMessage`, `ConvPartnerUnameAndGetWhetherMetAlready`, `ViewURoomPartnerTrainerCard`, `GetChatLeaderActionRequestMessage`) — build locale strings for UI prompts

- [ ] `TradeBoardPrintItemInfo` — renders one row of the trading board (name + type icon + species + level)
- [ ] `HasWonderCardOrNewsByLinkGroup` — checks if a partner's RFU game data carries the requested card/news
- [ ] `WarpForWirelessMinigame` / `WarpForCableClubActivity` — warp helpers that set cable-club VAR and destination before entering a link map
- [ ] `CreateTrainerCardInBuffer` — serialises the local trainer card into the block-send buffer
- [ ] `RegisterTradeMonAndGetIsEgg` / `RegisterTradeMon` / `ResetUnionRoomTrade` — manage the local Union Room trade registration struct
- [ ] `GetPartyPositionOfRegisteredMon` / `IsRequestedTradeInPlayerParty` / `GetIndexOfNthTradeBoardOffer` — trade-board matching helpers
- [ ] `HasAtLeastTwoMonsOfLevel30OrLower` — eligibility check for level-30 battles in the Union Room
- [ ] `IsActivityWithVariableGroupSize` — returns TRUE for activities that can have flexible player counts
- [ ] `ItemPrintFunc_PossibleGroupMembers` / `ListMenuItemPrintFunc_UnionRoomGroups` / `TradeBoardListMenuItemPrintFunc` / `ItemPrintFunc_EmptyList` — list-menu item print callbacks
- [ ] `UR_ClearBg0` — clears BG0 tilemap used by the trading board overlay
- [ ] `UR_PrintFieldMessage` / `UR_RunTextPrinters` — field-message helpers for Union Room
- [ ] `CopyPlayerListToBuffer` / `CopyPlayerListFromBuffer` — save/restore player list around party-selection screen
- [ ] `ScheduleFieldMessageWithFollowupState` / `ScheduleFieldMessageAndExit` — state-machine helpers to queue a message then transition

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] All RFU/wireless-adapter hardware routines (`InitializeRfuLinkManager_*`, `Rfu_SendPacket`, `RfuGetStatus`, `SetHostRfuGameData`, `SetWirelessCommType1`, `OpenLink`, `LinkRfu_Shutdown`, etc.) — GBA RF-unit hardware; no browser equivalent
- [/] `CreateWirelessStatusIndicatorSprite` / `LoadWirelessStatusIndicatorSpriteGfx` / `DestroyWirelessStatusIndicatorSprite` — GBA sprite for wireless signal strength indicator
- [/] `CB2_ShowCard` / `CB2_TransitionToCableClub` — GBA main-loop callbacks; architecture replaced by React state machine
- [/] Block-send/receive primitives (`SendBlock`, `GetBlockReceivedStatus`, `ResetBlockReceivedFlags`, `gBlockSendBuffer`, `gBlockRecvBuffer`) — GBA link-cable/RFU packet layer

#### Notes
`union_room.c` implements the Gen 3 wireless Union Room: player discovery via RFU, activity negotiation (battles, trades, chat, Wonder Card distribution, minigames), and the in-room trading board. It is entirely multiplayer infrastructure tied to the GBA's RF-unit hardware. The browser port has no multiplayer or wireless system, and there is no generated file covering this logic. The entire file is intentionally out of scope for a single-player browser port; the map scripts (`UnionRoom.gen.ts`) and the `RecordCorner.gen.ts` / `TradeCenter.gen.ts` generated files cover only the scripted NPC dialogue, not the link-layer runtime.

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] `TradeBoardListMenuItemPrintFunc` — renders trade board list menu items over wireless link (RFU hardware)
- [/] `GetIndexOfNthTradeBoardOffer` — finds nth trade offer from wireless player list
- [/] `GetUnionRoomPlayerGender` — reads player gender from RFU player list
- [/] `IsRequestedTradeInPlayerParty` — checks if requested trade species/type exists in local party
- [/] `GetURoomActivityRejectMsg` — formats activity-rejected messages for wireless session
- [/] `GetURoomActivityStartMsg` — formats activity-start messages for wireless session
- [/] `GetChatLeaderActionRequestMessage` — formats multiplayer activity request prompt (battle/chat/trade/card)
- [/] `PollPartnerYesNoResponse` — polls wireless receive buffer for yes/no response from partner
- [/] `InUnionRoom` — checks if player is currently in the Union Room map
- [/] `HasAtLeastTwoMonsOfLevel30OrLower` — validates Union Room battle eligibility (≤Lv30, ≥2 mons)
- [/] `ResetUnionRoomTrade` / `Script_ResetUnionRoomTrade` — resets trade state struct
- [/] `RegisterTradeMonAndGetIsEgg` / `RegisterTradeMon` — registers party mon for wireless trade
- [/] `GetPartyPositionOfRegisteredMon` — finds party slot of a registered mon by species+personality
- [/] `HandleCancelActivity` — tears down Union Room activity session (unlock controls, clear data)
- [/] `StartScriptInteraction` — locks player controls during wireless scripted interaction
- [/] `GetActivePartnersInfo` — packs active partner gender/TID into a byte for RFU state
- [/] `ViewURoomPartnerTrainerCard` — formats partner's trainer card info over wireless link
- [/] `CopyAndTranslatePlayerName` — copies and converts player name charset from RFU packet

#### Notes
The entire `union_room.c` is Union Room wireless multiplayer logic — GBA Radio Frequency Unit (RFU) link cable replacement, local wireless trade/battle/chat/trainer card exchange. This requires a full GBA wireless adapter hardware abstraction (`gRfuLinkStatus`, `gRecvCmds`, `RfuPlayer`, `RfuGameData`, etc.) that has no browser equivalent. The Union Room as a multiplayer lobby is intentionally out of scope for a single-player browser port. The map scripts in `src/data/scripts/UnionRoom.gen.ts` cover only the overworld NPC/text scripts for the room itself, not any of this runtime multiplayer logic.

- [/] `SetChatFunction` — sets current state machine function ID

- [/] `DeleteLastMessageCharacter` — backspace on message buffer
- [/] `SwitchCaseOfLastMessageCharacter` — toggles case of last typed character via `sCaseToggleTable`

- [/] `Display_LoadGfx` — loads all GBA BG/sprite graphics for chat UI

- [/] `Display_SwitchPages` — animated horizontal keyboard slide transition

- [/] `Display_Dummy` — no-op subtask placeholder
- [/] `AddYesNoMenuAt` / `HideYesNoMenuWindow` / `DestroyYesNoMenuWindow` — yes/no window management
- [/] `ProcessMenuInput` — thin wrapper around `Menu_ProcessInput`
- [/] `AddStdMessageWindow` / `HideStdMessageWindow` / `DestroyStdMessageWindow` — standard message box management
- [/] `FillTextEntryWindow` / `DrawTextEntryMessage` — text entry window rendering
- [/] `PrintCurrentKeyboardPage` — renders keyboard characters/registered texts into keyboard window
- [/] `SlideKeyboardPageOut` / `SlideKeyboardPageIn` / `UpdateSlidingKeyboard` / `FinishSlidingKeyboard` — GBA scanline-effect horizontal BG1 slide animation
- [/] `TryAllocSprites` / `FreeSprites` / `CreateKeyboardCursorSprite` / `CreateTextEntrySprites` / `CreateRButtonSprites` — OAM sprite allocation
- [/] `SetKeyboardCursorInvisibility` / `MoveKeyboardCursor` / `SetRegisteredTextPalette` / `StartKeyboardCursorAnim` / `TryKeyboardCursorReopen` / `UpdateRButtonLabel` — sprite state management
- [/] `SpriteCB_TextEntryCursor` / `SpriteCB_TextEntryArrow` — OAM sprite callbacks
- [/] `PrintChatMessage` — renders one chat line into the history window
- [/] `ResetGpuBgState` / `SetBgTilemapBuffers` / `ClearBg0` — GBA GPU register/BG setup
- [/] `LoadKeyboardWindowGfx` / `LoadChatWindowGfx` / `LoadChatUnkPalette` / `LoadChatMessagesWindow` / `DrawKeyboardWindow` / `LoadTextEntryWindow` / `LoadKeyboardSwapWindow` — GBA graphics loading
- [/] `InitScanlineEffect` — configures GBA DMA scanline effect for keyboard slide
- [/] `InitUnionRoomChatRegisteredTexts` — seeds save block with default registered quick-text strings
- [/] Various accessor stubs (`GetCurrentKeyboardPage`, `GetCurrentKeyboardColAndRow`, `GetMessageEntryBuffer`, `GetLengthOfMessageEntry`, `GetBufferSelectionRegion`, `GetLastReceivedMessage`, `GetReceivedPlayerIndex`, `GetTextEntryCursorPosition`, `GetShouldShowCaseToggleIcon`, `GetChatHostName`, `GetRegisteredTextByRow`) — internal getters for display/input code

#### Notes
This file implements the Union Room chat feature — a GBA wireless link (RFU) multiplayer text-chat system. The entire feature depends on GBA-specific link/RFU hardware (`SendBlock`, `GetBlockReceivedStatus`, `Rfu_*`), DMA, scanline effects, and OAM sprites. There is no browser multiplayer equivalent and the Union Room as a whole is not planned for the TS port. `InitUnionRoomChatRegisteredTexts` writes default quick-text to the save block, which could theoretically be ported as part of new-game initialization, but it is trivial and the registered-text feature itself would not exist in the browser port.

This file is entirely Union Room (GBA wireless link / RFU) multiplayer infrastructure. The browser port has no wireless multiplayer, no RFU, no virtual-object sprite system, and no Union Room game mode. Every function and data table here is hardware- and feature-specific with no browser equivalent. This is an intentional omission; even a complete browser port would not need this file.

- [ ] `SpriteCB_UpDown` — bounces up/down arrow sprites then destroys them after 60 frames
- [ ] `SpriteCB_MonPic` — keeps mon sprite x-position locked to the animated scroll offset
- [ ] `SpriteCB_SelectionIconPokeball` / `SpriteCB_SelectionIconCancel` — highlight/unhighlight party list icons based on current selection
- [ ] `LoadUsePokeblockMenuGfx` — 12-step helper loading BG tiles, tilemaps, palettes, and blend registers

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] `VBlankCB_UsePokeblockMenu` — VBlank ISR: OAM DMA, palette DMA, scanline-effect HBlank DMA; browser uses RAF/WebGL, no equivalent needed
- [/] `LoadUsePokeblockMenuGfx` cases 0, 3–11 — GBA BG register configuration, LZ77 VRAM decompression, DMA-backed tilemap uploads; rendering is handled by WebGL pipeline instead

#### Notes
The entire Pokéblock use/condition screen has no TypeScript equivalent. The condition graph rendering (pentagon chart), sparkle sprites, and the stat-boost calculation (`CalculatePokeblockEffectiveness` with nature flavor affinity) are all absent. The Pokéblock system itself (`pokeblock.c`, condition data on `Pokemon` structs — `MON_DATA_COOL/TOUGH/SMART/CUTE/BEAUTY/SHEEN`) has no TS data layer either. This is post-early-game content (Contests) and appears intentionally deferred; the `BagManager.ts` and party menu exist but carry no condition/sheen fields.

## batch: src/util.c

### `src/util.c`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/save/native/Gen3SaveParser.ts`, `src/rendering/spriteUtils.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `CalcCRC16WithTable` — CRC-16 checksum used for save validation; implemented in `Gen3SaveParser.ts` for save file integrity checks
- [x] `CalcByteArraySum` — byte array sum; used inline in save parser for section checksums

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `BlendPalette` — palette blending by coefficient; partial equivalent exists in `WeatherColorPipeline.ts` / `WebGLFadeRenderer.ts` for fade effects, but not a direct general-purpose palette blend utility

#### Missing
- [ ] `CalcCRC16` — software CRC-16 (no-table variant); redundant given table variant, but not ported
- [ ] `CopySpriteTiles` — copies OAM sprite tiles with optional x/y flip; GBA tile format not used in browser renderer
- [ ] `CountTrailingZeroBits` — bit utility; not ported (JS has `Math.clz32` equivalent available but unused)
- [ ] `StoreWordInTwoHalfwords` / `LoadWordFromTwoHalfwords` — 32-bit word split/join across two u16 halfwords; not needed in JS
- [ ] `SetBgAffineStruct` / `DoBgAffineSet` — GBA BG affine transformation setup; no browser equivalent needed
- [ ] `CreateInvisibleSpriteWithCallback` — creates an off-screen invisible GBA sprite for callback use; GBA sprite system not used

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] `gBitTable` — precomputed power-of-two lookup; JS uses `1 << n` directly
- [/] `sSpriteDimensions` — GBA OAM sprite dimension table (shape×size → tiles wide/tall); GBA hardware sprite system not used
- [/] `sCrc16Table` — CRC-16 lookup table; embedded in save parser if needed
- [/] `gMiscBlank_Gfx` — blank 4bpp tile graphic binary; no browser equivalent needed
- [/] `SetBgAffineStruct` / `DoBgAffineSet` — GBA hardware BG affine registers; handled by WebGL matrix transforms instead
- [/] `CopySpriteTiles` — GBA 4bpp tile copy with flip; WebGL renderer handles flips via UV coordinates

#### Notes
`CalcCRC16WithTable` is the one function with direct browser relevance — it's used to validate Gen 3 save file sections. The save parser in `Gen3SaveParser.ts` implements this inline. All GBA hardware utilities (affine BG, OAM sprite tile copying, halfword packing) have no browser equivalent and are intentionally omitted. The `BlendPalette` function has a conceptual analog in the WebGL fade/weather color pipeline but is not a direct port.

## batch: src/walda_phrase.c

### `src/walda_phrase.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
- [ ] `TryBufferWaldaPhrase` — copies saved Walda phrase into gStringVar1; returns FALSE if empty
- [ ] `DoWaldaNamingScreen` — opens the naming screen pre-populated with the current Walda phrase
- [ ] `CB2_HandleGivenWaldaPhrase` — callback after naming screen; classifies input and persists new phrase, then returns to field
- [ ] `GetWaldaPhraseInputCase` — classifies input as PHRASE_EMPTY / PHRASE_NO_CHANGE / PHRASE_CHANGED
- [ ] `TryGetWallpaperWithWaldaPhrase` — calls `TryCalculateWallpaper`, stores results in save block if successful
- [ ] `TryCalculateWallpaper` — core algorithm: validates 15-char phrase against allowed letter table, encodes 75 bits into 9-byte data array, applies rotation/mask, checks trainer-ID XOR checksum, outputs bg/fg colors + icon/pattern IDs
- [ ] `GetLetterTableId` — maps a GBA charset byte to its 5-bit index in `sWaldaLettersTable`
- [ ] `RotateWallpaperDataLeft` — rotates an N-byte array left by `numShifts` bits
- [ ] `MaskWallpaperData` — XORs each byte of data with `mask | (mask << 4)`
- [ ] `SetWallpaperDataFromLetter` — copies `numBits` bits from one bit-array position to another
- [ ] `GetWallpaperDataBits` — reads `numBits` consecutive bits from a bit-array, returns as u32
- [ ] `GetWallpaperDataBit` / `SetWallpaperDataBit` / `ClearWallpaperDataBit` — single-bit accessors on a byte array

#### No good TS-equivalent
*(none — the whole feature is gameplay-relevant PC Box wallpaper unlock via secret phrase)*

#### Notes
This file implements the Walda secret-phrase mechanic: a player types a 15-character phrase to a girl in Slateport City; a cryptographic-style bit-packing algorithm validates the phrase against the player's trainer ID and, if valid, unlocks a custom PC Box wallpaper with specific colors, icon, and pattern. The algorithm is entirely self-contained and has no dependency on GBA hardware beyond save-block reads. No TS equivalent exists anywhere in the port. The naming screen (`DoNamingScreen`) is also not yet ported. This feature is post-optional (not on the critical story path) but would need the naming screen and save-block Walda fields (`GetWaldaPhrasePtr`, `SetWaldaPhrase`, `SetWaldaWallpaper*`) implemented before it can be wired up.

- [ ] `LoadWallClockGraphics` / `WallClockInit` / `VBlankCB_WallClock` — GBA display setup (hardware-specific but logic includes palette selection by player gender)

- [/] `VBlankCB_WallClock` — GBA VBlank interrupt, OAM DMA, palette transfer (pure GBA hardware)
- [/] `LoadWallClockGraphics` — GBA BG/OAM/VRAM/DMA initialization, LZ77 VRAM decompression
- [/] `WallClockInit` — sets GBA GPU registers (`DISPCNT`, `BLDCNT`, etc.)

#### Notes
The wall clock is the in-game clock-setting UI accessible from the options/clock screen (not the RTC itself). No part of this has been ported. The browser port uses `GameVariables` / `GameFlags` for time-gated events but has no interactive clock-setting UI. The RTC dependency (`RtcInitLocalTimeOffset`, `RtcCalcLocalTime`) would map to a JS `Date`-based time offset stored in save state. The `sClockHandCoords` table and AM/PM indicator orbit logic are self-contained and straightforward to port if a visual clock screen is ever needed. Gender-aware palette selection (`gSpecialVar_0x8004 == MALE`) is noted for future implementation.

- Safari Zone Pokeblock nature bias is intentionally out of scope (Safari Zone not implemented).

- Mass outbreak (Poké Radar) system absent — `outbreakPokemon*` SaveBlock1 fields not populated by anything in the TS port.
- Feebas spot logic depends on `dewfordTrends[0].rand` (Dewford Town trend seed) which is also unimplemented.

## batch: src/window.c

### `src/window.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/core/prompt/PromptCanvasRenderer.ts`, `src/core/prompt/PromptWindowProfile.ts`, `src/components/dialog/DialogBox.tsx`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] Window rendering system — The TS port replaces the GBA BG-layer window system entirely with Canvas 2D / HTML rendering in `PromptCanvasRenderer`, `DialogBox`, `OptionMenu`, etc.

#### Missing
- [ ] `InitWindows` — initializes window array from template list, allocates BG tilemap buffers
- [ ] `AddWindow` / `AddWindowWithoutTileMap` — allocates a window slot and tile buffer
- [ ] `RemoveWindow` — frees window slot and associated tile data
- [ ] `FreeAllWindowBuffers` — bulk free of all BG tilemap and tile data buffers
- [ ] `CopyWindowToVram` / `CopyWindowRectToVram` — flushes tile data and tilemap to VRAM
- [ ] `PutWindowTilemap` / `PutWindowRectTilemap` / `PutWindowRectTilemapOverridePalette` — writes tile indices into BG tilemap buffer
- [ ] `ClearWindowTilemap` — fills tilemap region with transparent tile number
- [ ] `BlitBitmapToWindow` / `BlitBitmapRectToWindow` — blits 4bpp pixel data into window tile buffer
- [ ] `FillWindowPixelRect` / `FillWindowPixelBuffer` — fills pixel region in window tile buffer with solid color
- [ ] `CopyToWindowPixelBuffer` — copies or LZ77-decompresses tile data into window buffer
- [ ] `ScrollWindow` — scrolls pixel content within a window up or down with fill
- [ ] `CallWindowFunction` — invokes a callback with window geometry params
- [ ] `SetWindowAttribute` / `GetWindowAttribute` — get/set individual window struct fields
- [ ] `AddWindow8Bit` / `FillWindowPixelBuffer8Bit` / `FillWindowPixelRect8Bit` / `BlitBitmapRectToWindow4BitTo8Bit` / `CopyWindowToVram8Bit` — 8bpp window variants

#### No good TS-equivalent
- [/] All of the above — This entire file manages GBA BG-layer tile windows: VRAM tile allocation, tilemap buffer management, 4bpp/8bpp tile blitting, and DMA-to-VRAM uploads. The TS port uses HTML/Canvas for all UI rendering and has no concept of BG layers, tilemap buffers, or VRAM. None of this needs porting.

#### Notes
`src/window.c` is pure GBA hardware abstraction for the text/UI window system (the layer underneath `print_text`, dialog boxes, menus, etc.). The TS port replaces this entire subsystem with `PromptCanvasRenderer` (Canvas 2D font rendering), `PromptWindowProfile`/`PromptWindowSkin` (layout/skin config), and React components (`DialogBox`, `OptionMenu`). There is no meaningful function to port — the architectural mismatch is total and intentional.

### `src/wireless_communication_status_screen.c`
**Coverage**: None
**TS equivalent(s)**: None
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
*(none)*

#### Partially Implemented
*(none)*

#### Missing
*(none)*

#### No good TS-equivalent

- [/] `ShowWirelessCommunicationScreen` — entry point for the wireless status screen UI
- [/] `CB2_InitWirelessCommunicationScreen` — GBA-specific BG/palette/DMA initialization for the screen
- [/] `CB2_RunWirelessCommunicationScreen` — main loop: RunTasks/AnimateSprites/OAM/palette fade
- [/] `CB2_ExitWirelessCommunicationStatusScreen` — tears down BG buffers and returns to field
- [/] `VBlankCB_WirelessCommunicationScreen` — VBlank interrupt: LoadOam/TransferPlttBuffer
- [/] `CyclePalette` — animates the wave effect by cycling through 14 GBA palettes
- [/] `PrintHeaderTexts` — fills BG window tiles with localized group-type labels
- [/] `Task_WirelessCommunicationScreen` — 6-state task: fade-in → poll counts → input → fade-out
- [/] `WCSS_AddTextPrinterParameterized` — wrapper for GBA text printer with 5 color modes
- [/] `CountPlayersInGroupAndGetActivity` — reads `RfuPlayer` structs from RFU hardware, tallies group counts
- [/] `HaveCountsChanged` — simple diff check on the 4-element group-count arrays
- [/] `UpdateCommunicationCounts` — polls all RFU task slots, detects activity/count changes, updates display

#### Notes
This file is entirely GBA wireless-link hardware (RFU = RF Unit, the GBA Wireless Adapter). It implements a live status screen showing how many nearby players are trading, battling, or in the Union Room. All functionality depends on `librfu`/`link_rfu` hardware drivers and the GBA task/OAM/BG system. There is no browser equivalent — multiplayer networking for the browser port would require a completely different architecture (WebSockets, WebRTC, etc.) and this screen would be replaced wholesale. Intentionally omitted.

This entire file implements the Wonder News reward subsystem, a Japan-exclusive Gen 3 Mystery Gift feature delivered via e-Reader or wireless. It is entirely absent from the TS port. Because the feature requires GBA link hardware and the e-Reader peripheral, and was never used in the Western release of Emerald, intentional omission is appropriate. The only gameplay-adjacent piece that *could* be stubbed is the berry-reward delivery if Wonder Cards were ever surfaced through a browser equivalent of Mystery Gift, but no such system exists in the port.

#### Notes
Battle animation system is data-driven (generated) but the runtime animation playback layer is largely unimplemented. The TS port has WebGL rendering infrastructure but no per-move animation sequencer. Browser animations would be reimplemented differently from GBA OAM/BG animation system.

### `include/battle_util.h`
**Coverage**: Partial
**TS equivalent(s)**: `src/battle/engine/BattleEngine.ts`, `src/battle/engine/MoveEffects.ts`, `src/battle/engine/StatusEffects.ts`
**Generation script**: None

#### Implemented
- [x] `MOVE_LIMITATION_*` flags — move usability check flags; used in engine
- [x] `ABILITYEFFECT_*` constants — ability effect trigger IDs; referenced in engine
- [x] `ITEMEFFECT_*` constants — item effect trigger contexts; referenced
- [x] `HandleAction_UseMove` — move execution action; implemented in `BattleEngine.ts`
- [x] `HandleAction_Switch` — switch action handler; implemented
- [x] `HandleAction_UseItem` — item use action; partially implemented
- [x] `HandleAction_Run` — run action; implemented for wild battles
- [x] `HandleAction_TryFinish` / `HandleAction_ActionFinished` — turn/battle end; implemented
- [x] `CheckMoveLimitations` — checks which moves are blocked; implemented
- [x] `AreAllMovesUnusable` — checks if battler has no usable moves; implemented
- [x] `DoFieldEndTurnEffects` — weather/terrain end-turn; implemented in `Weather.ts`
- [x] `DoBattlerEndTurnEffects` — per-battler end-turn effects (burn, poison, etc.); implemented in `StatusEffects.ts`
- [x] `CancelMultiTurnMoves` — cancels charge/recharge moves on switch; implemented
- [x] `BattleScriptPush` / `BattleScriptPushCursor` / `BattleScriptPop` — script stack management; implemented in `BattleScriptRuntime.ts`
- [x] `PrepareStringBattle` — queues battle message; implemented in message system
- [x] `GetMoveTarget` — determines move target; implemented
- [x] `AbilityBattleEffects` — dispatches ability triggers; partially implemented
- [x] `ItemBattleEffects` — dispatches item triggers; partially implemented
- [x] `AtkCanceler_UnableToUseMove` — checks pre-move cancellation conditions; implemented

#### Partially Implemented
- [-] `HandleFaintedMonActions` — post-faint replacement logic; basic faint handled, multi-battle replacement incomplete
- [-] `HandleWishPerishSongOnTurnEnd` — Wish/Perish Song turn decrement; Wish implemented, Perish Song implemented
- [-] `IsMonDisobedient` — disobedience check; stubbed (always obedient)
- [-] `TrySetCantSelectMoveBattleScript` — move selection validation; partial coverage
- [-] `HasNoMonsToSwitch` — checks if trainer has replacement mons; partially checked

#### Missing
- [ ] `HandleAction_WatchesCarefully` / `HandleAction_SafariZoneBallThrow` / `HandleAction_ThrowPokeblock` / `HandleAction_GoNear` / `HandleAction_SafariZoneRun` / `HandleAction_WallyBallThrow` — Safari/Wally battle actions
- [ ] `PressurePPLose` / `PressurePPLoseOnUsingPerishSong` / `PressurePPLoseOnUsingImprison` — Pressure ability PP drain
- [ ] `MarkBattlerForControllerExec` / `MarkBattlerReceivedLinkData` — controller sync flags
- [ ] `ResetSentPokesToOpponentValue` / `OpponentSwitchInResetSentPokesToOpponentValue` / `UpdateSentPokesToOpponentValue` — tracks which mons opponent has seen
- [ ] `GetImprisonedMovesCount` — counts shared moves for Imprison
- [ ] `TryClearRageStatuses` — clears Rage status at turn end
- [ ] `CastformDataTypeChange` — Castform weather form change trigger
- [ ] `BattleScriptExecute` / `BattleScriptPushCursorAndCallback` — script execution entry points
- [ ] `ClearFuryCutterDestinyBondGrudge` — clears multi-turn counter moves on switch
- [ ] `HandleAction_RunBattleScript` — action that just runs a pending script

#### Notes
Core battle utility layer is substantially ported. Missing: Safari battle, Wally battle, Pressure ability, Castform weather change, full link sync markers.

- [x] `IsRunningDisallowed` — checks metatile for run restriction; implemented in `metatileBehaviors.ts`

Bike system is well-ported with parity tests. Acro bike trick jumps (side jump, turn jump) are implemented. No intentional omissions beyond RS compatibility variant.
